final_text: daniel greenfield a shillman journalism fellow freedom center a new york writer focusing radical islam in final stretch of election hillary rodham clinton has gone to war with fbi word unprecedented has been thrown around so often this election that it ought to be retired but it s still unprecedented for nominee of a major political party to go war with fbi but that s exactly what hillary and her people have done coma patients just waking up now and watching an hour of cnn from their hospital beds would assume that fbi director james comey hillary s opponent in this election fbi under attack by everyone from obama to cnn hillary s people have circulated a letter attacking comey there are currently more media hit pieces lambasting him than targeting trump it wouldn t be too surprising if clintons or their allies were to start running attack ads against fbi fbi s leadership being warned that entire leftwing establishment will form a lynch mob if they continue going after hillary and fbi s credibility being attacked by media and democrats to preemptively head off results of investigation of clinton foundation and hillary clinton covert struggle between fbi agents and obama s doj people has gone explosively public new york times has compared comey to j edgar hoover its bizarre headline james comey role recalls hoover s fbi fairly or not practically admits up front that it s spouting nonsense boston globe has published a column calling for comey s resignation not to be outdone time has an editorial claiming that scandal really an attack all women james carville appeared msnbc to remind everyone that he was still alive and insane he accused comey of coordinating with house republicans and kgb and thought vast right wing conspiracy was a stretch countless media stories charge comey with violating procedure do know what s a procedural violation emailing classified information stored bathroom server senator harry reid has sent comey a letter accusing him of violating hatch act hatch act a nice idea that has as much relevance in age of obama as tenth amendment but cable news spectrum quickly filled with media hacks glancing wikipedia article hatch act under table while accusing fbi director of one of most awkward conspiracies against hillary ever if james comey really out to hurt hillary he picked one hell of a strange way to do it not too long ago democrats were breathing a sigh of relief when he gave hillary clinton a pass in a prominent public statement if he really were out to elect trump by keeping email scandal going why did he trash investigation was he payroll of house republicans and kgb back then and playing it coy or was it a sudden development where vladimir putin and paul ryan talked him into taking a look anthony weiner s computer either comey most cunning fbi director that ever lived or he s just awkwardly trying to navigate a political mess that has trapped him between a doj leadership whose political futures are tied to hillary s victory and his own bureau whose apolitical agents just want to be allowed to do their jobs only truly mysterious thing why hillary and her associates decided to go to war with a respected federal agency most americans like fbi while hillary clinton enjoys a unfavorable rating and it s an interesting question hillary s old strategy was to lie and deny that fbi even had a criminal investigation underway instead her associates insisted that it was a security review fbi corrected her and she shrugged it off but old breezy denial approach has given way to a savage assault fbi pretending that nothing was wrong was a bad strategy but it was a better one that picking a fight with fbi while lunatic clinton associates try to claim that fbi really kgb there are two possible explanations hillary clinton might be arrogant enough to lash out fbi now that she believes that victory near same kind of hubris that led her to plan her victory fireworks display could lead her to declare a war fbi for irritating her during final miles of her campaign but other explanation that her people panicked going to war with fbi not behavior of a smart and focused presidential campaign it s an act of desperation when a presidential candidate decides that her only option to try and destroy credibility of fbi that s not hubris it s fear of what fbi might be about to reveal about her during original fbi investigation hillary clinton was confident that she could ride it out and she had good reason for believing that but that hillary clinton gone in her place a paranoid wreck within a short space of time positive clinton campaign promising to unite country has been replaced by a desperate and flailing operation that has focused all its energy fighting fbi there s only one reason for such bizarre behavior clinton campaign has decided that an fbi investigation of latest batch of emails poses a threat to its survival and so it s gone all in fighting fbi it s an unprecedented step born of fear it s hard to know whether that fear justified but existence of that fear already tells us a whole lot clinton loyalists rigged old investigation they knew outcome ahead of time as well as they knew debate questions now suddenly they are no longer in control and they are afraid can smell fear fbi has wiretaps from investigation of clinton foundation it s finding new emails all time and clintonworld panicked spinmeisters of clintonworld have claimed that email scandal just so much smoke without fire all that s here appearance of impropriety without any of substance but this isn t how react to smoke it s how respond to a fire misguided assault fbi tells us that hillary clinton and her allies are afraid of a revelation bigger than fundamental illegality of her email setup email setup was a preemptive cover up clinton campaign has panicked badly out of belief right or wrong that whatever crime illegal setup was meant to cover up risk of being exposed clintons have weathered countless scandals over years whatever they are protecting this time around bigger than usual corruption bribery sexual assaults and abuses of power that have followed them around throughout years this bigger and more damaging than any of allegations that have already come out and they don t want fbi investigators anywhere near it campaign against comey pure intimidation it s also a warning any senior fbi people who value their careers are being warned to stay away democrats are closing ranks around their nominee against fbi it s an ugly and unprecedented scene it may also be their last stand hillary clinton has awkwardly wound her way through numerous scandals in just this election cycle but she s never shown fear or desperation before now that has changed whatever she afraid of it lies buried in her emails with huma abedin and it can bring her down like nothing else has

us secretary of state john f kerry said monday that he will stop in paris later this week amid criticism that no top american officials attended sunday s unity march against terrorism kerry said he expects to arrive in paris thursday evening as he heads home after a week abroad he said he will fly to france conclusion of a series of meetings scheduled for thursday in sofia bulgaria he plans to meet next day with foreign minister laurent fabius and president francois hollande then return to washington visit by kerry who has family and childhood ties to country and speaks fluent french could address some of criticism that united states snubbed france in its darkest hour in many years french press monday was filled with questions about why neither president obama nor kerry attended sunday s march as about leaders of other nations did obama was said to have stayed away because his own security needs can be taxing a country and kerry had prior commitments among roughly leaders who did attend was israeli prime minister benjamin netanyahu no stranger to intense security who marched beside hollande through city streets highest ranking us officials attending march were jane hartley ambassador to france and victoria nuland assistant secretary of state for european affairs attorney general eric h holder jr was in paris for meetings with law enforcement officials but did not participate in march kerry spent sunday a business summit hosted by india s prime minister narendra modi united states eager for india to relax stringent laws that function as barriers to foreign investment and hopes modi s government will act to open huge indian market for more american businesses in a news conference kerry brushed aside criticism that united states had not sent a more senior official to paris as quibbling a little bit he noted that many staffers of american embassy in paris attended march including ambassador he said he had wanted to be present march himself but could not because of his prior commitments in india but that why am going there way home to make it crystal clear how passionately feel about events that have taken place there he said and don t think people of france have any doubts about america s understanding of what happened of personal sense of loss and deep commitment to people of france in this moment of trauma

m not an immigrant but grandparents are more than years ago they arrived in new york city from iran grew up mainly in central new jersey an american kid playing little league for raritan red sox and soccer for raritan rovers in travelled with family to ancestral land was only eight but old enough to understand that iranians had lost their liberty and freedom saw abject despair of a people who in a desperate attempt to bring about change had ushered in nationalist tyrants led by ayatollah khomeini what witnessed during that year in iran changed course of life in age wanting to help preserve blessings of liberty and freedom enjoy in america enlisted in us navy now with rise of donald trump and his nationalist altright movement ve come to feel that values sought to protect are in jeopardy in iran theocratic fundmentalists sowed division and hatred of outsiders of westerners christians and other religious minorities here in america right wing seems to have stolen passages directly from their playbook as it spreads hatred of immigrants particularly muslim ones this form of nationalistic bigotry islamophobia threatens heart of nation when chose to serve in military did so to protect what viewed as sacred foundational values of liberty equality and democracy now years later ve joined forces with fellow veterans to again fight for those sacred values this time right here home death to america as a child sat in class international school one sunny morning and heard in distance faint sounds of gunfire and rising chants of death to america that day would define rest of life it was tehran capital of iran in was attending a unique school for bilingual students who had been born in western nations it had become last refuge in that city with any tolerance for western teaching but that also made it a target for military fundamentalists as gunfire drew closer heard boots pounding marble tiles outside marching into building and thundering down corridor toward classroom as heard voices chanting death to america remember wondering if would survive to see parents again in a flash of green and black uniforms those soldiers rushed into classroom grabbed us by shirt collars and yelled us to get outside were then packed into school s courtyard where a soldier pointed his rifle group and commanded us to look up almost in unison classmates and raised eyes and saw flags of many nations being torn down and dangled from balcony then set ablaze and tossed still burning into courtyard as those flags floated to ground in flames soldiers fired their guns in air shouting they ordered us if ever wanted to see families again to swear allegiance to grand ayatollah khomeini and trample remains of burning symbols of home countries scanned smoke that was filling courtyard for friends and classmates and horrified watched them capitulate and begin to chant death to america as they stomped sacred symbols was so angry that young as was began to plead with them to come to their senses no one paid slightest attention to an eight year old and yet for first time in life felt something like righteous indignation suspect that born and raised in america was already imbued with such a sense of privilege that just couldn t fathom immense danger was in certainly was acting in ways no native iranian would have found reasonable across smokefilled courtyard saw a soldier coming and knew he meant to force to submit spotted an american flag still burning dropped to knees and grabbed charred pieces from underneath a classmate s feet as soldier closed in ducked and ran still clutching charred pieces of flag into a crowd of civilians who had gathered to witness commotion events of that day would come to define all that have ever stood for or against camel jockey ayatollah and gandhi parents and soon returned to united states and entered third grade more than anything just wanted to be normal to fit in and be accepted by peers unfortunately first name nader changed to nate upon joining navy and swarthy middle eastern appearance were little help that score eliciting regular jibes from classmates even that young age they had already mastered a veritable thesaurus of ethnic defamation including camel jockey sandnigger raghead ayatollah and ironically gandhi now take as a compliment classmates regularly sought to otherize in those years as if were a lesser american because of faith and ethnicity yet remember that tingling in chest when first donned cub scout uniform all because of american flag patch its shoulder something felt so good about wearing it a feeling still had when joined military it seems that flag tried to rescue in tehran was stapled to heart or that s how felt anyway as wore country s uniform when took oath of enlistment in us navy gave mom a camera and asked her to take some photos but she was so overwhelmed with pride and joy that she cried throughout ceremony and managed to snap only a few images of carpet she cried even harder when was selected to serve as first muslimamerican member of us navy presidential ceremonial honor guard that day was proud too and all taunts of those bullies of childhood seemed finally silenced being tormented because of ethnicity and religion in those early years had another effect it caused to become unusually sensitive to nature of other people somehow grasped that if it weren t for a fear of unknown there was an inherent goodness and frail humanity lurking in many of kids who bullied and harassed often discovered those same bullies could be tremendously kind to their families friends or even strangers realized then that if despite everything could lay bare and trust them enough to reach out in kindness might in turn gain their trust and they might then see too and stop operating from such a place of fear and hate through patience humor and understanding was able to offer as embodiment of people and somehow defang otherness of so much that americans found scary to this day have friends from elementary school middle school high school and military who tell that am only muslim they have ever known and that had they not met their perspective islam would have been wholly subject to prevailing fearbased narrative that has poisoned this country since september in became special assistant to master chief petty officer of navy and then in was recruited to serve defense intelligence agency in august transferred to naval reserve in wake of began to observe how so many of fellow americans were adopting a fundamentalist us vs them attitude towards muslims and islam suddenly found in an america where scattered insults had endured as a child took an overarching and sinister meaning and form where they became something like an ideology and way of life by time completed military service in had begun to understand that policies in middle eastsimilarly disturbed seemed in pursuit of little more than perpetual warfare that in turn was made possible by creation of a new enemy islam or rather of a portrait painted by powersthatbe of islam as a terror religion as a hooded villain lurking out there somewhere in desert waiting to destroy us knew that attempting to dispel through patient approach of childhood kind of islamophobia that now had country by throat was not going to be enough post attacks muslims in us and elsewhere were not merely childish taunts for first time in life in a country gripped by fear believed was witnessing a shift en masse toward an american fundamentalism and ultranationalism that reflected a wanton lack of reason not to mention fact as a boy in iran had witnessed dark destination down such a path could take a country now it seemed to in america s quest to escape very demons had sown by own misadventures in middle east and forsaking hallmarks of founding risked becoming everything sought to defeat boy in schoolyard grown up february three young american students yusor abusalha razan abusalha and deah shaddy barakat were executed an apartment complex in chapel hill north carolina killer was a guncrazy white man filled with hate and described by his own daughter as a monster those assassinations struck a special chord of sorrow and loss in mom and cried and prayed together for those students and their families incident in chapel hill also awoke in some version of righteous indignation had felt so many years earlier in that smokefilled courtyard in iran would be damned if stood by while kids in country were murdered simply because of their faith it violated every word of oath had taken when joined military and desecrated every value held in heart as a sacred tenet of nation white nationalists and bigots had by then thrown down gauntlet for so much of this using islamophobia to trigger targeted assassinations in united states this was terrorism pure and simple inspired by hatespeakers here home that moment reached out to fellow veterans who thought might be willing to help and it s true what they say about soul mates being irrevocably drawn to each other when contacted veterans for peace an organization dedicated to exposing costs of war and militarism found leadership well aware of inherent dangers of islamophobia and of need to confront this new enemy so executive director michael mcphearson formed a committee of vets from around country to decide how those of us who had donned uniforms to defend this land could best battle phenomenon and of course joined it from that committee emerged veterans challenge islamophobia vci it now has organizers in arizona georgia new jersey and texas and that s just a beginning totally nonpartisan vci focuses politicians of any party who engage in hate speech ve met with leaders of american muslim communities sat with them through ramadan and attended their iftar dinners to break fasts together in wake of orlando shooting vci also mobilized to fight back against attempts to pit muslim community against lgbtq community group was born of belief that as american military veterans had a responsibility to call out bigotry hatred and perpetuation of endless warfare want american muslim community to know that they have allies and that those allies are indeed veterans as well stand with them and for them and for those of us who are muslim among them nationalism and xenophobia have no place in american life and for part don t think donald trump or anyone like him should be able to peddle islamophobia in an attempt to undermine national unity without islamophobia there no longer exists a clash of civilizations without islamophobia whatever problems in world may be there no longer an us vs them and it s possible to begin reimagining a world of something other than perpetual war as of now this remains struggle of life for despite intense love for america some of countrymen increasingly see american muslims as other enemy mom taught as a boy that only thing that mattered was what was in heart now with her in mind and as a representative of vci when meet fellow americans always remember childhood experiences with bullying peers and still lay bare as did then give trust to gain trust but always knowing that these days this isn t just a matter of niceties it s a question of life or death it s part of a battle for soul of nation in many ways still consider that boy in school courtyard in tehran trying to rescue charred pieces of that flag from those trampling feet it s just that now m doing it in own country nate terani a veteran of us navy and served in military intelligence with defense intelligence agency he currently a member of leadership team common defense pac and regional campaign organizer with veterans challenge islamophobia he a featured columnist with arizona muslim voice newspaper had regular dates this way to bridge mile distance between them however endearing arrangement quickly came to an end after his facetime was left and caught something that left his girlfriend horrified baylee had been discussing regular things with her boyfriend yale gerstein who was other side of screen an otherwise average evening this video chat was not unlike all others she had with yale from his apartment near austin community college until yearold girlfriend heard some scratching sounds after facetime had been left according to kron baylee was midconversation with yale when scratches door caught both of their attention and he got up from his bed where computer was to see who was his door he barely turned handle to open in when masked men entered room and beat yale s face in and slammed him down his bed while shoving a pistol in his cheek intruders didn t seem to know or care that facetime was still and baylee s face seen in corner was watching everything terrified that she was about to see her boyfriend murdered in front of her as she watched him fight for his life admitting that she first thought it was a joke seconds later she came to horrid realization that he was being robbed and called her dad who was home with her in dallas into room was scared because they were saying m going to blow head off m going to kill baylee explained along with chilling feeling she got when intruder finally realized video chat was running and looked right her in camera m like wow seriously watching an armed robbery happen to somebody that care about she added screengrabs of intruder forcing yale down his bed while baylee and her father watch facetime in horror with a clear view of least one intruder s face baylee began taking screenshots of suspect in act as she and her dad called police to report what was going she got pictures right in time since seconds later intruder decided to disconnect computer as he and suspects took off with thousands of dollars worth of yale s music equipment although boyfriend s life was spared in traumatizing ordeal for two of them he said that thieves took something from him that can t be replaced had just finished first album as a solo artist yale said that s all lost since they took recordings equipment means nothing to thieves and everything to victim it s not often that hear of facetime solving crimes or potentially saving lives what happened in this case although it was difficult to watch baylee being there through technology was an instrumental part in protecting yale who hopefully learned that he better take advantage of texas great gun laws and arm himself with more than just a computer

a czech stockbroker who saved more than jewish children from nazi germany has died age of dubbed britain s schindler nicholas winton arranged to transport jewish youngsters from prague after germany annexed czechoslovakia in march though children were originally set to arrive in britain by plane german invasion forced winton to transport them by train through germany before they eventually reached england by boat winton arranged eight trains known as kindertransports children s transports to evacuate children and died anniversary of departure of one carrying largest number of children winton was knighted by queen elizabeth ii in for his efforts despite keeping it secret for nearly years

hillary clinton and donald trump made some inaccurate claims during an nbc commanderinchief forum military and veterans issues clinton wrongly claimed trump supported war in iraq after it started while trump was wrong once again in saying he was against war before it started trump said that president obama set a certain date for withdrawing troops from iraq when that date was set before obama was sworn in trump said that obama s visits to china saudi arabia and cuba were first time in history storied history of air force one when high officials of a host country did not appear to greet president not true clinton said that trump supports privatizing veterans health administration that s false trump said he supports allowing veterans to seek care either public or private hospitals trump said clinton made a terrible mistake libya when she was secretary of state but time trump also supported us action that led to removal of moammar gadhafi from power trump cherrypicked clinton s words when he claimed clinton said vets are being treated essentially just fine clinton had said problems in department of veterans affairs were not as widespread as some republicans claimed but she went to acknowledge problems including issue of wait times for doctors forum sponsored by nbc news and iraq and afghanistan veterans of america was held sept intrepid sea air space museum in new york city today show host matt lauer and members of military and veterans in audience questioned candidates separately trump said he was totally against war in iraq while clinton claimed that he supported iraq war before and after it started facts don t support either candidate s strong assertions review of trump s statements before and after iraq war started found no evidence that trump opposed war before it started in fact he expressed mild support for invading iraq when asked about it howard stern radio show sept about six months before war started stern asked trump if he supported a war with iraq and trump responded yeah guess so in nbc commander in chief forum trump cited an esquire article that appeared in august to show his opposition to war but that article appeared months after war started as for clinton who as a senator voted in october to authorize war in iraq democratic nominee claimed that trump supported it before it happened he supported it as it was happening and he record as supporting it after it happened but just as there no evidence that trump opposed iraq war before it started clinton campaign offered no evidence that trump supported war after it happened clinton campaign cited trump s interview march with neil cavuto of fox business just two days after war started cavuto asked trump about impact of war stock market trump said war looks like a tremendous success from a military standpoint and he predicted market will go up like a rocket after war but cavuto does not ask trump whether us should have gone to war with iraq or whether he supports war and trump doesn t offer an opinion as early as july trump expressed concern hardball with chris matthews about money being spent in iraq rather than in us two months later trump told msnbc s joe scarborough guess maybe if had to do it would have fought terrorism but not necessarily iraq clinton invited her audience to read trump s comments iraq war they can read timeline donald trump and iraq war trump said president obama set a certain date for withdrawing troops from iraq but that date was actually set by president george w bush nbc s matt lauer asked trump about his tendency to respond when pushed for details his military proposals that he s not going to give details because he wants to be unpredictable trump responded absolutely and went to criticize obama for revealing withdrawal date as said then republicans and democrats disagree whether obama or bush to blame for withdrawing all combat troops from iraq end of but that date was set when bush signed status of forces agreement dec it said all united states forces shall withdraw from all iraqi territory no later than december in nbc forum trump also called withdrawal of troops a terrible decision as ve explained before condoleezza rice bush s secretary of state later wrote that bush wanted an agreement for a residual force to remain but iraqi prime minister nouri almaliki objected once obama took office in january he had three years to renegotiate deal his administration tried to do to leave a residual american troop force but maliki still didn t agree negotiations broke down in october over issue of whether us troops would be shielded from criminal prosecution by iraqi authorities whether obama did enough a matter of opinion his then defense secretary leon panetta later wrote that president didn t press hard enough for a deal but some experts say iraq was more closely aligned time with iran and there wasn t a deal to be made with maliki so both presidents had a role in withdrawal of troops but trump wrongly said that obama was one who set a certain date for withdrawal and let us enemies know about it when that date was set before obama was sworn in it s worth noting that trump said in a march interview cnn that troops should be withdrawn quickly from iraq trump said that obama s visits to china saudi arabia and cuba were first time in history storied history of air force one when high officials of a host country did not appear to greet president that s not true other presidents have encountered similar lowkey greetings foreign trips aboard presidential aircraft trump referred to fact that cuba s president raul castro did not greet obama airport his historic visit to cuba in march that saudi arabia s king salman did not meet air force one start of obama s trip to riyadh in april and he referred to china s handling of president s arrival in hangzhou last saturday for a group of meeting whether or not those arrivals constituted snubs of a us president as trump claims a matter of debate but trump wrong facts when he claims it has not happened before it has in for example ronald reagan landed in beijing and was received by china s foreign minister rather than president whom he met only later similarly a trip to west germany reagan was met by foreign minister and not chancellor helmut kohl these and other examples were dug up by friend glenn kessler washington post s fact checker who researched a trump claim in april that cuba s and saudi arabia s handling of obama s visits were without precedent kessler said of trump once again he s wrong wrong wrong kessler also noted that during richard nixon s historic visit to china he was greeted airport by country s number two man premier zhou enlai his boss chairman mao didn t even agree to meet with nixon until after he had arrived a guest house clinton said that her plan to overhaul veterans health administration would not include privatization she said trump supports but trump refuted that statement when it was his turn to discuss his plan to help veterans would not do that trump said referring to clinton s claim that he supports privatization trump s campaign published goals of donald j trump s veterans plan its website last october it doesn t call for va to be completely privatized one of biggest changes that plan would make to current va health care system allowing veterans to get care any nonva medical center that accepts medicare under a trump administration all veterans eligible for va health care can bring their veteran s id card to any doctor or future weapons program western officials insisted to paper that uranium did not have to be sent overseas but could be disposed of in other ways new twist in talks comes just two days before deadline for both sides to agree a framework for a permanent deal final deadline for a permanent deal would not arrive until end of june however if iran insists keeping its uranium in country it would undermine a key argument made in favor of deal by obama administration times reports that if uranium had gone to russia it would have been converted into fuel rods are difficult to use in nuclear weapons it not clear what would happen to uranium if it remained in iran associated press reported sunday that irans position had shifted from from demanding that it be allowed to keep nearly centrifuges enriching uranium to agreeing to keep western officials involved in talks told associated press that tehran may be ready to accept an even lower number united states and its allies want a deal that extends time iran would need to make a nuclear weapon from present two months to three months to least a year however times reported sunday that a paper published by olli heinonen former head of inspections for uns nuclear watchdog estimated that iran could still develop a nuclear weapon in seven or eight months with around centrifuges tehran says it wants to enrich uranium only for energy science industry and medicine but many countries fear iran could use technology to make weaponsgrade uranium officials told associated press that another main dispute involved length of an agreement iran they said wants a total lifting of all caps its activities after years while us and five other nations talks russia china britain france and germany insist progressive removal after a decade a senior us official characterized issue as lack of agreement what happens in years to official spoke condition of anonymity in line with state department rules briefing about closeddoor talks limits irans research and development of centrifuges also were unresolved western officials said tehran has created a prototype centrifuge that it says enriches uranium times faster than its present mainstay model us and its partners want to constrain research that would increase greatly speed of making enough weaponsgrade uranium for a bomb once limits irans programs are lifted one official said russia opposed us position that any un penalties lifted in course of a deal should be reimposed quickly if tehran reneged any commitments both western officials said iran was resisting attempts to make inspections and other ways of verification as intrusive as possible there was tentative agreement turning a nearlyfinished reactor into a model that gives off less plutonium waste than originally envisaged plutonium like enriched uranium a path to nuclear weapons iran and us were discussing letting iran run centrifuges an underground bunker that has been used to enrich uranium machines would produce isotopes for peaceful applications officials said with tuesday deadline approaching and problems remaining us secretary of state john kerry canceled plans sunday to return to united states for an event honoring late us sen edward kennedy french foreign minister laurent fabius and frankwalter steinmeier his german counterpart scratched planned trips to kazakhstan kerry has been in discussions with iranian foreign minister mohammad javad zarif since thursday associated press contributed to this report click for more from new york times

donald trump s organizational problems have gone from bad to worse to flatout embarrassing here s politico with playbyplay from this weekend s colorado gop convention latest scene of trump s delegatesecuring failure unlike in most other states republicans in colorado scrapped plans for a more traditional primary or caucus to award its delegates to this summer s national convention in cleveland instead state gop selected three delegates from each of state s seven congressional districts individual contests in days leading up to state convention and then remaining delegates statewide event this past weekend it was a rather convoluted process that favored campaigns that understood rules and that had ground game necessary to take advantage of them or put another way not trump campaign first two districtlevel contests billionaire s team showed up without an approved list of delegates to pass out to attendees leaving supporters unsure of whom to back and then last thursday s contest two of three delegates trump campaign s list weren t actually official ballot since they failed to pay necessary registration fees this past weekend s mixup though was an even bigger embarrassment since least one of seven misnumbered names trumpsanctioned list lined up with a cruzsupporting delegate according to nbc news even without that mixup though cruz would have likely swept contest anyway given his welloiled delegateselecting machine trump camp though appeared to blame state gop for mistake pointing to discrepancies between delegate guides posted partys website and printed materials distributed event well do whatever it takes to protect legitimacy of support in colorado trump aide alan cobb told nbc suggesting campaign may challenge results clearly there are some serious issues with ballot and balloting trump remains favorite to arrive convention with most delegates to his name but he s far from assured of majority of delegates he d need to win nomination first ballot given that reality gop frontrunner recently retooled his campaign to address his clear weaknesses in undertheradar battle to send loyal delegates to convention if those efforts dont start paying dividends soon though trump very well may arrive in cleveland with most delegates and leave without nomination

washington cnn for months white house and congress have wrangled over a bill that would give lawmakers a greater say in iran nuclear deal administration hammering out along with other world powers and now less than two weeks after a framework agreement with tehran was reached senate foreign relations committee planning to take up measure tuesday committee will consider amendments to tennessee republican sen bob corkers iran nuclear agreement review act before deciding whether to have full senate vote it for many in congress its a very simple premise congress should weigh in terms of a final nuclear deal with iran but for white house and some democrats in congress thats an explosive idea that could derail talks and therefore must be stopped so whats in bill supporters of legislation have emphasized that it would allow congress to give a thumbs up or down deal president barack obama aiming to finalize with iran by negotiations june deadline backers think thats only fair since united states contemplating a major nuclear pact with a decadeslong enemy bill would give congress a chance to hold hearings host briefings and pave way to a vote a joint resolution that could express approval or disapproval of deal it also requires obama administration to quickly report to congress details of deal and regularly assess whether iran keeping its commitments and it spells out what president needs to do visavis congress if iran found violating terms but most important it would keep obama from waiving any congressional sanctions iran during congress day review period and if congress passes a joint resolution of disapproval then sanctions could not be lifted even after that time does this hurt diplomatic effort to reach a final deal irans primary reason for agreeing to deals limits its nuclear program to get sanctions relief iranian supreme leader ayatollah ali khamenei and president hassan rouhani have insisted sanctions must be lifted day one of deal while obama administration has said it would wait several months before lifting sanctions to verify that iran has taken major steps to roll back its program president counting having some latitude to waive sanctions in order to give iran an incentive to sign deal and then keep its commitments corker bill makes it less likely sanctions would be lifted and lifted in a timely way gives less encouragement to iran without bill could obama lift all sanctions iran not all but most least for a limited time without corkermenendez bill named after corker and lead democratic cosponsor sen bob menendez iran could see nearly all nuclearrelated sanctions least temporarily lifted those enacted through united nations and by presidential executive order could be fully removed and most of those passed by congress could be waived by obama until he leaves office when they would go back into effect unless next president continues to waive them if corkermenendez bill becomes law and a deal finalized obama wouldnt be able to lift congressional sanctions for least days during that day review phase bill says obama cant waive suspend reduce provide relief from or otherwise limit application of statutory sanctions how can obama waive sanctions passed by congress anyway biggest sanctions package approved by congress widely credited with crippling irans economy and bringing country to negotiating table was comprehensive iran sanctions accountability and divestment act it included a provision that gives president leverage to waive sanctions for a limited time if he determines its in us national security interest he wouldnt be able to permanently sunset sanctions though unless iran cuts its ties with and stops funding terror organizations around world an aspect not being addressed in current negotiations with iran and not predicted to happen any time soon administration says sanctions are where congress could eventually weigh in iran deal by repealing them but that would only happen if lawmakers deem deal a good one how would congress weigh in during day review period congress could pass a joint resolution approving of deal or disapproving of deal and forbidding any sanctions relief or it could do nothing allowing obama to implement sanctions and by implication deal after days are up a joint resolution disapproving of deal would need to muster a twothirds majority though to override veto obama has promised an unlikely but not entirely unthinkable scenario are there other reasons that obama opposes this bill first hes facing a combative republican majority in both houses of congress those republicans have sought to undermine his negotiations with iran every turn giving those lawmakers a legislative avenue to slam terms of an eventual deal would best be a political blow to obama and his administrations efforts to broker an agreement theres also white houses argument that executive branch has power to broker international agreements without congress meddling while congressional oversight of iran deal would set a dangerous precedent most international agreements arent treaties ratified by congress and obama has argued that us allies need to know they can count those agreements holding up virtually every senate republican supports bill and nine democrats have signed as cosponsors including independent sen angus king who caucuses with democrats several more left are leaning toward supporting bill that means backers will likely pass bill with a filibusterproof majority its unclear though if they can muster enough democrats willing to vote to override obamas veto its not just numbers though some powerful democrats are supporting legislation sen chuck schumer of new york no senate democrat who will become chambers democratic leader when minority leader harry reid of nevada retires end of his term a big proponent of legislation menendez of new jersey who has been working to rally democrats around legislation iran throughout negotiations mitch mcconnell has an unusual admonition for new republican majority as it takes over senate this week don t be scary incoming senate majority leader has set a political goal for next two years of overseeing a functioning reasonable majority capitol hill that scores some measured conservative wins particularly against environmental regulations but probably not big victories such as a full repeal of healthcare law mcconnell s priority to set stage for a potential gop presidential victory in don t want american people to think that if they add a republican president to a republican congress that s going to be a scary outcome want american people to be comfortable with fact that republican house and senate a responsible rightofcenter governing majority kentucky republican said in a broad interview just before christmas in his capitol office it s a far cry from his defiant declaration in that his single most important goal was to make president obama a oneterm president an antagonizing oath that democrats frequently invoke to embarrass gop leader obama won reelection comfortably in and mcconnell s party lost seats now in charge both ends of capitol republicans aim to avoid worst excesses of past four years and make sure public isn t fearful of gop s course there would be nothing frightening about adding a republican president to that governing majority mcconnell said explaining how he wants voters to view party eve of election think that s single best thing can do to not mess up playing field if will for whoever nominee ultimately but mcconnell who will become majority leader tuesday not planning to avoid conflict altogether he wants to use annual spending bills to compel obama to accept conservative policy riders that will divide democrats similar to december spending bill s inclusion of a provision benefiting wall street firms involved in risky derivative trades that rider brought a liberal outcry but did not end up torpedoing bill had obama s support mcconnell has been coaching his members to understand that in initial rounds they will have to almost unanimously support budget outline and spending bills because few democrats will support their policy riders with republicans in his caucus mcconnell knows that he s a long way from getting votes to override an obama veto and that it won t even be easy getting six democrats to regularly support legislation so that he can overcome likely filibusters led by incoming minority leader sen harry m reid dnev still some issues such as energy and taxes health industry mcconnell thinks there s enough bipartisan support to get bills onto obama s desk they d like to be relevant they d like to be part of process he said of discussions with some rankandfile democrats assuming will have most issues a largely unified conference don t expect that everything it wouldn t take a whole lot of democrats to actually pass legislation in senate but mcconnell said those who are craving some grand deal as a way to measure next two years should lower their expectations he s very skeptical of such bargains with democrats tough issues such as immigration and entitlement reform instead he believes three issues have potential common ground international trade deals an overhaul of tax code and new revenue streams for infrastructure projects could country use a lot more bet but there s no way can overcome a reluctant president something really large mcconnell said best he can do some of those bigger issues force obama to break out his veto pen so there a clear set of democratic policy stances republicans can campaign against in democrats are dubious of mcconnell s pledge to avert edgeofthecliff moments they believe he will run into same problems that have bedeviled house speaker john a boehner rohio during past four years including inability to corral rabblerousers such as sen ted cruz rtex to support an agenda that conservative critics will probably view as not bold enough in challenging obama appeasing those farright conservatives will lead to an agenda that democrats hope to exploit in what senator mcconnell wants people to think and what they will think when they see results for themselves are two very different things said reid s spokesman adam jentleson senator mcconnell heads a caucus that obsessed with rigging game against working people in favor of wealthy special interests that s a scary fact indeed and he won t be able to hide it while mcconnell s office adorned with a portrait of kentucky s only other senate majority leader alben w barkley whose tenure was often marked by clashes with his own party s president franklin d roosevelt his model more resembles that of george mitchell democratic majority leader from through without ever shutting down government mitchell stared down a president of opposite party george hw bush and won several liberal victories in a budget showdown mitchell also delivered a slew of legislation to bush s desk that drew a veto helping frame debate for election domestic policy mcconnell has recently studied other twoterm presidents how often they vetoed legislation in their last two years in office obama has issued only two minor vetoes over technical matters largely because previously democraticrun senate prevented anything from reaching his desk that he opposed mcconnell suggested that a veto strategy would help clarify issues for voters think his bureaucracy across board has done a lot of damage to country d least like for him to have to personally take responsibility for it even if he in end decides to veto bill over some restriction epa regulations mcconnell keenly aware of challenges of reining in some of impulses that his fellow republican lawmakers have developed over eight straight years in minority first test will come an energy debate could begin by end of this week mcconnell trying to keep his side from offering amendments not related to energy issues in recent years when some rankandfile republicans wanted to stop senate in its tracks they would threaten amendments related to how congress gets its health care ve asked members to restrain themselves he said restraint has been hard to come by in this political era particularly because a small army of conservative groups has made it a mission to push republicans to most strident stands even if it means shutting down government or risking default national debt mcconnell faced some of those groups firsthand when they supported his primary challenger and he faced a grinding general election against a democrat who was well financed it cost nearly million from his campaign war chest and tens of millions of dollars more from outside groups winning both races handily mcconnell delivering a message to republicans how to behave heading into don t try to reinvent yourself be yourself number one and don t be afraid of a primary will win all primaries did it in will do it in mcconnell said shortly after election day this fall mcconnell sat down with obama for a rare oneonone meeting in oval office a huddle that both sides kept largely secret in prechristmas interview mcconnell acknowledged that meeting s purpose was only to discuss areas where president and republicans might find bipartisan agreement because their disagreements other issues were too in trac table that meeting he said focused trade deals a tax code overhaul and infrastructure funding can get there tax reform trade infrastructure he said he eschewed idea of holding debt ceiling hostage to gain spendingcut concessions from obama a tool republicans had to use before because they were in minority are in majority can pass a budget can determine how much re going to spend he said avoiding those moments could make for a less scary congress giving republicans a better chance in to hit trifecta and gain white house his own horse already chosen sen rand paul rky but don t expect mcconnell to be out campaign trail or doing any other favors for his homestate colleague m going to be supporting rand paul but he knows that beyond that won t be involved in presidential politics ve got a big job here he said

misesorg november inferno a great thriller featuring tom hanks reprising his role as professor robert langdon previous movie adaptations of dan brown s books angels and demons and da vinci code were a success and expect inferno will do well in theaters too langdon a professor of symbology whose puzzle solving skills and knowledge of history come in high demand when a billionaire leaves a trail of clues based dante s inferno to a biological weapon that would halve world s population villain however has good motives as a radical malthusian he believes that human race needs halving if it to survive all even if through a plague malthus s name not mentioned in movie but his ideas are certainly there inferno provides us an opportunity to unpack this overpopulation fear and see where it stands today thomas malthus thought that potential exponential growth of population was a problem if population increases faster than means of subsistence then superior power of population cannot be checked without producing misery or vice overpopulation a problem economics of population size tell a different less scary story while it certainly possible that some areas can become too crowded for some people s preferences as long as people are free to buy and sell land for a mutually agreeable price overcrowding will fix itself as an introvert who enjoys nature and peace and quiet am certainly less willing to rent an apartment in middle of a busy crowded city prices m willing to pay for country living versus city living reflect preferences and to extent that others share preferences or even have opposite preferences use and construction of homes and apartments will economize in both locations demands and profitability of varied real estate offerings keep local populations in check but what about a global scale inferno villain was concerned with world population he stressed urgency of situation but don t see any reason to worry google tells that could fit entire world population in texas and everybody would have a small square meter plot to themselves indeed there are vast stretches of land across globe with little to no human inhabitants malthus and his ideological followers must have a biased perspective only looking crowded streets of a big city if it s not land that s a problem what about means of subsistence are risk of running out of food medicine or other resources because of growing population no a larger population not only means more mouths to feed but also more heads hands and feet to do producing also as populations increase so does variety of skills available to make production even more efficient more people means everybody can specialize in a more specific and more productive comparative advantage and participate in a division of labor perhaps this question will drive point home would rather be stranded an island with two other people or other people malthus wouldn t be a malthusian if he could see this data empirical evidence compelling too in graph below can see sort of world malthus saw one in most people were barely surviving especially compared to current situation stcentury world tells a different story extreme poverty decline even while world population increasing hans rosling a swedish medical doctor and celebrity statistician famous for his don t panic message about population growth he sees that as populations and economies grow more have access to birth control and limit size of their families in this video he shows that all countries are heading toward longer lifespans and greater standards of living finally there s hockey stick of human prosperity estimates of gdp per capita a global millennial scale reveal a recent dramatic turn inflection point coincides with industrial revolution embracing productivity of steampowered capital goods and other technologies sparked a revolution in human wellbeing across globe since then new sources of energy have been harnessed and computers entered scene now computers across world are connected through internet and have been made small enough to fit in pockets goods services and ideas zip across globe while human productivity increases beyond what anybody could have imagined just years ago don t think malthus himself would be a malthusian if he could see world today note views expressed misesorg are not necessarily those of mises institute best of jonathan newman tags jonathan newman was a and summer fellow mises institute and teaches economics auburn university

if want a glimpse into a presidential candidate s governing style take a look his campaign how does he build an organization how does he manage personnel how does he delegate responsibility does he value loyalty or honesty in advisers it s hardly conclusive but how a candidate deals with these challenges says a lot about what they d prioritize as president presumptive republican nominee has managed his campaign way he manages his casinos or his realty tv program haphazardly and with an unearned arrogance everything s about brand and nonsycophants are cast aside getting it right far less important than being right thus when prompted by advisers to dial down rhetoric and think more about his longterm viability trump has resisted he s yet to understand how different a primary and a general election are and he s too cocksure to listen to anyone who tries to explain it trump isn t as stupid as he pretends to be but his confidence seems to scale with his ignorance and that s a dangerous trait in a president given how consequential each decision can be as a candidate a confident idiot can make a lot of noise and fool a lot of voters but can t lead that way as president trump would need sober advice of serious professionals considering how little he understands about job and world this especially true in his case based latest behindthescenes reports trump s campaign as well as his entire history in real estate and television it s unlikely he would govern with humility and selfawareness required campaign has undergone several shakeups in recent months with key staffers like corey lewandowski being demoted to make way for veteran operatives like paul manafort trump s approach has remained very much same however everyone he brings in bends to his will or replaced by someone who does things have gotten more chaotic in last week or so as rick wiley was fired as campaign s national political director wiley was known to assert himself in ways that alienated trump and his loyalists washington post s sean sullivan and robert costa summarized broader situation over weekend for last two months donald trump has presided over a political team riddled with turf wars staff reshuffling and dueling power centers but tensions are more than typical campaign chaos they illustrate how trump likes to run an organization whether it s a real estate venture or his presidential bid interviews with current and former trump associates reveal an executive who fond of promoting rivalries among subordinates wary of delegating major decisions scornful of convention and fiercely insistent a culture of loyalty around him these are management techniques of a brandhustling magnate not a president of united states trump s dictatorial approach works well campaign trail but it d be a disaster in office a president has to persuade and compromise capacity to admit ignorance equally important trump by all accounts has no interest in any of these things trump doesn t know what he needs to know in order to be president nor does he care that he doesn t know if his campaign any indication he ll hire people to ensure his veil of ignorance never cracked that kind of megalomania why trump who he it s part of his outsized persona it s also clearest indicator have of kind of president he d be

in a previous article discussed stretching namely why stretching important for masculine man and several stretches that should use in training to maximize physical fitness and while that advice still valid neglected a very important concept in that first article techniques that detail how to stretch am not referring to a specific stretch or some sort of hypothetical stretching mindset but rather a set of techniques that can be utilized for any stretch to increase ones flexibility immediately but before can discuss those have to discuss incorrect way of stretching that many people still use how not to stretch many people believe that stretching a literal act of forcing muscles and connective tissue to stretch avoid this all costs first and foremost as have discussed previously in these pages should never apply any stretching pressure to connective tissue they evolved solely to hold fast and keep things in one piece they should never be stretched all muscles are anatomical feature that stretches as they evolved to do when stretching body should always be positioned in a way where connective tissues are stable and muscles are moving even when are positioned properly no part of stretching should involve athlete forcing his muscles to stretch as that risks muscular tearing a nagging injury that never truly goes away this because human body has naturally evolved what referred to as antistretch reflex to prevent muscular tearingstretching muscles increases in difficulty farther and deeper stretch and body responds to this stress with pain this a biological sign telling that if go further ll be risking muscle tears and should normally be a heeded warning however if want to do advanced stretching such as that nighimpossible benchmark of fitness splits will have to find a way to overcome this reflex without hurting yourself and as luck would have it there relax into stretching reflexes can be overcome with gradual and repeated practice just ask friendly neighborhood hooker about how she overcame her gag reflex similarly antistretch reflex that keeps joints actually muscles stiff and immobile can be overcome with a few techniques most basic of these techniques one that have had best results with as usual simple but difficult answer usually correct one and that titular concept of relaxing into a stretch with thanks to pavel tsatsouline for naming concept to use this technique take an easy form of stretch want to do using splits as an example would do a seated groin stretch engage stretch just to point where feel tension in target muscle and then sit and wait yes paradoxically relaxation key to increasing physical fitness in this context are literally going to sit there and wait for muscles to stop fighting stretch in other words re going to exhaust reflex until it stops being reflexive this not something that happens quickly from experience it will take minutes per stretch so it perfectly acceptable for to get a book or watch tv while doing this as a side note this literally only time where it s acceptable to have a visual distraction during exercise in opinion as might expect once muscles have relaxed and pain has melted away can increase stretch a little bit more and hold it for another minutes repeat this process until muscles are in pain and judge that can t go any further this a personal call that will have to decide for yourself as can t judge when muscles are demanding to stop this technique can be utilized for any stretch and in many cases will give progress that so desire however there are other methods in relax into stretch family of exercises that can be utilized as well such as meditation mentally relaxing will lead to muscular relaxation or can try forced relaxation where flex muscle simultaneously while stretching forcing muscle to relax either way slice it don t just brute force stretching utilize these techniques for better results read more why stretching essential for body with beginner stretches to get started

presidential hopefuls in both parties agree least one thing economic mobility and feeling of many americans that they are being shut out from nation s prosperity will be a defining theme of campaign former florida governor jeb bush last week became latest republican to signal a readiness to engage democrats what historically has been their turf putting issues of middleclass wage stagnation poverty and shared prosperity forefront of their political messages bush s framing of economic and social challenges facing country nearly mirrors that of likely democratic candidate hillary rodham clinton as well as other possible contenders left sen marco rubio rfla has written a book subject american dreams restoring economic opportunity for everyone to be published this week while sen rand paul rky has proposed policies for distressed communities that he sees as ticket to middle class and mitt romney gop nominee who was portrayed by democrats as insensitive to and out of touch with lives of middle and workingclass americans has told friends he considers poverty a topic du jour as he weighs another run in talk to any pollster democratic side or republican side they re in complete agreement idea that there has to be an economic populist message said matthew dowd a top strategist for former president george w bush s and campaigns then it comes down to are there credible solutions and there a credible candidate about million americans live or below poverty line according to last fall s census estimates while median household income in united states in was just under adjusted for inflation median percent lower than it was in last full year before recession and percent below what it was in wage stagnation has been a persistent problem for low and middleincome workers since late s wages for bottom percent of earners have been essentially stagnant and between and real wages fell for entire bottom percent of wage distribution lawrence mishel of liberal economic policy institute wrote in a paper published this month launching his new political action committee right to rise jeb bush asserted last week in pac s mission statement that president obama s tenure has been pretty good for those top of income scale but a lost decade for everyone else millions of fellow citizens across broad middle class feel as if american dream now out of their reach that politics are petty and broken that opportunities are elusive and that playing field no longer fair or level bush wrote too many of poor have lost hope that a path to a better life within their grasp bush s focus adds an unexpected element to coming debate and puts pressure entire field himself included to come forward with fresh policies that address nation s core economic problems shift also highlights a potential vulnerability for democrats in his campaigns obama twice promised to focus wealth inequality and middleclass stagnation but those problems persist even as economy revs up and unemployment falls clinton could be challenged now from both right and left to lay out solutions that go beyond obama s and also to develop a political voice of her own to articulate them democrats conceded after last fall s midterm elections that they had not found a compelling economic message democrats will look to clinton should she run to take lead in doing that although they also are looking to sen elizabeth warren dmass and her harderedged populist rhetoric clinton tested such a message last year in a major speech new america foundation in may she said that americans are finding it harder than ever to get their footing in changing economy dream of upward mobility that made this country a model for world feels further and further out of reach language of bush rubio and other republicans appears aimed avoiding problem that bedeviled romney throughout his campaign former massachusetts governor s memorable comment about percent of population who he said depend government and feel like victims haunted him during final months before election yuval levin editor of conservative journal national affairs said overall shift in emphasis by republicans an effort to move away from romney s more abstract message of job growth and to focus more specifically social mobility and solutions for those or near bottom it s a process of letting light out from under bushel levin said citing past conservative leadership in reforming welfare he added think republican ideas have always held this promise but conservatives have not always emphasized these elements a difference in emphasis not insignificant democrats however argue that revising their rhetoric will not be enough for republicans especially if jeb bush becomes gop nominee to gloss over former president george w bush s legacy democrats say tightened middleclass squeeze it s not like republican party has clean hands issue of rising inequality said neera tanden president of center for american progress and an adviser to clinton economic policy it s a weird thing to say care about how rest of country doing not just top earners when brother cut taxes for wealthy and party s economic position starts with undoing doddfrank law that tightened regulation of wall street obama hit road last week to highlight economic successes of his presidency from auto industry bailout to more robust overall growth in last half of still many democrats acknowledge that there a large gap between promise and results issues of middleclass insecurities that where bush and some other republicans are taking aim two factors have contributed to opening they see one continued problem of stagnating wages for broad middle class and income gap between rich and nonrich other new evidence that it has become harder for those bottom to rise into middle class and that risk of some born into middle class particularly minorities falling out of it growing democrats long have been seen as party more trusted to deal with middleclass economic issues republicans long have resisted policies that smack of economic redistribution could that be changing how do tell middle class re guy said grover norquist a conservative antitax advocate republicans feel comfortable saying that now because they feel guys they re running against are sufficiently discredited william galston of brookings institution said that issue of blocked social mobility one republicans will feel more comfortable engaging and their own terms that s what jeb bush saying can accept a definition of a problem but give unabashedly conservative responses to those challenges he said but galston a former domestic policy adviser to president bill clinton said bush s framing of issue this way means he has put down a marker that he will be expected to meet would expect to hear from him over coming months some of best and most innovative conservative thinking opportunity and upward mobility he said that will be a fair test how much gas there in conservative ideas tank other republicans have been working to reorient their party toward bluecollar economics and to put forward fresh economic ideas former senator rick santorum of pennsylvania and former arkansas governor mike huckabee two former presidential candidates who are looking running in long have talked about gop s need to recognize problems of working families house ways and means committee chairman paul ryan rwis has focused policies designed to lift people out of poverty in as romney s running mate ryan wanted to campaign in inner cities and give speeches promoting individual empowerment he was frustrated that romney s campaign team directed him to talk about other topics rubio has proposed a fundamental shift in antipoverty programs he has suggested devolving power and responsibility from washington to states by consolidating federal monies into a flex fund senator from florida has called for significant reductions in federal regulations that he says will spur creation of better jobs education he has offered a series of reforms designed to lessen burden of student loan debt and expand access to apprenticeship programs rubio and others also have proposed changes in earnedincome tax credit to extend its reach to more people democrats are skeptical that republicans can meet challenge with policy proposals that are much beyond calls for tax cuts to spur economic growth and further efforts to scale back size of government it sounds to like a traditional romney republican trickledown agenda but with a willingness to engage inequality and mobility said jared bernstein a former adviser to vice president biden who works center of budget and policy priorities democrats say it s too early to draw conclusions but they note that if bush and other republicans find new ways to engage middleclass voters with populist themes pressures clinton will mount significantly voters are going to say okay thank for acknowledging this problem but what are going to do about it bernstein said would argue that democrats are going to have to have more in their toolbox than raising minimum wage and universal prek

president now plans to continue a us ground force presence in afghanistan to help hold off taliban until he leaves office this an uncommon turnaround for a president who hasn t changed his mind foreign policy since drawing his muchballyhooed and promptly ignored red line in syria so what does this rare shift have to say about obama s foreign policy and america s future role in afghanistan not much mr obama started his presidency hoping to use afghanistan to demonstrate his willingness to roll up his sleeves and get his hands dirty in national security business he asserted that unlike bush s war in iraq fighting in place where attacks were concocted was worth it in approving a surge in afghanistan president made much of his role as deciderinchief yet even as he sent in more troops he tipped his hand that afghanistan was not as much an exception to talkdon tfight obama doctrine as toughguy image he sought to cultivate president gave commanders only half force and half time they requested to do job right it turned out commanders were right mr obama however had his eye next election and wanted to go into race showing he had put war a glidepath to wind down and pull out signaling a limitedtime interest in fighting an enemy rarely ends well taliban adopted logical and predictable strategy of simply waiting out mr obama also predictably halfmeasure surge and overly ambitious transfer of responsibility to afghan military resulted in unnecessarily heavy casualties for afghan security forces a persistent taliban and a shaky security situation nevertheless afghanistan far from a total failure indeed compared to president s other foreign policy dalliances from russian reset to libya to syria to iraq to ukraine it ranks as his best achievement yet why mr obama reportedly backing away from his plan to yank virtually all us troops out of afghanistan by end of next year instead he has agreed to leave current force of troops in place throughout most of next year and keep in theater thereafter it s a good call with taliban flexing its muscles anew and islamic state making inroads there completely pulling out makes little sense mr obama can ill afford another complete foreign policy disaster his watch further virtually everyone us military afghans pakistan india nato allies wants us to stay and see job through in addition there zero domestic pressure for president to make good his campaign promise an appearance of code pink about as common as a yeti sighting and in debates thus far presidential candidates have devoted about as much time to afghanistan as they have to defunding public television it makes complete sense for mr obama to tough it out in afghanistan but don t expect change in plans to make things better for afghans or larger security picture us presence bare minimum to hold more forces with loser rules of engagement would make country much safer much faster but holding probably best can hope for with this president here s hoping next commander in chief s attitude toward afghanistan will focus more solving problems than just wishing they would go away james jay carafano vice president of foreign and defense policy studies heritage foundation follow him twitter jjcarafano
