two days after supreme court justice antonin scalia died suddenly in remote west texas a former dc homicide commander raising questions about how death was handled by local and federal authorities as a former homicide commander am stunned that no autopsy was ordered for justice scalia william o ritchie former head of criminal investigations for dc police wrote in a post facebook sunday death of antonin scalia chaos confusion and conflicting reports scalia was found dead in his room a luxury hunting resort in state s big bend region by resort s owner it took hours for authorities to find a justice of peace when they did presidio county judge cinderela guevara pronounced scalia dead of natural causes without seeing body permissible under texas law and without ordering an autopsy sunday us marshals service provides security for supreme court justices said that scalia had declined a security detail while ranch so marshals were not present when he died when marshals were notified deputy marshals from western district of texas went to scene service said in a statement guevara said she declared scalia dead based information from law enforcement officials scene who assured her that there were no signs of foul play she also spoke to scalia s doctor who told her that justice had been to see him wednesday and thursday last week for a shoulder injury and that he had ordered an mri for scalia according to wfaatv in dallas yearold justice also suffered from several chronic conditions guevara said she said she was awaiting a statement from physician to complete scalia s death certificate manager of el paso funeral home that handled scalia s body said scalia s family insisted not having an autopsy done but decision has spawned a host of conspiracy theories online as well as skeptical questions from law enforcement experts such as ritchie have a supreme court justice who died not in attendance of a physician he wrote have a nonhomicide trained us marshal tell justice of peace that no foul play was observed have a justice of peace pronounce death while not being scene and without any medical training opining that justice died of a heart attack what medical proof exists of a myocardial infarction why not a cerebral hemorrhage in an interview with washington post guevara has said she rebutted a report by a dallas tv station that scalia had died of myocardial infarction she said she meant only that his heart had stopped ritchie also raised questions about marshals actions how can marshal say without a thorough post mortem that he was not injected with an illegal substance that would simulate a heart attack did us marshal check for petechial hemorrhage in his eyes or under his lips that would have suggested suffocation did us marshal smell his breath for any unusual odor that might suggest poisoning gut tells there something fishy going in texas a spokesman for marshals service said monday that marshals did not make a formal determination of death he directed questions to county judge who made call scalia s physician brian monahan a us navy rear admiral and attending physician for us congress and supreme court he declined to comment scalia s health when reached by telephone monday his home in maryland patient confidentiality forbids to make any comment subject he said when asked whether he planned to make public statement he s preparing for guevara monahan repeated same statement and hung up a reporter eva ruth moravec in austin contributed to this report this post has been updated why blocking obama s pick could backfire republicans supreme court nomination process sure to be epic debate

are wheels coming off iran deal less than a year after iran america and five other world powers inked a comprehensive nuclear accord a debate over its terms has erupted anew in washington braggadocio of a prominent white house aide fueling republican accusations that president obama deliberately deceived congress and country about iran and deal and in tehran frustration over residual impact of american sanctions has prompted increasingly resentful accusations from iranian leaders that united states has failed to live up to its end of bargain as a result some are fretting that deal risk and are laying blame white house doorstep both claims are spurious and deserve a more forceful rebuttal from obama administration in end however ruckus over recent comments by deputy national security advisor ben rhodes largely an insidethebeltway drama one that provides endless entertainment for washington insiders but has little real significance for deal or american diplomacy by contrast iran s dissatisfaction presents a serious diplomatic dilemma for washington but it should not be interpreted as evidence that deal unraveling rather chorus of complaints from tehran demonstrates accord signed in july working exactly as it was intended forestalling iranian nuclear ambitions while amplifying incentives for further reintegration into global economy obama s handling of this first real test of nuclear agreement will be crucial for sustaining its credibility for sake of deal and for any prospect of a durable thermidor for revolutionary state washington should resist temptation to assuage iran s postdeal growing pains if iranians wants wholesale economic rehabilitation their leadership needs to embrace kind of policies that would yield that in other words meaningful political economic and foreign policy reform not surprisingly iranian officials are seeking a quicker fix and they have mounted an intense campaign to wrest supplementary sanctions relief from washington their principal argument that theocracy has been stiffed an april visit to washington valiollah seif head of iran s central bank questioned benefits of nuclear agreement insisting that tehran has received almost nothing of sanctions relief that was promised as part of deal formally called joint comprehensive plan of action jcpoa mohammad javad zarif country s smoothspoken foreign minister has contended that united states needs to do way more warning that if one side does not comply with agreement then agreement will start to falter and iran s supreme leader ayatollah ali khamenei charged recently that americans are engaged in obstruction and deception adding paper americans say banks can trade with iran but in practice they act in such an iranophobic way that no trade can take place with iran adding fuel to fire are changes to us visa policies that are perceived constraining iran s economic rebound deliberately inflammatory rhetoric from us congress and a recent supreme court verdict that paves way for a billion payout to victims of terrorist attacks attributed to tehran or its proxies iranian president hassan rouhani has described decision as flagrant theft and evidence of enduring american hostility toward tehran tehran s narrative plays equally well to its essential constituencies revolution s power brokers steeped in official narratives of american treachery an iranian citizenry impatient for its long overdue peace dividend and a european business community anxious to reclaim its piece of pie after an unwelcome five years of having to forgo a lucrative market and it wouldn t be first time tehran got cold feet about its nuclear obligations out of an unfortunate sense of that payoff was insufficient in two years after a deal with britain france and germany to suspend core aspects of its nuclear program iran s leadership soured that deal and reneged anxiety about a repeat performance prompting new us efforts to facilitate business in iran and a mounting debate in press and capitol hill around additional american sanctions relief but iran s campaign grounded in a fundamental falsehood that washington has failed to live up to its end of bargain in fact washington has delivered fully sanctions relief pledged under jcpoa and officials in white house state department and even enforcement office of treasury department have engaged in extraordinary outreach to clarify remaining restrictions and underscore american commitment to terms of deal moreover rewards of iran s nuclear concessions are actually widely evident in volume of new trade and investment that already underway in scope and velocity of diplomatic and commercial reengagement with iran in swifterthananticipated revival of oil exports heads of state from italy and india from south korea to south africa are beating a path to tehran accompanied by contingents of eager investors meanwhile iranian officials including seif chief of same central bank that was formerly barred by sanctions headline swanky conferences aimed wooing european business players great iranian gold rush catch that money moving more slowly than iranian officials seem to have anticipated and trickledown effect has been almost nonexistent for average iranian explanation for this lag complex and multidimensional first and foremost iran hard hit by decline in oil prices have fallen by roughly percent since interim nuclear deal was signed in november even in best of times islamic republic was never a particularly easy place to do business and many of its structural economic problems have been exacerbated by a decade of sanctions and particularly egregious mismanagement of to tenure of iranian president mahmoud ahmadinejad iran s designation since as a highrisk and noncooperative jurisdiction by financial action task force a multilateral body established to combat money laundering and terrorist finance poses additional hurdles for banks in addition a host of other market distortions induce investor caution corruption a bloated and opaque banking system an inflexible labor market unattractive contract terms for energy investments traditional dominance of public sector tehran s challenges in luring capital further complicated by its reputation for provocative domestic and regional behavior torching embassies arresting tourists and dualnational businessmen testing ballistic missiles none of this provides a conducive context for iran s reintegration into global economy as old adage goes capital a coward and islamic republic a haunted house iran has seen this all before similar factors undercut tehran s previous efforts to open up to global economy in early s after long war with iraq thenpresident ali akbar hashemi rafsanjani sought foreign trade and investment as part of his massive reconstruction program initial outcomes were encouraging but falling oil prices excessive shortterm debt and perpetuation of an ideological foreign policy drove away investors and undermined his economic reforms this time around american sanctions have cast a long shadow nuclear deal left intact an array of restrictions primary us embargo iran as well as financial measures that preclude access to us dollar and penalize third countries for doing business with iranian individuals and entities that are involved with terrorism or other malfeasance vestiges of sanctions regime create truly epic compliance issues for any international investor and hangover effect of a decade of stringent and costly enforcement has generated a culture of overcompliance in international financial sector since institutional due diligence an integral dimension of industry s viability none of this should come as a surprise to tehran american officials were crystal clear throughout negotiations and in advocating behalf of deal that deal only removed nuclearrelated sanctions and that us measures imposed as a result of iran s support for terrorism its human rights abuses or other issues would remain intact and every sensible analyst looked past inflated rhetoric of deal s opponents who brayed against deal as a massive cash bonanza to recognize that residual sanctions regime would remain a significant factor in iran s postdeal economic picture as wrote time so if this was entirely predictable why tehran crying foul now unlike in united states where agreement s shortcomings were oversold if anything rather than downplayed in iran there was a triumphalism with deal was sold domestically this was mostly because of peculiarities of iran s political system to avoid appearance of contravening red lines articulated by khamenei country s ultimate authority iranian negotiators depicted jcpoa as delivering wholesale sanctions relief rouhani described outcome as a legal technical and political victory for country emphasizing that tehran achieved more than what was imagined iran s politically motivated embellishments were exacerbated by hype surrounding deal cultivated by entrepreneurs and aspiring middlemen who presented iran in hyperbolic terms as best emerging market for years to come and one of hottest opportunities of decade but while it may offend iranian ego relative scale of opportunity in iran more modest than other muchheralded economic openings such as china it hardly inconceivable that many banks and other firms have simply chosen to sit this first round out neither iran s economic challenges nor grievances of its leadership are fraying nuclear accord in fact they only highlight its underlying logic while deal s scope was finite it was not a wholesale rapprochement or rehabilitation many of its supporters argued that its logic would prove selfreinforcing iran s gradual reintegration into global economy would bolster case among its leadership for a broader moderation of its domestic and foreign policies precisely in order to boost their benefits tehran s dissatisfaction with payout to date suggests this formula working a little bit of sanctions relief has whetted entrepreneurial appetites of clerical state despite official invocations proclaiming a resistance economy trickle of new trade and investment from europe and asia into iran since deal was signed has only intensified pressure for more and for more tangible dissemination of its benefits among iranian population in other words it success of nuclear deal rather than its shortcomings that driving complaints that have emanated from iran united states not responsible for hesitancy of international capital and other economic hiccups that tehran has experienced in aftermath of nuclear agreement culpability resides as it always has with iran and risks that its government s policies pose for international business if iranians want to see their nascent opening to international community expanded if they want peace dividend they have been promised they need to look to their own leadership and its policies if iran s central bank governor wants normal conditions and access to us financial system as he demanded during his washington visit let him return to tehran and help instill kind of reforms that would make those goals possible there are sensible steps that washington can take to ensure that provisions of nuclear deal are fully feasible including limited mechanisms for enabling transactions such as repatriation of previously frozen assets that are specifically permitted under deal such exceptional measures are reasonable not because they help tehran but because they help sustain consensus between washington and its european partners and help preserve west s negotiating leverage with any future targets of american or multilateral financial sanctions however it would be profoundly detrimental for washington to provide significant unilateral relief to tehran without reciprocal additional iranian concessions and pr blitz by senior us officials to reassure iran s prospective foreign investors has taken an unseemly tone especially since existing sanctions prohibit us persons from facilitating transactions with iran by foreign entities these measures may be aimed building confidence but they ultimately have opposite effect eroding iran s incentives to abide by deal undermining any rationale for broader changes iran remains a risky place to do business and it in washington s interests as well as those of iranians and broader international community that tehran focuses mitigating those risks rather than seeking to subvert their penalties nuclear deal working iran s nascent reintegration into global economy intensifying internal debates and popular expectations this all to good but to get more tehran will have to give more

nasa s cassini spacecraft discovered a static hexagonal storm four times size of earth crowning saturn s north pole including a clearly defined eyewall based its size and movements scientists have concluded that it s a vast cloud pattern generated by a gigantic perpetual hurricane spinning center of planet s north pole scientists estimate that this storm has been raging for decades maybe even centuries each side northern polar hexagon approximately km long and whole structure rotates once every hours and minutes a day saturn in just four years saturn s hexagon has changed its color from blue to gold when saturn s north pole gears up for next year s summer solsticesaturn s hexagon a sixsided structure that spans roughly km miles in diameter and extends about km miles down into planet s dense atmosphere because saturn does not have land masses or oceans its surface to complicate weather way earth does its conditions should give scientists a more elementary model to study physics of circulation patterns and atmosphere said kevin baines an atmospheric scientist nasa s jet propulsion laboratory pasadena calif who has studied hexagon with cassini s visual and infrared mapping spectrometer last visiblelight images of entire hexagon were captured by nasa s voyager spacecraft nearly years ago last time spring began saturn after sunlight faded darkness shrouded north pole for years much to delight and bafflement of cassini scientists location and shape of hexagon in latest images match up with what they saw in voyager pictures longevity of hexagon makes this something special given that weather earth lasts order of weeks said kunio sayanagi a cassini imaging team associate california institute of technology it s a mystery par with strange weather conditions that give rise to longlived great red spot of jupiter hexagon was originally discovered in images taken by voyager spacecraft in early s it encircles saturn about degrees north latitude and has been estimated to have a diameter wider than two earths jet stream believed to whip along hexagon around meters per second miles per hour early hexagon images from voyager and groundbased telescopes suffered from poor viewing perspectives cassini has been orbiting saturn since has a better angle for viewing north pole but long darkness of saturnian winter hid hexagon from cassini s visiblelight cameras for years infrared instruments however were able to obtain images by using heat patterns those images showed hexagon nearly stationary and extends deep into atmosphere they also discovered a hotspot and cyclone in same region as observed by nasa s voyager and cassini spacecraft each point of hexagon appears to rotate its center nearly same rate that saturn rotates its axis along rim of hexagon a jet stream of air blasting eastward speeds of kmh mph while re pretty confident that know what saturn s hexagon big mystery how it got there in first place once have a giant whirlpool of air it s relatively easy to keep it spinning but force need to get it wound up in first place a whole lot more difficult to explain it s fascinating that cassini spacecraft could have observed two completely different colors in hexagon between november and september best hypothesis that this what it looks like when saturn changes seasons with a year that lasts earth years saturn changes seasons only once every seven years and increased sunlight over past three years could explain golden haze color change thought to be an effect of saturn s seasons in particular change from a bluish colour to a more golden hue may be due to increased production of photochemical hazes in atmosphere as north pole approaches summer solstice in may inside hexagon there are fewer large haze particles and a concentration of small haze particles while outside hexagon opposite true kunio sayanagi a cassini imaging team associate hampton university explained back in hexagonal jet stream acting like a barrier results in something like earth s antarctic ozone hole but since saturn reached its equinox in august point where sun directly over saturn s equator it s been gradually exposed to more and more sunlight means that for past three years aerosols have been produced inside of hexagon and around north pole making polar atmosphere appear hazy and golden when photographed last month other effects including changes in atmospheric circulation could also be playing a role nasa explained this week scientists think seasonally shifting patterns of solar heating probably influence winds in polar regions nasa scientists have an investigation underway to figure out what s actually going daily galaxy via nasajplcaltechspace science institutehampton university source daily galaxy

license dmca in an extraordinary and unprecedented action federal bureau of investigation has stepped into presidential campaign only days before election day sending a letter to congress announcing new investigative steps related to hillary clintons use of a private email server threeparagraph letter by fbi director james comey to eight congressional committees friday remarkably vague it states that in connection with an unrelated case fbi has learned of existence of emails that appear to be pertinent to investigation of clintons personal email server comey notes he had previously told congress was completed he states that he has agreed to allow investigators to review these emails to determine whether they contain classified information as well as to assess their importance to investigation he acknowledges that fbi cannot yet assess whether or not this material may be significant obvious question that arises why given fact that fbi has no idea whether these additional emails contain any significant information relative to clinton email case agency should make them a public issue within days of election media commentators noted that letter violates a longstanding informal fbi ban making politically sensitive announcements within days of a us election following report of comeys letter news media citing unnamed federal law enforcement officials said emails in question were found a laptop computer shared by clinton aide huma abedin and her husband former representative anthony weiner advertisement weiner under fbi investigation for allegedly sending sexually explicit text messages to an underage girl abedin announced her separation from weiner earlier this year after latest episode involving weiner and sexually explicit internet activity became public comeys letter was hailed by donald trump and republican party spokesmen as tantamount to an official reopening of fbi investigation and rescinding of decision announced by comey in july that no charges would be brought against democratic presidential candidate clinton spoke to press briefly friday evening demanding that fbi provide more information about substance of what it was reviewing including whether there was any connection to her use of a private email server she pointed out that more than million people have already voted and that many millions more will be going to polls over next week as early voting continues in response to questions she indicated that fbi has not contacted her and that she first learned of letter through media it this point impossible to determine with precision motivation behind comeys letter and political forces for he speaking however his attempt to present letter as a politically disinterested response to discovery of new information lacks any credibility this direct intervention into election by top policeintelligence agency can only be an expression of deep crisis and profound tensions within american ruling class and state election as a whole has been dominated by growth of social anger and antiestablishment sentiment yet it has ended in a contest between two rightwing representatives of richest percent who are despised by huge sections of electorate advertisement it has plumbed depths of political debasement part of both candidates fascistic billionaire trump seeking to channel discontent along most rightwing chauvinist and racist channels multimillionaire clinton relying sex scandals and a mccarthyite attack trump as an agent of russian president vladimir putin to bury incriminating revelations of corruption and lying and to swing public opinion behind a policy of military escalation and confrontation with nucleararmed russia entire process has been surrounded by an aura of violence and a breakdown of public confidence in political system it has unfolded under conditions of deepening economic crisis mounting international tensions and worsening crises for us imperialism around world ie ongoing debacle of washingtons war for regime change in syria signs of disarray in antichinese pivot to asia emergence of open conflicts with imperialist allies in europe particularly germany convergence of these crises generating bitter conflicts within american ruling class over policy questions magnified by fears of a rising tide of social opposition home whether intention of comeys letter was to inflict fatal damage to clintons candidacy shore up endangered republican majorities in senate and house or fire a shot across bow against an incoming clinton administration it makes clear that next administration will be mired in crisis from day it takes office

this has turned into whiplash election and it s virtually impossible to keep up with headsnapping revelations even most diligent journalist can get vertigo trying to investigate and evaluate each disclosure damages hillary clinton or donald trump and spawns dire predictions that their campaign toast only to fade in a news cycle or two some developments that in a normal election would badly wound a presidential candidate get meager exposure in rest of media because they are overshadowed by other megastories no one has bandwidth to do it all this more than a mere campaign its craziness has become beating heart of american culture debated in every cubicle and coffee shop and every level of increasingly toxic stew of social media just look last few weeks democrats and many members of media have taken up weapons and stormed a place called comey island fbi director shook up race by revealing a probe of emails that wound up laptop of a top aide s estranged husband under investigation for sending illicit messages to an underage girl new york times reported that trump avoided hundreds of millions of dollars in income taxes through a loophole scheme so dubious his own lawyers advised him that irs could declare it improper clinton campaign got advance questions before cnn town halls from a top democratic party official who was also a paid contributor network booted her washington post reported that donald trump has repeatedly sought credit for charity he had not given or had claimed other people s giving as his own back comey island fbi chief was reported to have argued that administration shouldn t accuse russia of using cyberwarfare to interfere with presidential campaign because yesit would look partisan so close to election hacked emails revealed that daughter of democratic nominee s husband himself a former president complained that his aides were cashing in clinton foundation and siphoning money from her parents trump foundation was ordered to stop fundraising by new york attorney general a democrat and clinton supporter clinton s top advisers reeling after email scandal broke complained that said she had terrible instincts and a pathological aversion to apologizing according to hacked emails back comey island fbi looked into whether trump s company had a secret email server communicating with a russian bank but found no direct link a clinton aide complained in a hacked email that speak in such a tortured nuanced way when don t get any advantage from nuance and just wind up looking political back comey island fbi opened a preliminary investigation of trump s former campaign manager and his ties to russian interests paul manafort said he knew nothing about trump boasted a decade ago a hollywood entertainment show that as a celebrity he could grab women by genitals he now dismisses as lockerroom talk back comey island fbi agents were investigating financial and ethical issues clinton foundation but were told to stand down by top lawenforcement officials according to wall street journal eleven women accused trump of sexually propositioning them or unwanted kissing or touching all of he denounced as lies trump held a news conference before a debate with paula jones and kathleen willey who have accused bill clinton of sexual misconduct and juanita broaddrick who has accused him of rape he has denied hillary clinton nearly collapsed while getting into a car and belatedly revealed that she had pneumonia and well could go many of these cases prompted media firestorms complete with predictions that trump or clinton would be seriously or even fatally damaged but then stories were overtaken by some new allegation or outrage in case of comey s renewed email investigation polls so far suggest that it hasn t moved needle much beyond tightening that was already taking place in race it may well be that ve all become numb in this whiplash campaign race so polarized and public attitudes toward trump and clinton so firmly established that each new frontpage headline or cable obsession changes few minds perhaps only consensus a sense of relief that this long strange trip finally coming to an end howard kurtz a fox news analyst and host of mediabuzz sundays am and pm et he author of five books and based in washington follow him howardkurtz click here for more information howard kurtz

democratic senator jeff merkley of oregon said he will vote to support iran nuclear deal a pledge that puts president barack obama only three votes short of protecting pact in congress merkley issued a statement sunday calling accord best available strategy to block iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon merkley s support brings to number of senators publicly favoring deal would ease economic sanctions iran in exchange for curbs country s nuclear program barring defections obama needs three more votes from senate democrats who have yet to declare their position to sustain a likely veto of legislation aimed killing pact if obama can assemble senate votes by getting most of remaining democrats board senate may not vote agreement all republicancontrolled congress has until sept to pass a resolution disapproving deal reached in july between six world powers and iran obama has pledged to veto that resolution if it gets to his desk while republicans have been united in opposing deal only two democratic senators charles schumer of new york thirdranking democrat in chamber and robert menendez of new jersey have joined them so far senator chris coons a delaware democrat foreign relations committee plans to announce his decision tuesday among other democrats yet to disclose a position are maryland s ben cardin and new jersey s cory booker only uncertain senate republican vote that of senator susan collins of maine who remains undecided and expected to make her decision after sept merkley in a statement his website pledged to vote for deal even while pointing to significant shortcomings that he said us must address with a massive intelligence program and monitoring merkley said he was troubled that deal allows iran to import conventional arms after five years and ballistic missile technology after eight years and sets no restrictions how iran can use money it reclaims when sanctions are lifted but he rejected a proposal from deal opponents to try to renegotiate accord for better terms if us rejects deal and iran resumes its nuclear program united states would be viewed by international community as undermining a strong framework for peacefully blocking a potential iranian bomb merkley said while republicancontrolled house has enough votes to pass a resolution rejecting deal it s unclear whether senate does assuming all senate republicans oppose accord they would need support from six democrats to get votes necessary to advance a resolution

am comments monday daily beast published a hugely important story about att s in house for profit surveillance operation called project hemisphere program has nothing to do with information sharing legally required under a warrant but rather consists of a business line through telecom giant stores customer data longer than peers in order to turn around and sell it to government agencies no warrant required this allows law enforcement to use secret and never disclosed evidence to build a cases against citizens via a shady and unaccountable practice known as parallel construction article titled att spying americans for profit new documents reveal and a must read here are some key excerpts in hemisphere was revealed by new york times and described only within a powerpoint presentation made by drug enforcement administration times described it as a partnership between att and us government justice department said it was an essential and prudently deployed counternarcotics tool however att s own documentation reported here by daily beast for first time shows hemisphere was used far beyond war drugs to include everything from investigations of homicide to medicaid fraud hemisphere isn t a partnership but rather a product att developed marketed and sold a cost of millions of dollars per year to taxpayers no warrant required to make use of company s massive trove of data according to att documents only a promise from law enforcement to not disclose hemisphere if an investigation using it becomes public these new revelations come as company seeks to acquire time warner in face of vocal opposition saying deal would be bad for consumers donald trump told supporters over weekend he would kill acquisition if he s elected president hillary clinton has urged regulators to scrutinize deal fact that this deal even being considered shows what a giant joke this county has become as noted twitter earlier this week why don t just merge entire sp into one company called oligarchy inc and get it over with michael krieger libertyblitz october while telecommunications companies are legally obligated to hand over records att appears to have gone much further to make enterprise profitable according to aclu technology policy analyst christopher soghoian att has a unique power to extract information from its metadata because it retains so much of it company owns more than threequarters of us landline switches and second largest share of nation s wireless infrastructure and cellphone towers behind verizon att retains its cell tower data going back to july longer than other providers verizon holds records for a year and sprint for months according to a retention schedule obtained by daily beast disclosure of hemisphere was not first time att has been caught working with law enforcement above and beyond what law requires a statement of work from shows how hushhush att wants to keep hemisphere government agency agrees not to use data as evidence in any judicial or administrative proceedings unless there no other available and admissible probative evidence it says but those charged with a crime are entitled to know evidence against them come trial adam schwartz staff attorney for activist group electronic frontier foundation said that means att leaves investigators no choice but to construct a false investigative narrative to hide how they use hemisphere if they plan to prosecute anyone once att provides a lead through hemisphere then investigators use routine police work like getting a court order for a wiretap or following a suspect around to provide same evidence for purpose of prosecution this known as parallel construction parallel construction a pernicious side effect of all this spying it s a very important topic covered last year in post how dea uses parallel construction to hide unconstitutional investigations this document here striking schwartz told daily beast ve seen documents produced by government regarding hemisphere but this first time ve seen an att document requires parallel construction in a service to government it s very troubling and not way law enforcement should work in this country federal government reimburses municipalities for expense of hemisphere through same grant program that blamed for police militarization by paying for military gear like bearcat vehicles there s government again tirelessly working against from behind scenes a minimum there a very serious question whether they should be doing it without a warrant a benefit to parallel construction they never have to face that crucible then judge defendant general public media and elected officials never know that att and police across america funded by white house are using world s largest metadata database to surveil people schwartz said sheriff and police departments pay from to upward of million a year or more for hemisphere access harris county texas home to houston made its inaugural payment to att of in according to a contract reviewed by daily beast four years later county s hemisphere bill had increased more than tenfold to att documents state law enforcement doesn t need a search warrant to use hemisphere just an administrative subpoena does not require probable cause dea was granted administrative subpoena power in just another reason to end failed war drugs and close down dea recall unconstitutional surveillance was actually pioneered by dea a decade before attacks see how nsa surveillance was birthed from drug war dea tracked billions of phone calls pre att stores details for every call text message skype chat or other communication that has passed through its infrastructure retaining many records dating back to according to times hemisphere report scope and length of collection has accumulated trillions of records and believed to be larger than any phone record database collected by nsa under patriot act times reported this summer switched cellphone service away from att and was able to reduce monthly bill by nearly should consider doing same in liberty

iran agreed in principle to accept significant restrictions its nuclear facilities for least a decade and submit to international inspections under a framework deal announced thursday after months of contentious negotiations with united states and other world powers in return international sanctions that have battered iran s economy would be lifted in phases if it meets its commitments meaning it could take a year or less for relief from penalties to kick in framework agreement a milestone in negotiations that began years ago not a final deal but it creates parameters for three more months of negotiations over technical details and some matters that remain unresolved any one of those issues could doom a comprehensive agreement among them pace sanctions will be suspended political understanding with details that have reached a solid foundation for good deal are seeking said secretary of state john f kerry sounding hoarse after an allnight negotiation session key moments in long history of usiran tensions agreement includes almost all restrictions iran s nuclear facilities laboratories mines and mills that united states had sought in recent months although it initially aimed for even tougher restrictions but iran would get several benefits that may make deal more palatable to politicians and public in tehran it would not have to close any of its three nuclear facilities though it would be left with only one that would enrich uranium levels low enough to create fuel for power plants but not high enough to create weaponsgrade material limitations would produce a oneyear breakout period meaning it would take iran a full year to build up enough material to build one nuclear warhead compared with current estimates of two to three months officials said many sanctions initially would be suspended rather than lifted permanently as iran sought so they could be snapped back into place if iran was discovered to be cheating officials said iran s apparent acceptance of so many conditions sought by united states could give obama administration a tool to fend off critics in congress who want to impose new sanctions to wring more concessions from iranians white house fears such steps could scuttle talks and prompt tehran to resume its nuclear program full tilt iran claims its nuclear program for peaceful civilian uses while negotiations will continue through june much of attention will now shift to white house and its defense of negotiations both in classified briefings to congress and in public arenas obama hailed agreement as a historic understanding and asked whether anyone really thinks that deal a worse option than risk of another war in middle east israeli prime minister benjamin netanyahu a persistent critic of negotiations told obama by telephone that a final deal based parameters announced thursday would threaten survival of israel according to an israeli statement kerry s predecessor state department hillary rodham clinton called framework agreement an understanding saying it was an important step toward a comprehensive agreement that would prevent iran from getting a nuclear weapon and strengthen security of united states israel and region announcement of agreement was made by wearylooking diplomats from iran european union united states and five other nations most had slept only one or two hours after previous day s talks stretched nearly through night they sounded exuberant even before they arrived a lausanne school a few miles from hotel where last rounds of talks had been held many diplomats had been cautious after negotiations failed to meet a march deadline but once bargaining ended thursday there was a flurry of excited tweets big day wrote kerry who shouldered most of direct negotiations with iran once agreement was reached diplomats walked onto a stage bearing flags of nations involved in talks iran germany france britain russia china and united states then mohammad javad zarif iran s foreign minister and chief negotiator in talks and european foreign policy chief federica mogherini made statements mogherini said iran and world powers were taking a decisive step before she listed main parameters for an eventual deal in iran would be permitted to pursue civilian use of nuclear technology a stepbystep guide to what iran agreement actually means under agreement iran s heavywater reactor in arak would be rebuilt so it could not produce weaponsgrade plutonium no nuclear fuel would be reprocessed and spent fuel would be exported or diluted iran s underground plant fordow would be converted from a uraniumenrichment site into a nuclear physics and technology center site was built secretly deep inside a mountain near qom and would be difficult to destroy by military attack as mogherini was speaking state department was emailing reporters a fact sheet outlining more details that it said iran had agreed to though it could not immediately be confirmed that iran was indeed board with every item in one of most significant points number of iran s centrifuges would be cut by twothirds to about according to statement it said they would be firstgeneration machines not more advanced ones that iran has sought keeping old centrifuges a key element in establishing oneyear breakout period a red line for washington fact sheet said iran further agreed not to enrich uranium above level of percent for least years that level of lowenriched uranium suitable as fuel for nuclear power plants but not as fissile material for nuclear weapons require uranium enriched to about percent purity iran has agreed to reduce its stockpile of about kilograms pounds of lowenriched uranium to kilograms pounds for years fact sheet said restrictions would be monitored through inspections conducted by international atomic energy agency and some of them would last years under accord zarif seemed to go out of his way to thank kerry for investing so much time and effort in negotiations he said had been conducted with mutual respect preliminary agreement could foretell beginning of a new chapter in iran s relationship with world and particularly united states though six nations negotiated with iran much of heavy lifting was done in meetings between united states and iran countries have been hostile toward each other for decades particularly since revolution and seizure of dozens of american diplomats who were held hostage in us embassy in tehran but united states and iran will remain odds many issues have serious differences with united states zarif said have built mutual distrust in past so what hope that through courageous implementation of this some of that trust could be remedied but that for us all to wait and see william branigin in washington contributed to this report key moments in long history of usiran tensions transcript iranian foreign minister zarif framework for a nuclear deal another nation blazed trail for iran in developing a nuclear program

whether or not christians should celebrate halloween has been a controversial topic for decades some view dressing up eating candy and enjoying festivities harmless and innocent while others view it as an offense to their faith americans spend nearly billion yearly making it second largest commercial holiday in country as commercialized as celebration has become many of its roots are completely paganist this a cause for christians to avoid entire celebration this a time of year filled with debate but not necessarily politics many christians are convinced that halloween a satanic holiday while rest of world has found their sweet spot complete with costumes and candy children and adults have opportunity to dress in accordance with their imagination confirming from its haunted history to modern festivities this holiday a big deal with decorations candy parties and costumes average american spends up to in spirit of celebration halloween holiday that links seasons of fall and winter reportedly it originated with one of ancient celtic festivals an event where people would wear various costumes and light bonfires in hopes of warding off roaming ghosts however by late s americans shifted theory of halloween into a holiday centered community and fun events focus for many has transitioned from witchcraft and ghosts to neighborhood celebratory events with evolving of focal point should christians change their stance to celebrate holiday despite having least partial roots from a christian tradition relationship between halloween and christians has long been complicated october martin luther essentially started protestant reformation in wittenberg germany when he nailed his theses to a door many of early christian groups that came to america rejected this holiday as pagan protestant reformation heavily influenced pilgrims puritans quakers and baptists causing great majority to frown upon it however that did not prevent halloween from finding its way to american shores in eighth century pope gregory iii dedicated november as a time to honor all saints and martyrs holiday became widely recognized as all saints day evening before was known as all hallows eve later became halloween word hallow originated from old english word for holy and e en an abbreviation of evening as such halloween represented night before all saints day over time halloween advanced into a secular communitybased holiday branded by childfriendly activities that include costumes neighborhood trickortreating and more recently trunkortreating along with a variety of pumpkinflavored foods parties for both children and adults have become a very common way to celebrate holiday some christians still choose to lock themselves indoors with lights off but others have found freedom in their faith and are liberty to decide when and how to participate in multiple countries around world as days grow shorter and nights get colder people continue to escort winter season in with candycoated gatherings and a wide range of costumes halloween a celebration that allows people of all ages to participate nonetheless question remains should christians celebrate due to efforts of community leaders and parents halloween has lost most of its illogical and religious undertones and now more about imagination than spooky interpretation there nothing sinful about a christian dressing up and participating in fun nonthreatening celebrations as a result many christians find no harm in dressing in costumes attending parties and festivals as well as allowing their children to participate in school and local activities by cherese jackson virginia sources history halloween kidsville news around world october grace to christians and halloween photo credits top image courtesy of billy wilson washington connecticut gov dan malloy d will sign an executive order monday barring statefunded travel to indiana because of states new law that could allow businesses to turn away gay and lesbian customers for religious reasons malloys move would make connecticut first state to boycott indiana over its religious freedom restoration act gov mike pence r quietly signed into law last week law allows businesses in state to cite religious beliefs as a legal defense opponents fear it offers legal protection for businesses to refuse service to lesbian gay bisexual and transgender people two cities san francisco and seattle have imposed similar bans in response to law businesses have also retaliated angies list pulling a campus expansion project in indianapolis and ceo of salesforce a billion software corporation announced plans to dramatically reduce investment in state because of law twenty states have rfra laws but indianas law substantially different while other state rfras apply to disputes between a person and a government indianas law goes further and applies to disputes between private citizens that means for example a business owner could use law to justify discrimination against customers who might otherwise be protected under law indiana law could result in employers landlords small business owners or corporations taking law into their own hands and acting in ways that violate generally applicable laws grounds that they have a religious justification for doing so reads their letter members of public will then be asked to bear cost of their employers their landlords their local shopkeepers or a police officers private religious beliefs thats in sharp contrast to states like connecticut has an rfra but one that pertains only to religious institutions not private establishments and unlike some other states connecticut also doesnt permit discrimination based sexual orientation in any private establishment or institution before signing order he called indiana law disturbing and outright discriminatory and said national collegiate athletic association should relocate its final four tournament from indiana to another state those games are set to begin later this week think that would be a wise choice for them to do if thats possible said malloy ill leave it up to them to make those decisions ncaa president mark emmert has said he surprised and disappointed by indianas law and that he waiting for some kind of clarification to law or an outright repeal before deciding whether to keep holding sporting events in state malloy said his executive order allows for any of states current contractual obligations with indiana to play out but said he doesnt plan to enter into any new ones somebodys got to stand up to this kind of bigotry and im prepared to do it he said

iran successfully testfired a mediumrange ballistic missile capable of striking us forces in region as well as israel third such test since nuclear agreement with western nations took effect in january multiple defense officials confirmed to fox news rogue nation conducted test in defiance of a united nations resolution that calls iran to cease work its ballistic missile program iran has to abide by un resolutions with regard to ballistic missiles tests and if they have violated or not been consistent with those resolutions that clearly would be a concern for us pentagon press secretary peter cook said any ballistic missile launch by iran tracked by us military spy satellites pick up flash during launch this case was no different according to officials gen ali abdollahi deputy chief of armed forces headquarters said latest missile tested very accurate within meters eight meters means nothing it means its without any error he said he did not elaborate in march iran testfired two ballistic missiles one emblazoned with phrase israel must be wiped out in hebrew that set off an international outcry since december iran has shipped out its lowenriched uranium disabled its heavy water reactor in arak and weeks ago sold more than million worth of heavy water to us in compliance with nuclear deal however iran has ignored separate un resolutions barring islamic republic from ballistic missile tests fox news was first to report a secret iranian ballistic missile launch in november testfiring was carried out two weeks ago irans semiofficial tasnim news agency quoted abdollahi as saying tasnim close to countrys powerful revolutionary guard in charge of iranian ballistic missiles program agency said missile has a range of miles enough to reach much of middle east iranian military commanders have described them as a strategic asset and a strong deterrent capable of hitting us bases or israel in event of a strike iran analysts say iran likely seeking to demonstrate it making progress with its ballistic program despite scaling back nuclear program following deal that led to lifting of international sanction tehran last month gen amir ali hajizadeh chief of guards airspace division said a new upgraded version of sajjil a solid fuel highspeed missile with a range of miles that was first tested in would soon be ready but it was not immediately clear if missile abdollahi referred to was new sajjil landmark deal does not include provisions against missile launches and when it came into effect jan security council lifted most un sanctions against tehran including a ban it had imposed in iran testing missiles capable of carrying nuclear warheads to deal with restrictions in nuclear agreement council adopted a resolution last july only calls iran not to carry out such tests fox news lucas tomlinson and associated press contributed to this report

monday made case that al gore should run for president but theres another obvious contender out there too joe biden over yahoo matt bai makes case for biden biden he writes a better candidate than most pundits have ever given him credit for yeah hes sloppy and meandering and says some nutty stuff but thats all part of being genuine and threedimensional may be most valuable trait in modern politics and not a bad contrast to clintons robotic discipline and bidens certainly got resume when president barack obama wanted to make sure stimulus money didnt disappear to fraud he turned to biden nobody messes with joe he said and biden succeeded when white house wanted to avoid fiscal cliff it was biden who closed deal with minority leader mitch mcconnell when obama flubbed first debate against mitt romney it was biden who restored tickets mojo by bullying his way past rep paul ryan when democrats held their convention it was bidens speech that pulled highest ratings beating both bill clinton and obama bidens most offthereservation moment meanwhile kind of thing that should help him in he pushed obama administration to embrace gay marriage before it was quite ready time it looked like a gaffe now it looks prescient and yet according to a recent marist poll biden running points behind hillary clinton for democratic nomination in and only one point ahead of sen elizabeth warren a quick scan of realclearpolitics roundup of democratic primary polls shows thats no outlier its not like biden has been out of public eye for last seven years so why if hes such a good politician doesnt he command more support in democratic party heres guess theres a cultural gap between biden and party he seeks to represent biden an oldschool white male politician in a party thats increasingly young multicultural and female bidens gaffes matter because they tend to reinforce perception among democrats that he belongs to a different era when biden calls shady lenders shylocks or says obama first mainstream africanamerican who articulate and bright and clean and a nicelooking guy he ends up coming off as in new york magazine words accidentally racist grandma that leaves biden facing something more toxic than opposition condescension what wrote of biden in january still true today think in continuing drama that obama presidency biden often appears as comic relief hes zany neighbor adorable uncle as a result his presidential ambitions burn brightly even today have mostly been laughed off somehow sitting vice president of united states former chairman of both senate foreign relations committee and senate judiciary committee a man whos a nickname basis with many of worlds most powerful leaders seen in many quarters as lacking gravitas to be president there much thats weird about this hillary clinton a powerful candidate but theres nothing about last few months that makes her look invulnerable shes shown real rust in interviews pissed off liberals and found herself in an email scandal but while biden isnt much older than clinton shes somehow been more adept signaling cultural affinity with young democrats than hes been though shes occasionally struggled too most notably in her interview with terry gross gay marriage she also has a connection to female voters he cant touch even her memes are better dont know exactly how biden fixes this or even if he can clinton isnt inevitable and biden should by all rights pose a real threat to her but though bidens always been known as a great speaker he needs to learn to talk to a different party than one he grew up in

washington game now requires that any unindicted politician with a bit of ambition even yearold jerry brown let it be known that he thinking about maybe just possibly running for president but here s flip side media culture now demands to know whether these pols are quietly plotting a white house bid and treats it as sort of strange if answer no what do mean re not feverishly plotting a presidential bid three years before next election there something wrong with guess don t like to take no for an answer take paul ryan a natural leader of conservative movement republican vp nominee last year a man who can translate beltway jargon into main street concerns wisconsin congressman in spotlight because he just hammered out a budget agreement with patty murray that passed senate yesterday with votes three fewer than supported a cloture vote after winning bipartisan approval in house modest deal noteworthy mainly because it temporarily ends washington gridlock and threats of shutdown and default but it also brought sniping from more militant conservatives who see ryan as selling out to democrats dustup triggered a spate of whither paul ryan pieces in press he running he not running it s already have to know in interviews hill conducted with more than two dozen house republicans from across ideological spectrum over last couple of weeks many of ryan s colleagues said they are doubtful he will run for president in most believe that concerns for his young family will lead him to lay claim to job he s always wanted chairman of house ways and means committee ryan wants to be a hill poohbah and not move into white house ryan tuesday told wall street journal that he plans to lead ways and means committee in next congress ryan and his wife janna have three children and his friends say that his concern about hardship of an month presidential campaign a genuine factor in his consideration this human factor that journalists rarely pause to consider running for president a meat grinder that chews people up and ryan got a taste of that as mitt romney s running mate don t know whether ryan being coy or whether with highest positive rating among iowa republicans he ll change his mind and jump into race but do think press ought to take him his word until there s evidence to contrary guess it wasn t a big story when john podesta compared republicans to a suicide cult but now that podesta joining president obama s inner circle politico asking can john podesta save him boy that s a tall order in its profile of podesta who stepping down as head of center for american progress this incendiary quote was slipped into narrative without comment white house officials said podesta need to focus executive action given that they are facing a second term against a cult worthy of jonestown in charge of one of houses of congress podesta was comparing john boehner to jim jones who led his followers to kill themselves didn t that set off any alarm bells but quote caused a stir now that former bill clinton chief of staff joining white house and he tweeted an apology in an old interview snark got in front of judgment apologize to speaker boehner whom primary it was remarkable it was historic and it netted him four more delegates than clinton in state meanwhile in mississippi clinton won with more than percent of vote and gained more delegates than of that means that clinton has percent of delegates she needs to become democratic party s nominee subtract superdelegates clinton dominating even among this group of elected officials and party luminaries and she has delegates to sanders s a margin of worth noting that a wider lead than margin by clinton ever trailed thensen barack obama in long slog of primary race that lead may not seem momentous after all almost delegates are yet to be allocated in primaries and caucuses to come problem for sanders that democrats allocate their delegates proportionally in every state meaning that where clinton s point win translated to a net gain of delegates southern states help clinton extend her lead over sanders sanders s one big win came in new hampshire s primary but his point margin polling released sunday morning suggests that sanders has a big hill to climb clinton leads senator percent to percent in florida and has an edge of percent to percent in ohio according to nbc marist surveys race in illinois according to nbcmarist closer with clinton percent and sanders percent but wait say polling in michigan had sanders down points and he won there so this polling could be wrong too sure it could problem for sanders this let s say each of nbcmarist surveys off by points in clinton s favor note that this a thought experiment very much doubt a credible pollster such as this one would be off by even close to that amount that would mean sanders loses florida by single digits essentially ties clinton in ohio and wins illinois by points delegate allocation from that trio of a recent draft of transpacific partnership freetrade deal would give us pharmaceutical firms unprecedented protections against competition from cheaper generic drugs possibly transcending patent protections in us law politico has obtained a draft copy of tpp s intellectual property chapter as it stood may start of latest negotiating round in guam while us trade officials would not confirm authenticity of document they downplayed its importance emphasizing that terms of deal are likely to change significantly as talks enter their final stages those terms are still secret but public will get to see them once twelve tpp nations reach a final agreement and president obama seeks congressional approval still draft chapter will provide ammunition for critics who have warned that tpp s protections for pharmaceutical companies could dump trillions of dollars of additional health care costs patients businesses and governments around pacific rim highly technical page document cluttered with objections from other tpp nations shows that us negotiators have fought aggressively and least until guam successfully behalf of big pharma draft text includes provisions that could make it extremely tough for generics to challenge brandname pharmaceuticals abroad those provisions could also help block copycats from selling cheaper versions of expensive cuttingedge drugs known as biologics inside us restricting treatment for american patients while jacking up medicare and medicaid costs for american taxpayers there s very little distance between what pharma wants and what us demanding said rohit malpini director of policy for doctors without borders throughout tpp talks obama administration has pledged to balance goals of fostering innovation in drug industry means allowing higher profits and promoting wider access to valuable medicines means keeping prices down us trade representative michael froman has pointed out that pharmaceutical companies often have to invest hundreds of millions of dollars to get a new drug to market they would have little incentive to do without strong protections for patented product but froman has also recognized value of allowing much cheaper generic drugs to enter market after those brandname patents expire in us generics now comprise more than fivesixths of all prescription drugs but only about onequarter of drug costs advocates for global poor senior citizens labor unions and consumers as well as generics industry have accused administration of abandoning that balance pushing a pharmaceuticalcompany agenda expense of patients and taxpayers one critic hoping to illustrate point and rally opposition to tpp in congress gave politico draft chapter was labeled this document contains tpp confidential information every page us officials said key point to remember about trade deals that no provision ever final until entire deal final and that major compromises tend to happen very end of negotiations they expect real horsetrading to begin now that obama has signed fasttrack legislation requiring congress to pass or reject tpp without amendments negotiations intellectual property are complex and continually evolving said trevor kincaid a spokesman for froman pharmaceutical products are working closely with stakeholders congress and partner countries to develop an approach that aims to make affordable lifesaving medicine more widely available while creating incentives for development of new treatments and cures striking this important balance heart of work draft chapter covers software music and other intellectual property issues as well but its most controversial language involves rights of drug companies text reveals disputes between us often with support from japan and its tpp partners over a variety of issues what patents can cover when and how long they can be extended how long pharmaceutical companies can keep their clinical data private and much more every issue us sided with drug companies in favor of stricter intellectual property protections some of most contentious provisions involve patent linkage would prevent regulators in tpp nations from approving generic drugs whenever there are any unresolved patent issues tpp draft would make this linkage mandatory could help drug companies fend off generics just by claiming an infringement obama administration often describes tpp as most progressive freetrade deal in history citing its compliance with tough labor and environment protections enshrined in socalled may agreement of set a framework for several trade deals time but mandatory linkage seems to be a departure from may pharmaceutical provisions in an april letter to froman heather bresch ceo of generic drug company mylan warned that mandatory patent linkage would be a recipe for indefinite evergreening of pharmaceutical monopolies leading to automatic rejection of generic applications us already has mandatory linkage but most other tpp countries do not and bresch argued that us law includes a number of safeguards and incentives for generic companies that have not made it into tpp with all due respect ustr has cherrypicked single provision designed to block generic entry to market bresch wrote generics are thriving in us despite linkage saving americans an estimated billion drugs in but us world s largest market and advocates fear that generic manufacturers may not take risk and expense of litigation in smaller markets if tpp tilts playing field against them one generics manufacturer hospira reportedly testified a tpp forum in melbourne australia that it would not launch generics outside us in markets with linkage opponents are also worried about treaty s effect us market because its draft language would extend mandatory patent linkage to biologics next big thing in pharmaceutical world biologics can cost hundreds of thousands of dollars a year for patients with illnesses like rheumatoid arthritis hepatitis b and cancer and first knockoffs have not yet reached pharmacies critics say that extending linkage to biologics can have hundreds of patents would help insulate them from competition forever it would be a dramatic departure from us law and it would put a real crimp in ability of less expensive drugs to get to market said kj hertz a lobbyist for aarp people are going to look this very closely in congress drug companies are already pushing for tpp to guarantee them years of exclusivity for their data regarding biologics although draft text suggests other tpp nations have not agreed jay taylor vice president of pharmaceutical research and manufacturers of america said it s crucial for tpp to protect intellectual property that emerges from years of expensive research so that drug companies can continue to develop new medicines for patients around world these innovations could be severely hindered if ip protections are scaled back taylor said this especially important in area of biologic medicines could hold key to unlocking treatments for diseases that have thwarted researchers for years us officials would not discuss status of tpp talks but they suggested may agreement did include a milder form of linkage although it didn t prevent regulators from approving generics mired in patent disputes they also believe a us law included a form of linkage for biologics although again that laws dispute resolution process for patent issues was not as prescriptive as tpp draft and they cautioned that any preguam draft would not reflect recent negotiations over transition periods that would delay stricter patent standards in developing countries like vietnam in any case kincaid said us negotiators are determined to strike a balance between innovation and access in final product while this touchstone negotiations are still very much in process and details of a final outcome cannot yet be forecasted he said but malpani of doctors without borders said us negotiators have basically functioned as drug lobbyists tpp countries have percent of global economic output and deal widely seen as establishing new benchmarks for some of most complex areas of global business malpani fears it could set a precedent that crushes generic drug industry under a mountain of regulation and litigation consider this worstever agreement in terms of access to medicine he said it would create higher drug prices around world and in us too
