by now everybody knows injuries that contributed to freddie grays death baltimore authorities and representatives for gray s family agree that yearold sustained fatal trauma to his neck and spine some point while in police custody following his arrest april although a full autopsy hasnt yet been released family has said that gray s spine was nearly severed and that his doctors had attempted to repair three fractured neck vertebrae and a crushed voice box last week baltimore sun spoke to medical experts who said that grays injuries were in papers words comparable to those seen in victims of highspeed crashes while this may end up being a significant detail of investigation much still unclear about circumstances of grays death including how grays head might have hit wall of van hard enough to kill him over past few weeks baltimore police have provided few answers about how gray went from seemingly healthy enough to flee police morning of april to dead april after a week in a coma wednesday april hours after stating that they would not give public their forthcoming internal report grays death police leaked a different document to washington post it was first new piece of information from police in nearly a week but instead of clarity it offered more confusion in its reporting of incident baltimore sun found witnesses who refuted millers account to court in an april article sun reporter kevin rector wrote kevin moore a yearold friend of grays from gilmor homes said he rushed outside when he heard gray was being arrested and saw him screaming for his life with his face planted ground one officer had his knee grays neck moore said and another was bending his legs backward they had him folded up like he was a crab or a piece of origami moore said he was all bent up others claimed to have seen officers beating gray with batons one point during arrest according to police commissioner an officer pulled out a stun gun moore claimed to have seen this as well according to sun police investigators failed to obtain footage from a convenience store surveillance camera that may have captured incident more clearly instead officers found that moment in question had been taped over by time they got there this was a common theme throughout investigation as officers reportedly failed to obtain footage from other cameras that may have recorded key moments in grays subsequent ride to police station a third perspective grays arrest was added to mix wednesday when cnn interviewed an anonymous relative of one of arresting officers woman said officer believes gray was injured outside paddy wagon though she expressed personal concern that six officers are going to be punished behind something that maybe one or two or even three officers may have done to freddie gray this critical question and there still no clear answer witness video shows gray screaming time he was loaded into vehicle according to official police timeline van made its first stop four minutes later so that officers could shackle an irate gray he was removed from van and was placed in leg irons police and witnesses then agree that gray was returned to van in both hand and leg restraints officers have admitted they didnt buckle grays seat belt a violation of police department policy that has led to suggestions that gray may have been victim of a rough ride an illegal but not uncommon technique in officers drive vans in such a way as to cause injury to detained passengers am about minutes after gray was first put in van vehicle stopped for a third time after driver asked an officer to perform a check gray this had previously been explained as second stop and exactly what happened there was reportedly a key part of investigation batts has said that responding officers had to pick gray up off floor and place him seat and that gray requested a medic this time he was ignored again fourth and final stop was made minutes later to pick up another prisoner this point its unclear what state gray was in davis has suggested that gray was again found floor but responsive enough to make another request for a medic if this request was indeed made it was evidently denied other reports suggest that gray may have already lost consciousness when this second person was picked up details of allens account conflict with earlier reports grays time in van including reports from police officials themselves according to post allen told investigators that gray was banging against walls and intentionally trying to injure himself but allen later told wbal when got in van didnt hear nothing it was a smooth ride went straight to police station all heard was a little banging for about four seconds just heard little banging just little banging

female prison employee center of an investigation into escape of two killers pleaded not guilty friday night to helping them flee maximum security facility prison tailor shop instructor joyce mitchell was arraigned a felony charge of promoting prison contraband and misdemeanor count of criminal facilitation authorities said mitchell accused of aiding in escape of inmates david sweat and richard matt from clinton correctional facility in upstate new york last week she entered court room with her hands cuffed in front of her clad jeans and a lime green top looking terrified she did not speak she was ordered held in jail cash bail or bond felony county and due back in court monday morning district attorney andrew wylie said earlier friday contraband did not include power tools used by men as they cut holes in their cell walls and a steam pipe to escape through a manhole last weekend wylie didnt elaborate charges friday as more than officers continued to search for escapees concentrating in a rural area around prison in adirondacks near canadian border earlier residents reported seeing tow men jumping a stone wall outside dannemora maj charles guess of new york state gave a stern warning to convicts an evening press conference were coming for and will not stop until are caught he said he added that mitchells arrest represented one large piece of puzzle in quest to find men asked about any clues to escapees whereabouts guess said there was no conclusive evidence that either had left area none of reports of possible sightings has been confirmed he added its day six he said and if they have not escaped area youve got to assume theyre cold wet tired and hungry as to whether they split up guess said theres no reason to believe theyre not together but were planning for both eventualities earlier wylie said mitchell a supervisor in prisons tailoring shop brought contraband into prison but he declined to elaborate what specifically she gave men albany timesunion reported late thursday that mitchell told new york state police she gave matt and sweat access to a cell phone and smuggled tools into prison a police source close to investigation confirmed to fox news friday that mitchell planned to provide a getaway car for two convicted murderers but had a change of heart last minute mitchell instead checked herself into a hospital some miles away from prison complaining of panic attacks according to law enforcement mitchell joined prison staff in march of and earned a year she was suspended without pay effective friday police meanwhile said friday that two men believed to be inmates were spotted jumping over a stone wall in woods in saranac a few miles from prison buffalo news reported according to newspaper a law enforcement officer said he believes he saw two am friday and authorities converged area colleen cringle who lives cringle road in saranac told newspaper police were using her home as a staging area to search for killers new york gov andrew cuomo has vowed that state law would come down hard any prison system employee who crosses line with inmates if do it will be convicted and then youll be other side of prison that youve been policing and that not a pleasant place to be cuomo said governor also said investigators are talking to several people who may have facilitated escape timesunion also reported that mitchell had been investigated in recent months by state corrections departments inspector general after a fellow prison worker complained that she had gotten too close to sweat and matt that investigation did not result in any discipline prison employees and correction officers are prohibited from having relationships with inmates or performing favors for them matt was serving years to life for kidnap torture and hacksaw dismemberment of matts yearold former boss whose body was found in pieces in a river matt and an accomplice stuffed william rickerson in a car trunk in his pajamas and drove around with him for hours because he wouldnt tell them location of large sums of money he was believed to have according to testimony matt bent back elderly mans fingers until they broke and later snapped rickersons neck with his bare hands after killing matt fled to mexico where he killed a man outside a bar sweat was doing life without parole for his part in killing of also noted that he would be squarely behind bush if christie s florida rival emerges victorious out of gop primaries if jeb bush republican nominee will do everything can to help him get elected he among prominent party financiers a sharp contrast with more than million that republican governors association brought in under his recent chairmanship while christie aides have credited fundraising contributors that he supports vaccination for diseases such as measles spencer zwick mitt romney s finance chairman in said attitude of former christie backers home telling kyrillos a very good reporters job to comfort afflicted and afflict comfortable a credo that humorously was originally written as a smear of selfrighteous nature of journalists and so justification for going after a public figure increases in proportion to his or her stature bigger figure looser restraints after a quarter of a century national stage theres no more comfortable political figure to afflict than hillary clinton and shes in for a lot of affliction over next year and half thats generally a good way for reporters to go about their business after all more power a person wants in republic more voters should know about her or him but its also an essential frame for thinking about longtoxic relationship between clintons and media why coverage of hillary clinton differs from coverage of other candidates for presidency and whether that difference encourages distortions that will ultimately affect presidential race clinton rules are driven by reporters and editors desire to score ultimate prize in contemporary journalism scoop that brings down hillary clinton and her familys political empire least in that way republicans and media have a common interest understand these dynamics well having cowritten a book that demonstrated how bill and hillary clinton used hillarys time state to build family political operation and set up for their fourth presidential campaign that to say ive done a lot of research about clintons relationship with media and experienced it firsthand as an author felt that owed it to and reader to report investigate and write with same mix of curiosity skepticism rigor and compassion that would use with any other subject wanted to sell books of course but easier way to do that proven over time to write as though clintons are purest form of evil same holds for daily reporting want to drive traffic to a website write something nasty about a clinton particularly hillary as a reporter get sucked into playing by clinton rules this what ive seen in colleagues and in one of former colleagues a hardnosed reporter who has put countless political pelts his wall once told that everyone in public life has something to hide who goes down in flames of scandal politicians decide to go after that may not be percent true but its true enough act of choosing time and again to go after same person has effect of tainting that person even when an investigation or reporting turns up nothing nefarious and its time not spent digging into his or her adversaries original source of alleged malfeasance could come from other party within a politicians party or from reporters own observations and industrious digging but two things are crystal clear if theres no investigation theres no scandal and if theres no scandal theres no scalp clintons have been under investigation for about years now theres little doubt theyve produced more information for investigators lawyers and journalists about their finances their business and philanthropic dealings and their decisionmaking processes in government than any officials in american history theyve watched countless friends frogmarched into congressional hearings and in some cases to jail they know theres a good chance that any expressed thought will become part of public record and twisted for political gain most absurd allegations against hillary clinton have been bookends her public career so far that she had something to do with suicide of clinton white house aide vince foster and that she bears responsibility for terrorist attack that killed us ambassador to libya chris stevens but in between there was travelgate filegate and whitewater some were less legitimate than others when clinton surprisingly claimed that she and her husband were dead broke when they left white house it was because they had spent all of their money to defend themselves against an eightyear barrage of investigations its understandable then why clintons have a bunker mentality when it comes to transparency but their paranoia leads them to be secretive and their secrecy leads republicans and press to suspect wrongdoing that spurs further investigation only makes clintons more secretive paranoia and persistent investigation feed each other in an endless cycle of probe and parry along way political class and public are forced to choose imperfect sides power couple that always seems to be hiding something or a washington investigation complex that overly partisan and underwhelming in its ability to prove gross misconduct this for republicans a reasonable strategy they know that if they keep investigating her it will do two things keep media writing about scandals that might knock her out and turn off voters who dont want a return to bloodsport politics of s they leak partial stories to reporters hungry for that one great scoop that will give them biggest political scalp of them all but they also err in jumping gun in accusing her of wrongdoing allows clinton to defend herself by pointing folly of her adversaries in touring country to promote book in coauthor and were repeatedly lobbied to assert that clinton a lesbian one gentleman pushed issue during a qa a barnes and noble upper west side of manhattan one of few places might expect that kind of thing to get a rest national enquirer published a story in april alleging that clinton wiped her personal email server clean because it contained references to her lesbian lovers meanwhile conservative media are also convinced clinton preparing to wage a war christianity if she wins presidency but one thing revealed in her state department emails that clinton shared daily religious reflections with her friends its not just outofthebox allegations that keep media machine spun up a year before chelsea clinton got married clinton staffers were kept busy by mainstream journalists who were absolutely sure she had already gone through with secret nuptials and a more serious note remember benghazi flu many political opponents and members of media were unable to accept idea that clinton was forced to cancel planned senate testimony benghazi because shed suffered a concussion now three years later it seems ridiculous to think that clinton was making an excuse shes since testified both sides of hill or that she suffered as karl rove suggested brain damage and if she was making up concussion to avoid testifying how did she suffer brain damage from a fake fall conservative media echo chamber bounces innuendo from rush limbaugh to fox news and back again ensures that most damning story lines true or not stay alive benghazi attacks are a perfect example terrorists killed four americans conservative echo chamber seems convinced hillary clinton fault reasonable argument to make that shouldnt have been in libya in first place and murders were a downthechain result of bad policy but right wing wants to prove that they happened because of clintons actions or inaction security matters theyve talked about security requests denied for libya never mind that stronger contingent would have been in tripoli not benghazi and that theres no evidence clinton herself was aware of requests a standdown order that prevented reinforcements from arriving in benghazi never mind that they wouldnt have gotten there until after fighting was done and that even a house republican committee found that there was no such order and most of absurd of all that clinton knew attack was coming this how limbaugh put it in may freedom of conservative media to make wild allegations often acts as a bulldozer forcing reporters to check into charges and in doing so repeat them by time theyve been debunked theyre part of american publics collective consciousness or as its been said a lie gets around world before truth gets out of bed one outgrowth of clintons terrible relationship with reporters that journalists often assume she acting in bad faith theres good reason for that though shes added some new pros to her press staff for this campaign her operations stance toward media was always a reflection of way bill clintons white house handled journalists back in mids bill clinton relied a series of machiavellian spin doctors to keep press bay with clinton white house modus operandi was to stonewall as long as possible lie if necessary or just out of habit and turn questions around questioners after all bill clinton once wagged his finger a press conference and told reporters did not have sexual relations with that woman ms lewinsky hed lied in a deposition too so press has plenty of precedent for believing that when clintons arent forthcoming and sometimes even when they are theyre covering something up and clintons given history of somesmokenofire investigations launched against them have plenty of precedent for being mistrustful of press result a brutally dysfunctional relationship both sides clintons believe press acts in bad faith and press believes clintons attitudes toward press are evidence that clintons are hiding something that attitude carried over to hillary clintons campaign and to some degree her tenure as secretary of state standard response to a reporters question not an answer it to ignore question or to engage in a socratic debate by asking a question in return its clear clinton doesnt like media one bit as glenn thrush and maggie haberman reported last year a july fourth parade this past weekend clinton aides used rope to create an impromptu moving barrier for reporters keeping them away from candidate and voters she treated them like cattle and they responded by putting video television for last three days mistrust among journalists a problem for clinton and as media an amplifier for public its also little wonder that so many voters are inclined to believe shes often acting in bad faith most americans say shes not honest and trustworthy this view shared by many reporters and most of public makes it much easier to treat clintons actions as though they are uniquely sinister case in point she made a ton of money giving paid speeches to people with business before government so did jeb bush of course but until bush recently released an accounting of some of those speeches media had little interest in his dealings kudos to ken vogel of politico who did some digging that for a story published thursday imbalance in assumptions about clintons motivations another way in clinton code has a distorting effect public perception of her and it too selfperpetuating it leads clinton to assume press biased against her leads her to treat press poorly leads more reporters to assume shes trying to hide something from them when clinton keynoted an annual fundraiser for david axelrods epilepsy charity in june several major news outlets sent reporters to cover speech that was more than three years before election every word every gesture every facial expression scrutinized video of clinton ordering a burrito bowl a chipotle became first viral image of her campaign reporters gave fodder to latenight comedians earlier this year when they made a mad dash to catch up as her campaign van rolled by this coverage of every last detail of course isnt a oneway street it wasnt until a reporter was tipped off to chipotle visit that anyone knew about it she craves attention even more than she detests it but that too has a distorting effect as with royal family in london normally private moments become part of a public narrative her husbands affair her daughters wedding birth of her granddaughter all attention has effect of making clinton seem to casual observer hungrier for press than even average politician and theres no doubt that part of lovehate relationship an intense desire to attract and manipulate coverage but clinton understands that sometimes its better not to be in spotlight best example of that was when she declined requests to appear sunday political talk shows right after benghazi attacks susan rice then ambassador to un and now obamas national security adviser leaped chance to stand in for clinton those appearances ended up costing rice nomination to succeed clinton as secretary of state when many senators concluded she had lied about origin and nature of attacks press has such fascination with clintons that coverage would be there whether hillary clinton wanted it or not for someone who lost a big lead in presidential primary and ceding ground to bernie sanders right now clinton given a lot of credit for her political acumen her detractors see in every move including birth of her granddaughter a grandly conceived and executed political calculation and clinton plays into that by using positives in her life for political gain that doesnt make her different from other candidates for presidency it makes her just like them new jersey gov chris christie talked about his grandmothers his mom his wife and his kids when he launched his bid for presidency last week was that calculated to send messages about christie to public of course best example though was tear one that rolled down clintons cheek as she campaigned in new hampshire after having come in third in iowa caucuses in new york timess maureen dowd pilloried her for what dowd saw as a window into dark part of clintons soul how far political journalism has come from castigating ed muskie for crying to accusing clinton of calculating that tears would help her win shes not that good politics take a dim view of idea that journalists successfully anoint political winners media might have been in bag for barack obama but he didnt win because he got positive coverage he won because he had better strategy a better message and better skills delivering that message in primary and in two general elections he won that said media can definitely weigh down and even destroy a candidate emphasis a candidates flaws real or perceived comes cost of candidates ability to focus his or her message and cost of negative attention to other candidates this a problem for clinton and it seems unlikely to go away hillary clinton comfortable enough to be a target for a lot of journalistic affliction and powerful enough that no one needs to comfort her from that affliction but these double standards are an important factor to keep in mind when judging her against her rivals for presidency whether justices anthony m kennedy and antonin scalia were born in same year chosen by same president live same northern virginia street and in serving together supreme court longer than any other current pair of justices have many times voted same conservative way but one issue how constitution protects gay citizens divides and defines two like no other this week s historic hearing samesex marriage both logical extension and ultimate showdown in a decadeslong argument that so far kennedy has always won here s what fallout of a ruling could be in various states each of kennedy s bold and lyrical rulings behalf of gays times can blind us to certain truths and later generations can see that laws once thought necessary and proper in fact serve only to oppress he wrote in lawrence v texas has been just as reliably followed by a meticulous and fiery denunciation from scalia court has taken sides in culture war departing from its role of assuring as neutral observer that democratic rules of engagement are observed scalia answered in lawrence case kennedy has written all of supreme court s most important decisions gay rights protecting civil rights of homosexuals in romer v evans abolishing antigay sodomy laws in lawrence and ruling in united states v windsor two years ago that federal government must recognize samesex marriages each was a steppingstone to supreme court s consideration tuesday of whether constitution forbids states from prohibiting gay couples to marry if pattern continues and court renders a landmark ruling favoring gay marriage it will likely once again be kennedy whose words memorialize that decision and scalia who will articulate dissent it not a conflict everyone would have predicted for two of ronald reagan s choices for court scalia ascended to bench in and kennedy followed months later two born opposite coasts in are consistent comrades issues important to corporate america and in dismantling campaign finance laws they see restricting political speech after chief justice john g roberts jr sided with liberals to declare affordable care act constitutional scalia and kennedy united with justices clarence thomas and samuel a alito jr in a jointly written page dissent mocking majority opinion and saying entire act should be found invalid kennedy often deciding vote when ideologically divided court splits to but in twothirds of those cases he sides with conservatives but if they often arrive same conclusion one obstacle for samesex marriage proponents in current case kennedy s allegiance to states rights kennedy and scalia could not be more different in how they view a judge s role their different approach to gay rights reflects their more fundamental disagreement about how to think about liberties protected by constitution said paul m smith a washington lawyer who was winning side in lawrence case scalia believes only freedoms that should be viewed as protected by constitution are those that have been protected under american law throughout history defined most specific level smith said otherwise people decide kennedy smith said believes that each generation has right to conceive of newer and broader forms of liberty that merit constitutional protection he sees history as a guide but not a straitjacket their battle compelling said allison orr larsen a william and mary law professor because it brings to forefront theoretical question in constitutional law how should courts respond to change when interpreting constitution michael dorf a professor cornell law school and a former kennedy clerk said his former boss s decisions gay rights were not constructed to lead ultimately to a decision samesex marriage but they provided a foundation for how to view new constitutional rights if that s where country moves scalia other hand champions cause of originalism and edward whelan a former scalia clerk and president of ethics and public policy center said his former boss learned quickly that kennedy s judicial approach was not anything close to what scalia s a basic tenet of originalism that it s not role of judges to impose their own moral philosophies whelan said scalia understands constitution to leave vast bulk of policy issues to democratic processes and rejects notion that it s his role to read his own views into constitution it s worth remembering differences among president reagan s choices for supreme court he fulfilled his campaign pledge to name a woman to bench with sandra day o connor pragmatic arizona politician and judge who quickly became court s center scalia s selection was celebrated by conservatives eager to see a new method of constitutional interpretation forcefully advocated court kennedy was a compromise reagan s third choice for seat he once hoped would be filled by conservative robert bork whose nomination was defeated in senate this was bork seat said smith things could have been much different kennedy s views dorf said were those of a moderate california republican although he never ruled for gay rights as a lower court judge kennedy expressed concern about policy even as he upheld military s right to dismiss gay servicemen and frank j colucci a political science professor purdue university who has written a book about kennedy s jurisprudence recalled that kennedy in a speech criticized supreme court s decision in bowers v hardwick that upheld a georgia statute criminalizing sodomy he came about as close as can as a lower court judge to saying it was wrongly decided colucci said once court kennedy was able to say just that in first gay rights case romer kennedy wrote for majority in striking down a colorado constitutional amendment after some cities in state began passing laws protecting gays from discrimination in housing employment and other areas voters through a referendum approved amendment precluding such government protections colorado s amendment kennedy wrote classifies homosexuals not to further a proper legislative end but to make them unequal to everyone else this colorado cannot do a state cannot so deem a class of persons a stranger to its laws that began something of a callandresponse issue kennedy delivering majority s opinion scalia replying with a scalding dissent read from bench for emphasis this court has no business imposing upon all americans resolution favored by elite class from members of this institution are selected pronouncing that animosity toward homosexuality evil scalia wrote in romer vigorously dissent in lawrence kennedy got chance to reverse court s decision sodomy and did bowers was not correct when it was decided and it not correct today private homosexual conduct between consenting adults he wrote involves liberty of person both in its spatial and more transcendent dimensions o connor wrote a concurring opinion to say ruling did not touch matter of whether gays would be able to marry scalia wrote that homosexuals should be free to promote their cause through democratic means but many americans do not want persons who openly engage in homosexual conduct as partners in their business as scoutmasters for their children as teachers in their children s schools or as boarders in their home they view this as protecting themselves and their families from a lifestyle that they believe to be immoral and destructive he wrote court views it as discrimination two years ago in windsor kennedy wrote that federal government s refusal to offer same government benefits available to heterosexual couples to legally married gay couples tells all world that their otherwise valid marriages are unworthy and humiliates their children again scalia blasted decision saying majority was merely being coy in saying decision did not address whether states are required to give licenses for samesex marriage by formally declaring anyone opposed to samesex marriage an enemy of human decency majority arms well every challenger to a state law restricting marriage to its traditional definition he wrote most federal courts have taken scalia literally court agrees with justice scalia s interpretation of windsor wrote us district judge robert j shelby of salt lake city shelby s decision to strike a marriage ban in utah was first such ruling following windsor and began path that continues to tuesday s oral arguments and will end with court s decision in june years later ve got license and faggot letter right finds a voice samesex marriage gay rights religious rights and a compromise in an unlikely place utah

washington former florida gov jeb bush will lay out a vision of american foreign policy wednesday aimed pushing his nascent presidential campaign out of shadow of his father and brother two former presidents who waged overseas wars love father and brother but am own man and views are shaped by own thinking and own experiences bush will say in a speech to chicago council global affairs according to excerpts provided to reporters late tuesday night in his first major foreign policy address likely republican frontrunner will make case for increased military spending so america can project power and enforce peaceful stability in faroff areas of globe he will also criticize president barack obamas foreign policy calling it inconsistent and indecisive having a military that equal to any threat makes it less likely that will need to put men and women in uniform in harm s way bush plans to tell attendees adding that he believes fundamentally that weakness invites war and strength encourages peace idea that a bigger us military would act as a bigger deterrent to potential foes one that reached its apex during cold war but has been repeatedly challenged in st century by rise of global terrorism and sectarian conflicts obama administration currently working to devise responses to a number of unconventional threats to global security chief among them are rise of socalled islamic state in middle east and russianbacked separatists waging guerrilla war in ukraine from his speech it appears that bush would have america play a greater role than it already does in these conflicts america does not have luxury of withdrawing from world security prosperity and values demand that remain engaged and involved in often distant places bush will say have no reason to apologize for leadership and interest in serving cause of global security global peace and human freedom this vision of americas role in world directly contradicts view held by kentucky sen rand paul another potential candidate that us should avoid involvement in foreign conflicts unless they pose a direct threat to american lives but even as bush stressed that he would devise his own way forward plenty of his foreign policies invite comparisons not contradictions with those of his neoconservative brother george wbush and his realist father george hw bush one reason for this could be that jeb bush has consulted many of same advisers his father and brother relied to help them craft foreign policy while each was in office advisers represent a spectrum of conservative viewpoints from pragmatic james baker former secretary of state in george hw bush administration to fiercely ideological paul wolfowitz who served as deputy secretary of defense in george w bush administration wolfowitz and jeb bush have held similar foreign policy ideas since least when they were both signatories to a set of principles devised by conservative project for new american century were described by group as a reaganite policy of military strength and moral clarity other signatories include former vice president dick cheney and former defense secretary donald rumsfeld

worked for kasich capitol hill yes he s very conservative but there are a few surprises in there and he s in bizarro world with his surprising secondplace finish in new hampshire primary ohio gov john kasich has vaulted out of pack of alsorans to become latest hope of two distinct groups of people republican operatives fearing what donald trump s berserker candidacy might do to their party and ordinary citizens simply hoping to have a sane presidential candidate to vote for until this past week kasich has not attracted much attention mainly because current dynamic of media coverage encourages histrionics and preening but by appearing halfway normal as he did last saturday s debate in south carolina and not engaging in theatrics about carpetbombing waterboarding or ripping up treaties he has become default choice of those who would worry if kasich s opponents ever got their hands nuclear switch he has advantage and disadvantage of a long record had a chance to see him in action as worked for him for years budget and armed services issues although ve had no connection to him in over a decade or to his campaign and am now a political independent in s dawn of his congressional career many members of his own party considered kasich a conservative bomb thrower he would offer his own budget something backbenchers weren t supposed to do in those days he has not changed ideologically or temperamentally his effort to roll back labor protection for state employees was too extreme and ohio voters overwhelmingly defeated it but while kasich has remained a staunch conservative much of his party has lurched so far right it has entered bizarro world this fact accounts for kasich s reputation as a rino republican in name only and why it may be more difficult for him to win his own party s nomination than general election his apostasy in having ohio accept medicaid under affordable care act unforgivable to republicans who consider health insurance for poor to be an instrument of satan likewise in he voted for an assault weapons ban pure heresy for burgeoning ted nugent wing of his party kasich s practice of occasionally dissing wealthy gop donors who are accustomed to blind deference considered a minus among party s bigwigs those deeds might help though in a general election so might his national security experience years house armed services committee counts for far more involvement in that field than all his opponents combined refreshingly as a congressman he avoided kneejerk military interventionism that now mandatory for gop candidates he opposed authorizing marines mission in lebanon in bombing of marine barracks in october of that year and president reagan s decision to quickly withdraw vindicated kasich s stand similarly he opposed president clinton s bombing of serbia in his view amounted to a driveby shooting with cruise missiles in a conflict in no discernible us interests were involved he was also able to work across aisle with democrats to put a stake through heart of b bomber a cold war anachronism billion a copy had become an unaffordable luxury as budget committee chairman kasich was also present creation of first balanced budget since eisenhower era while other factors such as an avalanche of revenues from dotcom bubble set stage for achievement he had a formidable job just to keep his own colleagues task defense hawks were always itching to bust budget caps with more pentagon spending and thenspeaker newt gingrich who appeared to suffer from add occasionally threatened to derail difficult march to a balanced budget with novel and unvetted policy visions general election downside just as there are legitimate questions about hillary clinton s receiving from goldman sachs for three speeches kasich as a nominee would surely face scrutiny over his eight years as a managing director lehman brothers investment bank s collapse in september biggest bankruptcy in us history nearly pitched global economy into abyss lawsuits to settle dismantling of lehman are still reverberating through financial world ohio voters gave him a pass but national inspection of his record would be more thorough certainly if clinton s campaign had anything to do with it stories occasionally surface about kasich s anger management problem as a former employee can corroborate those allegations as well as his tendency to preachy selfrighteousness that a constant temptation of professional politicians these are perhaps pardonable sins both in view of tasteless vaudeville act being played out by several of his gop competitors and his own positive accomplishments

existent copy and no one has heard it since until today when he played it for observer tape minutes and contains much that no longer relevant such as analysis of reelection battle that sen joe lieberman was then facing in connecticut but a seemingly throwaway remark about elections in areas controlled by palestinian authority has taken new relevance amid persistent accusations in presidential campaign by clinton s republican opponent donald trump that current election rigged speaking to jewish press about january election for second palestinian legislative council legislature of palestinian national authority clinton weighed in about result was a resounding victory for hamas seats over uspreferred fatah seats do not think should have pushed for an election in palestinian territories think that was a big mistake said sen clinton and if were going to push for an election then should have made sure that did something to determine who was going to win chomsky recalls being taken aback that anyone could support idea offered by a national political leader no less that us should be in business of fixing foreign elections some eyebrows were also raised when thensenator clinton appeared to make a questionable moral equivalency regarding capturing combatants in war june capture of idf soldier gilad shalit by hamas militants who came across gaza border via an underground tunnel was very much front of mind clinton can be heard tape saying and then when know hamas know sent terrorists know through tunnel into israel that killed and captured know kidnapped young israeli soldier know there s a sense of like oneupsmanship and in these cultures of know well if they captured a soldier ve got to capture a soldier equating hamas to this day remains state department s official list of foreign terrorist organizations with armed forces of a close american ally was not what many expected to hear in jewish press editorial offices were then third avenue and third street in brooklyn paper s office has since moved to boro park section of brooklyn use of phrase these cultures also a bit of a headscratcher according to chomsky clinton was gracious personable and pleasant throughout interview taking about an hour to speak to in addition to himself managing editor jerry greenwald assistant to publisher naomi klass mauer counsel dennis rapps and senior editor jason maoz another part of tape highlights something that was relatively uncontroversial time but has taken new meaning in light of current campaign speaking to leaders with whom country not best terms clinton has presented a very tough front in discussing russia for example accusing trump of unseemly ardor for strongman vladimir putin and mocking his oftstated prediction that as president he d get along with putin chomsky heard tape asking clinton what now seems like a prescient question about syria given disaster unfolding there and its looming threat to drag us iran and russia into confrontation do think it s worth talking to syria both from us point of view and israel s point of view clinton replied know m pretty much of mind that don t see what it hurts to talk to people as long as re not stupid and giving things away mean talked to soviet union for years they invaded hungary they invaded czechoslovakia they persecuted jews they invaded afghanistan they destabilized governments they put missiles miles from shores never stopped talking to them an answer that reflects her mastery of facts but also reflects a willingness to talk to russia that sounds more like trump than clinton shortly after she said but if say they re evil re good and re never dealing with them think give up a lot of tools that need to have in order to defeat them so would like to talk to enemy because want to know more about because if want to defeat ve got to know something more about need different tools to use in campaign against that s take it a final bit of interest to current campaign involves an articulation of phrases that trump has accused clinton of being reluctant to use discussing need for a response to terrorism clinton said think can make case that whether call it islamic terrorism or islamofascism whatever label re going to give to this phenomenon it s a threat it s a global threat to europe to israel to united states therefore need a global response it s a global threat and it needs a global response that can be sort of statement of principle so think sometimes having global vision a help as long as realize that underneath that global vision there s a lot of variety and differentiation that has to go it s not clear what she means by a global vision with variety and differentiation but what s quite clear that thensenator just five years after her state was epicenter of september attacks was comfortable deploying phrase islamic terrorism and even more strident islamofascism least when meeting with editorial board of a jewish newspaper in an interview before observer heard tape chomsky told observer that clinton made some odd and controversial comments tape irony of a decadeold recording emerging to feature a candidate making comments that are suddenly relevant to voters today was not lost chomsky who wrote original story time oddly enough that story headlined hillary clinton israel iraq and terror no longer available jewishpresscom and even a short summary published free republic site offers a broken link that can no longer surface story went to bosses time chomsky told observer jewish press had this mindset that they would not want to say anything offensive about anybody even a direct quote from anyone in a position of influence because they might need them down road bosses didn t think it was newsworthy time was convinced that it was and held onto it all these years disclosure donald trump fatherinlaw of jared kushner publisher of observer media

belgian authorities were searching for clues early friday after police killed two in raids aimed jihadists returning from syria who were planning to launch a belgian charlie hebdo attack officials said police were searching in verviers where raid took place and greater brussels area as part of a weeklong investigation that started well before terrorism spree last week that led to deaths in paris area belgian operations had no apparent link to terrorist acts committed in france and unlike paris terrorists who attacked office of a satirical newspaper and a kosher grocery store suspects in belgium were reportedly aiming hard targets police installations they were verge of committing important terror attacks federal magistrate eric van der sypt said a news conference in brussels across europe anxiety has grown as manhunt continues for potential accomplices of three paris terrorists all of whom were shot dead by french police authorities in belgium signaled they were ready for more trouble by raising national terror alert level from to secondhighest level prime minister charles michel said increase in threat level was a choice for prudence there no concrete or specific knowledge of new elements of threat he said suspects in verviers opened fire police when they closed in them near citys train station magistrate told reporters there was an intense firefight for several minutes video posted online showed a dark view of a building amid blasts gunshots and sirens and a fire with smoke billowing up two terror suspects were killed in shootout and another was arrested no police were wounded or killed in clash occurred height of rush hour in a crowded neighborhood of this former industrial town of about miles southeast of capital brussels belgian news site lavenir as well as le soir and france reported late thursday that government prosecutors office said a dozen operations were launched against suspects across belgium in verviers brussels and halvilvoorde some of those targeted are known to have returned recently from syria belgian news site reported that based phone intercepts in homes and cars of three individuals involved in a shootout in verviers authorities believed three were in process of carrying out imminent attacks inside belgium french daily le soir reporting that investigations were launched against verviers suspects least two weeks ago after they returned from syria where they were thought to be involved in fighting there raids earlier thursday included one an apartment above a bakery in eastern city of verviers authorities said authorities said terror cell had ties to isis and was planning a major attack earlier thursday belgian authorities said they were looking into possible links between a man they arrested in southern city of charleroi for illegal trade in weapons and amedy coulibaly who killed four people in a paris kosher market last week man arrested in belgium claims that he wanted to buy a car from wife of coulibaly van der sypt said this moment this only link between what happened in paris van der sypt said that of course naturally are continuing investigation first man came to police himself claiming there had been contact with coulibalys commonlaw wife regarding car but he was arrested following a search of his premises when indications of illegal weapons trading were found a belgian connection figured in a french criminal investigation into a foiled terrorist plot in coulibaly was one of convicted coconspirators plotters included a brusselsarea contact who was supposed to furnish both weapons and ammunition according to french judicial documents obtained by associated press several other countries are also involved in hunt for possible accomplices to coulibaly and other gunmen in french attacks brothers cherif and said kouachi in spain authorities said coulibaly drove his commonlaw wife from france to madrid dec and was with her until she took a jan flight to istanbul spains national court said in a statement it was investigating what coulibaly did in countrys capital with his wife hayat boumeddiene and a third person who wasnt identified but suspected of helping boumeddiene get from turkey to syria france edge since last weeks attacks began satirical newspaper charlie hebdo paper repeatedly threatened for its caricatures of muslim prophet muhammad buried several of its slain staff members thursday even as it reprinted another weekly issue with muhammad its cover also defense officials said france was under an unprecedented cyber assault with cyberattacks launched after countrys bloodiest terrorist attacks in decades frustrating authorities as they try to thwart repeat violence around security forces are deployed to prevent future attacks calling it an unprecedented surge adm arnaud coustilliere head of cyberdefense for french military said about french websites had faced cyberattacks in recent days some carried out by wellknown islamic hacker groups attacks mostly relatively minor denialofservice attacks hit sites as varied as military regiments to pizza shops but none appeared to have caused serious damage he said military authorities launched roundtheclock surveillance to protect government sites still coming under attack kouachi brothers claimed allegiance to al qaeda in yemen and coulibaly to islamic state group associated press panama city florida cnn donald trump tearing grand old party apart tension that has simmered in republican party for years shutting down government and nearly bringing nation to default escalated into an outright civil war tuesday conflict not only threatens partys ability to make any realistic attempt reclaiming white house next month but also previews conflicts and divides that could consume gop for years to come if trump loses one side trump who spent much of tuesday lashing out social media his gop foes such as speaker paul ryan and sen john mccain and lamenting lack of party unity hes backed by conservative lawmakers including iowa rep steve king and throngs of loyal supporters who attend his rallies including one here in panama city florida tuesday where he renewed his call for a government investigation into his opponent hillary clinton some are even raising potential of denying ryan speakership after election other side ryan who devoting full resources of his stature to maintaining a congressional majority that dominance of capitol hill suddenly threatened and not just in senate where there are many competitive races but also in house where gop majority was considered untouchable until recently infighting sparked by release friday of a video depicting trump describing women in vulgar and sexually aggressive terms isnt likely to ease in days before election day trump made clear tuesday that if he loses in november he wont go down quietly or alone he began day with a series of shots taken over twitter ryan saying its hard to do well when speaker isnt supportive he followed up about an hour later calling ryan a weak and ineffective leader and nearly two hours after that trump posed his most explosive tweet of day shackles have been taken off trump continued his attacks ryan wednesday during a rally in central florida where he said hes a disadvantage when have leadership not putting their weight behind people he also complained about getting no credit from party leaders for his sunday night debate performance wouldnt think paul ryan would call and say good job he said it got just about largest audience for a second night debate in history of country youd think theyd say great going don lets beat this crook no he doesnt trumps turn his own party may seem counterproductive it hardly allows him to improve his chances of catching clinton but it does allow him satisfaction of vengeance against party leaders he believes have never treated him fairly since his stunning outsider campaign captured nomination earlier this year and by blaming republican leaders for their failure to wholeheartedly endorse his campaign trump also opens up possibility of a facesaving excuse if he crashes to defeat in november but cost to republican party of trumps burnitdownaroundhim strategy already high could become more extreme and potentially leave gop badly damaged long after he has left political scene to begin with estrangement between trump and party leaders blowing open a gaping split between partys grass roots and its establishment leaders that republican national committee chairman reince priebus and others worked so hard to bridge over summer it a divide that will be hard to overcome if trump loses election forging unity could be impossible if hordes of trump voters blame party leaders for defeat of a man who electrified grass roots supporters in a way no other republican has managed in decades king iowa republican congressman warned tuesday that a purge of party elites might be necessary saying establishment wing of party could simply be amputated out in this effort thats going right now theyve gone so far out this limb king said laura ingraham radio show meltdown in gop culmination of forces that have been building for years intense antipathy towards congressional leaders over their failure to more forcefully oppose president barack obama gave rise to tea party and sent waves of antiestablishment lawmakers to washington in successive elections trumps adoption of a factuallychallenged style of campaigning would have been impossible without power of conservative media that has been building for decades and now fused with gop presidential ticket through role of stephen bannon head of breitbart news who serves as ceo of trump campaign trumps turn against his own party could also reverberate in downballot races republicans have long known that their hold senate was tenuous whoever ran top of their ticket but trumps slumping poll numbers now threaten to drag down vulnerable incumbents too very least a trump implosion that cuts deeply into ryans majority could complicate speakers already tough task of corralling his volatile majority coalition if an antitrump landslide sweeps away house gop members in more moderate districts it could hand more relative power to ultraconservative freedom caucus and give him same kinds of fits that it imposed john boehner his predecessor dilemma especially difficult for republican senators running for reelection some are rejecting trump because of revulsion his remarks among more moderate voters but same time they risk alienating trump supporters in statess where former reality star racked up high margins in primary race new hampshire sen kelly ayotte fits into this category and her desperate effort to walk fine line between condemning and embracing trump during this election has become a symbol of wider gop conundrum ayotte finally said she could not vote for trump after video emerged friday but another star of gop florida sen marco rubio said tuesday hes not yet ready to back away from trump rubio looks certain to need trump voters to maintain his narrow lead in his reelection race but same time if more explosive video emerges about trump rubio who has presidential ambitions in his future risks being tarnished by association with republican nominee another lawmaker in a tough reelection race who hedging his bets north carolina gop sen richard burr who said that while trumps comments were indefensible he still plans to support him for her part turmoil consuming gop would seem to provide a substantial boost to clintons white house bid an nbc newswall street journal poll released tuesday found democratic nominee enjoying a point lead among likely voters in a fourway race but her aides caution against excessive optimism theres concern inside campaign that an increasingly negative race could only become darker in days ahead could turn off voters and make them less likely to show up polls in that instance a lower turnout could create an advantage for trump this seems to be their strategy disgust everyone with democratic dialogue so that they wont come out to polls john podesta clintons campaign chairman told reporters tuesday think it very unbecoming a presidential candidate clinton said as much herself during an interview with a florida radio station tuesday despite all of terrible things trump has said and done he still trying to win this election she said and cannot be complacent cannot rest

it time to reconsider lifetime appointments to supreme court unexpected death of justice antonin scalia and looming faceoff between white house and senate over his replacement have revived proposals that would limit tenure of us supreme court justices legal scholars from both political parties renewed a call tuesday to reconsider how much time justices spend high court many of them cited with disapproval a bruising and protracted clash building between president obama and gopcontrolled senate over when and how to fill scalias vacancy point of life tenure to keep justices insulated from politics said george washington university law professor orin kerr that didnt quite pan out for years now lawyers have been floating proposals that future highcourt justices spend no more than years a time supreme court bench plan would space out appointments so presidents would make appointments every two years supporters said that would bring regular turnover and fresh thinking to court and align with longer life spans of americans since nations founding they argue it just sounds undemocratic said gabe roth executive director of an advocacy group called fix court about lifetime court appointments theres definitely concern about justices being out of touch there have been a number of cases with modern technology whether it be smartphones or bulk data collection or different types of ways of getting tv over airwaves or over internet thomas merrill a law professor columbia university and a former deputy us solicitor general said hes been a little bit skeptical of idea of term limits for supreme court justices but merrill said his thinking has evolved current cautious endorsement of this based perception that whole issue of appointments to supreme court has become incredibly contentious partisan political almost to point where political system freezes up as were witnessing right now with scalia death merrill said it would be a good thing not to have type of armageddon it looks like were about to have termlimit supporters point to a poll by reutersipsos after highprofile rulings obamas health care legislation and samesex marriage that suggested twothirds of respondents would support a year limit tenure supreme court but actually changing system could be remarkably difficult for a political system thats already near paralysis some lawyers said change could be made by lawmakers but others have concluded it would require amending constitution to do that house and senate would need to vote their support and then threequarters of states would have to approve another path would be for twothirds of states to call for a constitutional convention not likely but possible kerr of george washington university said well see how this year goes it may become unbearable with president and senate duking it out every day

hillary clinton has earned enough delegates to clinch democratic presidential nomination according to an associated press count released monday night an assessment swiftly challenged by primary rival bernie sanders and his campaign ap released its updated tally showing former secretary of state winning enough delegates to become first woman to top a major party s presidential ticket eve of last major day of primary voting ap said clinton reached delegates needed to become presumptive democratic nominee with a weekend victory in puerto rico and late burst of support from superdelegates those are party officials and officeholders many of them eager to wrap up primary free to support whichever candidate they want really need to bring a close to this primary process and get to defeating donald trump said nancy worley a superdelegate who chairs alabamas democratic party and provided one of last endorsements to put clinton over top its time to stand behind presumptive candidate said michael brown one of two superdelegates from district of columbia who came forward in past week to back clinton before citys june primary shouldnt be acting like are undecided when people of america have spoken clinton touted news a long beach calif campaign event saying campaign now brink of a historic unprecedented moment but even she stressed that six states are yet to vote tuesday and urged supporters to cast their ballots for her in those contests six states to vote tuesday include new jersey north dakota new mexico montana california and south dakota campaign manager robby mook said in a statement this an important milestone but there are six states that are voting tuesday with millions of people heading to polls and hillary clinton working to earn every vote look forward to tuesday night when hillary clinton will clinch not only a win in popular vote but also majority of pledged delegates sanders campaign rejected declaration that clinton had clinched party nod citing its longstanding position that superdelegates should not count until they actually vote convention as they are free to switch sides before then there nothing to concede sanders told ktvu late monday a rally in san francisco secretary clinton will not have requisite number of pledged delegates to win democratic nomination she will be dependent superdelegates they vote july th so right now goal right this moment to do everything can to win primary tomorrow sanders spokesman michael briggs accused media of a rush to judgement and ignoring democratic national committee s clear statement that it wrong to count votes of superdelegates before they actually vote convention this summer job from now until convention to convince those superdelegates that bernie by far strongest candidate against donald trump briggs said monday sanders supporters expressed disappointment that calls were made before californias primary and urged senator to continue despite pronouncements were going to keep fighting until last vote counted said kristen elliott a sanders supporter from san francisco who attended rally said another attendee patrick bryant of san francisco its what bookies do they call fights before theyre over house minority leader nancy pelosi endorsed clinton during an interview good morning america before her home state s primary im a voter in california and have voted for hillary clinton for president of united states and proud to endorse her for that position pelosi said though adding its not over until its over clinton has won pledged delegates in primaries and caucuses she also has support of superdelegates according to an associated press count ap surveyed all superdelegates repeatedly in past seven months and only remain publicly uncommitted all superdelegates counted in clintons tally have unequivocally told ap they will back her convention and not change their vote since start of aps survey in late no superdelegates have switched from supporting clinton to backing sanders clinton s presumptive victory monday came nearly eight years to day after she conceded her first white house campaign to barack obama back then she famously noted her inability to shatter that highest hardest glass ceiling campaigning this time as loyal successor to obama clinton fended off a surprisingly strong challenge from vermont sen sanders he mobilized millions with a fervently liberal message and his insurgent candidacy revealed a deep level of national frustration with politicsasusual even among democrats who have controlled white house since clinton outpaced sanders in winning new superdelegate endorsements even after his string of primary and caucus wins in may following results in puerto rico it no longer possible for sanders to reach needed to win nomination based remaining available pledged delegates and uncommitted superdelegates according to ap clinton leads sanders by more than million cast votes by pledged delegates and by superdelegates she also won caucuses and primaries compared to his victories echoing sentiments of california gov jerry brown who overcame a decadeslong rivalry with clinton family to endorse her last week many superdelegates expressed a desire to close ranks around a nominee who could defeat trump in november beyond winning over millions of sanders supporters who vow to remain loyal to selfdescribed democratic socialist clinton faces challenges as she turns toward november including criticism of her decision to use a private email server run from her new york home while serving as secretary of state her deep unpopularity among republicans has pushed many leery of trump to nevertheless embrace his campaign this to about saving country and preventing a third progressive liberal term what a clinton presidency would do house speaker paul ryan told ap last week after he finally endorsed trump weeks after new yorker clinched gop nomination yet clinton showed no signs of limping into general election as she approached milestone leaving sanders behind and focusing lacerating trump she said electing billionaire businessman who has spent months hitting her and her husband with bitingly personal attacks would be a historic mistake he not just unprepared he temperamentally unfit to hold an office that requires knowledge stability and immense responsibility clinton said last week in a speech that was striking in its forcefulness previewing a brutal fivemonth general election campaign to come even without nomination sanders can claim ideological victory his liberal positions pushed issue of income inequality into spotlight and drove clinton to left issues such as trade wall street and campaign finance reform but she prevailed in part by claiming much of coalition that boosted obama she won overwhelming support from women and minorities catapulting her to decisive victories in diverse delegaterich states such as new york and texas when clinton launched her campaign last april she did so largely unopposed having scared off more formidable challengers by locking down much of partys organizational and fundraising infrastructure vice president joe biden seen as her most threatening rival opted not to run in october of four opponents who did take her sanders was only one who emerged to provide a serious challenge he caught fire among young voters and independents his campaign gaining momentum from a narrow loss in iowa in february and a commanding victory in new hampshire his ability to raise vast sums of money online gave him resources to continue into spring but clinton vowed not to repeat failings of her campaign and focused early winning delegates hiring help from obamas old team before launching her campaign they pushed superdelegates into making early commitments and held campaign appearances in areas where they could win most pledged delegates her victory in nevada in late february diminished concerns from allies about her campaign operation decisive wins in southern states super tuesday and a sweep of march contests gave her a significant delegate lead became insurmountable by end of april after big victories in new york and in northeast she now moves to face trump whose ascent to top of republican party few expected brash real estate mogul and reality tv star has long since turned his attention from primary foes to clinton debuting a nickname crooked hillary and arguing she belongs in jail for her email setup after a long primary campaign clinton said this past weekend in california she was ready to accept his challenge were judged by words and deeds not race not ethnicity not religion she said saturday in oxnard so it time to judge donald trump by his words and his deeds and believe that his words and his deeds disqualify him from being president of united states associated press contributed to this report

united states comes up constantly when talk to russians about their countrys place in world but conversations tend to go a lot differently than many americans might expect in us common view that russians feel aggrieved by loss of soviet union and all respect that came with being a global superpower russias acts of aggression in europe in this telling are all about challenging americanled order as a way to prove russias might and importance this aggression wildly popular among russians many americans believe because it makes them feel patriotic and powerful to bully west and particularly us they blame for russias problems russia took off its ideological blinders in but america still seems to have them there certainly truth to this but its just a piece of truth rather when speak to influential people across institutions and political spectrum in russia as amanda taub and did during a recent reporting trip there story hear over and over one of russias fundamental weakness and hear a preoccupation with united states that goes far beyond what even many americans who are famously narcissistic about country would expect in this telling moscow capitulated end of cold war and even tried to make itself a friend to far more powerful united states but an irrationally aggressive america has instead sought repeatedly to weaken control or even destroy russia their country in this view insecure against an overwhelmingly powerful west its actions that see as aggressive are actually defensive and moscow kept safe only by careful vigilance and by nuclear arsenal that hear russians cite over and over this version of history hear in russia from detached foreign policy pragmatists from proputin ideologues and antiputin ideologues even from members of prowestern political opposition who support what they believe be to a western agenda of weakening russia theres a quote that speaks perfectly to this russian worldview and how americans misunderstand it in most recent issue of russia in global affairs a russian foreign policy journal that widely considered to reflect views of russias foreign policy establishment quote from a qa with vladimir lukin a prominent russian diplomat and liberal politician who previously served as ambassador to us interviewer in his book diplomacy henry kissinger writes that integrating russia into international system a key task for united states but as he was saying this americans were actually pushing moscow away with their policy why vladimir lukin it in genes america has a simple ideology that there only one truth in world that truth held by god and god created united states to be an embodiment of that truth so americans strive to bring this truth to rest of world and to make it happy only after that will everything be well this ideology has a strong influence their policy a wise traditionalist and a geopolitical expert kissinger had good reason to call such politicians trotskyites for advocating a world revolution albeit in their own way but always in front and in shining armor this a tempting ideology and has been professed by different countries different times not only united states lukin hardly seen as an antiamerican hardliner in russia rather hes considered to be an objective expert united states and a highly professional diplomat he a founding member of liberal opposition party yabloko that he would get united states so obviously wrong what americans would call defending democracy and human rights he sees as a far more radical and explicitly religious agenda of advocating a world revolution troubling but his view a common one and that tells a great deal interviewers response similarly telling so russia took off its ideological blinders in but america still seems to have them soviet union gone but policy against it not this narrative of an inherently aggressive america one heard over and over in moscow not just from people who support russian president vladimir putin and his aggressive antiamerican policies but even from those who oppose them in this view american politics and policies are bent and in many ways driven by a hatred of russia and desire to destroy or least control it russia has had no choice but to meet american aggression with defensive actions such as putting nuclearcapable missiles in europe or arming eastern ukrainian militias threat of genocidal extermination by americanbacked fascists if only to deter us from further actions that could lead to allout war its not hard to poke holes in this russian worldview as stephen sestanovich a longtime senior state department official who helped engineer clinton administrations russia policies wrote in a recent article for american interest even pragmatic russian case for annexing crimea in march makes little strategic sense yet this was worldview heard even from professionals and politicians in russia who oppose antiwestern policies one foreign policy expert who wished for rapprochement with west sighed to us that it would be impossible because hillary clinton whom he said was widely viewed as irrationally antirussian would soon take office another meeting a political opposition leader remarked offhand that he hoped us would be successful in its efforts to engineer regime change in moscow as sestanovich writes in his essay idea that united states aims a color revolution in moscow single most frequently repeated theme of official russian rhetoric this more than paranoia or government propaganda it accepted worldview that russia under constant threat from a hostile and irrational united states lukin another point in his qa lamented that us had rejected moscows gestures cooperation in s and instead sought to surround russia with a hostile nato alliance thus forcing russia into a defensive crouch and creating todays tensions it was biggest mistake west made he said and gradually led to current situation these fears about america are likely to worsen in russia if hillary clinton becomes president many people told us she seen by many russians especially russian policymakers as unbendingly hostile to moscow and bent putin governments destruction hillary worst option of any president fyodor lukyanov an influential russian foreign policy expert who edits a leading foreign affairs journal and heads a foreign policy think tank told us many people here believe that she and her team will try to come back to line of s to encourage russia into an internal transformation lukyanov added not by force of course but to encourage some kind of social development that will upend current system and will promote a new one watch what most people miss about war in ukraine
