secretary of state john kerry found himself defensive thursday a senate hearing where he was hardpressed to find support for iran nuclear deal from either side of aisle and sharply sparred with republicans who accused him of being fleeced and bamboozled senate foreign relations committee hearing was first controversial deal to lift economic and other sanctions in exchange for concessions of islamic states nuclear program with congress taking up deal and expected to vote it sometime in september hearing underscored deep resistance obama administration faces from both parties from perspective mr secretary im sorry believe youve been fleeced committee chairman bob corker rtenn told kerry claiming agreement paves path for iran to eventually develop a nuclear bomb critics repeatedly suggested obama administrations negotiating team gave in to pressure from iranians key points they question whether sanctions indeed can be reinstated once theyre lifted whether iran might be able to stall international inspectors and whether iran might be closer to a weapon once deal expires sen james risch ridaho said guys have been bamboozled kerry though vigorously defended agreement calling it fantasy plain and simple to think united states failed to hold out for a better deal bargaining table let underscore alternative to deal weve reached isnt what ive seen some ads tv suggesting disingenuously he told senate foreign relations committee it isnt a quote better deal some sort of unicorn arrangement involving irans complete capitulation energy secretary ernest moniz also said deal not built trust some lawmakers tried to play referee when hearing got heated sen barbara boxer dcalif said remarks from corker and risch were disrespectful and insulting if were bamboozled world has been bamboozled thats ridiculous boxer told kerry kerry still had to contend with skeptical democrats notably sen bob menendez dnj who questioned whether language in deal tough enough and like corker said deal aids iran in building an industrialscale nuclear program kerry earlier warned that iran will not come back to negotiating table to pursue a new deal voicing frustration that weve got secretaries of state hearing comes as lawmakers raise new concerns about alleged secret side deals struck with tehran over its nuclear program sen tom cotton rark and rep mike pompeo rkan first brought attention to them tuesday saying they learned from international atomic energy agency iaea that there were two side deals between iran and iaea according to lawmakers one agreement covers inspection of parchin military complex and other concerns potential military aspects of irans nuclear program former they said iran would be able to strike a separate arrangement with iaea concerning inspections parchin house speaker john boehner and senate majority leader mitch mcconnell joined cotton and pompeo in sending a letter to president obama wednesday requesting that agreements be made available to congress so that they can be reviewed request transmit these two side agreements to congress immediately so may perform duty to assess many important questions related to jcpoa joint comprehensive plan of action letter says national security adviser susan rice while defending overall nuclear agreement appeared to acknowledge existence of side deals wednesday she said matter of iran nuclear programs possible military dimensions pmd has long been an issue between iran and iaea she said they negotiated and concluded an agreement to deal with this issue of pmd was one of major sticking points in dealings she added these documents are not public but nonetheless have been briefed those documents know their contents were satisfied with them and will share contents of those briefings in full in a classified session with congress so theres nothing in that regard that know that they wont know pompeo also asked kerry about secret deals in a briefing wednesday and said afterwards that kerry confirmed that there were in fact side deals and himself had not seen agreement was incredibly surprised to learn there were components of deal that congress was not going to be privy to pompeo said adding that he had expected that american negotiators would have demanded to see side deals being cut kerry said after thursdays hearing though there are no side deals letter to obama expressed concern that congress was being kept in dark most troubling iran and iaea reached agreement to resolve issues related to research parchin but congress will not have ability to review this agreement nor will know results of iaeas assessment until december letter says it goes to request access to side deals so that congress can effectively review deal as a whole failure to produce these two side agreements leaves congress blind critical information regarding irans potential path to being a nuclear power and will have detrimental consequences for ability of members to assess jcpoa letter says associated press contributed to this report

hillary clinton told a joke speaking to a roomful of goldman sachs bankers in june clinton said that goldman ceo lloyd blankfein could leave wall street firm that s made him a billionaire to start a soup kitchen this exchange was written down by clinton s aides as they gathered information what parts of her paid wall street speeches could prove damaging should they leak to press her team filed it under heading awkward this awkward quote and hundreds of other previously hidden nuggets about clinton have spilled out into public view recently over last two weeks julian assange s whistleblower platform wikileaks has published about pages of emails illegally stolen from john podesta clinton s campaign chair strangest thing about ensuing uproar that none of podesta emails has so far actually broken any fresh scandals about woman track to be next president instead they ve mostly revealed an underbelly of ugliness to multiple clinton controversies that ve already known about questionable relationship between clinton foundation and its donors clinton s ease with powerful interests wall street her ties to wealthy campaign contributors goldman soup kitchen joke a perfect example if there s one thing to really get mad about here it s something ve known for years that clinton took millions from big wall street banks right before running for president seeing her and wall street titans share a laugh about helping hungry might turn stomach but most important question was it wrong for clinton to take big checks from goldman right before running in no way new clinton s campaign refused to comment individual emails instead blaming russians for hacking emails and providing them to wikileaks this from what ve seen so far real story of wikileaks podesta emails yes they have not found any major bombshells no they re not going to sink clinton s campaign but by filling in storylines that have long dogged her campaign with new and vivid detail are getting clearest picture yet of how sausage or if prefer creamy risotto gets made in clintonworld it can be an ugly sight ve now read hundreds of podesta emails as well as upward of stories from across leftwing mainstream and conservative media outlets about what they entail should stress that what ve found far from all bad dozens of these emails show clinton s team genuinely striving to discover correct position an issue many of them show real determined efforts to find right solution to some public policy crisis in general especially compared to vicious infighting that characterized her presidential run come away from podesta emails thinking that clinton has assembled an admiringly loyal group of aides that believes in candidate and mission of campaign there s some backbiting but could imagine far far worse then there s other stuff emails podesta presumably wish never leaked to help make sense of what ve learned ve broken out interesting new bits into what think can be moreorless characterized as four distinct categories vox reached out to clinton campaign for comment and spokesperson glen caplin replied that they are still not authenticating any individual emails campaign also referred foundationrelated questions to foundation itself and referenced several times that leaks were tied to a russian attempt to influence election as it has to other reporters clinton campaign did not dispute accuracy of any of individual emails should be clear that these podesta email leaks have nothing to do with multiple other clinton email scandals percolating over past few years so they aren t as some news outlets have incorrectly reported related to fbi investigation into clinton s private server or allegations that she went around transparency laws instead since these emails emerge from private account of clinton s campaign chair they tend to tell us far more about candidate clinton than they do about secretary of state clinton there however one exception to that general rule clinton foundation since campaign began clinton foundation has been center of an intense debate most extreme critics like donald trump have alleged that clinton used state department to transactionally reward charity s donors there s no evidence for that meanwhile campaign and foundation have fallen back one consistent defense that there s been no proof of a quid pro quo between donor and foundation clinton foundation really did do inarguably lifesaving work but good government experts have argued that clintons accepted private donations in a way that they should have known would have created dangerous conflicts of interest this more nuanced attack faults clinton foundation for dangerously blurring distinction between private and public podesta leaks back up that story one way it does so by uncovering a private audit conducted by a widelyrespected new york city law firm review concluded that clinton foundation s board had failed to oversee potential conflicts of interest and that some donors expected quid pro quo benefits interviewees reported conflicts of those raising funds or donors some of whom may have an expectation of quid pro quo benefits in return for gift audit found it s not clear they received them but either way audit a striking confirmation that even attorneys hired by clinton recognized danger in relationship between donor and foundation then there s another disclosure emerging from podesta emails that qatari officials sought to present bill clinton with a million gift his birthday during his wife s tenure as secretary of state as new york times noted this revelation suggests that foreign governments were able to gain an audience with bill clinton in exchange for a check times couldn t confirm if million check was ever cashed last revelation in leaks about foundation may also be most unusual chelsea clinton apparently was running around raising alarm bell over possible conflicts of interest suggesting clintons themselves were aware of potential problems politico s kenneth vogel has a detailed blowbyblow of chelsea s concerns over overlapping roles of a consulting firm named teneo nothing here represents a major revelation if weren t bothered by clinton foundation before this probably isn t going to trouble but if were having an audit and chelsea clinton share fears will fuel sense that something suspicious was afoot here over course of election donald trump and bernie sanders leveled a similar critique of clinton that she s too wedded to political establishment for sanders that usually meant that clinton didn t have independence to challenge powerful actors wall street and in washington dc trump has used similar rhetoric going after crooked clinton for her big dollar campaign contributors whatever think of merits of those attacks it s clear that majority of american people think it s correct least in broad strokes seven in voters consider clinton part of establishment just percent trust her to take special interests it s an impression that podesta emails only deepen even if they don t provide groundbreaking new controversies around it again none of this revelatory nobody who has closely followed clinton will be shocked to find her campaign was attuned to wishes of donors her affinity for israel well known critics of her approach to campaign finance are mad about decision to take money from big donors not internal discussion over whether or not to do so but watching how all of this unfolded seeing for yourself how clinton spoke gently to wall street won t make disclosures any easier for her detractors to swallow it s a confirmation of what already know but that doesn t make it any less astonishing least for clinton s critics clinton s close ties to wall street and big donors are certainly part of story revealed by podesta leaks but only one part indeed dozens of interesting tidbits have also emerged that allow us to see inside clinton campaign s infrastructure they have showed that some times clinton campaign openly discussed political implications of her deciding to get behind one policy or another they also show clinton campaign other times responding to more highminded policy concerns in one leak for instance clinton s team discussed length whether they should endorse reinstatement of glasssteagall act would restrict commercial banks ability to engage in some investment activity clinton aide mandy grunwald worries that reversing course and backing law would lead to phoniness charges while not doing so could lead sen elizabeth warren to endorse bernie sanders jake in following exchange jake sullivan a top clinton adviser then there s a lengthy exchange in clintonworld about a carbon tax proposal as vox s brad plumer explains emails show how fears of embracing an unpopular idea dominated internal discussion robby mook a top clinton aide said that embracing carbon tax would prove lethal in general election but other revelations have pointed to how clinton campaign got behind positions it found genuinely worthwhile in one exchange highlighted by washington post clinton team talked about forming signature pillars of a future progressive agenda like a significant middleclass tax cut exchange about carbon tax did involve frank political talk but as plumer also noted podesta elsewhere makes genuine efforts to convince his colleagues about menace posed by climate change and need for genuinely huge solutions to address it there are other examples a debate over cadillac tax taxes most expensive health insurance plans showed twin impulses fighting against each other as vox has written tax widely seen as an essential way to raise revenue for obamacare but it s also hated by unions whose votes and endorsements clinton wanted to cultivate during primary emails reveal clinton s policy advisers arguing for a fix it strategy while political team pushed harder for her to call for a full repeal they ultimately came down fully political side none of clinton s critics will be surprised to find her team debating political ramifications of certain policies it s certainly widely understood that this how almost all politicians make their decisions but a fair appraisal of emails doesnt reduce team clinton to opportunism even behind closed doors they appear motivated by a genuine embrace of progressive beliefs and causes least much of time that but not always lets be honest everyone who has worked in a big enough office has said or written something about a coworker he or she wouldnt say to that coworker s face clintonworld no exception but most offices don t have to deal with essentially all of their internal communications being dumped unceremoniously web team clinton s internal gossip and snipings have been neatly organized into a searchable database that whole world can use these are understandably best catnip for reporters like other revelations they also don t tend to reveal anything genuinely earthshattering but by laying bare bitter grievances generally already knew about these emails are fueling added frustration and old grudges for instance emails include this kind of stuff of course has least to do with public policy or positions of campaign but this category of emails perhaps most interesting to people who work for clinton in a terrific article in politico annie karni and glenn thrush detailed psychological impacts it s having clinton campaign as karni and thrush note this should be a heady time for clintonworld she s cruising to victory in polls donald trump has sunk in polls and clinton has trounced him in three consecutive presidential debates in general critics most upset about podesta emails are ones who have confirmed what clinton s innercircle thought about them ironically that dynamic now appears to apply to clinton s own team as well two clear conclusions jump out when trying to determine what these emails tell us about a future clinton presidency one that clinton appears genuinely responsive to pressure from outside groups her team has clear goals but they re also closely attuned to polls and to winning over organizations union backers environmentalist groups black lives matter activists whose support they think they need in private conversations clinton tells audiences in front of her more or less what they want to hear but while this appreciation for her listeners may reflect political savvy it also suggests a flexibility that may worry those her left what happens if president clinton gets polling suggesting a majority of voters support slashing entitlements what if country clamors for a war in iran what if she can win over republican voters by tacking to center and second more than anything podesta emails show how clinton transactional politician many have long suspected that s a dispiriting conclusion for some who may wish she was a pure progressive but it also helps clarify battle lines for what looks like coming clinton administration persuade her team they need and might have a shot getting them side

corporations over national governments facilitation of fossil fuel extraction and infrastructure in face of dire climate change consequences a financial system sucking wealth away from population and by all accounts loretta lynch president obama s choice to replace eric holder as attorney general was very impressive in first day of hearings in fact she was so impressive that even rightwing bloggers powerline called her ernie banks of nominees judging from their eulogy for real ernie banks very good indeed but then they also said committee was throwing softballs so metaphors got a little confusing it was a very good day for loretta lynch much to their chagrin she was appropriately confident dignified and boring and thus made for a dull affair that was unlikely to derail her nomination second day of hearings in both republicans and democrats committee invited both people to testify was a little bit more lively knew it would be when committee announced their guests democrats offered a group of former colleagues and law professors republicans offered a couple of conservative law professors to denounce president obama s usurpation of democracy and some farrightwing activists to complain about being victimized first person to testify was someone who had never met or had any knowledge of loretta lynch this was former reporter and current rightwing icon sharyl attkisson who told a harrowing story of harassment including her questionable allegation that government bugged her computer obviously shocking likes of chuck grassley and orrin hatch to depth of their souls it s one thing for government to relentlessly pursue reporters like james risen who report serious and important stories they wholeheartedly support justice department in such cases attkisson however resigned from cbs news because she felt entire network was biased in favor of obama administration and refused to allow her to pursue scandals she just knew were there these were scandals like benghazi and fast and furious scandals that have been investigated approximately times under every committee in congress and have turned up zilch somehow they ve managed to morph this professional dispute into a story of attkisson being victimized by authoritarian police state it would have been interesting to hear testimony about government s pursuit of leaks and reporters over past few years really a scandal and should form basis of questioning for new attorney general but since both parties are generally in favor of this practice that wasn t to be instead republicans called one of their celebrity martyrs to testify about how hard it for a conservative to live in this world next activist they called to testify about loretta lynch was a man who admittedly has absolutely no knowledge of her but had something to say about how eric holder was very mean to police officers around country he an africanamerican sheriff from wisconsin named david clarke who up to this point best known for his appearances fox and friends in he says things like naacp nothing more than a propaganda entity for left and that behavior of black people reason they are getting shot so often he suggested that federal government waging a war police and should butt out of local business final activist of day was a citizen who has been working her heart out trying to protect electoral system from scourge of nonexistent voter fraud that would be a woman named catherine englebrecht who runs a farrightwing group called true vote sends out groups of observers to africanamerican and hispanic polling places to make sure they re not stealing votes of decent americans upon eric holder s resignation her group put out a press release stating that holder had carried out a radical racialist assault voting rights she was there to complain about department of justice failing to immediately interview her over alleged irs scandal and to excoriate them over nightmare of citizen targeting she then testified that loretta lynch was wrong in saying that laws had been passed throughout south to prevent some people from voting sen jeff sessions pointed out that an alabama tea party leader had been victimized by irs and nobody irs cares sen orrin hatch expressed his deepest sympathy for what she s been through and assured her that they were going to get to bottom of it her voice cracked with emotion as she said thank sir this was first committee hearing of new senate judiciary committee under gop rule a hearing to determine fitness of a presidential nominee to most powerful law enforcement office in land and they called a group of partisan crackpots and grassroots loons to relitigate moldy pseudoscandals and complain about nonexistent government persecution and it s not as if they couldn t have found some real scandals to talk about obama justice department hardly perfect but they are married to their foxified version of reality and simply can t see forest for trees whole spectacle was a nostalgic trip back in time to days of clinton madness when judiciary committees were able to leverage any public hearing into a scandal fest press seems less inclined to go along these days a fact that sharyl attkisson would surely attribute to its political bias but truth that these scandals are just sad echoes of scandalmongering of s energy gone issues seem pale and lifeless not that there was ever any there there but they are now just going through motions but hope democrats don t get too complacent president obama has never been able to get their scandal mojo rising but there s every reason to suspect that this just an offseason workout for what they see as a return to big leagues if hillary clinton wins presidency can be sure there will be a return to fiery investigations whenever republicans hold either house of congress and if history serves press will likely be eager to join team but this sad little show reveals just how much they are in need of some spring training loretta lynch may be a genial public servant in mold of ernie banks but when it comes to scandal management clinton babe ruth they are going to need to up their game significantly

washington cnn this time eight years ago when she first ran for president hillary clinton was already officially a candidate im in it to win it she said in a youtube video posted january but even though a second hillary clinton for president campaign all but certain she and those close to her are debating when she should jump in race potentially delaying her entry by months there no waiting for republicans who are engaged in a furious behindthescenes scramble for advisers and donors mitt romney republicans nominee in announced friday he would bow out after just three weeks presidential speculation treadmill three republican senators two current governors and one former governor have all made active moves toward campaigns there could be ten or more republican candidates by this summer that might be when hillary clinton gets around to officially moving toward a campaign if she heeds some confidantes who are privately arguing for an announcement in july to coincide with start of third fundraising quarter delaying until summer an idea that said to be gaining momentum against those who want to stick to plan for an april start date possibility of delay very real but still unsettled would say its percent in direction of those arguing for a delay said one democrat who supports a spring debut for clintons presidential campaign another democrat who saw merits in both time lines put odds of a delay percent democrats both sides of debate spoke to cnn condition of anonymity so they could make their case without upsetting clinton or those close to her for talking openly about internal deliberations some clinton loyalists worry that as increasingly crowded republican race heats up attacks her could begin to stick without an apparatus in place to answer them liberal superpac american bridge has been countering republican attacks clintons behalf but many democrats think its no substitute for a campaign messaging operation theyre doing terrific research said one but they dont know what her specific policy agenda going to be she should get in and start putting together a substantive policy agenda so attacks that are going to begin to come from every single republican who jumping in to race can be answered democratic national committee beginning to take a larger role in an effort to protect clinton and party brand but many democrats are concerned even that wont be enough other supporters want clinton to lay low as republican field heats up convinced clinton will avoid some fire if shes undeclared and gop candidates will take aim each other instead never interrupt opponent when its destroying itself that event in iowa nobody hated that more than rnc chairman reince priebus said one democrat referring to recent iowa freedom summit first gop cattle call for prospective candidates of many republican presidential hopefuls though noticeably neither jeb bush mitt romney nor rand paul attended lets get sarah palin out there lets get donald trump out there whole clown car some democrats believe its also in clintons best interest to wait until president obama whose approval ratings have begun to rebound becomes more popular since a campaign by his former secretary of state will undoubtedly be seen as an extension of his presidency its a view shared by many white house who eye entry of clinton into contest as beginning of obamas lame duck phase but if clinton waits she could run risk of looking like shes taking democratic nomination for granted american people dont like to see a candidate assume that something theirs for taking warned one clinton supporter if hillary clinton trying to avoid a coronation it really a terrible way to go about it it sends a message that dont have to campaign in primaries said a democratic operative in iowa who warned it leaves an opening it really does require another candidate to fill that void and so far no one has vice president joe biden former maryland governor martin omalley former virginia senator jim webb and vermont senator bernie sanders have all made trek to iowa in last year but none are being particularly agressive in recruiting staff or taking clinton omalley hired one staff member other day and thats all anyone talking about said iowa operative of unusually quiet political scene in early state its kinda weird in clintons air of inevitability was off putting to many voters clinton and her advisers have been looking to avoid it this time around but without an insurgent obamalike candidate waiting in wings clinton insiders are now pretty much convinced that populist darling and massachusetts sen elizabeth warren wont run despite initial concerns she could mount a serious challenge from left many loyalists argue clinton safe to wait if shes out there working hard making her case speaking to voters thats whats going to matter said a washingtonbased clinton backer who thinks a delayed campaign launch could benefit her it wont benefit her campaign coffers however money will not flow until shes actually running said one democrat who cited powerful donor support for a clinton run but acknowledged people dont give that kind of money speculation numerous clinton loyalists interviewed for this piece admit there are arguments for both timelines but perhaps most important factor in deciding when to jump in race hillary clintons personal inclination to put off campaigning last time she ran for president she entered race in january almost two years before election democratic primary contest turned into a bruising slog that she not eager to repeat cant dance in that spotlight for two years a clinton loyalist said shes not rand paul shes most famous woman earth and every move scrutinized

conclusion of a number of scholars and experts who are urging united states supreme court to consider this confrontation when it hears oral argument april in a potential landmark case involving samesex marriage one side are gay couples who are seeking full benefits of equal treatment and dignity in a society that has long forced them into secondclass status or substantially worse other side are religious conservatives who say they are being coerced to support andor participate in activities that offend their religious beliefs gay rights activists sensing an approaching victory supreme court are growing more aggressive in challenging conservatives many argue that any accommodation of religious beliefs in context of gay rights would amount to a license to discriminate conservatives counter that issue freedom of conscience religious accommodations traditionally have been provided to avoid prospect of coercion against one s faith they say indiana and arkansas were just latest flashpoints in what could become a major turning point for first time in us history a sizeable social and political force seems intent sharply restraining or eliminating religious accommodations according to scholars who study religious freedom now with supreme court poised to take up samesex marriage some advocates are hoping high court will offer muchneeded guidance to a nation torn between conflicting values some states like indiana have tried to address religious accommodation side of equation while others like utah have taken a more comprehensive approach addressing religion accommodations and antidiscrimination laws together stakes involve more than just whether samesex couples will be able to obtain a cake or photographs or flowers for their wedding ultimately stake a quintessential requirement of life in america tolerance each side for other douglas laycock a leading scholar of religious liberty has staked out a middle ground position in looming confrontation between gay rights and religious rights he calls his approach liberty and justice for all samesex civil marriage a great advance for human liberty but failure to attend to religiousliberty implications will create a whole new set of problems for liberties of those religious organizations and believers who cannot conscientiously recognize or facilitate such marriages writes mr laycock a professor university of virginia school of law in a friendofthecourt brief he urging high court to recognize a constitutional right to samesex marriage and he believes states should pass laws barring discrimination based sexual orientation but he also favors robust religious freedom restoration laws state level and he urging justices to make clear in their opinion that religious conservatives retain broad freedom to live their lives in accord with their highest sense of morality and faith gain for human liberty will be greatly undermined if samesex couples now force religious dissenters to courts to protect religious liberty as well as samesex civil marriage he told justices in a speech last fall sen orrin hatch r of utah warned that religious liberty was under attack and losing ground what was once a broad consensus here in united states that religious freedom deserves special protection has recently crumbled he said senator said that gay rights organizations and other advocacy groups were increasingly opposed to religious exemptions that a few years ago would have passed without dissent from perspective it appears that now these groups believe they are winning argument and therefore have no need for religious accommodations he said whereas in past they were willing to respect religious freedom now that they believe they have upper hand they are ready to disregard religious liberty altogether last june supreme court pushed back against this trend in a to decision upholding a religious accommodation for owners of hobby lobby within weeks of that ruling senators voted to overturn decision and eliminate accommodation tally only four votes short of needed to advance bill included every senate democrat and three republicans uproar sparked by indiana s passage of its own religious freedom restoration act rfra in late march illustrates potential high stakes for religious conservatives indiana lawmakers and republican gov mike pence encountered a swift and unrelenting backlash by a wellorganized gay rights campaign supported by major business leaders democratic governors and others threatening to unleash an economic boycott against an entire state those threats prompted republican leaders in indiana and arkansas to quickly retreat and water down their religious freedom laws it also directed a national spotlight intersection of gay rights and remember last fall when pundits and politicians were trying to talk themselves into paul ryan as speaker of house because he would lay down law with hardright wing of republican caucus when he was man who could bring some muchneeded order to ranks who could maybe end this habit of careening from crisis to crisis that congress has fallen into because two parties are unable to agree anything down to whether toilet paper should be rolled over or under in capitol hill bathrooms let alone a budget to fund basic functions of government as kids like to say lol release of president obama s eighth and final budget tuesday has forced into open seething tensions that never really went away after a spending agreement was reached last year in part to ease mr ryan s transition into speaker s suite that deal set spending until end of october of this year levels that president adhered to and senate republicans hope to make stick but a core group of house republicans who gave mr ryan a pass back then now say they want to toss those numbers out like so much flotsam and pass their own budget with far tighter spending restrictions that core group of house republicans house freedom caucus band of or so feral meerkats who did much of heavy lifting in driving john boehner into retirement but they aren t only republicans who look like infants here rep tom price rga and sen mike enzi rwy chairs of house and senate budget committees last week announced they will not even let director of office of management and budget present proposed budget to congress this practice a common courtesy extended to presidential administrations for last years or so no one would expect any congress to rubberstamp a president s budget proposal of course not even if same party controlled both chambers and white house but what s notable here this quote from one member of freedom caucus right except re not passing a republican budget any more than president obama expects to pass a democratic budget president s proposal opening to a negotiation to be hashed out between two parties anyone available who can explain job of legislating to these legislators but like so many other legislative norms that heaveho yet d bet cash money some of these legislators like mulvaney and price are also just astonished that bullying idiot donald trump stomping their party s candidates for republican nomination paul ryan knows how this supposed to work he was after all ranking republican member of house budget committee for four years democrats controlled house from to then when gop regained majority in chamber he chaired committee for another four years until if anyone in house knows how budget sausage gets made knows all little compromises over appropriations levels and priorities that go into lifting weights could ward off dementia and make smarter by vicki batts tags weight lifting dementia brain health naturalnews there are many reasons to partake in strength training weightbaring exercises are known for their health benefits but could lifting weights also boost brain recent research indicates that may just be caseto begin study researchers asked a group of people aged to to engage in a mix of weight lifting and brain training exercises all of people who partook in study had been diagnosed with mild cognitive impairment a precursor to alzheimers disease and an early sign of dementiawhile this particular study did not examine whether benefits of exercise could be extended to general population results were quite impressive published in journal of american geriatrics society study found that weightbaring exercises could indeed provide some brain benefits researchers found a casual relationship between an increase in muscle strength and an increase in brain function that basis team recommended that more people begin a strength training regime so that worlds aging population can hopefully be a little healthier it currently projected that about million people will have dementia by year same team behind this most recent research also published a paper in that revealed that weight training provided cognitive benefits to just about every area of brain something cognitive training failed to dowhile discussing their most recent data one of studys researchers dr yorgi mavros of sydney university commented what found in this followup study that improvement in cognition function was related to their muscle strength gains stronger people became greater benefit for their brainfor strength training study participants were asked to lift weights that were equivalent to about percent of their maximum capacity twice a week for six months similar to way in many athletes train and as participants got stronger amount of weight they lifted went up as well in order to maintain desired percent of their maximum effortbrain scans revealed that certain regions of brain actually increased in size for those who took part in exercise regime dr mavros says that benefits were profound enough to warrant recommending weight training for everyonethe more can get people doing resistance training like weight lifting more likely are to have a healthier ageing population he told independent dr mavros also added that best way to ensure that get most benefit from exercise by maintaining a regular routine exercising frequently and with some intensity key to getting most out of what youre doingthis new research not first to suggest that exercise can provide benefits to brain health body of research linking physical exercise to better cognitive function has only continued to grow over last several years science has indicated that in addition to better mental health exercise can also promote both better memory and concentrationdr james pickett head of research alzheimers society also had a few things to say about this new study he noted new research beginning to unravel how physical exercise may have benefits for brain as people get older this study suggests that people with minor memory and thinking problems known as mild cognitive impairment may benefit from weight training to improve their brain healthpickett also noted that while it not yet clear if exercise can reverse dementia they do know that it one of most important factors in its prevention along with being active he says that not smoking and eating a healthy balanced diet are all essential to reducing risk sources

pope francis making history s first papal address to us congress thursday implored america s leaders to accept those born in other countries as their own children urging lawmakers to set aside political differences and embrace people who travel north in search of a better life pope wrapped traditional catholic teachings into a celebration of american icons including abraham lincoln and martin luther king jr drawing lessons from their work to gently but firmly push congress to move beyond partisan paralysis that has blocked progress immigration reform climate change and other issues each son or daughter of a given country has a mission a personal and social responsibility yearold pontiff said in heavily accented english own responsibility as members of congress to enable this country by legislative activity to grow as a nation president obama watched speech television according to white house press secretary josh earnest pope francis made appropriate observation for united states to live up to high standards that set for earnest said live updates latest pope francis in america capitol hill call to action kicked off a second full day of gleeful crowds and emotional visits to catholic institutions faithful gathered apostolic nunciature or vatican embassy to greet pope when he emerged more lined streets as his motorcade traveled to capitol where thousands waited mall to watch his speech giant screens after his address to congress pope went directly from grandeur of capitol hill to spare st patrick s catholic church in a neighborhood that has flipped over past decade from marginalized to magnet he prayed with people who variously wore suits and torn tshirts and he blessed meals of more than homeless people upon arrival john f kennedy international airport in new york pope traveled by helicopter and motorcade to a very different st patrick s soaring cathedral manhattan s fifth avenue for an evening service there pontiff began his homily by expressing sentiments of closeness with muslims after tragic stampede that killed more than pilgrims near mecca thursday first day of eid aladha one of holiest muslim holidays unite with all prayers to almighty god merciful he said he spoke directly to nuns and women in church saying what would church be without he also referenced clergy sex scandal for second time this visit lamenting shame caused by brothers who harmed and scandalized church in most vulnerable of her members pontiff will carry his message to world leaders friday when he speaks a meeting of united nations general assembly in his speech to congress francis crafted an address saturated in american references with special praise for nation s role as a land has inspired so many people to dream he was pointed times urging abolition of death penalty and end of arms trading and warning of dangers of religious extremism worldwide and he was oblique points never directly mentioning abortion or united states rapid embrace of samesex marriage saying only that family being threatened and that fundamental relationships are being called into question as very basis of marriage and family he saved his most specific prescription for combating climate change a cause he said united states has a special obligation to lead call for a courageous and responsible effort to redirect steps and to avert most serious effects of environmental deterioration caused by human activity pope said am convinced that can make a difference m sure and have no doubt that united states and this congress have an important role to play now time for courageous actions and strategies pope who helped broker a diplomatic opening with cuba offered himself and his example as a pastoral link between opposing points of view it duty to build bridges and to help all men and women in any way possible to do same he said a good political leader one who with interests of all in mind seizes moment in a spirit of openness and pragmatism although members of congress largely avoided ostentatious displays of partisan cheering that have come to characterize president s annual state of union addresses an ideological divide was apparent times in response to francis s passage about climate change democrats mostly stood and cheered while some republicans stayed seated and applauded mildly if all but response to pope s passionate words about embracing immigrants seemed to strike a bipartisan chord sen marco rubio rfla a presidential candidate and son of cuban immigrants wiped away tears as pope called himself son of immigrants pope francis always was interested in politics pope francis tempered his call for action with a statement of support for role that business plays in society calling it a noble vocation directed to producing wealth and improving world creation and distribution of wealth he said a vital element in fight against poverty and climate change those looking for signs of this pope s political direction could find evidence in speech s repeated references to a pantheon of liberal heroes from dorothy day who dedicated her life to a battle against poverty and war to thomas merton whose letters to a white liberal written in urged christians to follow their faith and join fight for civil rights for black americans pope praised king for his focus liberty in plurality and nonexclusion day for social justice and merton for dialogue and openness to god reasons pope s speech could be awkward for some lawmakers francis implored congress to reject a mindset of hostility and embrace immigrants who come to this land to pursue their dream of building a future in freedom pope noting that many in congress are also children of people who made risky journey to america said nation must follow golden rule and treat others with same passion and compassion with want to be treated must not be taken aback by their numbers he said but rather view them as persons seeing their faces and listening to their stories trying to respond as best can to their situation francis s emphasis immigrants a matter of selfinterest for a us church that rapidly becoming majority latino parishes across white northeast are shuttered while many in west and south are bursting with spanishlanguage masses that focus also dovetails with major theme of his american trip so far a series of reminders that his papacy very much about renewing church s focus poor and powerless pope this year opened a bedroom homeless shelter just steps from vatican he had showers set up for homeless people in st peter s square and he invited about homeless people to a private viewing of sistine chapel in new york he will get a direct look innercity catholic education when he visits a school in east harlem and meets with immigrants and refugees in philadelphia where he will end his us visit pope will visit a prison to meet with inmates in washington his visit st patrick s immediately and pointedly followed his hour in majestic house chamber plain sanctuary of yearold downtown church was filled with people who need basic life services but as francis noted in prayer there no first or second class need prayers support pontiff said standing a simple wooden podium before a reverentially silent flock would like to pray together hush broke like uncorking of a bottle yes americans are largely supportive of pope s engagement economic social and environmental issues but american catholics who make up about onefifth of us electorate remain deeply divided over their church s directives poll americans love this pope his church not as much one catholic congressman rep paul a gosar rariz skipped pope s appearance to protest francis s advocacy for strong action against global climate change and what gosar sees as pope s failure to speak out with moral authority against violent islam many lawmakers who were inside chamber thursday emerged with bipartisan agreement that pope s central message was simple just get along capitol lawn many of those who watched pope s congressional address giant video screens came away convinced that congress should but probably won t take his message to heart was not expecting him to address bipartisan divide said emily warn a writer from seattle it s as if he was trying to heal congress in a country where oldline catholic population diminishing because many families are having fewer children though a wave of hispanic immigrants partly making up for that decline in numbers pope spoke to young catholics especially those who are disoriented and aimless trapped in a hopeless maze of violence abuse and despair they will have children he said only if nation provides them with a greater sense of possibilities for future after address francis walked through capitol s second floor to statuary hall and paused statue of junípero serra california missionary whom he had canonized wednesday pontiff then joined vice president biden house speaker john a boehner rohio and other congressional leaders speaker s balcony overlooking west front of capitol greeting an enthusiastic crowd that numbered in thousands he said a few words of thanks in spanish and then to great cheers switched to english thank very much and god bless america sarah pulliam bailey deneen l brown pamela constable jessica contrera ed o keefe michael e ruane and kelsey snell contributed to this report

congress faces a june deadline for laws expiration and pauls speech underscored deep divisions over national security agencys nsas bulk collection of americans phone records was revealed by former contractor edward snowden there comes a time in history of nations when fear and complacency allow power to accumulate and liberty and privacy to suffer kentucky senator said pm edt when he took to senate floor that time now and will not let patriot act most unpatriotic of acts go unchallenged he finished pm having not sat for more than hours house overwhelmingly passed a bill to end bulk collection and senate majority leader mitch mcconnell rkentucky has said senate will act issue before beginning a memorial day recess scheduled for weeks end but mcconnell along with presidential hopefuls sens marco rubio rflorida and lindsey graham rsouth carolina favors extending law final congressional approval of bill before deadline no certainty paul plunged into a lengthy speech declaring patriot act unconstitutional and opposing renewal of program with a hefty binder his desk he spelled out his objections occasionally allowing republican and democratic senators to pose questions and getting support from a handful of house members seated back of chamber dont think were any safer looking every americans records paul said pauls campaign sent out a fundraising appeal while his longstanding opposition to bulk collection a pillar of his campaign stirred social media throughout night several democratic senators and a few republicans gave his voice occasional breaks by speaking several minutes to ostensibly ask him questions paul kept control by yielding for questions without yielding floor and by not sitting surveillance issue has divided republicans and democrats cutting across party lines and pitting civil libertarians concerned about privacy against more hawkish lawmakers fearful about losing tools to combat terrorism as paul made his case a justice department memo circulated capitol hill warning lawmakers that nsa will have to begin winding down its bulk collection of americans phone records by end of week if congress fails to reauthorize patriot act after may national security agency will need to begin taking steps to wind down bulk telephone metadata program in anticipation of a possible sunset in order to ensure that it does not engage in any unauthorized collection or use of metadata department said if congress fails to act several key provisions of law would expire including bulk collection a provision allowing socalled roving wiretaps fbi uses for criminals who frequently switch cellphones and a third that makes it easier to obtain a warrant to target a lone wolf terror suspect who has no provable links to a terrorist organization last week house backed usa freedom act would replace bulk collection with a system to search data held by telephone companies a casebycase basis vote was and house republican and democratic leaders have insisted senate act their bill but mcconnell and several other top republicans prefer to simply reauthorize postsept law mcconnell has agreed to allow a vote house bill but has indicated there may not be enough votes to pass it in senate justice department also said that if congress allows law to expire and then passes legislation to reauthorize it when lawmakers return to washington week of june it would be effective in making authorities operative again but may expose government to some litigation risk in event of legal challenge white house backs house bill and has pressed for senate to approve legislation and send it to president barack obama for his signature house bill result of outrage among republicans and democrats after snowdens revelations about nsa program although paul called his action a filibuster it technically fell short of senate rules since bill senate was considering was trade not patriot act

street journal not only will drones be equipped with tasers but there also talk of them being autonomous meaning that an actual human won t necessarily be needed to fly drone this actually already convergence of expanding robotic artificial intelligence along with political will to continue allowing robots more and more autonomy as they carry out traditional duties of military and police some first televised presidential debate between hillary clinton and donald trump was a bonanza for fact checkers here are some of candidates statements and how they compare with facts claim donald trump repeated his insistence that he was against invasion of iraq claiming clintons assertions to contrary were mainstream media nonsense put out by her reality check verdict trump did not publicly speak out against war before it started september radio host howard stern asked trump if he supported a potential invasion of iraq he replied yeah guess so during debate he tried to explain that away by saying comment had been made lightly he said he had been arguing in private to fox newss sean hannity that war would destabilise middle east but have no evidence to support that so far he did start to express doubts after invasion claim clinton attacked trump over his boasts about his business acumen know donald was very fortunate in his life and thats all to his benefit clinton said he started his business with m borrowed from his father reality check verdict trump says he received a m loan from his real estate mogul father he also got loan guarantees and money from his future inheritance and inherited a share of his fathers property holdings claim trump alleged that clinton called transpacific partnership trade deal gold standard of trade agreements clinton denied this but added did say hoped it would be a good deal reality check verdict give this one to trump clinton did more than express hope that deal would turn out well and in a trip to australia said it would be gold standard this tpp sets gold standard in trade agreements to open free transparent fair trade kind of environment that has rule of law and a level playing field claim clinton said people have looked both of plans have concluded that mine would create million jobs and yours would lose us million jobs reality check verdict clinton has made this claim before it based an optimistic reading of a report by moodys analytics says that most of million new jobs would be created anyway by an expanding economy if all of clintons economic policies became law report says unlikely they would account for million of million jobs same company analysed trumps plans and suggested they would tip us into recession and lead to million job losses something strongly disputed by trump campaign but two reports cover different time frames author mark zandi told cnn money a more accurate comparison to million jobs created under clinton would be jobs lost under trump not million claim trump has frequently tried to blame rise of islamic state militants secretary of state hillary clinton this his latest attempt reality check verdict possibly strangest claim of night clinton years old socalled islamic state can trace its roots back to although it did not start referring to itself as islamic state of iraq and syria isis until read more here claim clinton accused trump of calling calling climate change a hoax invented by chinese he insisted did not say that reality check verdict this claim relates to a tweet trump later said was a joke he said concept of global warming was created by and for chinese in order to make us manufacturing noncompetitive claim clinton said race determines how people are treated in criminal justice system reality check verdict this a tough one to prove as there are no figures percentage of crimes that result in arrest what do know that black people are locked up five times more frequently than white people africanamerican people make up about of united states population white people make up but black people make up of prison population and white people it does not mean that black people who tend to live more in urban heavily policed areas are five times as likely to commit crime however clinton also claimed that african american men are more likely to be killed by guns than other demographics something that largely borne out by statistics claim murders in new york city are up reality check verdict as so often with crime statistics it depends how slice it murder rate in new york city close to record lows but did increase slightly between and according to fbi figures but very latest figures from new york police department show murder rates are down murder rates across us as a whole went up in biggest singleyear percentage jump since with a big spike in a handful of cities including chicago washington dc and baltimore trump also claimed that stop and frisk tactics worked very well in new york and brought crime rates down this hotly disputed by researchers and commentators violent crime rates have continued to decline in new york as part of a long term trend even though number of stop and frisk searches has gone down dramatically in recent years research by jeffrey fagan of columbia university found indiscriminate searches had little effect crime although his research also found crime was reduced when police stopped and frisked civilians after observing someone acting violently selling drugs or casing a joint claim trump says he cant release his tax returns because he in middle of a tax audit he also says publishing them would not reveal much clinton suggests he will never publish them as they might reveal he not as rich as he says he does not pay federal tax and does not give as much to charity as claimed reality check verdict being audited by internal revenue service does not prohibit release of tax returns they would reveal trumps annual income how much he pays in tax and how much he gives to charity trump claims he has given m to charity in past five years but a washington post investigation could not find any cash donated by trumps businesses after trumps actual wealth has been assessed by forbes bn compared with bn he claims interestingly trump has never provided evidence that he actually under audit by tax authorities according to associated press a letter released by his tax lawyers never used word merely describing his tax returns as under continuous review

heres this what it must feel like to be death row to be waiting for moment when iron door clangs open for last time and four burly guards escort arminarm to room where life will be extinguished that same sense of dread hangs over presidential election of no one happy about election and no one anticipates better days ahead america s glory days appear to be in rearview mirror while steady downward slide seems to be gaining pace this year s presidential campaign has brought all anger anxiety and frustration bubbling to surface nerves are raw people are edge and trepidation so thick could cut it with a knife all recent surveys tell same story americans are sick of mudslinging sick of scandals sick of recriminations sick of two party duopoly and sick of two candidates two most distrusted and reviled candidates in country s year history this from new york times an overwhelming majority of voters are disgusted by state of american politics and many harbor doubts that either majorparty nominee can unite country after a historically ugly presidential campaign according to final preelection new york timescbs news poll with more than eight in voters saying campaign has left them repulsed rather than excited rising toxicity threatens ultimate victor mrs clinton democratic candidate and mr trump republican nominee are seen as dishonest and viewed unfavorably by a majority of voters after weeks of mr trump s accusations that election rigged a little more than six in of his supporters say they will accept results as legitimate if he loses more than a quarter of mr trump s supporters say they will probably not accept outcome if mrs clinton declared winner and nearly percent of them say they have little or no confidence that americans votes will be counted properly voters express disgust over us politics in new times cbs poll new york times growing sense of desperation in america today palpable and it goes far beyond this one isolated election cycle steady erosion of confidence in nation s main institutions evident in congress s public approval ratings seem to be stuck in singledigit territory public probably feels equal contempt for loretta lynch justice department loaded with clinton toadies that have done their best to quash any investigation into illicit paytoplay machinations clinton foundation and let s not forget media has lost whatever shred of credibility it managed to salvage after its myriad of warpromoting lies about wmd mobile weapons labs aluminum tubes and assad s imaginary chemical weapons attacks attacks that were invented from whole cloth one of washington s many neocon think tanks where these fake ideas are typically hatched forth estate s latest gambit an idiotic attempt to prove that vladimir putin trying to hack thoroughlycorrupted third world voting system to achieve some nebulous political gain what a joke no hillary putin not gaming system like did in primaries with bernie sanders nor did he put a gun to head and force to delete missing emails from private server that was handiwork ms clinton although have a done a masterful job in deflecting attention from yourself and passing buck for own sleazy criminal activities onto moscow but back to media this from gallup americans trust and confidence in mass media to report news fully accurately and fairly has dropped to its lowest level in gallup polling history with saying they have a great deal or fair amount of trust in media this down eight percentage points from last year gallup began asking this question in and a yearly basis since over history of entire trend americans trust and confidence hit its highest point in in wake of widely lauded examples of investigative journalism regarding vietnam and watergate scandal after staying in low to mids through late s and into early years of new century americans trust in media has fallen slowly and steadily it has consistently been below a majority level since bottom line slide in media trust has been happening for past decade before it was common for a majority of americans to profess least some trust in mass media but since then less than half of americans feel that way now only about a third of us has any trust in fourth estate a stunning development for an institution designed to inform public americans trust in mass media sinks to new low gallup designed to inform public gotta be kidding droopy confidence in media a triumph for ordinary working people who have begun to see through charade of unbiased coverage and realize that corporate owners of press manipulate news to shape perceptions and maintain their stranglehold power that s what s really going and that s why a growing number of people have swarmed to donald trump s campaign they see trump s lack of political correctness as a sign that he not owned by washington oligarchy of racketeers who invent teleprompter candidates like obama and clinton who are never certain what they actually believe until they see it printed in bold letters screen in front of them order it now to large extent trump owes his shocking rise to top of gop ticket to fact that he shoots from hip and that media hates him what was once a liability has become an asset as trust for despised media has plunged to depths never seen before but that doesn t explain what s really driving this election and why are american people so overcome by desperation it s all about economic insecurity it s all about fact that standards of living are slipping that an entire generation bogged down with student debt that all goodpaying jobs have been shipped to other countries that family incomes are shriveling that a good portion of population feel threatened by immigration that health care costs have skyrocketed that retirement plans have been postponed and that great bulk of nation s wealth has been transferred to percent plutocrats and wall street landsharks who dictate policy through their congressional lackeys and their allies federal reserve that s what election really all about people are waking up to fact that american dream dead that us no longer land of opportunity and that lives of their children are going to be worse than their own far worse this why everyone so upset so frustrated so hopeless they are looking for a political ally who will address their needs and instead they get bromides transgender bathrooms or glass ceilings or any of other soothing slogans democrats use to pacify masses and to keep them in flock only now it s not working as well now a sizable portion of blue collar vote has shifted into trump s camp mainly because they see through phony democrat rhetoric and all jobeviscerating free trade deals they ve pushed for years trump has skillfully tapped into collective psyche of millions of working people who feel democratic party tossed them under trackhoe years ago and never looked back and he s right too dems have sold out their supporters and it s only going to get worse under clinton or should say madame tpp here s how nile bowie summed it up in a recent article counterpunch economic disempowerment and political disenfranchisement have accelerated under president obama to detriment of american middle class white bluecollar americans have witnessed offshoring of their jobs and erosion of their status in society and trump has masterfully stroked their resentment and discontent by playing their fears of muslims immigrants and minorities trump s real problem with washington establishment that he isn t part of it his campaign represents an insurgent faction of oligarchical class that aims to displace and replace standing political elites bipartisan opposition to trump grounded in belief that he would be an unreliable proxy and a liability someone too narrow and unpredictable to manage common affairs of ruling class and us deep state moreover us establishment not interested in being led by such a contentious figure who would draw protest and public opposition in a way that more conventional establishment candidates largely do not for example trump s rhetoric immigration seems to engender more public outrage than immigration policy under obama who has deported more people than any other president in history election a political system in crisis nile bowie counterpunch big money guys don t like trump and they make no bones about it but trump isn t going away and neither are his followers a vast number of whom will not respect results of election if hillary wins that s a big problem for elites who like to manage population through popular election sham now all that s risk and it s not like trump hasn t bent over backwards to ingratiate himself with deepstate powerbrokers either he has his first olive branch to elites was selection of mike pence as his running mate pence a diedinthewool establishment republican neocon who can be trusted to pursue same extremist agenda gop has followed since gingrich revolution but there was another big move that trump made that escaped notice of media and really underscores his willingness to play by to rules here s story from zero hedge six months ago steven mnuchin became finance chair for trump campaign having successfully helped to raise s of millions of dollars for campaign former goldman sachs partner and soros fund management employee now positioned for something much larger as donald trump reportedly told his aides today that he wants mnuchin to serve as his treasury secretary ironically trump has often criticized clinton and his former competitor ted cruz for their links to big banks know guys goldman sachs they have total total control over him just like they have total control over hillary clinton trump said in one debate but as noted previously he had no qualms however in hiring one of most prominent goldman alums to raise money for him for trump a selfprofessed antiestablishment candidate who has repeatedly stated he not for sale to special interest groups his sudden call for seemingly most wall street of wallstreeters to become treasury secretary may come as a big surprise to some and will leave many of his supporters demanding an explanation trump wants former goldman partner and soros employee to serve as treasury secretary zero hedge another head of treasury from gsax that figures trump great with rabblerousing take back country tirades and all gibberish about rigged system but he also knows how to cave in when it suits his interests he knows he s not going to be president without wall street s nod so he s enlisted a trusted insider to take care of business treasury it s a signal to bigwigs that they don t have to worry about donald going off reservation wink wink so much for trump s independence eh and what can say about hillary clinton that hasn t been said a million times before clinton who still holds a slim lead in most of polls clearly establishment candidate in a year when hatred for corrupt washington oligarchy has reached levels not seen in last hundred years fact that hillary can run for nation s highest office while being investigated by fbi while being savaged by daily releases of new incriminating emails from wikileaks and while promoting a hawkish neocondriven foreign policy that portends a direct military confrontation with russia speaks to fact that traditional liberal democrats are either still hoodwinked by democratic party s manipulation of identity politics or simply terrified of alternative donald trump and that s why everyone so utterly dejected and depressed about election because instead of voting for a candidate they really want or admire most people are simply voting for candidate that either disgusts or scares hell out of them least what kind of choice that in less than hours most agonizinglywretched campaign of all times will be over ballots will be counted and new president will be named only thing that certain that whoever wins lose mike whitney lives in washington state he a contributor to hopeless barack obama and politics of illusion ak press hopeless also available in a kindle edition he can be reached reprinted from counterpunch by permission of author or representative
