wednesday new york state police superintendent joseph damico said authorities are looking behind every tree under every rock and inside every structure for fugitives richard matt and david sweat pair made a brazen escape over weekend from clinton correctional facility in dannemora search now in its fifth day involves more than law enforcement officers state offering a reward in a news conference outside maximumsecurity prison about miles south of canadian border vermont gov peter shumlin said search area had expanded to his state based information inmates believed new york was going to be hot and vermont cooler in terms of law enforcement vermont state police vessels and additional troopers will conduct patrols lake champlain cuts across states in addition searches will include campsites and public campgrounds have information that would suggest that vermont was discussed as a possible location new york gov andrew cuomo said same news conference if they are headed toward vermont vermont engaged and vermont mobilized and are working hand in glove and will be coordinating several times a day to make sure every lead have every piece of information shared under an agreement with vermont new york state troopers will be allowed across state lines if needed officials said but damico said authorities had no hard information men had left new york state law enforcement personnel were going doortodoor in both homes and seasonal residences and conducting checkpoints in hamlets and towns surrounding dannemora cuomo said inmates may have gotten a head start of several hours before manhunt began these men are nothing to be trifled with cuomo said for first time since escape law enforcement officials acknowledged publicly that a woman who worked with convicts in tailoring shop prison may have played a role in elaborate breakout damico without elaborating said mitchell had befriended men and may have had some sort of role in assisting them she has not been arrested or charged in connection with their escape nor has anyone else source added that mitchell cooperating with police having provided information as needed her cell phone was used to call several people connected to matt according to another source with knowledge of investigation its not clear who made these calls when they were made or if mitchell knew about them mitchell went to hospital this weekend because of panic attacks according to one of sources by then authorities had discovered during a am saturday bed check that matt and sweat had escaped mitchell has worked clinton for seven years as an industrial training supervisor according to jennifer freeman a spokeswoman for new york state comptroller her salary was a year mitchells son tobey mitchell told nbc news his mother was in a hospital saturday evening because she was having severe chest pains and she was concerned about that he added that his mom who works prison with her husband worries a lot about everything but strongly challenged suggestions she had done anything wrong she not kind of person thats going to risk her life or other peoples lives to let these guys escape from prison he said his wife paige mitchell told cnn wednesday that of what being said about her motherinlaw not true they dont have facts to prove this she said this just slander and rumor paige mitchell said she believed matt may have persuaded her motherinlaw to contact people for him who knew about art he was interested in art paige mitchell said of matt her heart was in right place paige mitchell denied that her motherinlaw was to be getaway driver or helped provide power tools used in escape saturdays hospital visit stemmed from fact that joyce mitchell a very nervous person she said rural area rough and can be deadly if escapees did indeed have a designated driver imagine their horror when they popped out of a manhole sometime late friday or early saturday and found no accomplice waiting that must have been just a complete panic their part now what where are going to walk to this small rural area said cnn law enforcement analyst tom fuentes a former fbi assistant director its going to be hard to hide day or night for very long and they wouldnt have been prepared to deal with elements theyd have to find food water and money while also trying to get their hands weapons or a vehicle that would put every family in that rural area in extreme danger fuentes said if theyre feeling like cornered animals out there they are going to do something drastic to try to ensure their physical survival and their continued freedom out of that prison without any help like a getaway driver someone who escapes from clinton can easily get lost said jeff hall who teaches city university of new york and did his dissertation northern new york prisons environment formidable said hall who grew up near dannemora prison where his father worked its rough terrain and if youre not familiar with it it can be deadly warning tell police if spot anything unusual first came in dannemora after midnight friday about five hours before authorities discovered men had escaped another focus about miles southeast in willsboro a town of about people along lake champlain thats where a resident spotted two men overnight monday walking in a torrential rainstorm a rural road willsboro town supervisor shaun gillilland said as witness car approached them they took off both reports could be false leads as often happens in manhunts former us marshal service regional commander lenny depaul said he thinks its important that people be lookout still authorities are clearly focusing rural swath of new york near vermont and canadian province of quebec matt and sweats escape was so extraordinarily complex that experts say two must have had help using power tools they cut through a cell wall that included a steel plate maneuvered across a catwalk shimmied down six stories to a tunnel of pipes followed that tunnel broke through a doublebrick wall cut into a inch steam pipe climbed through steam pipe cut another hole so they could get out of pipe and finally surfaced through a manhole aside from mystery of how they got necessary power tools many wonder how they could have used them without detection hole in cells steel wall suggests they used a cutoff wheel ironworker ernesto ernie peñuelas said but using that tool would have produced a loud sound and detectable odor their time lam also remarkable most escapees in new york are captured within hours according to data compiled by state of inmates who fled between and only one was free for more than two days escaping from detention happens thousands of times each year federal statistics show but most are from minimum security facilities where prisoners just walk away in there were cases of prisoners who escaped or were absent without permission sweat was serving a life sentence without parole for fatally shooting and then running over broome county sheriffs deputy kevin tarsia in matt was convicted for kidnapping a businessman for hours and when he didnt comply with his pleas for money killing him torture probably an understatement lee bates who drove a car carrying one of matts victims told cnns anderson cooper of killing he said matt shoved a knife sharpener in his victims ear broke his neck and then dismembered body despite a yearold protester has been charged with shooting two police officers in ferguson mo last week authorities said sunday county prosecuting attorney robert mcculloch said jeffrey williams was charged with two counts of assault in first degree one count of firing a weapon from a vehicle and three counts of armed criminal action mcculloch said williams admitted firing shots but said he was shooting someone else were not sure buy that part of it mcculloch said adding that handgun used in shooting has been recovered he said williams was involved in demonstration that was wrapping up when incident took place williams he said was being held in lieu of cash bail mcculloch said information provided by public was key to arrest he encouraged anyone who knows anything about shooting to contact police saying investigation was continuing williams has had several minor runins with police and one felony arrest in st louis court records show he spent two days in jail last march for failing to appear for court hearings for traffic infractions including driving without a valid license and operating a motor vehicle without maintaining proper insurance he also spent two days in jail in january for speeding police arrested williams in june for receiving stolen property a felony and credit card fraud he was sentenced in march to two years probation bishop derrick robinson of kingdom destiny fellowship international who has been an organizer of ferguson protests later told cnn he spoke with williams and that williams said he was not involved in protests he said williams told him shooting occurred after he had been robbed by an unknown assailant officers were shot during a protest just after midnight thursday one was shot in face other in shoulder they were released from hospital later thursday ferguson has been scene of sometimes violent protests since shooting death of unarmed black man michael brown by a white police officer in august shooting and subsequent investigation brought national attention and a justice department probe to st louis suburb justice department investigation found systemic racism in police department prompting resignation of city manager a local judge and citys police chief attorney general eric holder issued a statement lauding investigative cooperation between federal authorities and st louis county officials this arrest sends a clear message that acts of violence against law enforcement personnel will never be tolerated holder said in statement in days ahead will continue to partner with authorities in st louis county to secure justice for all those affected by this heinous and cowardly crime and will continue to stand vigilant in support of public safety officers and communities they serve officers wounded thursday were from st louis county and webster groves after shootings highway patrol and county police took control of security duties from ferguson department hours after shooting holder and obama condemned attack theyre criminals they need to be arrested obama said and then what need to do make sure that likeminded goodspirited people both sides lawenforcement who have a terrifically tough job and people who understand they dont want to be stopped and harassed because of their race that were able to work together to try and come up with some good answers

a wave of apprehension and anguish swept republican party thursday with many gop leaders alarmed by donald trump s refusal to accept outcome of election and concluding that it probably too late to salvage his flailing presidential campaign as republican nominee reeled from a turbulent performance in final debate here in las vegas his party s embattled senators and house members scrambled to protect their seats and preserve gop s congressional majorities against what republicans privately acknowledge could be a landslide victory for democratic nominee hillary clinton with less than three weeks until election republican party in a state of historic turmoil encapsulated by trump s extraordinary debate declaration that he would leave nation in suspense about whether he would recognize results from an election he has claimed will be rigged or even stolen immediate responses from gop officials were divergent and vague with no clear strategy how to handle trump s threat candidate was defiant and would not back away from his position telling a roaring crowd thursday in ohio that he would accept results if win and reserving his right to legally challenge results should he fall short for seasoned republicans who have watched trump warily as a generalelection candidate aftermath of wednesday s debate brought a feeling of finality campaign over said steve schmidt a trump critic and former senior strategist george w bush s and john mccain s presidential campaigns calling a refusal to accept election results disqualifying schmidt added question how close will clinton get to electoral votes she ll be north of and she s trending towards and trend line taking place in very red states like georgia texas and arizona third debate trump wont commit to accepting election results if he loses clinton and trump appeared together and traded jabs in delivering mostly lighthearted roasts thursday night alfred e smith memorial foundation dinner a whitetie gala benefiting catholic charities candidates used searing humor to taunt each other reflecting personal animus display in debates trump s routine was times unsettling drawing some rare boos from audience held waldorf astoria hotel in new york dinner a traditional event calendar for presidential nominees meanwhile top democrats fanned out to battleground states thursday to hammer trump for what they described as an unprecedented attack country s political system and to attempt to yoke trump to republican candidates down ballot campaigning in miami president obama said trump s doubts about election outcome are not a joking matter that dangerous president eviscerated republicans who have stood by trump singling out sen marco rubio rfla who called trump a dangerous con artist and condemned his more controversial comments during gop primaries but now plans to vote for him marco just seems to care about hanging to his job obama said calling senator s positioning height of cynicism and in arizona where polls show an unexpectedly tight presidential race first lady michelle obama said trump threatening very idea of america itself by suggesting he would not honor election results do not keep american democracy in suspense obama said in phoenix rubio once a shooin fights antitrump tide in florida sen tim kaine of virginia clinton s vicepresidential running mate held a rally a downtown charlotte brewery where he said trump s claims of a rigged election reminded him of third world politicking he had seen as a young missionary in honduras bigger can win by harder it for him to whine and have anyone believe him kaine said trying to galvanize supporters first day of early voting in north carolina debate stage trump amplified what he had been saying for weeks his rallies that election rigged questioned directly as to whether he would accept results should clinton prevail trump said ll keep in suspense clinton called trump s answer horrifying both in debate and to reporters overnight her flight home to new york trump s advisers and surrogates struggled to explain candidate s position campaign manager kellyanne conway said it was too early to determine whether voting irregularities could make difference between winning and losing she and other trump backers drew a parallel to thenvice president al gore s concession call to thentexas gov george w bush he later withdrew as he awaited a recount in florida m going to keep reminding everybody about election when al gore said he would accept results of election and then did not conway said he retracted his concession reince priebus chairman of republican national committee contended that trump and party would stand by results unless margin small enough to warrant a recount or legal challenges priebus said trump merely preserving flexibility in event of a contested result all he s saying look m not going to forgo right to a recount in a close election priebus said accept results as long as re not talking about a few votes where it actually matters know him know where his head s promise that s all this other trump surrogates took a different interpretation keith kellogg a retired army lieutenant general accused media of splitting hairs and insisted that trump was not threatening democratic norms while former new york mayor rudolph w giuliani argued that any republican would be stupid to accept integrity of results before they are known suppose she wins pennsylvania by votes giuliani said he speculated without evidence that democrats would steal a lot more than votes in philadelphia guarantee of that and ll tell how they will do it they ll bus people in who will vote dead people s names four five six times or have people in philadelphia paid to vote three four and five times democrats expressed dismay that republican nominee and his backers were advancing idea of widespread voter fraud he just trying to find an excuse for fact that he s going to lose and perhaps fact that he s going to lose to first woman president stings a little sharper than it might otherwise said jennifer palmieri clinton campaign s communications director prominent republican senators in tough reelection bids distanced themselves from trump s posture donald trump needs to accept outcome sen kelly ayotte rnh said in a statement mccain ariz who lost to obama eight years ago said in a statement didn t like outcome of election but had a duty to concede a concession isn t just an act of graciousness it an act of respect for will of american people as of thursday afternoon neither house speaker paul d ryan rwis nor senate majority leader mitch mcconnell rky had offered any comment underscoring party s unease with its own nominee and political dangers of tangling with him benjamin l ginsberg a lawyer jones day who has been national counsel for several republican presidential campaigns said trump s stance puts party in quite a difficult position there will be republican candidates who are winning by narrow margins and losing by narrow margins ginsberg said party as a whole has a collective interest in having results upheld republican pollster whit ayres said that las vegas debate trump blew his last chance to turn it around but he said am not convinced that rest of party will have as bad a night election day as donald trump going to have because trump brand so distinct from republican brand escalating republican angst was trump s rally thursday in delaware ohio where he advanced conspiracies swirling around farright websites about clinton he referred to reports that democratic operatives with no direct connection to clinton campaign hired people to violently disrupt trump events this criminal behavior that violates centuries of tradition of peaceful democratic elections a campaign like clinton s that will incite violence truly a campaign that will do anything to win trump said going to call clinton a candidate who truly capable of anything including voter fraud trump also mentioned an email surfaced wikileaks through an illegal hack that us authorities blame russian government in interim democratic national committee chairwoman donna brazile seemed to suggest to clinton team that she had knowledge of a question that would come up in a primary forum earlier this year while brazile has denied that cnn provided any questions in advance trump called her actions cheating highest level even as his party loses faith trump proclaimed that he was poised for victory bottom line re going to win he told boisterous ohio crowd re going to win re going to win so big re going to win so big jenna johnson in delaware ohio david weigel in charlotte krissah thompson in phoenix and juliet eilperin jose a delreal and karoun demirjian in washington contributed to this report

have a political impact writes john allen jr crux a catholic news site for one he writes meeting means that francis has significantly strengthened hand of us bishops and other voices in american debates defending religious freedom meeting also could give more direct encouragement to other us officials who have declined to offer marriage licenses to samesex couples a number of clerks and other government officials have followed davis s lead refusing to abide by supreme court s june ruling declaring samesex marriage a constitutional right problem with that strategy however that davis for one from jail she changed rowan county license form to take name of county off form a change plaintiffs argue violates judge s order kim davis gives religious liberty a bad name and endangers it for in natural medicine all know that it s important to eat vegetables least that s what most of us have heard since were kids what mother s told us as when were young doctors tell us as get older sometimes though it helps to have a more specific reason than high cholesterol or even a motherly because said so especially for people who aren t big fans of eating organic greens according to a study conducted oregon state university s linus pauling micronutrient research institute confirms that sulforaphane a phytochemical found in broccoli and related cruciferous vegetables such as cauliflower and cabbage have a natural ability to target and attack prostate cancer cells without harming neighboring cells unconnected studies suggest it may have similar promise for breast cancer active chemicals found in everyday foods such as broccoli are often much more potent than people would imagine if fact determining how to safely adapt these chemical ingredients for medical use one of biggest hurdles researchers face even edible plants that are considered rich in a given nutritional substance contain relatively low amounts of it by volume vast majority of these compounds may also become toxic to humans if taken in large enough concentrations while a number of previous investigations have proven that sulforaphane able to attack both benign and malignant cancer cells oregon state study one of first to prove that it effective without disrupting otherwise healthy tissue this gives researchers a tremendous tool for developing new lowrisk treatment options and likely to encourage additional research into healing potential of other seemingly mundane edible plants realistically it could be some time before these findings are applied to any sort of drug development or cancer treatment in a traditional hospital setting meanwhile though researchers behind study recommend that all eat more organic cruciferous vegetables besides broccoli a number of readily available cruciferous vegetables contain naturally large amounts of sulforaphane some good examples of foods high in this important phytochemical include mild and spicy radishes turnips watercress cabbage arugula kale chard and most other leafy greens unrelated studies also suggest a variety of other cancerfighting compounds may be present in other herbs and garden vegetables celery and parsley for instance are especially rich in apigenin a substance that has shown remarkable promise for fighting breast cancer trace amounts of apigenin are also found in oranges apples and some tree nuts problem it s very difficult for body to effectively extract it from any of these foods its own references oregon state university study confirms safety cancertargeting ability of nutrient in broccoli news research communications june submit review

by lilian ia alessa culturalsurvivalorg western science and indigenous worldviews are often seen as incompatible with indigenous view usually being far less valued by society large but an inside look indigenous ways of knowing shows that they offer unique and dependable insights in precisely areas where western science often weakest grew up in a family where bible study was mandatory yet despite firm christian branches that shaded home there were traditional roots that anchored daily life grandmother spoke no english and went about her tasks singing she sang to things couldn t see to stones and water she spoke to breezes that came off sea this was not odd to no question of sanity or need of counseling entered mind it was simply mechanics of living of praise to god to creator she wove fibers into amazing patterns placing them in water while singing when she finished singing coarse strands would be soaked and pliable and she would sing again until pattern was done her songs came to realize were timers for different tasks she had no watch knew no math indeed had been denied opportunity to get education that became currency of world in her adulthood and old age instead she had acquired a sophisticated methodology to transform resources that yielded to her hand and her hand only there were no power tools no mechanical devices to ease her work there was only an elegance of skill that no machine could replicate as a child she was magic to and her deathbed shock of her mortality severed faith in these songs turned to precision of western learning so that such a fate would never befall so would know world and in that knowledge somehow control it desire to shun those things that had no firm margins grew as came to learn beauty and remarkable perfection of universe through eyes of those scholars who like elders of youth had discovered these things before as sat in uncomfortable chairs in lecture halls a number in a sea of students and despaired pain of examinations in those same chairs a profound awe of very molecules that composed body and everything surrounding settled when realized that ability to pursue this learning fell squarely ability to navigate a system of hard edges panicked had been raised in a home swirling with soft fluidity of being and now learning rejected these things but numbers sing too their words are clear and distinct and their combinations were refrains of certainty slow draining of deep convictions of upbringing and generations of women who had sustained children with their hands became a steady flow here lay solution could understand all things by measuring them and in knowing those words felt could rewrite song profound awe felt as a student failed when took a job as a faculty researcher a university came to realize that western science hummed words much of time could see it coming there were too many failures too many times when it was apparent that politics egos and cliques were white noise that drowned out song like death of grandmother it was a sound blow western science as a way of knowing has precision and discipline and unlike most other ways of knowing it can be faithfully replicated most of time and understood by practitioners around world regardless of their language but was led to believe that it could explain more than it really could its limitations could be found not only in oversimplification of world but also in murky stupidity of politics greed and hubris and so in s found faith in western science fall away like a rock cast off a mountain for second time in life in rush to compose rather than hear song was missing synergy of wisdoms of two worlds one called traditional and other called western phrases traditional ecological knowledge traditional local knowledge and folk knowledge are often associated with fuzzy knowledge kind that comes from funneling information through a human instrument whereas western science suggests an absolute objectivity immune from human bias in order to discern between two one must understand how different cultures including knowledge seekers of both come to exist survive and thrive in their worlds bottom line that both address knowing world using different yet ultimately similar approaches western science excels unraveling unseen medical technology a testament to this precision while traditional knowledge reveals dynamics of larger systems particularly animals plants and habitats and wisdom of place among them in general western science and traditional knowledge are usually perceived as two separate distinct and somewhat incompatible entities why this in part it simply stubbornness and fear both sides in practice they are very similar and in results they are highly complementary because one works well small scales and other large scales but in their origins they differ western science relatively new and evolved from philosophies of aristotle and bacon that sought to standardize information so that it could be used by groups of people who did not necessarily live in same region people who moved from one region to another relied this information to aid growth of their crops health of their livestock and survival of their young not to mention development of weaponry defenses and trade aristotle stated that humans were separate from rest of natural world this including animals plants and places they lived this was a pivotal time in history medicine was advancing and people were making connections between cleanliness and protecting food sources from competing interests such as rats also spread disease government were providing security for more and more people most of whom had descended from tribes that survived by hunting and gathering and competing for these resources with neighboring tribes through conflict and less often fragile treaties of cooperation with this shift from conflict to more and more centralized organization came more time to observe components of world not directly related to survival while not new speculations a class of observer started documenting way humans behaved with each other and other curious habits of species this class of observer was more often than not composed of members of religious sects such as clergy and likely evolved from strong shamanic heritage of their ancestral traditions as these observations amassed and humans were ideologically cleansed of their socially offensive ties to animal world human nature sought to explain observations tied into this desire was an increasing belief that surrounding world was less and less a living interacting system and more and more a source of resources composed of parts each of could be isolated understood and manipulated usually for benefit of humans this point any oral histories that linked societies to their environments were rapidly being relegated to outlying villages and remnants of nomadic peoples in other words uneducated so observers or scholars had isolated themselves from their environments and were increasingly reliant a hierarchy of workers to support their existence and lifestyles distancing them from lands and waters that sustained them could this be point where western science and traditional knowledge diverged as two distinct socially constructed approaches to knowing that remains to be studied but perhaps one can link this early form of systematic observation and explanation to relatively recent process called scientific method often invoked to settle informationdependent conflicts it opinion that an important distinction must be made between scales of knowledge with respect to scientific method and traditional knowledge tech nologies such as microscopes and antibodies have given us insights into unseen worlds of microscale processes that would otherwise never have acquired as increase level of space for example a cell in body and time increase level of complexity or how many things interact with each other any given time by time arrive ecosystems interactions of organisms and their habits have accumulated an enormous amount of complexity it becomes increasingly difficult to resolve what causing effect as a consequence scientific method and western approach to understanding more tenuous and it this intersection of time and space that traditional knowledge most apparent as another approach by necessity western science must simplify things to develop testable hypotheses about how they work both precise and useful smaller scales in process however it eliminates details many of are considered descriptive and either not important to understanding or too confounding a hallmark of traditional knowledge that details are exquisitely noted and communicated in such a way that user can detect small changes and respond accordingly this approach to traditional knowledge has existed as long as have as a species act of residing surviving and thriving in a place means that resident must know her environment in such a way as to repeatedly have a high likelihood of regularly acquiring necessary resources whether they are physical or not a regular basis consequence of failure not ridicule of one s peers or failure to get a research grant it sickness suffering and death one could say that stakes in traditional knowledge are much higher and hence so precision traditional knowledge requires something that with few exceptions western science has failed to accomplish long periods of observation in same place and transmission of these observations to others in that place so that they can use them practically and often from a young age some western schools of thought romanticize traditional knowledge and perceive that somehow possessing it brings ultimate harmony of user with his world no mistakes will be made because there exists a magical link where all things are known this part of devalidation of traditional knowledge because it fails to acknowledge that it like scientific method a process where information accepted or rejected based receiving knowledge continuously both directly from system and from one s colleagues friends family and mentors usually to benefit community and future generations it should not be surprising that somebody suggests that approach of traditional knowledge not limited to humans have only recently become aware that elephants have very calculated ways of using and moving through their environments they will find their food raise their young interact and bury their dead in ways that are distinct to their clans locations and preferences and they will transmit this information from one generation to next using a complex subsonic language grandmother told similar stories about ravens that were really not that different and that if searched memories really hard could actually see someone knew in those brilliant wise winter eyes lilian ia alessa of salish ancestry she received a doctorate in cell biology from university of british columbia and now works in area of adaptive resource management in alaska using tools from both traditional and western ways of knowing this article adapted from alessa s chapter what truth where science and traditional knowledge converge in alaska native reader edited by maria sháa tláa williams and published in by duke university press for more information go to duke university press website wwwdukeupressedu

president obama and russian president vladimir putin laid out sharply competing visions monday about how to tackle ongoing conflicts in syria and elsewhere in middle east with each blaming other for region s turmoil even as they signaled a willingness to address it together in speeches to un general assembly less than two hours apart each leader said he embraced a foreign policy approach that respects international norms that are essential to global stability later in day two met privately to hash out their differences and to see whether there was room for cooperation closeddoor session lasted more than an hour and a half ending just before obama was scheduled to host a reception for delegates after session putin left for moscow in brief remarks to russian reporters he described relations between two countries as regretfully a rather low level due to us resistance but said that now have an understanding that work needs to be strengthened least bilateral basis are now thinking together creation of appropriate mechanisms a white house official who spoke condition of anonymity to discuss private meeting said that while it gave obama clarity their objectives two sides continued to disagree syrian president bashar alassad s role in conflict and his future russia s objectives are to go after isil and to support government official said using an acronym for islamic state administration officials have expressed concern that new russian deployments in syria would bolster assad s fight against his opponents rather than degrade militants in his speech obama took direct aim russia s military buildup in syria as well as its support for ukrainian separatists saying are told that such retrenchment required to beat back disorder that it s only way to stamp out terrorism or prevent foreign meddling but stand before today believing in core that nations of world cannot return to old ways of conflict and coercion cannot look backward and if cannot work together more effectively will all suffer consequences for obama a week that showed world as he wants it and how it really putin for his part charged that attempts by western nations to impose democracy including in iraq and libya were responsible for upheaval in middle east and north africa while people in those regions clearly wanted and deserved change export of revolutions this time socalled democratic ones he said had resulted in violence and social disaster instead of a triumph for democracy then putin had a question cannot help asking those who have forced this situation do realize now what have done he said in remarks that never mentioned but were clearly directed united states policies based selfconceit and belief in one s exceptionality and impunity have never been abandoned beyond barbs two raised prospect of cooperating more closely fighting islamist terrorists and brokering a political solution in syria where war has raged for years obama and putin who opened their first extended formal meeting in two years with a stiff handshake before cameras remain divided over assad whom obama wants ousted and putin continues to back united states prepared to work with any nation including russia and iran to resolve conflict obama said in his minute speech but must recognize that there cannot be after so much bloodshed so much carnage a return to prewar status quo and so assad and his allies cannot simply pacify broad majority of a population who have been brutalized by chemical weapons and indiscriminate bombing putin by contrast insisted that no one but assad s forces and militias are truly fighting islamic state he said it would be an enormous mistake to refuse to cooperate with syrian government and its armed forces russia has directly challenged us military and diplomatic dominance in region and usled coalition air campaign against islamic state in iraq and syria over past month putin has expanded russia s longrunning provision of weapons to assad with deployments of tanks and aircraft over weekend russia and iraq announced that they would establish a rival antimilitant coalition in baghdad to include iran and syria in his speech before un delegates iranian president hassan rouhani echoed putin s comments saying that while united states was responsible for current tumult in middle east his government was willing to help bring democracy to syria russia maintains that western intervention in syria a violation of international law putin proposed creating a broad international coalition that he compared to antihilter coalition during world war ii russia current chair of un security council has called for a meeting next month to discuss how to better combat extremism moscow has also proposed a meeting among itself united states and governments of iran syria saudi arabia turkey and egypt coordination over syria obama administration has not yet responded to latter proposal and it remains unclear whether saudi arabia most powerful sunni arab state opposed to assad and iran syrian leader s other main backer would agree to cooperate any syrian initiative leading european members of usled coalition has been conducting air attacks against islamic state in iraq and syria over past year have indicated that they believe idea and some cooperation with russia worth exploring french president françois hollande who last week authorized his country s first air attacks in syria said monday that today moderate opposition weak bashar alassad weak and isil strong can t stick to original enemies he said in a morning session with reporters have to gather everybody together if saudi arabia and iran can find agreement future of syria then there can be an answer hollande said in comments last week british prime minister david cameron and german chancellor angela merkel appeared to be softening their position toward assad least temporarily have to speak with many actors this includes assad but others as well merkel said not only with united states of america russia but with important regional partners iran and sunni countries such as saudi arabia european views have been influenced not only by stagnation in syrian conflict and islamic state s expansion in iraq but also by hundreds of thousands of refugees many of them from syria who have been pouring into their countries un secretary general ban kimoon praised those european countries that have provided asylum but urged them to do more after second world war it was europeans seeking world s assistance ban said brazilian president dilma rousseff who was first world leader to address general assembly monday drew applause when she noted that her country has already provided shelter to many syrian refugees brazil loosened restrictions two years ago and has issued more than visas to syrian refugees this point more than any other country in latin america have arms open to welcome refugees she said are a multi ethnic nation obama and putin also offered differing accounts of what had transpired in ukraine where russia has annexed crimea and backed separatists in country s southeast although putin devoted bulk of his speech to syria and terrorism he repeated russia s charge that overthrow of ukraine s government early last year was orchestrated from outside he said russia would adhere to minsk agreements once they gave adequate representation to legitimate demands of eastern ukraine separatists that moscow has backed obama however said west would continue to impose economic sanctions russia unless it reversed course cannot stand by when sovereignty and territorial integrity of a nation flagrantly violated while relations with russia rank highest obama s priority list during this visit his agenda includes other major bilateral meetings and working sessions president met privately monday with indian prime minister narendra modi pressing him to adopt a more stringent target for cutting india s carbon emissions in coming decades and he convened a summit bolstering un peacekeeping forceson tuesday president will sit down with cuban president raúl castro and kazakhstan president nursultan nazarbayev indian officials say they will announce their post climate target later this week while they have indicated that they will embrace an ambitious goal for expanding renewable energy sources they have balked idea of committing to cutting their overall carbon output in near future obama has privately pressed modi to do more as part of runup to un climate talks in december dispatching a senior adviser brian deese to new delhi this month to discuss matter after their meeting monday president said and what indicated to prime minister that really think that india s leadership in this upcoming conference will set tone not just for today but for decades to come modi meanwhile reiterated his country s commitment to install gigawatts of renewable energy by president and share an uncompromising commitment climate change without affecting ability to meet development aspirations of humanity he said

cnn as goes walmart so goes nation everyone from apple ceo tim cook to head of ncaa slammed religious freedom laws being considered in several states this week warning that they would open door to discrimination against gay and lesbian customers but it was opposition from walmart ubiquitous retailer that dots american landscape that perhaps resonated most deeply providing latest evidence of growing support for gay rights in heartland walmarts staunch criticism of a religious freedom law in its home state of arkansas came after company said in february it would boost pay for about workers well above federal minimum wage taken together company emerging as a bellwether for shifting public opinion hotbutton political issues that divide conservatives and liberals and some prominent republicans are urging party to take notice former minnesota gov tim pawlenty who famously called gop to be party of sams club not just country club told cnn that walmarts actions foreshadow where republican party will need to move republican party will have to better stand for ideas helping middle class said pawlenty head of financial services roundtable a washington lobbying group for finance industry partys leaders must be willing to put forward ideas that will help modest income workers such as a reasonable increase in minimum wage and prohibit discrimination in things such as jobs housing public accommodation against gays and lesbians walmart employs more than people in arkansas emerged victorious wednesday hours after companys ceo doug mcmillon called republican gov asa hutchinson to veto bill governor held a news conference and announced he would not sign legislation unless its language was fixed walmarts opposition to religious freedom law once again puts company odds with many in republican party companys political action committee has tended to support it has been a gradual transformation for walmart its easy for someone like a chickfila to take a really polarizing position said dwight hill a partner retail consulting firm mcmillandoolittle but in world of largest retailer in world thats very different hill added samesex marriage while divisive its becoming more common place here within us and businesses by definition have to follow trend of their customer backlash over religious freedom measures in indiana and arkansas this week shining a bright light broader business communitys overwhelming support for workplace policies that promote gay equality after indiana gov mike pence a republican signed his states religious freedom bill into law ceos of companies big and small across country threatened to pull out of hoosier state resistance came from business leaders of all political persuasions including bill oesterle ceo of businessrating website angies list and a onetime campaign manager for former indiana gov mitch daniels oesterle announced that his company would put plans hold to expand its footprint in indianapolis in light of states passage of religious freedom act nascar scheduled to hold a race in indianapolis this summer also spoke out against indiana law what were seeing over past week a tremendous amount of support from business community who are standing up and are sending that equality good for business and discrimination bad for business said jason rahlan spokesman for human rights campaign national republicans are being forced to walk fine line of protecting religious liberties and supporting nondiscrimination by end of week indiana will be in right place bush said a reference to pences promise this week to fix his states law in light of widespread backlash others in gop field are digging in sen ted cruz of texas only officially declared republican presidential candidate said wednesday that he had no interest in secondguessing pence and lashed out business community for opposing law meanwhile former secretary of state hillary clinton who previously served walmarts board of directors called hutchinson to veto arkansas bill saying it would permit unfair discrimination against lgbt community jay chesshir ceo of little rock regional chamber of commerce in arkansas welcomed hutchinsons pledge wednesday to seek changes to his states bill he said businesses are not afraid to wade into a politically controversial debate to ensure inclusive workplace policies when it comes to culture and quality of life businesses are extremely interested in engaging in debate simply because it impacts its more precious resource and thats its people chesshir said therefore when issues arise that have negative or positive impact those things then business community will again speak and speak loudly

by adalia woodbury sun oct th pm friday supreme court decided to weigh in transgender rights in case of gloucester county school board vs gg this gives right wing another chance to take civil rights away from people they don t like plain and simple share twitter print this post friday supreme court decided to weigh in transgender rights in case of gloucester county school board vs gg this gives right wing another chance to take civil rights away from people they don t like plain and simple issue whether a transgender student who identifies as a boy has a right to use bathroom that corresponds with gender he identifies with last summer court granted school board s request to put a lower court ruling in student s favor hold until board filed its petition for review by supreme court justice breyer joined conservative justices in that ruling as a courtesy virginia school board established a policy mirroring bathroom police laws in red states like north carolina that required students to use rest rooms and locker rooms to correspond with gender they were assigned birth in this case district court ruled against gg by relying a regulation allowing schools to provide separate toilet locker room and shower facilities based sex provided those facilities are comparable to those provided to opposite sex in january department of education s office of civil rights issued a letter opining that if schools separate students in restroom and locker rooms based their sex a school must treat transgender students in a manner consistent with their gender identity because of that letter us court of appeals for th circuit reversed lower court ruling in favor of gg it relied a supreme court decision that courts generally should defer to an agency s interpretation of its own regulation in reality right wing hopes to achieve two political objectives with this case first this about denying basic rights and dignity to people who are transgender consistent with their ideological opposition to rights for women poc and members of lgbt community second right wing hoping to weaken federal agencies by attacking their ability to interpret their own regulations

archives michael television if hillary clinton charged with obstruction of justice she could go to prison for years by michael snyder october th in world of politics coverup often worse than original crime it was his role in watergate coverup that took down richard nixon and now hillary clinton s coverup of her email scandal could send her to prison for a very very long time when news broke that fbi has renewed its investigation into hillary clinton s emails it sent shockwaves throughout political world but this time around aren t just talking about an investigation into mishandling of classified documents haven t heard anyone talking about this but if fbi discovers that hillary clinton altered destroyed or concealed any emails that should have been turned over to fbi during original investigation she could be charged with obstruction of justice that would immediately end her political career and if she was found guilty it could send her to prison for rest of her life have not seen a single news report mention phrase obstruction of justice yet but am convinced that there a very good chance that this where this scandal heading following relevant part of federal statute that deals with obstruction of justice whoever knowingly alters destroys mutilates conceals covers up falsified or makes a false entry in any record document or tangible object with intent to impede obstruct or influence investigation or proper administration of any matter within jurisdiction of any department or agency of united states or any case filed under title or in relation to or contemplation of any such matter or case shall be fined under this title imprisoned not more than years or both if hillary clinton sent to prison for years that would essentially be for rest of her life have a feeling that fbi going to find a great deal of evidence of obstruction of justice in huma abedin s emails but unfortunately there not likely to be a resolution to this matter before november th because according to wall street journal there are approximately emails to search through as federal agents prepare to scour roughly emails to see how many relate to a prior probe of hillary clinton s email use surprise disclosure that investigators were pursuing potential new evidence lays bare building tensions inside bureau and justice department over how to investigate democratic presidential nominee metadata found laptop used by former rep anthony weiner and his estranged wife huma abedin a close clinton aide suggests there may be thousands of emails sent to or from private server that mrs clinton used while she was secretary of state according to people familiar with matter it will take weeks a minimum to determine whether those messages are workrelated from time ms abedin served with mrs clinton state department how many are duplicates of emails already reviewed by federal bureau of investigation and whether they include either classified information or important new evidence in clinton email probe of those emails an inside source told fox news that least would be of interest to investigation this point fbi officials have not even begun searching through emails because a search warrant has not been secured yet following comes from cnn government lawyers haven t yet approached abedin s lawyers to seek an agreement to conduct search sources earlier told cnn that those discussions had begun but law enforcement officials now say they have not either way government lawyers plan to seek a search warrant from a judge to conduct search of computer law enforcement officials said but fbi reportedly already searching a laptop that was coowned by anthony weiner and huma abedin and no warrant was necessary for that search because weiner cooperating with fbi many have been wondering why fbi director james comey would choose to make such a bold move just over a week until election day surely he had to know that this would have a dramatic impact election and it unlikely that he would have done so unless someone had already found something really big in addition comey was reportedly eager to find an opportunity to redeem himself in eyes of his peers fbi following an excerpt from a daily mail article that was written by ed klein author of a recently released new york times bestseller about clintons entitled guilty as sin atmosphere fbi has been toxic ever since jim announced last july that he wouldn t recommend an indictment against hillary said source a close friend who has known comey for nearly two decades shares family outings with him and accompanies him to catholic mass every week some people including department heads stopped talking to jim and even ignored his greetings when they passed him in hall said source they felt that he betrayed them and brought disgrace bureau by letting hillary off with a slap wrist according to source comey fretted over problem for months and discussed it great length with his wife patrice he told his wife that he was depressed by stack of resignation letters piling up his desk from disaffected agents letters reminded him every day that morale in fbi had hit rock bottom so what happens next in most likely scenario fbi will not have time to complete investigation and decide whether or not to charge hillary clinton before election this means that would go into november th with this scandal hanging over clinton campaign and that would seem to be very good news for donald trump however it possible that once fbi starts searching through these emails that they could come to conclusion very rapidly that charges against clinton are warranted and if that happens could still see some sort of announcement before election day in unlikely event that does happen could actually see hillary clinton forced out of race before november th once again this appears to be very unlikely this point but it still possible if clinton was forced to step aside democrats would need to come up with a new nominee and that process would take time in an article later today most important news will reveal who believe that nominee would be in such a scenario democrats would desperately need time to get their act together and so could actually see barack obama attempt to delay or suspend election legality of such a move highly questionable but barack obama has not allowed two emails hillary rodham clintons private server that an auditor deemed top secret include a discussion of a news article detailing a us drone operation and a separate conversation that could point back to highly classified material in an improper manner or merely reflect information collected independently us officials who have reviewed correspondence told associated press sourcing of information could have significant political implications as presidential campaign heats up clinton frontrunner for democratic nomination agreed this week to turn over to fbi private server she used as secretary of state and republicans in congress have seized involvement of federal law enforcement as a sign that she was either negligent with nations secrets or worse monday inspector general for spy agencies that make up what known as intelligence community told congress that two of emails in a random sample of emails clinton gave state department for review contained information deemed top secretsensitive compartmented information one of governments highest levels of classification two emails were marked classified after consultations with cia where material originated officials said officials who spoke to ap condition of anonymity work in intelligence and other agencies they wouldnt detail contents of emails because of ongoing questions about classification level clinton did not transmit sensitive information herself they said and nothing in emails she received makes clear reference to communications intercepts confidential intelligence methods or any other form of sensitive sourcing drone exchange officials said begins with a copy of a news article that discusses cia drone program that targets terrorists in pakistan and elsewhere while a secret program it wellknown and often reported copy makes reference to classified information and a clinton adviser follows up by dancing around a top secret in a way that could possibly be inferred as confirmation they said several officials however described this claim as tenuous but a second email reviewed by charles mccullough intelligence community inspector general appears more suspect nothing in message lifted from classified documents officials said though they differed where information in it was sourced some said it improperly points back to highly classified material while others countered that it was a classic case of what government calls parallel reporting different people knowing same thing through different means emails came to light tuesday after sen chuck grassley riowa reported that mccullough found four highly classified emails unusual homebrew server that clinton used while she was secretary of state two were sent back to state department for review but grassley said other two were in fact classified closely guarded top secretsci level in a fourpage fact sheet that accompanied a letter to clinton supporters clinton spokeswoman jennifer palmieri stressed that clinton was permitted to use her own email account as a government employee and that same process concerning classification reviews would still be taking place had she used standard stategov email account used by most department employees state department meanwhile stressed that it wasnt clear if material issue ought to be considered classified all still developments suggested that security of clintons email setup and how she guarded nations secrets will remain relevant campaign topics even if emails highlighted by intelligence community prove innocuous she will still face questions about whether she set up private server with aim of avoiding scrutiny whether emails she deleted because she said they were personal were actually workrelated and whether she appropriately shielded such emails from possible foreign spies and hackers clinton says she exchanged about emails in her four years as secretary of state she turned over all but what she said were personal emails late last year department has been making those public as they are reviewed and scrubbed of any sensitive data state department advised employees not to use personal email accounts for work but it wasnt prohibited but clintons senior advisers state department would have been briefed upon basic protocol for handling classified information and retaining government records in clintons time most officials saved their emails onto a separate file or printed them out when leaving office only recently has department begun automatically archiving records of dozens of senior officials including secretary of state john kerry in emails clintons advisers appear cognizant of secrecy protections in a series of august emails clinton aide huma abedin told clinton that us pointman for afghanistan richard holbrooke and another official wanted to do a secure conversation to discuss afghan elections clinton said she could talk after she received a fax of a classified holbrooke memo also a secure line later abedin wrote he can talk now can send secure fax now and then connect call but other times line was blurred among clintons exchanges now censored as classified by state department was a brief exchange in october with jeffrey feltman then top us diplomat for middle east both clinton and feltmans emails about an egyptian proposal for a reconciliation ceremony with hamas are marked b classified for national security reasons and completely blacked out from email release a longer email same day from clinton to former sen george mitchell then mideast peace envoy also censored mitchell responds tersely and carefully that egyptian document has been received and being translated well review it tonight and tomorrow morning will consult with pals palestinians through consul general and then ill talk with gen s again well keep advised

behavior underground jon rappoport individual power power it stands as essence of what founding documents of american republic are all about once scratch below surface a millimeter or so therefore it stands to reason that colleges and universities would be teaching courses in individual power as soon as write that though all fall down laughing because understand absurdity of such a proposition can quality to combination re way way outside consensus re crazy re committing some kind of treason in order to spot deepest versions of educational brainwashing have to have some standard against can power clean it off hose off dirt polish it look it think about it remember it then ll see some gradea prime mind control everywhere because schools either don t mention it or they discredit it back in days when was writing assignment for newspapers and magazines pitched a story about individual power to an editor wanted to trace its history as an idea over past ten years he looked for a few seconds should teach a course in individual power positive aspects no group stuff just individual he frowned a deep intellectual frown as if d just opened jacket and exposed a few sticks of dynamite strapped individual heroes who have considerable power these entertainment businesses bank billions of dollars because people want to immerse themselves in that universe where individual supreme they want it but one issue guns president barack obama lets public mask slip revealing ire boiling within before cameras moved by massacres of innocents that have punctuated his presidency obama has wept his voice has cracked hes visibly shaken with frustration hes lashed out lawmakers he sees as cowards and even led a congregation in amazing grace tuesday as he faced a room filled with parents and relatives of victims of gun violence he stopped speaking grew silent and wiped away tears that began to fall when he recalled first graders killed in a connecticut elementary school three years ago every time think about those kids it gets mad obama said in east room of white house times president has questioned nation he leads asking why no other advanced country seems so blighted with regular killing sprees and wondering aloud why americans will not choose to stop bloodshed evolving through a cycle of sadness poleaxing grief frustration and outright fury obama has even offered hints of selfrecrimination his own earlier failure to touch perfidious politics of gun control himself but so far all emoting anger and frustration have added up to little despite an expansive flexing of his executive powers obama hampered by a republican congress and wary democrats has failed to significantly tighten gun control laws are not inherently more prone to violence but are only advanced country earth that sees this kind of mass violence erupt with this kind of frequency it doesnt happen in other advanced countries its not even close obama said somehow become numb to it and start to feel that this normal and instead of thinking about how to solve problem this has become one of most polarized partisan debates despite fact that theres a general consensus in america about what needs to be done he added do have to feel a sense of urgency about it in dr kings words need to feel fierce urgency of now because people are dying he will also press for public support issue a live town hall meeting hosted by cnn thursday night making gun violence a priority of his final year in office days before his valedictory state of union address obama insisted tuesday that he merely wants to enact a few commonsense gun safety measures but gun lobby mobilizing and republicans led by frontrunner donald trump insist obama out to make it impossible for people to buy guns from campaign trail to tucson to aurora obamas often demoralizing and politically radioactive experience with politics of gun control started when he first set eyes white house and have confounded him ever since he got off wrong foot with second amendment advocates with an offhand remark during his campaign when he said people in midwest communities hit hard by economic blight cling to guns or religion that seemed to many critics as disdainful of lawful firearms owners themselves a slip his foes have used again and again to warn president coming to get their guns obama also appeared to underestimate potency of gun lobby and national rifle association back then as well what have to do get beyond politics of this issue obama he said a democratic debate in philadelphia but when he took office gun control seemed far from his mind with a financial crisis raging and other priorities like health care reform demanding political capital obama was absent issue while democrats controlled both chambers of congress and to be fair there were few democrats especially those in red states who would have welcomed tough votes gun control a memorial service a saddened obama comforted relatives and cracked hearts when he hoped youngest victim nineyearold christina taylor green was jumping in rain puddles in heaven but he used tragedy not to make kind of outspoken call for gun control that would become familiar in later years but to call americans to cleanse their poisoned politics rather than pointing fingers or assigning blame lets use this occasion to expand moral imaginations to listen to each other more carefully he said in months to come obama would be called upon to react to more shootings including one a sikh temple in wisconsin in six people died and a massacre a movie theater in aurora colorado in were killed but emotional strain was clearly beginning to take a toll as he wondered after aurora how he would feel if his daughters had been caught scene for obama dam broke what he later named as worst day of his presidency in december a few days later a heartsearing memorial service loud sobs rang out in a school auditorium as obama slowly read roll of those killed while crossing a threshold no longer reluctant to start talking politics as he mourned dead as a nation are left with some hard questions can truly say as a nation that were meeting obligations obama said offering to put whatever power his office held to prevent more tragedies and obama kept to his word for several months process did not only leave obama looking like hed let relatives of newtown dead down it appeared to further sour him washington itself a town he no longer saw as moveable by forces of hope and change this was a pretty shameful day for washington obama said in a stunningly frank appearance in white house rose garden after lost senate vote in april it came down to politics worry that that vocal minority of gun owners would come after them in future elections said obama they worried that gun lobby would spend a lot of money and paint them as antisecond amendment obama did enact executive actions that white house says have been successful though officials admit that true reform only possible through congress but gun control once again slipped down presidents agenda after white house apparently concluded politics of issue were impossible it took another year shattered by tragedies in from a bloodbath in a church in charleston south carolina to a massacre a community college in roseburg oregon to draw obama back into fray in one of seminal moments of his presidency obama broke into amazing grace a memorial service for a preacher and eight others killed in south carolina by a gunman who wanted to incite a race war after another weary appearance white house podium after oregon shooting obama made clear his heart was sick whole futile business of going before cameras to bemoan another massacre somehow this has become routine obama told reporters he made no apologies for politicizing issue and warned this not something can do before going extra step of worrying about his nations soul would ask american people to think about how they can get government to change these laws he said but killings went after a radicalized muslim couple staged a mass killing in san bernardino california obama faced a buzzsaw of republican opposition when he spoke of need to stop potential terrorists getting hold of guns gop presidential candidates said he should be focused threat from radical islam not making it more difficult for law abiding americans to defend themselves now obama back to try again but even president admits measures are limited in scope maybe cant save everybody but could save some just as dont prevent all traffic accidents but try to reduce traffic accidents obama said tuesday as ronald reagan once said if mandatory background checks could save more lives it would be well worth making it law of land and with prospects bleak for any significant action in congress obamas latest effort may be more about making peace with relatives of dead and his own conscience as result of a realistic assessment that change possible now

cleveland has reached a settlement with department of justice over a pattern of excessive force and civil rights violations by its police department and it could be announced as soon as tuesday a senior federal law enforcement official said official who wasnt authorized to speak publicly about settlement before formal announcement spoke monday condition of anonymity news of settlement came days after a white police officer was acquitted of manslaughter for firing final rounds of a shot police barrage through windshield of a car carrying two unarmed black suspects in suspects backfiring vehicle had been mistaken for a gunshot leading to a highspeed chase involving police cruisers once suspects were cornered officers fired car case prompted an month department of justice investigation into practices of police in a scathing report released in december department required city to devise a plan to reform police force specifics of settlement were unavailable messages left for a department of justice spokeswoman and cleveland police department seeking comment werent returned department of justices report spared no one in police chain of command worst examples of excessive force involved patrol officers who endangered lives by shooting suspects and cars hit people over head with guns and used stun guns handcuffed suspects supervisors and police higherups received some of reports most searing criticism report said officers were poorly trained and some didnt know how to implement useofforce policies it also said officers were illequipped mobile computers that are supposed to be in patrol cars often dont work and even when they do officers dont have access to essential databases report said police chief calvin williams said in december that while it wasnt easy to have to share federal government findings with his member department he was committed to change people of this city need to know will work to make police department better williams said investigation marked second time in recent years department of justice has taken cleveland police to task over use of force but unlike in when department left it up to local police to clean up their act federal authorities this time have been negotiating a consent decree designed to serve as a blueprint for lasting change among police several other police departments in country now operate under federal consent decrees that involve independent oversight department of justice in last five years has launched broad investigations into practices of more than police forces including in ferguson missouri where a white police officer shot and killed unarmed black teenager michael brown and in baltimore where another black man freddie gray suffered a spinal cord injury in police custody and later died brown and gray cases spawned protests that sometimes turned violent saturdays bench verdict manslaughter charge against cleveland patrolman michael brelo led to a day of mostly peaceful protests but also more than arrests two other highprofile policeinvolved deaths still hang over cleveland a predominantly black and largely poor city a yearold boy holding a pellet gun fatally shot by a rookie patrolman and a mentally ill woman in distress who died after officers took her to ground and handcuffed her

but m pretty sure what he believes as political attacks go this one particularly heavyhanded equivalent of saying as far as know opponent not a swindler and a degenerate a politician who tried this religiously exclusive walker s baptist upbringing he son of a pastor does put a particular emphasis personal acceptance of christ it was another baptist governor jimmy carter who elevated idea of being born again into realm of presidential politics for evangelicals in general there no such thing as a birthright christian faith requires a conscious and highly consequential decision a choice that some do not make but here obama has been as forthright as anyone could be am a christian and am a devout christian he said in a christianity today interview believe in redemptive death and resurrection of jesus christ believe that that faith gives a path to be cleansed of sin and have eternal life but most importantly believe in example that jesus set by feeding hungry and healing sick and always prioritizing least of these over powerful didn t fall out in church as they say but there was a very strong awakening in of importance of these issues in life didn t want to walk alone this course will find whole idea that human beings can make profoundly consequential religious choices to be foreign and they may find proselytization necessary correlate of religious choice to be offensive but here a little patience might be in order in many cases adult converts have come through low points of addiction humiliation or crisis they believe they have found past limits of their own strength something extraordinary and undeserved they can only describe as grace may find such converts to be deluded or annoying but there little doubt that they have had a profound experience appear vanishingly insignificant they can say with gk chesterton and all these things are less than dust to because name lazarus and live in such cases people amazed by grace are wont to talk about it thankfulness but this type of faith by definition unlikely to publicly judge faith of others it founded recognition of personal limits and failure in new testament grace often moves in stealthy and unexpected ways lavished lepers samaritans and tax collectors perhaps modern equivalents would be people with hivaids illegal immigrants and tax collectors in any case christians should not look to
