sen bernie sanders of vermont easily won democratic primary in west virginia tuesday first of a string of potentially strong showings this month that may drag out but not block frontrunner hillary clinton s march toward democratic nomination additionally donald trump won republican primaries in west virginia and nebraska virtually foregone conclusions given that he was only republican remaining in race it a great honor to have won both west virginia and nebraska especially by such massive margins trump said in a statement tuesday night time spent in both states was a wonderful and enlightening experience for heading into tuesday clinton held a formidable lead in delegates and because delegates will be awarded proportionally sanders s west virginia victory was not expected to make much of a dent in that lead however his enduring popularity large rallies and insistence staying in race until democratic convention in july have highlighted some of clinton s weaknesses and prevented her from fully turning her attention to a generalelection contest against trump west virginia a workingclass state and like many other states in this country including oregon working people are hurting sanders said a rally in salem ore tuesday night and what people of west virginia said tonight and believe people of oregon will say next week that need an economy that works for all of us not just percent sanders s advantage over clinton in west virginia was clear in preliminary exit polling according to data published by cnn roughly in democratic voters identified as an independent a group that sanders won by nearly points just over in wanted next president to continue president obama s policies less than half share who said this across previous primaries this year clinton has promised repeatedly to continue to build many of obama s policies and has consistently performed best among voters who support his agenda sanders also benefited from support among democratic primary voters who said they would favor trump over clinton or sanders in a general election roughly in primary voters said they would back trump in general election over clinton and sanders won twothirds of their votes clinton was weighed down by her own troubles three in democratic primary voters said they or a family member were employed in coal industry and sanders won those voters by more than percentage points ahead of primary clinton was forced to reckon with comments she made earlier in campaign about putting coal industry out of business sanders used west virginia victory as a rationale to stay in race until last vote cast less than minutes after polls closed sanders sent out an email to supporters declaring victory and asking for money to help him in next two contests in kentucky and oregon recent polls show sanders likely to perform well in a string of primaries this month in oregon kentucky and washington states with smaller minority populations where clinton may face similar challenges as in west virginia electorate nevertheless clinton may have found a purpose to these contests in addition to trying to improve her performance against sanders to connect with workingclass white voters who may be crucial in a generalelection matchup against trump in runup to west virginia s primary clinton toured state holding small intimate meetings with voters including some detractors who challenged her comments she made about coal miners clinton proposed tax changes that would assist families with cost of child care a contrast with trump s lack of a specific policy agenda among other details clinton said she would limit childcare costs to no more than percent of a family s income it just doesn t make sense clinton said a stop in lexington ky of cost of highquality care for young children and struggles of working parents to pay for it it s most important job that any of us can do and re making it really hard and really expensive bill clinton was expected to tour kentucky thursday clinton camp also sought to hold trump to a tax platform that they called risky reckless and regressive anticipating that trump may attempt to walk back some of those policies including tax cuts for wealthy donald trump has put forward a tax plan that paces him squarely side of super wealthy and corporations expense of middle class and working families said jake sullivan a senior clinton policy adviser in a call with reporters monday trump became presumptive nominee after sen ted cruz tex and ohio gov john kasich dropped out following his victory in indiana primary last week he has maintained a lighter schedule than usual since effectively securing nomination he visited west virginia last week only to ask republican primary voters not to bother voting for him tuesday save vote for general election okay forget this one primary gone trump told a massive crowd in charleston last week he later made a swing through oregon and washington continuing to target upcoming primary states while also like clinton reaching out to white voters who are expected to play a big role in november trump has also stepped up his attacks clinton he has given her nickname crooked hillary and has sharpened his attacks her judgment for instance foreign policy international trade deals and her vote for iraq war he has also characterized her as an enabler of her husband s indiscretions once a clinton stronghold west virginia s political preference has shifted dramatically since she won by a landslide against obama in presidential primary win or lose in remaining contests she likely to maintain a significant lead over sanders in both votes and delegates necessary to clinch democratic nomination sanders in vowing to fight eyeing democratic primary in california where a huge delegate prize potentially awaits winner june sanders campaigned in sacramento to a crowd of thousands monday he rallied in oregon tuesday and he was scheduled to campaign later in week in south dakota also votes in june political establishment getting nervous sanders said monday they should be getting very nervous because real change coming sanders made a pair of trips to west virginia during two weeks leading up primary where he emphasized jobs lost to trade deals and persistent poverty in state during his most recent trip sanders devoted a speech last week to latter subject staged a food bank in mcdowell county where nearly half children live in poverty sanders also touted a billion plan to transition ailing coal workers into new industries in leadup to west virginia primary sanders also aired television ads in state something clinton did not do heading into tuesday s contest clinton held a formidable lead of pledged delegates over sanders according to a tally by associated press once superdelegates are factored in clinton s lead stands delegates clinton won democratic primary in nebraska tuesday but it was an advisory primary that followed caucuses in march sanders won majority of delegates more delegates sanders accumulates between now and democratic convention in july more leverage aides say he will have in shaping party s platform if he not nominee sanders has said he would like to push clinton to adopt his position issues including universal health care and raising minimum wage sanders has continued to insist that he has a narrow path to nomination that involves catching or least coming close to clinton in pledged delegates are allocated based performances in primaries and caucuses sanders needs to win nearly twothirds of remaining pledged delegates to do that gearan reported from louisville and lexington ky scott clement jose a delreal and emily guskin contributed to this report

las vegas democratic presidential candidates are holding their first debate tuesday night in las vegas it marks first time frontrunner hillary clinton and her main challenger so far socialist sen bernie sanders ivt will appear same stage in this campaign along with other three candidates this campaign was expected to be a cakewalk for former first lady but it hasnt turned out that way one big reason seems to be story that just wont away how she used a private email server to conduct government business its fed a perception that she can play by a different set of rules even so clinton still has a decent lead nationally over socialist senator from vermont but sanders coming strong hes beating her comfortably in key state of new hampshire and close in iowa too plus sanders one drawing tens of thousands of people to his events democratic voters seem to be listening intently to his populist messages with threat from sanders a reality clinton now trying to reposition herself a little more leftward just recently she came out against president barack obamas big transpacific partnership free trade agreement thats a reversal because while serving as secretary of state she was for it this new position puts her more in line with liberal base of her party as of today am not in favor of what have learned about it clinton said dont believe its going to meet high bar have set so tonight drama will center how much sen sanders and other democrats confront clinton directly debate stage expect former maryland gov martin omalley to be an instigator hes trying to get traction and has taken some shots clinton over last few months as for clinton herself shes trying to avoid a repeat of when she went in as heavy favorite then ran into a buzz saw called barack obama this time around buzz growing for bernie sanders

settlercolonial regime of united states palestinians are too experiencing a genocide and ethnocide within homelands from settlercolonial state of israel image by carlos latuff water life for all of us palestinian activists join standing rock sioux tribe to protest dapl palestinians join standing rock sioux to protest dakota access pipeline nadya raja tannous perhaps only in north dakota where oil tycoons wine and dine elected officials and where governor jack dalrymple serves as an adviser to trump campaign would state and county governments act as armed enforcement for corporate interests in recent weeks state has militarized reservation with road blocks and licenseplate checks lowflying aircraft and racial profiling of indians local sheriff and pipeline company have both called protest unlawful and gov dalrymple has declared a state of emergency it s a familiar story in indian country this third time that sioux nation s lands and resources have been taken without regard for tribal interests sioux peoples signed treaties in and government broke them before ink was dry when army corps of engineers dammed missouri river in it took riverfront forests fruit orchards and most fertile farmland to create lake oahe now corps taking clean water and sacred places by approving this river crossing dave archambault ii chairman of standing rock sioux tribe opinion piece in ny times bakken formation in northern united states and southern canada listed by us energy companies as one of most promising options for national oil extraction only surpassed in size by oil fields in alaska fields in north dakota have been increasingly targeted for bakken shale oil resources over past years and they are quite familiar with public controversy many of us remember proposal of infamous keystone xl pipeline from was held in starkly low public opinion and struck down twice by obama administration proposed dakota access pipeline dapl not so different from its failed counterpart it mapped out for same length of miles as keystone xl and targeting same bakken shale reserves for carry across upper midwest proposed billion dollar dapl would transport barrels of crude oil per day across four states and cross missouri river itself parent company energy transfer partners selling pipeline as an economic booster job creator and sure investment for future of american people yet who exactly are they referring to and who did they consult in hills outside of bismarck north dakota standing rock sioux reservation sitting along banks of cannonball river a tributary to missouri river pipeline construction sites can now be seen from reservation but many people here saw pipeline coming before it even arrived just as energy transfer partners and transcanada failed to consult native tribes who live along planned pipeline route and whose sacred lands ancestral lands and main water sources will be compromised by construction there has not been a single tribal consultation around proposed dapl april st sacred stone spirit camp was erected bank of cannonball as a residence for water protectors many whom came from within and off reservation to stand against pipeline construction call for water preservation and call for recognition of federal treat ies held with great sioux nation what started out as a few hundred people quickly increased into thousands stemming creation of oceti sakowin and red warrior camps other side of cannonball protectors support and solidarity with standing rock are arriving from all edges of world many of them representing indigenous nations own caravan set out from california nd week of september preceding palestinian youth movementusa caravan that arrived soon after as a contingent of indigenous peoples in diaspora and recent settlers turtle island attest that those standing standing rock are standing for present and future as well must in turn stand for each other against present future and historical supremacies of erasure active legacy of settlercolonialism and viciousness of greed pipeline company seems to remain unconcerned by risk of polluting reservation s main water source highly probable degradation of land and sacred sights and their trespass against a series of federal laws and they are becoming increasingly reactionary to flow of protectors in and out of protector camps and surrounding areas just a few weeks ago september th alarming images and video were released of armed police and militarystyle vehicles cornering protectors holding a prayer ceremony a north dakota construction site video portrayed intensity ground and just how vulnerable protector camps are without gaze of public eye they are moving in they won t let us leave they have locked us in both sides they ve got their weapons drawn they ve got snipers top of hill they re blocking facebook they are arresting everyone now everyone running share this far and wide transcript of livestream video via unicorn riot militarized forces blocked only exit from site to public road before arresting protectors other attendees posted photos of a crop dusting plane releasing a gas or chemical over crowd there has been little clarity thereafter of makeup of compound or purpose of spray participation and planning of direct actions against dapl construction however are continuing with over cars caravanning out to construction sites week of october rd and successfully halting construction for day local authorities private security hires and national guard are seemingly disturbed by presence of protectors as well and are going out of their way to restrict access in and out of protector camp area and intimidate newcomers indeed own caravan coming from california was discouraged from approaching reservation main road running from bismarck nd due to checkpoints erected by north dakota authorities longwinded encounter with highway patrol way to north dakota who insisted not only checking all of ids followed by standing side of highway outside of car for an hour but also passed information down line to authorities higherup including suspicions of illegal activity seemed to be motivated to dissuade an influx of supporters into area stories of license plate checks racial profiling of native and ethnic drivers andor car passengers as well as arrests roadblocks circulated through camps democracy now new york times huffington post and many independent news sources also reported these same tactics why did go in first place because somewhere in awkward power dynamic of being a us citizen a nonnative inhabitant of turtle island and a palestinian in diaspora saw struggle for livelihood and culture struggle against settlercolonialism struggle to protect sacred and maintain own legitimacy and ever ominous force of erasure and historical amnesia what later saw standing rock both embodied this and became bigger than it as a mohawk elder said to without water humans are infertile dust a council fire in oceti sakowin during stay indigenous nations were thanked for their support and representation camps movement leaders sacred stone spirit camp have repeatedly stated that gatherings of different indigenous nations near cannonball nd largest in past years north american continent council fire sits mouth of main entrance of oceti sakowin camp outlined by rows of flags representing many of indigenous nations who have come to stand with standing rock end of one of rows palestinian flag seeing it filled equally with joy and sadness because it confirmed two things that had pondered throughout long drive from california to north dakota first thought that power of collective resistance against greed and settlercolonialism a mighty force that thought was embodied by joy to see a representation of will by presently unseen palestinian siblings who had come to take a stand against destructive powers second thought was embodied by sadness for if struggle for protection of water culture land heritage and livelihood truly mirrored in standing rock and palestine then struggle ahead both vast and uncompromising spoke with many inspiring protectors from maori in new zealand indigenous representatives from ecuador canadian representatives from blackfoot nation who were longtime activists in idle no more mobilizations and dakotalakotanakota from standing rock and neighboring reservations among so many others from a variety of perspectives and personal stories same foundational message was repeated back to this stand isn t just about standing for native rights it about protecting water protecting earth and securing livelihood of next generations water life for all of us and fellow members of palestinian youth movement united states branch had reflected latter thought when authored statement of solidarity with standing rock sioux great sioux nation and other native sisters brothers and siblings in fight against dapl circulated september th segments read condemn all forms of state violence against first nation siblings and denote that undermining of their sovereignty and livelihood a part of continuing dialectic of settlercolonialism transnationally since arrival of settlers turtle island first nations have resisted genocide and displacement from seizure of land to reservations from boarding schools to massacres state has done everything in its power to erase and eradicate first nation peoples yet they are still with us today and they continue to resist protecting their land people and future generations from dapl a testament to their strength and resilience as native communities face an ongoing genocide and continue to resist imperialist settlercolonial regime of united states palestinians are too experiencing a genocide and ethnocide within homelands from settlercolonial state of israel comparisons are uncanny had spent most of hours road to north dakota contemplating connections between obstacles and oppressions facing those in standing rock and obstacles and oppressions facing palestinians under occupation and apartheid however upon arriving standing rock no longer just thought about similarities felt them in bones when protectors standing rock asked about what palestinians experience in own fight against settlercolonialism oppression and greed answered sometimes through language of statistics yet more often told them narratives of genocide exile delegimitzation broken promises and resounding resilience sitting around a fire burning sage and cedar wood darlene meguinis of blackfoot nation in canada reflected beginnings of idle no more movement in she still an active organizer she told everything must start with prayer and ceremony especially organizing she reminded that founders of idle no more elders nina waste jessica gordon sheelah mcleen and sylvia mcadams had rooted movement in ceremony result of doing so meguinis maintained was to center focus of collective actions for change native youth in nodapl youth council standing rock reiterated similar ideas about dapl actions two youth leaders recounted to are striving for results that want to see but are being directed by ancestors are here acting now for children intention and prayer surrounded much of daily camp life and easily dispersed tensions outside even as dapl company and national guard helicopters flew low over camps each morning afternoon and night something that pointedly reminded of life in palestine some mornings along bend of cannonball river delineates oceti sakowinred warrior camp from sacred stone spirit camp native artists reflected beauty around them in paintings and art installations one of organizers was albuquerque artist monty singer whose picture shown below time set out to create art and music to gather around fires and drum circles to participate in prayer and ceremony with each other uplifted vibrant energy of camps and people within them cheered prayed and supported direct actions as best could every day donations from across us and internationally flooded into main entrance in afternoons and community kitchens and donation booths ran to maintain swelling of protector numbers hundreds of people ebbed and flowed into camps every single day sheer power required to uphold movement sobering in light of failed injunction by standing rock sioux tribe against us army corps of engineers lower court level a federal appeals court officially halted construction of pipeline underlining same temporary hold parameters as decree proposed september th by department of justice doj that hold applies solely within miles either side of lake oahe near missouri river other locations planned pipeline route are still open for construction and though direct actions sites of dapl construction have not wavered they are increasingly receiving less and less media attention with increasingly severe charges being applied to protectors for example protectors who strapped themselves to bulldozers an active dapl construction site miles down hwy from reservation during stay oceti sakowin camp were slapped with felony charges for criminal trespassing same charges outlined against amy goodman in her arrest warrant as a result of her coverage of dapl in early september although her charges time constituted a misdemeanor and were thankfully dropped october th after a court hearing some of those arrested were even extradited back to their home states to face their charges from north dakota in addition to preexisting protest charges in other states last night in standing rock spoke with a woman by name of terry a resident of bismarck nd asked her why had met so few nonnatives from local area standing rock her response was direct and had very little to do with sheriff s implemented checkpoints and roadblocks it because of media propaganda for example during dog attacks bismarck news covered a worker s injury site and hospitalization of a guard no one gave popular air time or writing space to cover effects of dog attacks protectors she mentioned that an article in conservative paper town hall soon after attacks read so dogs were unleashed these protestors good she and a few others from bismarck came to camps because they saw past media pressure understand that fight for clean water and recognition of native sovereignty affects everyone in surrounding area she told would become increasingly apparent if oil leakage wells up in bakken region in geneva september th dave archambault ii chairman of standing rock sioux tribe urged un human rights council to stand with tribe in opposing dapl project and advocate for recognition of their sovereign rights including protection of water and sacred places protectors are remaining vigilant and off site many walking to pay respects to graves of dakotalakotanakota ancestors that have been disturbed by construction martina looking horse a longtime writer from cheyenne river reservation has been camping standing rock for over a month she told that she and her family plan to stay until pipeline defeated but stressed that conditions camp are not easy to live under torrential rainstorms swings of hot and cold and impending north dakota winter discourage many from staying longer than a few weeks yet looking horse affirmed her belief that she and many others will carry with or without support of mainstream media hope she reaffirmed that national and international people of conscience will continue to support in all ways that they can hold us government accountable to their promises and not forget that protectors are still there taking a stand day that left pymunited states branch s official caravan came into oceti sakowin bringing supplies people power and small gifts for tribal council as visitors to land they also read statement tribal council fire and met many people as had who stated how glad they were to see palestinians supporting front lines against movement suppression solidarity with palestine for all of us who participated in caravans from pym was overwhelming what was supposed to be a fewday trip was extended into a week inspired by stories people call to moral responsibility to protect each other and water that keeps us alive hope to return back to standing rock and bring supplies for winter friends of sabeel north america also sent forward a statement of solidarity in part remarking know that settler colonialism depends exploitation of land and natural resources to detriment of indigenous communities today see sioux nation and members of other native american tribes who have joined to protect water of missouri river and stop dakota access pipeline taking a stand for all life embodiment of resilience as israeli occupation continues palestinian land stolen ancient olive trees are uprooted and blood shed struggle inspires work and redouble efforts to witness and nonviolently resist stand in full support of indigenous sovereignty and selfdetermination light of hope in standing rock not fizzling out upon returning to bay area came across many art builds and donation efforts and have been seeing many more events publicized by friends and family in new york state virginia north carolina florida and arizona thanks to caleb duarte and wonderful youth from fremont high school in oakland recently arrived unaccompanied youth from chimeltenango guatemala who made this solidarity banner art build in oakland ca recent unaccompanied minors from guatemala write water life in maya photo nadya tannous dignidad rebelde woodblock print oakland art build for standing rock photo nadya tannous remember thinking as left standing rock to return to california peoples suppressed by power and greed have strength when they rise together there a poignant uniting force through something as important as world that sustains us river was quiet when left with lots of green and tall grass its banks river flats lay muddy and fertile slow current reflecting sky day and night water turning pink and orange by sunset a water protector strapped to heavy machinery down hwy shouted out before being removed to jail this pipeline a pipeline to past need to be building sustainable infrastructure for future not destructive unsustainable industries that hurt land that hurt water that hurt people everything wrong about this pipeline re here standing in solidarity with human remains recovered from crash site of egyptair flight showed burn marks and were very tiny suggesting an explosion brought down plane a senior egyptian forensics official told associated press tuesday meanwhile a us official briefed latest intelligence told fox news all signs continue to point to terrorism official who spoke to ap claimed he personally examined remains of some of planes passengers and crew a cairo morgue he said all pieces brought to cairo so far are small and that there isnt even a whole body part like an arm or a head he added that least one part of an arm had signs of burns an indication it might have belonged to a passenger sitting next to explosion us official speaking to fox news said american satellites could have missed a potential explosion over eastern mediterranean contrary to popular belief cannot see every part of earth all time official said most us satellites would be trained to positons land and known areas of interest and not focused over an empty part of sea particularly eastern mediterranean official would not rule out an explosion took place to date no terrorist group has claimed responsibility for egyptair disaster airbus a crashed early thursday morning near end of a fight from paris to cairo an independent cairo newspaper alwatan quoted an unnamed forensics official tuesday as saying plane blew up in midair but that it was unclear whether blast was caused by an explosive device or something else official also said remains retrieved so far are no larger than size of a hand but egypt s head of forensics denied statements tuesday reuters reported citing state news agency mena everything published about this matter completely false and mere assumptions that did not come from forensics authority mena quoted hesham abdelhamid as saying analysts who spoke to fox news also said body parts could have been broken up in a similar way upon impact with water family members of victims arrived tuesday a cairo morgue s forensics department to give dna samples to help identify remains of their kin a security official said official also spoke condition of anonymity because he was not authorized to talk to reporters an international effort to hunt for planes cockpit voice and data recorder resumed tuesday with ships and planes from britain cyprus france greece and united states taking part in search search area roughly halfway between egypts coastal city of alexandria and greek island of crete where water to feet deep head of egypts staterun provider of air navigation services ehab azmy told associated press monday that plane did not swerve or lose altitude before it disappeared off radar challenging an earlier account by greeces defense minister azmy head of national air navigation services company said that in minutes before plane disappeared it was flying its normal altitude of feet according to radar reading that fact degrades what greeks are saying about aircraft suddenly losing altitude before it vanished from radar he added there was no turning to right or left and it was fine when it entered egypts fir flight information region took nearly a minute or two before it disappeared azmy said greeces defense minister panos kammenos had said plane swerved wildly and dropped to feet before it fell off radar greek civil aviation authorities said flight appeared normal until air traffic controllers were to hand it over to their egyptian counterparts pilot did not respond to their calls and then plane vanished from radars it was not immediately possible to explain discrepancy between greek and egyptian accounts of air disaster a report by egyptian ministry of civil aviation records that airbus in crash made an emergency landing in cairo that year shortly after taking off its way to istanbul when one of engines overheated it said that egyptair a gcc took off from cairo airport heading to istanbul and that when it reached an altitude of feet pilot noticed that one engine had overheated a warning message appeared screen reading engine number stall after checking best measures to take pilot headed back to cairo s airport where a maintenance engineer inspected engine disconnected it and sent it to be repaired there were no injuries no fire and no damage to plane report read adding that engine had a technical problem report one of over reports classified by ministry as incidents serious incidents and accidents that took place between and among them involved a airbus planes highest among any other aircraft experts contacted by ap said that while an overheated engine not a common problem it unlikely to cause a crash david learmount a widely respected aviation expert and editor of authoritative flightglobal magazine said engine overheat rare but it happens he said that pilot can shut down engine and aircrafts can operate with a single engine dont think engine overheat alone has ever caused an aircraft to crash an engine fire could cause a crash but has not done so in modern aviation era he added fox news lucas tomlinson and associated press contributed to this report

us counterterrorism officials are reviewing databases of known terror suspects and other materials after deadly paris shooting wednesday morning as obama administration opened door to increasing security in us in response terror attack a satirical french publication known for lampooning islam left least dead and attackers remain large us officials already are in touch with their french counterparts attackers reportedly shouted allahu akbar arabic for god great before escaping two french officials speaking condition of anonymity named suspects to associated press as frenchmen said kouachi and cherif kouachi in their early s as well as yearold hamyd mourad whose nationality wasnt immediately clear one of officials said they were linked to a yemeni terrorist network all three remain large cherif kouachi was convicted in of terrorism charges for helping funnel fighters to iraqs insurgency and sentenced to months in prison fox news told investigators had been reviewing terror databases including for individuals who have traveled to syria they also looked to closedcircuit television and evidence from crime scene in addition to identifying suspects focus will be determining whether this isolated or part of a series of attacks it thought to be an attack carried out by a small cell distinguishes this from recent lonewolf attacks including one in ottawa canada and another more recent attack in sydney australia officials are looking level of premeditation given eyewitness accounts that gunmen asked for individuals by name as they stormed office in addition fox news told assailants showed skill and familiarity with their weapons and their escape also showed premeditation a department of homeland security official told fox news that department closely monitoring situation dhs will not hesitate to adjust security posture as appropriate to protect american people official said urging public to report suspicious activity to law enforcement however office of us embassy in france tweeted that there are no plans to close embassy in paris or other diplomatic facilities in france despite misleading press reports us investigators are expected to get more formally involved but only after a request from french government based conversations with government officials and analysts they are looking three categories of suspects selfradicalized individuals members of al qaeda and its affiliates specifically al qaedas offshoot in north africa known as aqim and those who have traveled to syria and gotten training from al qaeda or islamic state latest edition of inspire magazine from al qaeda affiliate in yemen calls for attacks france as well as america and other allies rep peter king rny told fox news he has confidence in french authorities who are pursuing suspects as well would think that one way or other they will find them he said king also said attack should be a wakeup call for congress not to cut funding for dhs marshy area near us highway a main road through village of about people near wisconsin border and about miles northwest of chicago he was pronounced dead scene lake county undersheriff raymond rose told chicago tribune that gliniewiczs gun was found nearby he added that searchers were were working with limited descriptions of suspects described by police as three males two white and one black who should be treated as armed and dangerous helicopters aided about officers who searched area overnight a sheriffs office spokesman said meanwhile several schools in area announced that they would red state electoral maps utah has gone for democratic presidential candidate exactly once in past years in antigoldwater wave of but this year it possible that utah will not be in republican column with trump and unlike many christian evangelical leaders who have decided to stay hitched to his wagon despite allegations of improper sexual conduct utahans are bolting putting state in play young turks sent michael shure to state to find out what was people s minds and what many of them had to tell him was quite clear they don t think trump a good representative of their christian beliefs none of young utahans shure spoke to said they planned to vote for trump some said they would vote for gary johnson one college student indicated that he would consider a vote for hillary but name that people who dont believe that unauthorized immigrants in us should be given legal status tend to emphasize that it would be unfair to immigrants who are in us legally because they deserve a reward for settling in us right way but what if legal immigration process itself unfair a new study by researchers mit and brown university suggest that might be whats going unintentionally they looked applications for employmentbased green cards among immigrants who were already in us what they found was that in standard approval process latin american immigrants were much less likely than average to get approved and asian immigrants were more likely but when government went through a slower but more complete approval process disparities disappeared for this study researchers emilio j castilla of mit and ben a rissing of brown evaluated phase in an application for an employmentbased green card where us department of labor has to approve or deny immigrants labor certification for a particular job these applications get filed when a company decides to take an immigrant whos almost always already in country a temporary visa like a work visa or student visa and sponsor him or her for a green card would let him or her stay in us permanently and eventually apply for citizenship so this phase in process where temporary immigrants can get approved to become permanent ones technically this supposed to be evaluating immigrants wouldbe employer not immigrant him or herself in order to get immigrant labor certified for a green card or any work visa wouldbe employer has to prove that they tried to find a us citizen to fill job but failed one thing that isnt supposed to be a factor in application immigrants country of origin but even when researchers controlled for as many variables as they could from temporary visa that an immigrant held in us when he filed greencard application to skill level of job they found that approval rates varied widely from one nationality to next percent of asian immigrants were approved for labor certification but only percent of latin american immigrants were regional disparity even showed up in immigrants applying for same type of job immigrants from asia seeking employment as restaurant cooks are percent more likely to be approved than immigrants from latin america all else being equal researchers write its a problem for highskilled workers too asian immigrants werent any more likely than canadians for example to get approved to work as computer software engineers but latin american immigrants were over percent less likely biggest problem with study government agents looking over immigrants applications could see each immigrants educational background but researchers couldnt that could be a huge factor explaining variation maybe latin american immigrants are simply less educationally qualified for positions theyre applying for but study indicates that cant be whole story for example researchers looked immigrants who were already hb highskilled visas percent of whom have a bachelors degree or higher but were applying to upgrade to green cards among that group asian immigrants were still percent more likely than canadians to get approved for green cards and hbholding latin american immigrants were percent less likely theres also previous research showing that government officials profile immigrants based their countries of origin in one study in officials were asked to look over fictional visa applications author said that region of origin was being used strongly as a criterion of a visa applicants desirability most of time dol makes decisions based basic information about immigrant job and employer as well as evidence of employers failed attempt to recruit us citizens percent of those cases get approved and theyre typically approved or denied in under months but in a few cases thanks to a process that partly random and partly not applications are audited and agents take a more indepth look an immigrants background and requirements for position in those cases only percent of applications are approved and it takes about years to come to a decision according to new study audited applications had one big advantage over nonaudited ones disparities in approval rates between immigrants from different regions disappeared that might indicate that whatever happening to favor asian immigrants and disfavor latin american ones in quicker process unintentional since government officials dont appear to think theres good reason to be more suspicious of latin american applicants studys authors suggest an easy fix making it impossible for an official to see an applicants country of origin just like officials arent allowed to see applicants ages or sexes if this really a significant problem however its not something that changing process in future will be able to fix if government really making it harder for latin american immigrants currently in us legally to get green cards through their employers theyre unfairly forcing latin american immigrants to make difficult choice between leaving country theyve been living in for years and staying in us after their visas expire in other words governments approvalrate problem might be driving more legal latin american immigrants to become illegal

for weeks hillary clinton has looked for knockout blow that finally forces bernie sanders out of democratic primary she may have gotten it tonight in kentucky sanders has to start winning every state by a landslide victory to have even a mathematical chance of catching clintons nearly delegate lead kentucky was called for clinton as an apparent winner around pm by nbc and after pm by kentuckys secretary of state who called her unofficial winner sanders has maintained that hell stay in race until end of voting and dont have any new reason to believe hell fly white flag after clintons victory tonight and his hardline response to democratic party over this weekends events in nevada certainly doesnt suggest hes ready to call it quits but sanders needed to win kentucky to maintain an increasingly farfetched path to democratic nomination fact that he lost tonight albeit by what appears to have been a very small margin will only dramatically increase calls for him to exit race loss particularly tough for sanderss campaign given that kentucky was one of more favorable states for him remaining in race given west virginia results think sanders probably favored said kyle kondik of university of virginias center for politics in an interview tuesday morning before voting began sanders has a very good chance in kentucky sanders will probably face steeper odds in upcoming contests in california and new jersey where have polls have clinton leading by as much as double digits polling from kentucky was scarce but states largely white and rural voters were widely expected to break for sanders as they have throughout country kentucky has a large number of registered democrats who are much more conservative than rest of party a group that has in other states backed sanders in part as a protest vote against partys establishment according to kondik more than half of kentucky s registered voters are signed up with democratic party even though state s election results have hewed decidedly republican in recent years wrote polling firm morning consult in a preview of tonights contest that s an indication of rightward shift of downscale whites especially in once unionheavy coal country those are voters who used to call themselves yellow dog democrats these voters broke for sanders by a big margin in west virginia and they very well may have in kentucky as well but even if they did it does not appear to have been enough to offset big difference between kentucky and west virginia diversity africanamerican voters are far more numerous in kentucky in part because of its cities like louisville and lexington kentuckys primary electorate was percent black compared to just percent in west virginia black voters have rescued clintons campaign since her first big win in south carolina in february and tonight they may have helped push her opponent out of race correction a previous version of this story incorrectly said kyle kondik works for center for responsive politics rather than university of virginias center for politics

timothy stanley a historian and columnist for britains daily telegraph he author of new book citizen hollywood how collaboration between la and dc revolutionized american politics opinions expressed in this commentary are solely those of author cnn obama has called islamic state face of evil but hes now under pressure from those who say hes not doing enough to beat it some insist that an attack france was an attack nato and that its time to go to war but only a fool would confuse caution for weakness contrary to defeat enemy have to fully understand who enemy what they want and what kind of conflict were involved in here there are good reasons to proceed cautiously to clear something up are effectively war with isis right now a usled coalition has been bombing targets in syria and iraq for over a year and in recent months russia has been doing same how well its worked disputed obama has rhetorically shifted his objectives from crushing isis to containing it nevertheless late last week there were signs of success kurds took sinjar a strategically significant area in northern iraq mohammed emwazi a vicious killer and propagandist was likely killed in a drone strike paris has obviously eclipsed news of these breakthroughs who or what are fighting isis different from al qaeda group behind latter operated as an alliance of cells spread across world isis by contrast seeks to create a geographic space within to build a caliphate this shift in strategy perhaps explains why isis has been even more successful than al qaeda hitting so many different foreign targets with so many different methods from sinai to beirut to paris isis caliphate offers a haven for tens of thousands of foreign jihadists they come they train and then many return home to create havoc caliphate also provides money and moral encouragement of having an earthly paradise to fight for in his groundbreaking essay motivations behind isis graeme wood describes an isis recruiter calling it a vehicle for salvation its fighters are obsessed with recreating islam in its earliest form or as they interpret it to have been because early caliphate was far kinder and believe that most other muslims have fallen from standard one that includes uses of crucifixion and slavery whereas al qaeda limited itself to comparatively rational political objectives like expelling westerners from arab peninsula isis wants to bring apocalypse it not nihilist it deeply if distortedly religious and need to learn to take its brand of religion seriously good news that isis isolated applying phrase world war here unhelpful because it conjures images of rival equally sized nation states engaged in total war but while isis reach global it does not command sizable support beyond its shifting boundaries meanwhile alliance against it one of largest and most diverse in history including america britain france russia and iran saudi money may well have once supported it but saudi state now opposed indeed exceptional evil of isis leads us to view many of regional political agendas in a different light iran for instance certainly exporting its theocratic government to other countries but it doesnt desire end of world regime murderous and must be contained but it can be engaged complexity of islamic world politics highlights another aspect of this conflict it cannot be resolved entirely by force of arms isis has exploited sunni dissatisfaction with shiadominated government in baghdad this may mean iraq as a whole has to be split up to work turkey probably has to accommodate kurdish desires for a homeland and most importantly of all bashar alassad dictator of syria will have to depart stage there can be no constructive government of syria until there law order and democratic elections that legitimize proper opposition parties if give rebels impression that west wants to force assad them again they will resist us too finally there question of how handle islamic presence within europe itself this partly a matter of improving security measures and making sure returnees from syria dont just disappear into crowd theres also a refugee crisis to confront but while demographic pressures and security problems of allowing hundreds of thousands of people to cross europe have to be addressed in a firm way theres no escaping fact that a large settled part of eus population now islamic and how respond to isis has consequences for interfaith relations some american politicians have suggested a religious test for refugees seeking access to united states this kind of prejudiced rhetoric adds to that false sense that this a world warstyle clash between conservative muslims one side and christian democracies other it also unchristian and cruel moreover while americans might fear islamification as an existential concept here in europe have actual experience of living with muslims and can report that living easy muslims are friends family and coworkers they fear and despise isis as much as anyone else and those of us in centerground of european politics are determined not to alienate or discriminate against citizens who are british french or german of course it equally irritating to see politicians who seem to counsel doing nothing and westerners lacerating themselves because they believe their countries are to blame for all evil in world isis evil real concrete evil it must be stopped but must proceed carefully with a grand game plan and with desire to build just and representative arab regimes that last legacy of poorly chosen words or unilateral action there for all to see

use of extreme rhetoric by presidential candidates has sparked nationwide debates about american political polarization republican presidential candidates donald trump and sen ted cruz have been center of media attention for their use of inflammatory language notably when trump referred to mexican immigrants as rapists and drug traffickers and when cruz called for carpetbombing of islamic state group although news coverage of candidates rhetoric may be polarizing americans and deepening partisan divide it has also arguably contributed to current success of trump and cruz in presidential race explained duke faculty and student leaders there s a kind of anxiety in air probably across country but certainly with certain populations in us that cruz and trump are tapping into said frederick mayer professor of public policy political science and environment both of those candidates have found a way to frame their message that s really resonating with anxieties about american decline threats from terrorists abroad and home perceived threats to american way of life in last tuesday s state of union address president barack obama assured americans that country not in decline despite inflammatory claims told earlier all talk of america s economic decline political hot air he said well so all rhetoric hear about enemies getting stronger and america getting weaker let tell something united states of america most powerful nation earth period mayer noted that factors such as low unemployment low crime levels and fewer instances of terrorism american soil should also ease anxiety of americans it s ironic because can make claim that have less to fear today than just about any people in any time in history he said all these factors that should make us feel good junior adam lemon president of duke college republicans said that extreme rhetoric was necessary for success of trump and cruz in a field of more than candidates where standing out in any way was imperative he also noted that cable news has used stories about trump to boost ratings think part of reason that donald trump polling so high that his name just so ubiquitous through all of news media said freshman steve hassey communications director for duke democrats him being such a polarizing figure makes mass coverage of him even more polarizing both lemon and mayer discussed how news can have a negative impact when it focuses polarizing comments however mayer said he was optimistic that bipartisan reform possible and will occur certain issues campus there have been a few bipartisan discussions such as battle of brains debate between college democrats and republicans hosted by duke student government last november in students discussed issues ranging from racial discrimination to national debt although there have been bipartisan discussions lemon noted that there are barriers to fostering discussion between students of opposing political parties campus there are a lot more liberals campus than conservatives lemon said liberals tend to talk amongst themselves conservatives are afraid to express their opinions so they don t really talk and neither side really willing to engage too much mayer attributed polarization and lack of engagement between two sides to something more than just extreme rhetoric some deep level many of problems ve been talking about are really manifestations of a loss of trust in each other in institutions and like he said this whole dysfunction a trust problem stopped trusting institutions don t trust politicians a world where have very little trust a much less functional world

president obama set out monday to help seal a global climate pact opening of a major summit in paris though he faces stiff opposition home from congressional republicans and states worried his proposals will cost thousands of jobs president joined more than world leaders for twoweek conference where countries are trying to negotiate an agreement aimed slowing an increase in global temperatures in opening remarks obama called meeting a potential turning point for effort what should give us hope that this a turning point that this moment finally determined would save planet fact that nations share a sense of urgency about this challenge and a growing realization that it within power to do something about it he said with summit getting under way in wake of devastating terror attacks in same city some republicans have questioned whether obama focusing too much global warming and not enough security but obama monday called negotiations an act of defiance toward attackers what greater rejection of those who would tear down world than marshaling best efforts to save it obama said president said that as leader of world s largest economy and secondlargest emitter after china embrace responsibility to do something about it president also met oneonone monday with leaders of other nations responsible for largest carbon emissions chinese president xi jinping and indian prime minister narendra modi sidelines of summit obama met as well with russian president vladimir putin to discuss security matters but as obama makes a personal press for a climate deal he faces practical challenges back in washington president has pledged that us will cut its overall emissions by percent to percent by and a centerpiece of that a push to reduce emissions from us power plants but half states are suing to block power plant rules claiming obama has abused his authority under clean air act further republicans capitol hill are threatening to block committing us dollars to a un green climate fund designed to help poorer countries combat climate change in days before paris summit republicans warned that any paris deal with legally binding provisions must come before senate for a vote and without that approval they warned lawmakers will not greenlight green climate fund money without senate approval there will be no money period sen john barrasso rwyo said a recent hearing barrasso and sen jim inhofe rokla chairman of senate environment and public works committee also sent a letter to obama signed by more than three dozen senators likewise urging president to have his special envoy relay to developing nations representatives that congress will not be forthcoming with green climate fund money absent a senate vote president wants to direct billion including million in nearterm for un green climate fund paris conference aimed most farreaching deal ever to tackle global warming last major agreement kyoto protocol required only rich countries to cut emissions and us never signed among several sticking points money how much rich countries should invest to help poor countries cope with climate change how much should be invested in renewable energy and how much traditional oil and gas producers stand to lose if countries agree to forever reduce emissions with that in mind least governments and leading world investors were announcing billions of dollars in investments to research and develop clean energy technology with goal of making it cheaper backers include obama microsoft cofounder bill gates facebook founder mark zuckerberg billionaires george soros and saudi prince alaweed bin talal and jack ma of chinas alibaba meanwhile obama met sidelines with putin to discuss civil war in syria as well as turkey s shootdown of a russian jet last week amid allegations it crossed into turkish airspace according to a white house official obama expressed his regret for recent loss of a russian pilot and crew member and reiterated united states support for deescalation between russia and turkey obama though also reiterated that syrian leader bashar alassad must leave power as part of any political transition associated press contributed to this report

ucla reported wednesday that nearly patients were exposed to a potentially deadly superbug contaminated medical instruments that infected seven patients and may have contributed to two deaths a total of patients ronald reagan ucla medical center were exposed to antibioticresistant carbapenemresistant enterobacteriaceae or cre during endoscopic procedures between october and january university said in a statement bacteria may have been a contributing factor in deaths of two patients university said those who were exposed are being sent free hometesting kits that university will analyze similar outbreaks of cre have been reported around nation they are difficult to treat because some varieties are resistant to most known antibiotics by one estimate cre can contribute to death in up to half of seriously infected patients according to national centers for disease control and prevention bacteria can cause infections of bladder or lungs leading to coughing fever or chills cre infections have been reported in every state except idaho alaska and maine according to cdc ucla said infections may have been transmitted through specialized endoscopes used during diagnosis and treatment of pancreatic and bileduct problems instruments are inserted into patients throats outbreak was discovered late last month during tests a patient two medical devices may have carried bacteria even though they were sterilized according to manufacturers specifications ucla said devices have been removed and decontamination procedures upgraded university said notified all patients who had this type of procedure and were using seven different scopes only two of them were found to be infected in an abundance of caution notified everybody said dale tate a university of california los angeles spokeswoman thursday us food and drug administration issued an advisory warning doctors that even when a manufacturers cleaning instructions are followed infectious germs may linger in devices their complex design and tiny parts make complete disinfection extremely difficult advisory said national figures bacteria are not kept but states have seen cases cdc said a similar outbreak occurred in illinois in dozens of patients were exposed to cre with some cases apparently linked to a tainted endoscope used advocate lutheran general hospital hospital later changed its sterilization procedures a seattle hospital virginia mason medical center reported in january that cre linked to an endoscope sickened least patients and died although it was unclear whether infection played a role in their deaths this bacteria emerging in us and its associated with a high mortality rate dr alex kallen an epidemiologist in cdcs division of healthcare quality promotion told la times dont want this circulating anywhere in community associated press contributed to this report click for more from myfoxlacom

hillary clinton s campaign looks like a new media startup democratic frontrunner has a staff of dozens producing original content including bylined news stories and professional video all managed by an audience development team a model similar to digital news pioneers buzzfeed or vox a blog called feed anchored by five fulltime writers pumps out articles interactive trivia quizzes gifs of clintons latenightshow appearances and other content designed to engage supporters and court potential voters across social media channels like facebook twitter and snapchat they seem to be trying to mimic a publisher said michael wertheim an adviser to media and tech startups and a former strategy director upworthy president obamas team was undisputed powerhouse of and cycles now his digital mastermind teddy goff helming clinton s efforts but goff says succeeding in far more challenging in past campaigns felt that could pretty much reach people need to reach by running a really good twitter and facebook account he said obama for america built facebook and twitter followings of more than million and million respectively now people have more power over what they consume said goff they have a higher set of expectations for how they re going to be served he said and are steering away from overtly political messages when put those together it s a pretty difficult task clinton approach made possible by its resources said katie harbath facebook s global politics and government outreach director others are having to be leaner be more resourceful and deal with a smaller staff she said yet in this election resources don t necessarily mean they ll resonate more or create more of a discussion platform said jenna golden twitter s director of political ad sales while democrat s brooklynbased team crafts images and stories optimized for mobile viewing targeted both broad and specific demographic audiences republican candidate donald trump s approach more basic he sits his computer and sends out missives and bernie sanders her main rival for democratic nomination has more overall digital interactions than clinton trump s posted over times since june mostly twitter according to crowdtangle a social analytics tool that monitors social media since june of last year trump has generated nearly million interactions positive and negative his campaign accounts include facebook twitter instagram and youtube sanders comes in million and clinton million according to facebook trump generated most interactions across social media network week after his controversial proposal to temporarily ban noncitizen muslims from entering country clintons peak came last week of october after a successful debate and a marathon performance before a house benghazi hearing trump absolutely dominating social this election cycle and it s not even close said brandon silverman crowdtangle chief executive it s a way to skip media and go directly to his audience he said yet dominating conversation doesnt necessarily translate into votes trump and clinton have very different strategic goals said silverman while trump brand building clinton converting her traffic into actions such as site registrations to acquire data critical to her getoutthevote efforts whether clinton or trump model more effective will be part of election postmortem said jake horowitz founder of mic a top millennial website have a very sophisticated operation and then have someone who s a total monster with media attention and they re competing for attention across social said horowitz a lot of focus appears to be evoking emotion as former first lady battles perceptions that she isnt personable a recent post getting a lot of clicks shows clinton retelling in a speech story of army captain humayun khan a yearold muslim american who died in iraq after waving his unit away from a vehicle that exploded video set to background music includes a cutaway shot to a visibly anguished man who as clinton speaks bows his head and and chokes back tears as his fingers tweeze bridge of his nose it s gotten over million views facebook there s also a regular series called quick question that catches candidate spontaneously discussing fun topics including lessons from her mother and what it s like to watch football in clinton household they catch her randomly and she just answers it captures her said goff they re probably trying to get her to react in a genuine fashion and trigger some emotion said wertheim similar to what d find buzzfeed or huffington post clintons site interactive in december a quiz asked readers to guess whether certain statements came from donald trump or someone else another notes her summer job after college in alaska sliming fish or removing guts from salmon with a spoon another featured five vintage photos telling story of how bill and hillary clinton fell in love many readers are probably unaware some posts are from campaign since a number feature people other than candidate including one of a cute yearold and another highlighting a letter from a grandfather who fled germany ahead of wwii aimed illustrating historic role nation has played in taking in refugees ultimately though digital experts say social media success depends something only candidate can deliver authenticity candidates who are willing to be more authentic and show who they are as a person get a lot more engagement said horowitz

instinctively makes us want to help scores of protesters gathered outside seat of british prime minister saturday holding signs calling government to save aleppo and impose a nobomb zone now while anger understandable way it being channeled reflects a circumscribed policy debate there are other options than a nofly zone should be avoided as it would harm rather than help efforts to alleviate suffering of syrian civilians in any area of policy mainstream debate revolves around policy alternatives that reflect establishment divisions for example in economics there no alternative to neoliberalism least there wasn t until keynesianism was rediscovered by some elites after crisis debate over what to be done over syria revolves around two policy alternatives hawks including likely next us president hillary clinton advocate a nfz and doves including current us administration maintain that sanctions regime should be increased this effectively reflects a division within establishment how to proceed serious policy alternatives are not discussed in particular discussion of increasing aid and support to refugees surely most obvious way of directly helping civilians in syria and what un has called industrialized countries to do curious by its absence this circumscribed debate does not logically follow from its supposed pretext stopping civilian loss of life in fact a nfz a policy that would unavoidably lead to civilians dying enforcing a nfz means destroying air defenses are located to defend cities ie they are located in areas where there are many civilians even flagbearer for hawks hillary clinton has admitted privately that with a nofly zone re going to kill a lot of syrians such intervention will take a lot of civilians this realization would seem inconsistent with oftenused humanitarian pretext but it makes sense given hillary clinton s recent admission that her top priority in syria removing syrian president assad there a clear parallel with imposition of a nfz in libya prolonged conflict and worsened situation for civilians nato bombing directly led to scores of civilian deaths and facilitated overthrow of regime by rebel militias that have killed and are continuing to kill thousands particularly repugnant was ethnic cleansing of black people including through public lynching in a paper alan kuperman a harvard academic argued that nato intervention extended war by a factor of and increased death toll to times given that libya now a failed state torn apart by warlords can safely say that these estimates were too conservative president obama privately calls situation in libya a shit show only last month a report from foreign affairs committee of british parliament found that humanitarian justification was an insufficient pretext and based falsehoods supposedly limited intervention led ineluctably to regime change and that british government and by implication other participating western powers did not seriously consider diplomatic alternatives to military action regardless mantra of western foreign policy it will be different this time unlike all recent western military interventions this one will be limited successful and won t leave a worse humanitarian situation in its wake although if dire humanitarian situation in aleppo necessitates immediate action then why are there not equally loud calls for action for civilians facing similar situations us bombing in manbij and kobane in syria and ramadi and fallujah in iraq has resulted in thousands of civilian deaths and flattened entire neighbourhoods more than a third of us and uk backed saudi airstrikes in yemen have hit civilian sites including schools hospitals weddings and funerals talking about this not meant as a distraction or relativization fact that us and saudi arabia are engaged in similar activities does not make bombing of aleppo less objectionable however it does raise questions about motives of those pushing so hard for a nofly zone if western foreign policy actors and their allies in press were motivated by humanitarian concerns then surely stopping these atrocities should appear policy agenda especially given that action required easier and does not risk war with russia if humanitarian considerations were really important factors in foreign policy debate then there would be discussion legitimacy of aerial bombardment of cities and towns given that this invariably leads to civilian deaths international agreements have been successful in making chemical and biological weapons illegal a prohibition generally followed though not always first wellpublicized use of aerial bombardment nazi bombing of guernica in caused righteous popular outrage tragically however its use became normalized during second world war and a ban aerial bombing of cities was not included in postwar international settlement fact that this seems so hopelessly idealistic reflects fact that it geopolitics not humanitarian considerations that govern international relations and foreign policy discussion human suffering nothing more than a useful pretext for whatever actions want to take in order to secure geopolitical advantage us and its allies want to remove syria from russia s orbit so therefore dictator there must go but airstrikes to support dictator in yemen are fine because dictator there a friend of close us ally saudi arabia there an added complication with nfz in syria in that it marks a return to coldwar era brinkmanship and possible armed confrontation with russia logic of brinkmanship runs that to make geopolitical gains one must escalate to a level that will make other side back down partly by convincing enemy are ready to commit irrational acts there an inherent danger in this game both states are nuclear armed and consequences of a spiral of escalation could be devastating syria hawks or what close obama aide ben rhodes called prostupid shit caucus argue that a nfz will lead russia to effectively back down for example clinton argued that it will give us leverage in conversations with russia interestingly in exchange this quote from clinton indicated war goal a nfz will make russia put assad future political and diplomatic track ie russia will be forced to accept regime change after all despite panic in press russia actually a feeble successor state to a superpower and would come off worse in a direct conflict with preeminent might of north america or so logic runs however dovish western foreign policy actors point to danger that advanced russian materiel support to syria poses to enforcement of a nfz unlike other recent us military adventures enforcing a nfz in syria could lead to significant and politically unpalatable american casualties pilots will be shot down this realization means that saner establishment figures are opposed to a nfz for example us army general carter ham who oversaw nfz in libya said a nfz a violent combat action that results in lots of casualties and increased risk to own personnel instead relative doves like current secretary of state john kerry advocate intensifying sanctions regime against syria and russia again this does not seem to be seriously about helping civilians a leaked un report has revealed that existing western sanctions regime preventing humanitarian aid and creating a humanitarian disaster that threatens to rival that caused by oilforfood program in iraq during s history of sanctions tends to show that costs are borne by civilians and a petersen institute study of all sanctions incidents since wwii shows that sanctions failed to achieve their goals in about ⅔ of cases and are even less successful when applied against enemies and autocrats so what to be done what should people in west and globally push for to help people of syria unfortunately there do not seem to be any quick fixes situation in syria complex and involves diverse actors with competing interests immediate relief to refugees fleeing conflict should be a priority in terms of foreign policy campaigning for deescalation and diplomacy against constant militarism remain best solutions for syrian people and humanity generally
