heres something interesting from unz review recipient name recipient email make america great again was slogan of donald trump s election but immediate impact of his victory to make us less of a power in world for two reasons american prestige and influence will be damaged by a general belief internationally that us has just elected a dangerous buffoon as its leader perception pervasive but not very deeply rooted and likely be temporary stemming as it does from trump s demagogic rants during election campaign those about relations with foreign countries were particularly vague and least likely to provide a guide to future policy more damaging in long term for america s status as superpower likelihood that us now a more deeply divided society than ever trump won election by demonising and threatening individuals and communities mexicans muslims latinos and his confrontational style of politics not going to disappear verbal violence produces a permanently overheated political atmosphere in physical violence becomes an option same time election campaign was focused almost exclusively american domestic politics with voters showing little interest in events abroad this unlikely to change governments around world can see this for themselves though this will not stop them badgering their diplomats in washington and new york for an inkling as to how far trump s offthecuff remarks were more than outrageous attempts to dominate news agenda for a few hours fortunately his pronouncements were so woolly that they can be easily jettisoned between now and his inauguration real foreign policy positions will only emerge with formation of a trump cabinet when it becomes clear who will be in charge but if future policies remain unknowable supercharged american nationalism combined with economic populism and isolationism are likely to set general tone trump has invariably portrayed americans as victims of foul machinations of foreign countries who previously faced no real resistance from an incompetent selfserving american elite this sort of aggressive nationalism not unique to trump all over world nationalism having a spectacular rebirth in countries from turkey to philippines it has become a successful vehicle for protest in britain france germany austria and eastern europe though trump frequently portrayed as a peculiarly american phenomenon his populist nationalism has a striking amount in common with that of brexit campaigners in britain or even chauvinism of president recep tayyip erdogan in turkey much of this can be discounted as patriotic bombast but in all cases there a menacing undercurrent of racism and demonisation whether it directed against illegal immigrants in us asylum seekers in britain or kurds in south east turkey in reality trump made very few proposals for radical change in us foreign policy during election campaign aside from saying that he would throw out agreement with iran its nuclear programme though his staff now being much less categorical about this saying only that deal must be properly enforced nobody really knows if trump will deal any differently from obama with swathe of countries between pakistan and nigeria where there are least seven wars raging afghanistan iraq syria yemen libya somalia and south sudan as well as four serious insurgencies most serious wars in us already militarily involved are in iraq and syria and here trump s comments during campaign suggest that he will focus destroying isis recognise danger of becoming militarily overinvolved and look for some sort of cooperation with russia as next biggest player in conflict this similar to what already happening hillary clinton s intentions in syria though never fully formulated always sounded more interventionist than trump s one of her senior advisers openly proposed giving less priority to assault isis and more to getting rid of president bashar alassad to this end a third force of prous militant moderates was to be raised that would fight and ultimately defeat both isis and assad probably this fantasy would never have come to pass but fact that it was ever given currency underlines extent to clinton was one with most deadinthewater conventional wisdom of foreign policy establishment in washington president obama developed a much more acute sense of what us could and could not do in middle east and beyond without provoking crises exceeding its political and military strength its power may be less than before failed us interventions in iraq and afghanistan following but it still far greater than any other country s currently it us successfully coordinating offensive against isis s last strongholds in mosul and raqqa by a multitude of fractious parties in iraq and syria it was never clear how seriously one should have taken clinton s proposals for safe zones and trying to fight isis and assad same time but her judgements events in middle east since iraq invasion of all suggested a flawed idea of what was feasible trump s instincts generally seem less wellinformed but often shrewd and his priories have nothing to do with middle east past us leaders have felt same way but they usually end up by being dragged into its crises one way or other and how they perform then becomes test of their real quality as a leader region has been political graveyard for three of last five us presidents jimmy carter was destroyed by consequences of iranian revolution ronald reagan was gravely weakened by irancontra scandal and george w bush s years in office will be remembered chiefly for calamities brought by his invasion of iraq barack obama was luckier and more sensible but he wholly underestimated rise of isis until it captured mosul in reprinted from independent by this was not a subject that was anybody s mind until brought it up announcement not anyone s mind for years immigration has been subject of nearconstant often bitter argument within gop but it true that trump has brought debate to a new place first with his announcement speech about whether mexican migrants are really rapists and now with somewhat more nuanced trump plan much of it visa tracking everify withholding funds from sanctuary cities predates trump even building great wall not particularly new for one have been advocating that in this space since dominating discussion however are his two policy innovations a abolition of birthright citizenship and b mass deportation if are born in united states are an american citizen so says th amendment barring some esoteric and radically new jurisprudence abolition would require amending constitution would take years and great political effort and make gop anathema to hispanic americans for a generation and for what birthright citizenship a symptom not a cause if regain control of border number of birthright babies fades to insignificance time and energy it would take to amend constitution are far more usefully deployed securing border moreover real issue not birthright babies themselves but chain migration that follows it turns one baby into an imported village chain migration however not a constitutional right it s a result of statutes and regulations these can be readily changed that should be focus not a quixotic constitutional battle last sunday trump told nbc s chuck todd that all illegal immigrants must leave country although once they ve been kicked out will let good ones back in its own terms this crackpot wouldn t save a lot just mayflower moving costs if chose good ones first before sending swat teams to turf families out of their homes loading them buses and dumping them other side of rio grande less frivolously it estimated by conservative american action forum that mass deportation would take about years and cost about billion for all police judges lawyers and enforcement agents and bus drivers needed to expel million people this would all be merely ridiculous if it weren t morally obscene forcibly evict million people from their homes it can t happen it shouldn t happen and of course it won t ever happen but because it s view of republican frontrunner every other candidate now required to react so instead of debating border security guestworker programs and sanctuary cities where republicans are firm moral and political ground they are forced into a debate about a repulsive fantasy for republican party also political poison mitt romney lost hispanic vote by points and he was advocating only selfdeportation now party discussing forced deportation it not just hispanics who will be alienated romney lost asian vote too by points and many nonminorities will be offended by idea of rounding up million people vast majority of whom are lawabiding members of their communities donald trump has every right to advance his ideas he not to be begrudged his masterly showmanship his relentless candor or his polling success strongly oppose idea of ostracizing anyone from gop or conservative movement whose authority let people decide but that not to say that he should be exempt from normal scrutiny or from consideration of effect of his candidacy conservatism s future if are a conservative alarmed country s direction and committed to retaking white house should be concerned about what trump s ascendancy doing to chances of that happening democrats presumptive candidate flailing badly republicans have an unusually talented field with a good chance of winning back presidency do they really want to be dragged into swamps right now immigration that will make that prospect electorally impossible yes understand anger frustration etc etc that trump channeling but how are these alleviated by yelling m mad as hell and proceeding to elect hillary clinton read more blame government not markets for monopoly corporate mergers and hostile takeovers can promote economic efficiency by ron paul november when timewarner announced it planned to merge with another major communications firm many feared new company would exercise neartotal monopoly power these concerns led some to call for government action to block merger in order to protect both timewarner s competitors and consumers no am not talking about timewarner s recent announced plan to merge with att but reaction to timewarner s merger with then internet giant aol in far from creating an untouchable leviathan crushing all competitors aoltimewarner merger fell apart in under a decade failure of aoltimewarner demonstrates that even biggest companies are vulnerable to competition if there open entry into marketplace aoltimewarner failed because consumers left them for competitors offering lower prices andor better quality corporate mergers and hostile takeovers can promote economic efficiency by removing inefficient management and boards of directors these managers and board members often work together to promote their own interests instead of generating maximum returns for investors by providing consumers with affordable quality products thus laws making it difficult to launch a hostile takeover promote inefficient use of resources and harm investors workers and consumers monopolies and cartels are creations of government not markets for example reason media dominated by a few large companies that no one can operate a television or radio station unless they obtain federal approval and pay federal licensing fees similarly anyone wishing to operate a cable company must not only comply with federal regulations they must sign a franchise agreement with their local government fortunately internet has given americans greater access to news and ideas shut out by governmentlicensed lapdogs of mainstream media this may be why so many politicians are anxious to regulate web government taxes and regulations are effective means of limiting competition in an industry large companies can afford costs of complying with government regulations costs cripple their smaller competitors big business can also afford to hire lobbyists to ensure that new laws and regulations favor big business examples of regulations that benefit large corporations include food and drug administration s fda regulations that raise costs of developing a new drug as well as limit consumers ability to learn about natural alternatives to pharmaceuticals another example doddfrank legislation has strengthened large financial intuitions while harming their weaker competitors legislation forcing consumers to pay outofstate sales tax their online purchases a classic case of business seeking to use government to harm less politicallypowerful competitors this legislation being pushed by large brickandmortar stores and internet retailers who are seeking a governmentgranted advantage over smaller competitors many failed mergers and acquisitions result from distorted signals sent to business and investors by federal reserve s inflationary monetary policy perhaps most famous example of this aoltimewarner fiasco was a direct result of fedcreated dotcom bubble in a free market mergers between businesses enable consumers to benefit from new products and reduced prices any businesses that charge high prices or offer substandard products will soon face competition from businesses offering consumers lower prices andor higher quality monopolies only exist when government tilts playing field in favor of wellconnected crony capitalists therefore those concerned about excessive corporate power should join supporters of free market in repudiating regulations taxes and subsides that benefit politicallypowerful businesses most important step to end boombust business cycle by ending federal reserve

focusing particularly a sense that many americans are struggling economically monday senator sanders offered an enthusiastic endorsement of clinton saying he was proud to stand with her some clinton delegates say democrats optimism also offers a counterbalance to trump s focus looking backward think there are solutions moving forward solutions and don t want to go back to s thank very much bear for first time trump shrinks in spotlight fiorina steals show donald trump was once again center stage wednesday nights debate hosted by cnn second debate among gop candidates for president this time however he had a harder time holding spotlight again and again throughout seemingly interminable threehour spectacle attention of audience migrated to smallest figure set carly fiorina it could be lost no one that fiorina was only woman in cast of hopefuls nor that she had been admitted to group only after cnn altered its original rules to take note of her recent surge in polls even with this upgrade propelled by her standout performance in august debate among lesser candidates fiorina has been languishing in low single digits in most national polls that could be about to change any leveling off in trumps astonishing trajectory will create a need for new storylines and fiorina seems poised to provide other outsider phenomenon ben carson did not shine as brightly in cnn event as he had fox in august softspoken and mildmannered neurosurgeon disagreed with trump about vaccines and autism but shied from any real conflict with him issue other times he seemed vague in explaining his iraq war position and his willingness to deemphasize armed conflict fiorina however was a picture of decisiveness and tothepoint presentation like consummate sales professional she fiorina came armed with pithy precise answers she had neatly anticipated nearly all questions thrown her way including ones she seized in general scramble for air and camera time she was ready with exact dimensions of larger military she wanted and specifics of rebuttal to attacks her stewardship of hewlettpackard trump made repeated rather clumsy thrusts fiorinas executive history she whipped them back him with replies that cracked in air trump show in general did not seem as dominating as in first debate or in many news cycles since when anything and everything he said made headlines wags soon dubbed night lady and trump but that was not whole story either trump was also set upon by a pack of his opponents several of whom seemed to have boned up his history of casino bankruptcies he fended these off with breezy references to way business works but these did not seem to be working as well as in past jeb bush though still a bit ill ease made his arguments with dignity and seriousness and jousted in his own restrained way with trump as well as with rand paul and others much has been written and said about trumps capturing imagination of white older workingclass voters within republican electorate this he has done but not by himself he has had an assist from wideeyed media promoting summer of trump with wonder and hunger for more times this night there were signs that this dynamic was losing some of its energy that shtick might be beginning to wear thin this impression crept in despite effects of cnn debate format allowed any candidate mentioned by name to respond to that mention as most of candidates were in varying degrees gunning for trump moderator jake tapper was continually turning to donald for replies intended or not this device kept billionaire center of attention even when tapper was trying to distribute airtime more equitably still there was more than enough running room for marco rubio and ted cruz senators from florida and texas fired broadsides against both obama administration and masters of senate that have been their stockintrade since tea party movement first sent them to washington both did well connecting with hardcore conservatives who agree gop leaders in congress are useless rubio did so with more human appeal however using his remarkably empathetic facial appeal cruz seemed intent softening his image tossing verbal bouquets to his wife and his parents chris christie also seemed to thrive in mix this occasion this in part because he interrupted trumpfiorina fussing over who had done what in business yearold construction worker whos out of a job doesnt care about careers christie scolded and he reinforced point in an eloquent closing statement about refocusing national policy to benefit ordinary americans john kasich governor of ohio once again offered a mix of highlevel resume and awshucks americana tossing out more numbers than any of his datahappy rivals but still managing to be more boyish than wonkish kasich surely has credentials to be legitimate in this race but he remains credential candidate in an antiestablishment and even anticredential year among those getting short end of attention supply was scott walker governor of wisconsin walker had his lines down as well as anyone but they all seemed to go back to madison in and massive protests against his first budget walker has raised eyebrows in past by saying his facedown of these protests proved he could tame isis vladimir putin and ayatollahs of iran but he was back that theme again wednesday night rand paul provided multiple moments of dissent opposing not only iraq war of but also proposals to intervene in middle east today mike huckabee was reliable issue of religious liberty he defined as including right of kentucky county clerk kim davis to defy supreme court and deny marriage licenses to samesex couples one final note about happy hour debate that preceded main event both louisiana gov bobby jindal and sen lindsey graham of south carolina turned in sharp performances with jindal stressing harsh antiobama rhetoric and graham a grim assessment of threats to national security replete with a pledge to return more boots ground to middle east difference was that graham mixed in a strong dose of sly carolina humor eliciting chuckles and even guffaws for things like a pledge to have more drinking in washington as fiorina promotion this time around proved so worthwhile perhaps in future gop and sponsoring media will see fit to elevate unofficial winner of each undercard debate to participate in main event in next round it could become a kind of tradition provided of course that undercard debate survives another round in this campaign

talk about election process for a moment there a single person in this country who feels better able to choose between trump and clinton today than a year ago what have learned about these candidates but uniquely polarizing nature of major party nominees this time and fact that unusually they were both wellknown to american people before they even entered race if two relatively obscure candidates had been nominated perhaps cries of pain from public wouldn t be quite as anguished there s more to this question though than familiarity with or contempt for any individual politician any given time length of election campaigns in america causes real structural problems throughout democratic system regardless of who candidates are first relentless posturing involved in nearpermanent campaigns contributes to hyperpartisanship that makes reasonable debate public policy issues increasingly difficult an environment where any halfthoughtful comment can almost instantly find its way into an political campaigns a direct factor in systemic corruption that such a notable feature of american democracy in fact if look what actually happens in congress and in state legislatures it difficult to argue that america in any meaningful sense of word a democracy all it a donocracy where funders of election campaigns literally buy outcomes they want from political system look too role of organized labor providing campaign infrastructure volunteers get out vote operations none of that would be touched by campaign finance reform and then look what unions get in return in home state of california budgets that increase compensation for corrections officers while cutting spending public schools and state universities way to go progressives truth that even if clinton wins and by some miracle enacts some version of campaign finance reform assorted lobbyists bloodsuckers sleazebags and other hangerson in washington dc will breathe a huge sigh of relief because they know that for them life it was a tough night for frontrunners in new hampshire and a good night for governors marco rubio hit a wall named chris christie donald trump couldn t put down an aggressive jeb bush and ted cruz had to issue a public apology to ben carson christie was relentless prosecutor bush was knowledgeable and in contrast to some earlier performances tough and direct ohio gov john kasich carved out space as a candidate ready and willing to work across party lines given timing and state of republican nomination contest few debates have had potential to shape order of finish in a primary campaign more than saturday night s forum saint anselm college trump holds a big lead but competition underneath him fierce and outcome tuesday likely to be consequential those with most to lose were ones who tried to make most out of their time stage no one had a rockier night than rubio senator from florida whose strong thirdplace finish in iowa made him candidate rise in republican presidential race but under a blistering attack from christie who characterized him as a politician with no notable accomplishments who had run away from immigration reform bill he had cosponsored rubio faltered rubio knew attacks were coming but instead of answering them directly he sought to change subject once twice three times he offered a quick counterpunch and then slid off criticisms to turn to an attack president obama repeating his language almost word for word and drawing boos from audience when he accused christie of having to be shamed into going back to new jersey to deal with recent snowstorm christie came roaring back that s what washington dc does christie said driveby shot beginning with incorrect and incomplete information and then memorized second speech that exactly what his advisers gave him what was most striking was fact that rubio played directly into criticism that while he a gifted and natural communicator he overly scripted and returns to his standard stump speech as quickly as he can strategy has worked for him in past debates but repetition got better of him saturday rubio wanted to debate obama and hillary clinton warning time and again that president has deliberately led country in wrong direction and that clinton would extend those policies those kinds of attacks bring cheers his campaign rallies but were far less effective during saturday s debate in later stages of debate rubio seemed to regain his footing both foreign policy and a question about abortion in he talked about balance between a woman s right to make her own decisions and right of an unborn child to live even when bush drew a distinction with rubio over whether there should be exceptions for rape incest and life of woman bush favors rubio said he would sign a bill as president that included those exceptions but that he still stood by his personal convictions rubio said he would rather lose an election than be wrong issue trump struggled when subject of eminent domain a hotbutton issue for many conservatives who dislike government was introduced billionaire builder initially offered a ringing defense of practice of government taking private property for such projects as roads and bridges bush countered initially agreeing that eminent domain necessary for public good but then attacking trump for attempting to use practice to take property from an elderly woman in atlantic city nj for purpose of a parking lot for one of his casinos trump reverted to attacks he has long leveled bush that he lacks strength or energy he wants to be a tough guy trump said dismissively but bush came right back him how tough it to take away a property from an elderly woman he said sarcastically when trump told bush to keep quiet audience let out another round of boos to trump said that s all his donors and special interests cruz who won iowa caucuses had two difficult moments start of evening first came over criticism cruz leveled trump earlier in week saying don t know anyone who would be comfortable with someone who behaves this way having his finger button trump was given first word and defended himself he reminded everyone that he had opposed war in iraq adding m not one with trigger other people up here believe would be a lot faster he said when cruz was asked to explain why he thought trump lacked temperament to be president he ducked assessment voters are making here in new hampshire and across country they are evaluating each and every one of us he said reminded by abc news anchor david muir that he had not explained why he had made earlier comment cruz ducked again trump responded he didn t answer question and that s what s going to happen with enemies and people compete against people back down with trump and that s what like and that s what country going to like that didn t end early hazing for cruz he was asked why his advisers had called iowa volunteers night of caucuses to say that carson might be quitting race and to encourage voters to back senator from texas cruz blamed problem what he said was a misleading news report cnn that he said was not corrected for several hours but he also apologized to carson and said he had done so day after caucuses carson in his typically quiet way accepted apology but not explanation in fact timeline indicates that initial tweet from cnn was followed by another one within one minute that clarified that was not dropping out he said more than in other previous debates this one turned into governors against others three share common experiences and when they talk about one another it s clear there mutual respect though they are in a battle in they cannot all survive and whether by design or accident three seemed to reinforce one another in taking down candidates who finished first second and third in iowa christie delivered one of strongest performances of campaign clearly determined to knock down rubio whom he described this past week as a boy in bubble fighting for his political life he was merciless in attacking rubio for lacking courage to fight for immigration reform legislation that passed senate but faltered in house rubio countered by attacking christie s record in new jersey noting that state has gone through repeated credit downgrades during his two terms audience seemed to side with christie whether voters will do same test for next three days kasich was happy warrior a governor who stressed jobs economy and dealing realistically with issues not basis of pure ideology bush has tried for many months to make his record in florida centerpiece of his candidacy only now in these closing days has he appeared more comfortable as a candidate saturday s debate here offered a last opportunity for candidates to make a persuasive argument before a primary election likely to winnow field of realistic hopefuls to four most new hampshire electorate famously fickle for upending frontrunners and defying conventional wisdom and turning its back winners in iowa a more charitable description that this a state where voters keep their options open as long as possible while it s true that many voters have been locked in for weeks or months a sizable number are spending these last few days shopping and pondering they are every rally this final weekend looking for that connection that tips them firmly in one direction or another that makes pollsters nervous and campaigns hopeful candidates know lightning can strike and they grasp for every sign that it happening to them in days after iowa there has been some movement in polls but nothing definitive yet what makes this year s gop primary distinctive from past campaigns number of candidates this final weekend who still think they have a chance to move to succeeding rounds of primaries and caucuses saturday s debate underscored both sense of possibility and sense of urgency that surrounds last days of campaigning here

public funding of elections that relying tax revenue more than private donations to fund candidate campaigns a popular campaign finance reform proposal if one that many americans don t fully embrace public funding often thought to free candidates from burden of fundraising and reduce influence of wealthy donors and special interests that all sounds good who likes special interests after all now new research shows that public funding has an unexpected consequence increased polarization that public funding makes it harder not easier to elect moderate candidates that conclusion of political scientist andrew hall he focuses state legislative elections and compares trends in five states that implemented robust public funding programs arizona connecticut maine minnesota and wisconsin to trends in other states here what he finds below a graph showing distribution of ideology np scores in legislatures in states that implemented public financing treated states and those that did not control states first group of states became more polarized after implementation of public financing but no such change occurred during this time in states that didn t implement public financing in a more elaborate statistical analysis hall examines gap between republican and democratic legislators representing similar districts in a polarized legislature a republican and a democrat will tend to vote in very different ways even though they represent essentially same constituents hall finds that public financing increases this gap between parties by percent why does public financing appear to have this effect hall argues that public financing weakens influence of a maligned but moderating force in elections accessoriented interest groups public financing reduces funding supplied by these groups by over per race average problem that these groups give relatively little to ideologically extreme legislators and much more to moderates individual donors however have no such preference this leads to a broader point about campaign finance and polarization much of debate over campaign finance based distinctions between good donors and bad donors good donors are taxpayers in a public financing system or in a privately funded system good donors are ordinary citizens sometimes called small donors bad donors are political party organizations wealthy people political action committees and interest groups problem that if want to reduce polarization this way of thinking about donors gets things exactly backward small donors are a polarizing influence as adam bonica has shown meanwhile wealthy people tend to be a moderating influence same true of many political action committees and interest groups same true of political party organizations as brian schaffner and ray la raja argue in this post that s not to say that there aren t good reasons to favor public financing or small donors but favoring those things may also mean living with tradeoff a more polarized and probably less functional politics note for a response see this followup post by seth masket and michael miller
killing obama administration rules dismantling obamacare and pushing through tax reform are early todo list

anatomy of crisis and decline of us empire submitted by danny haiphong tue tweet widget by danny haiphong there are multiple dimensions to crisis that afflicts us imperialism latest election evidence of a crisis of legitimacy for ruling parties americans are estranged from a government that spies every one its citizens and rest of world too unemployment poverty racist state repression and war are all system has to offer unable to escape a year economic slump us instead plots destruction of its rivals anatomy of crisis and decline of us empire by danny haiphong vast majority of oppressed communities particularly black workers have seen their labor become disposable in a postindustrial society whether one analyzes economic military or political spheres of us imperialism one thing abundantly clear very fabric of united states in deep crisis crisis largely misunderstood by vast majority of working and oppressed people living under it but a specter haunts us and it isnt anything like hollywoods scary movies that specter possibility that people will become a conscious force of opposition to crisis and seek to dismantle system of capitalist empire that governs it crises are genuinely thought of in economic terms economic base of capitalism indeed suffering from protracted economic crisis us capitalist economy and thus world capitalist economy pegged to its hip entered a period of stagnation in mid to late s what followed was a slowdown in production facilitated by increased monopolization financialization and increased technological capacity of system capitalisms source of profit labor was now being exploited by an apparatus too big to expand profits of system without intensified exploitation aftermath of capitalisms periodic collapses from overproduction and under consumption have been characterized ever since by a complete and total assault all workers wages have declined or remained stagnant for nearly four decades conditions of crisis speak for themselves workers in us and entire western world for that matter have seen conditions rapidly deteriorate as capitalist system has sought to maximize profits in face of productive slowdown free trade agreements such as nafta have given corporations freedom to eliminate production domestically in order to seek a better deal internationally wages have declined or remained stagnant for nearly four decades unemployment has become a permanent fixture of life for millions and nearly one of two people in us are considered poor or near poor this time us a lowwage capitalist economy dominated by service oriented precarious employment racism has played a large part in disparity inherent under these conditions wealth gap between black america and white america larger than it was in civil rights era not only has black america been target of racist housing policies from predatory lenders leading up to crisis but burden of privatization and austerity has been directly aimed black families hedge funds for example have used working class black communities as guinea pig to test effectiveness of massive school closures and teacher layoffs as well as expansion of charter schools thousands of black teachers have lost their jobs as a result to mostly white demographic of teach for america corps members wealth gap between black america and white america larger than it was in civil rights era however it not enough to understand crisis of capitalism through an economic lens crisis possesses many forms repressive state activity has become more pronounced especially in aftermath of war terror racist repression in particular has intensified as vast majority of oppressed communities particularly black workers have seen their labor become disposable in a postindustrial society nearly black americans are killed every year by law enforcement all over country war black and indigenous peoples that laid foundation of united states has only become more severe as evidenced by fact that one of every eight prisoners in world a black american dakota access pipeline struggle has shown that not even concentration camps forced upon indigenous people are safe from profitseeking tentacles of crisisridden system and every american can guarantee that civil liberties are a thing of past nsa fbi and rest of intelligence community possess access to entire populations mail and phone devices a massive surveillance dragnet accountable to no one but ruling class allows us state to keep tabs whoever resists conditions of crisis war home ultimately a reflection of broader war being waged around world us capitalist system a global system with largest military state in human history war has thus played a critical role in response to system crisis us military acts as enforcement arm of neocolonialism and capitalist exploitation around world it has expanded into nearly every african state through us african command africom us military state continues to support fascism in ukraine and fundamentalist islam in places like saudi arabia and qatar it has destabilized a number of nations in last decade alone including iraq afghanistan and libya us has collaborated with nato israel and turkey to militarily encircle russia and china militarily and sponsor terror groups responsible for massacres in syria us imperialist system predicated expansion of global capital by any means its disposal including use military force to clear way for corporate plunder but us military in crisis too it plagued by a disillusioned rank and file and inevitability of a global confrontation with russia and china if it continues current course demands of a stagnating global capitalist economy and everincreasing exploitation of masses of working people offer no potential for a reversal of fortune us imperialist system predicated expansion of global capital by any means its disposal including use military force to clear way for corporate plunder us military state has grown both in size and in violence in order to prevent global shift of power currently underway russia and china have become number one challengers to us global hegemony chinas economy will soon surpass that of us and russias recovery from postsoviet collapse has propelled putinled nation back onto global scene as a major factor in world affairs these two powers are becoming increasingly close both economically and militarily this has made us ruling class increasingly nervous in midst of economic decline to maintain hegemony us military state set world ablaze through endless war in every region of world that dares to seek ties with russia and china this point us imperialist system cannot peacefully compete in any way with its socalled rivals to east contradictions of system have become unmanageable unemployment poverty racist state repression and war are all system has to offer another economic collapse horizon crisis built into global capitalist systems constant drive to accumulate profit in face of global misery decline of us imperialism and empire will not change regardless of election what sure to change mass reaction to decline as life becomes more and more unbearable under grip of empire danny haiphong an asian activist and political analyst in boston area he can be reached email protected

bill white november ways to prepare for an economic crisis using past economic collapses as an example can see that people s lifestyles changed dramatically even those who managed to keep their jobs and businesses have to make radical adjustments in their lies just to be able to survive there no reason for us to think that things will be any different here in united states than they were in argentina in fact they could very well end up being worse reason say it could be worse that there will be nobody to bail out united states as has been done with other countries know from housing collapse that anything negative that happens in us economy has a worldwide effect since other countries will end up suffering as well there no way that they will be able to help us liberals have touted idea of redistributing wealth of wealthy in order to take care of country s woes m not going to discuss morality or ideology of that right now but will say this if were to take all wealth of richest people in united states and add it together it wouldn t pay federal government s bills from january st till tax day of course since their wealth isn t really in cash but rather in ownership of properties and companies there s no way of using it to pay government s costs or debts other thing that could make collapse worse here in united states that most americans aren t prepared to live without all of comforts if go to other countries people are more accustomed to doing things themselves instead of expecting society to do them they know how to do basic things like slaughter a hog and pluck a chicken things that average american hasn t had to do for generations here are a number of lifestyle changes can help family to be ready to survive meltdown pay off home home mortgages are dangerous in a financial crisis if don t have enough income coming in to make payment home then could very easily lose it no matter how prepared are if don t have home re going to be in trouble there are a number of strategies around for paying off home mortgage early won t go into them here because this really isn t a book about personal finances can find necessary information how to pay off home early in a number of places highly recommend looking into dave ramsey s teachings subject another option may want to consider downsizing by selling existing home and moving into something smaller might be able to reduce mortgage payments or even length of mortgage that would help to get rid of mortgage sooner in case of financial crash coming before manage to pay it off very likely payments will be smaller making it easier for to keep making those payments pay off all other debts any debt a liability enslaving family s finances to others by paying off outstanding debt eliminate risk of lenders coming to take what have while other debt not as important as home mortgage paying it off can make it easier to pay off mortgage quicker paying off debt also reduces monthly cost of living freeing up more money for use in preparing for pending crash or some other activity family wants to do vast majority of americans have too much debt greatly limits their options and decisions that they can make learn to do things without electricity so much of modern lifestyle depends upon electricity are used to using it for literally everything from preserving food to entertaining us however loss of electricity a common problem during times of economic meltdown oh electricity probably won t go out and stay out but can count a lot of service interruptions when service lost many of things take for granted are lost as well ability to store and cook food compromised as well as ability to work lighting gone as well as most of communications for many people loss of electricity means loss of being able to work as well for everything use in life that electric powered need an alternative that means having something that can use to do same job should power go out in some cases might be able to do without that thing but need to analyze that and make that determination not just accept it as an assumption redo budget probably one of best things can do to prepare for a financial meltdown to redo budget establishing a more frugal lifestyle chances are when economic collapse happens re going to have to be living that more frugal lifestyle by establishing it ahead of time not only train yourself and family to be more careful of how use money but also save money can then use to buy and stockpile necessary supplies many people today live from paycheck to paycheck that doesn t mean that they re using their money wisely though their budget may include eating out three times a week spending per month their cell phones and another per month entertainment yet they complain about not having enough money to buy some basic emergency supplies there are a lot of places where average family spends more than they need to buying new cars another one banks and auto industry make a lot of money off of families who are making payments two cars a time for some their combined car payments are higher than their house payment while reliable vehicles are a necessity having two car payments every month isn t it would be better to buy older cars and not have those high payments to make eat healthy how can eating healthy be part of preparing for a financial meltdown easy most expensive things that most of us eat are junk food as a nation spend a fortune prepared foods snack foods and sweets when financial meltdown comes probably won t be able to afford all that junk ll end up eating much simpler foods carry more nutrition same time eating all that junk food not good for health medical expenses can be extremely high especially for those who have ignored eating healthy while it may sound a bit extreme eating healthy can make difference between life and death by helping protect from a lifethreatening medical problem get in shape this one goes handinhand with eating healthy people who are in good physical condition are much less likely to have medical problems especially high blood pressure diabetes and high cholesterol all of can become lifethreatening however there s another part to this as well that preparing body for physical rigors of survival living without all modern conveniences requires much more physical work than living with them that s why so many of us are out of shape ve become accustomed to allowing machines to do things that used to do ve become softer many of us can t do physical work necessary for surviving without all those modern machines find likeminded people many experts survival and preparedness recommend banding together with other likeminded people and forming a prepping community idea that in wake of a disaster community would gather in one place to live and work together each member of team would have their assigned area of responsibility based upon their unique skills there are many advantages of working together in a prepping community right community can increase chances of survival however wrong one can cause severe problems with people leaving group and taking most of supplies with them be careful what group join investigating morals and personalities of people first to make an informed decision about whether group will stick together to help each other or become selfish and steal from each other ancestors survived harsh times and their secrets can help survive during most treacherous conditions if world struck with a tragedy bill white for survivopedia total views views today

sufficient in today s political climate none of this surprising trump s a tv man with a gift for selfpromotion his entire career has been preparation for this moment this campaign he saw broken perverted process with clear eyes and he s exploited it with aplomb have to give him credit for that while republican party ultimately responsible for trump they welcomed him into their big tent after all media equally culpable for calamity that his candidacy every american entitled to be as undiscerning and uncritical as they like there s nothing in social contract that demands voters educate themselves a democracy for most part can tolerate its share of credulous citizens but media has a special obligation in a free society it s only profession protected by us constitution for a reason it s a check power and a gadfly for crooked politicians and abusers of power media has failed spectacularly this election it trump s campaign may not be good for america said les moonves ceo and out an interviewer offering noncommittal soundbites and incoherent rejoinders until he hears phrase let s move he takes advantage of slipshod shallow techniques journalism has made routine writes that s why interviewers must do their homework and be prepared to go least questions deep any issue great idea but it s difficult to dive deep when interviewers are forced to shoehorn serious questions in limited time between competing toothpaste commercials trump gets away with this because of mutually beneficial relationship he has with press he knows people like moonves are interested in selling penis pill ads not informing electorate voters for their part are happy to project whatever they want onto empty vessel that trump meanwhile truth an afterthought and whole sordid circus continues unimpeded gitlin urges journalists to honor good name of their profession and take off kid gloves sound advice but m not holding breath

and said elie life and work inspires and gives meaning to words never again wish could promise elie that lessons of history have been learned can only urge leaders of world not to repeat mistakes of deems israeli demand unrealistic netanyahu who has hinted prospect of unilateral strikes as a last resort iranian nuclear sites told lawmakers israel would stand alone if needed but he made no threat by claire bernish thursday police from no less than five states sporting full riot gear and armed with heavy lethal and nonlethal weaponry pepper spray mace a number of atvs five tanks two helicopters and militaryequipped humvees showed up to tear down an encampment of standing rock sioux water protectors and supporters armed with nothing under orders from nownotorious morton county sheriff s office this ridiculously heavyhanded standing army came better prepared to do battle than some actual military units fighting overseas but target of their operation a group of slightly more than native american water protectors and supporters opposing construction of dakota access pipeline never intended to do battle with armed taxpayerfunded corporatebacked statesponsored aggressors reports vary but no less than people were arrested thursday and according to witnesses police marked numbers arrestees arms and housed them in cementfloored dog kennels without any padding before they were transported as far away as fargo it goes back to concentration camp days asserted ocetisakowin coordinator mekasi camphorinek who along with his mother was marked and detained in a mesh kennel reports los angeles times although thursday s incident remained relatively peaceful for some time with only shouts chants and occasional attempts by water protectors to convince this standing army to examine its motives and reconsider clashes nonetheless broke out solely because of gratuitous police aggression after facing off for a couple hours these militant cops began closing in water protectors to shut down treaty of camp in reference to fort laramie treaty of that year established a large parcel of land designated exclusively native american territory not to be disturbed by us government prior to his arrest camphorinek had established camp stating as cited by indigenous rising today oceti sakowin has enacted eminent domain dapl lands claiming treaty rights this unceded land highway as of this point blockaded will be occupying this land and staying here until this pipeline permanently stopped need bodies and need people who are trained in nonviolent direct action are still staying nonviolent and are still staying peaceful despite water protectors commitment to nonviolence militarized police response went as would be expected horribly awry a prayer circle of elders including several women was interrupted and all were arrested for standing peacefully public road stated a press release from indigenous environment network a tipi was erected in road and was recklessly dismantled despite law enforcement statements that they would merely mark tipi with a yellow ribbon and ask its owners to retrieve it a group of water protectors was also dragged out of a sweat lodge ceremony erected in path of pipeline thrown to ground and arrested claims to contrary by morton county sheriff kyle kirchmeier aside native american and indigenous water protectors and supporters have refrained from violent acts whole preferring instead peaceful prayer vigils and acts of civil disobedience no matter how peacefully opposition acts armed defenders of big oil interests seem determined to brutalize disrespect and generally incite and inflict violence against those who desire unsullied water for generations to come in fact beginning of september a private security firm hired by energy transfer partners company responsible for pipeline construction indiscriminately unleashed vicious attack dogs water protectors press and supporters for reasons as yet unknown during savage attack a pregnant woman young girl and many others suffered serious dog bites thanks to ineptitude of dogs handlers afterward a warrant for inciting a riot was issued democracy now journalist amy goodman for doing her job filming events as they happened though charges were subsequently thrown out although etp and some law enforcement officers defended barbarous actions of private security mercenaries guardian now reports that because guards lacked proper licensing they could now face criminal charges wednesday morton county sheriff s office made determination that dog handlers were not properly licensed to do security work in state of north dakota bob frost owner of ohiobased frost kennels told guardian all proper protocols were already done pulled guys out next day because weren t there to go to war with these protesters frost insisted he had cooperated with authorities investigating incident but sheriff s department disagrees seven handlers and dogs were deployed to scene in early september allegedly in response to reports of trespassers but according to guardian police have only managed to identify two people sheriff s department claims frost has not provided necessary information and unnamed security officials cited in report said that there were no intentions of using dogs or handlers for security work however because of protest events dogs were deployed as a method of trying to keep protesters under control in a statement cited by guardian morton county captain jay gruebele said although lists of security employees have been provided there no way of confirming whether list accurate or if names have been purposely withheld water protectors in meantime are left to deal with absurdly disproportionate state violence and altogether unacceptable disrespectful and demeaning insult of being relegated to dog kennels after being arrested for exercising their rights as lakota country times editor brandon ecoffey wrote in an editorial thursday over course of last several months abuse of detainees by morton county law enforcement has overstepped every boundary guaranteed by american constitution water protectors have been seen being bound and hooded by police people are being stripped searched and abused within their jail for misdemeanor crimes and police have employed use of mass surveillance through drones protector camps this isn t a war zone this north dakota claire bernish writes for thefreethoughtprojectcom where this article first appeared share

chief that citizens have a heightened duty to show up to cast their ballot against him some trump supporters presumably including those who chant lock her up gop rallies feel same way about hillary clinton lets be clear elections aren t just about who votes but who doesn t vote first lady michelle obama said last week a clinton campaign rally la salle university in philadelphia with a cautionary message aimed millennials and if vote for someone other than hillary or if don t vote all then are helping to elect hillary s opponent and stakes are far too high to take that chance too high some among antihillary brigades have decided in deference to their exquisite sensibilities to stay home election day rather than vote for mrs clinton she wrote in fridays wall street journal but she warned her election alone what stands between american nation and reign of most unstable proudly uninformed psychologically unfit president ever to enter white house team trump new jersey gov chris christie and former new york mayor rudy giuliani are among those who argue it clinton whose election would pose a threat to republic and some republicans who oppose trump make case for sending a message by supporting a thirdparty candidate or not voting all former florida governor jeb bush vanquished by trump in gop primaries told reporters thursday after delivering a guest lecture harvard university that he planned to vote but not for trump or clinton if everybody didnt vote that would be a pretty powerful political statement wouldnt it he said trumps temperament criticism of muslimamerican gold star parents a latenight twitter storm against a former beauty queen and policy views that include skepticism of nato alliance and admiration for russian president vladimir putin have fueled alarm even among some lifelong republicans about whether he should be trusted with nations highest office former president george hw bush and presidential nominee mitt romney are among those who have said they wont vote for trump but concerns about clinton including questions about her honesty and trustworthiness have complicated calculations by some voters about just whom to support all that has boosted libertarian candidate gary johnson whose level of support in realclearpolitics average of recent nationwide surveys higher than any thirdparty candidate election day since ross perot in when both majorparty candidates get negative ratings from most americans will more voters just stay home inspirational contenders tend to do best in drawing voters to polls highest rate of turnout since world war ii was of votingeligible population in when a youthful massachusetts senator john f kennedy narrowly won white house after decades of moreorless steady decline it spiked again to in when illinois senator barack obama was elected nations first africanamerican president but scholars say fear can be as compelling as hope fear a pretty big motivator just as enthusiasm says michael dimmock president of nonpartisan pew research center has studied voters and nonvoters turnout in presidential elections tends to dip in years such as and when voters dont see stakes as particularly high or differences between two major candidates as particularly sharp neither factor would apply this time and high levels of interest this year may signal higher turnout in a pew study released this summer more than eight in said they were following news about candidates closely highest level of interest in a quarter century eight in said they had thought quite a lot about election three of four said it really matters who wins that said twothirds called tone of campaign too negative and only four in were satisfied with their choices lowest level in two decades just one in said either candidate would make a good president four in said neither would weve never had candidates like this for president says michael mcdonald a political scientist university of florida who heads united states elections project he turns to senate races for comparison citing delaware contest when democrat chris coons crushed republican christine odonnell she was a controversial tea partybacked contender who among other things aired a tv ad denying she was a witch despite a contest that wasnt seen as competitive turnout in first state was among highest in country last year guess people are going to be activated to vote against these candidates mcdonald says they may not like their particular candidate but they certainly dont like other partys candidate its not how much do like these people says jan leighley an american university professor and coauthor of who votes now demographics issues inequality and turnout in united states its does it make a difference between this person do not like opposed to that person do not like this year trump has heightened that issue with rhetoric that seems outside scope of reasonable conversation or respectable public dialogue she says if he continues to do that she says question for voters could be this one being irresponsible by not casting a ballot in first tentative clues about turnout this year mcdonald says requests for absentee ballots in congressional districts in iowa maine and north carolina likely to favor clinton have been running slightly ahead of requests this point four years ago requests in districts in those three states likely to favor trump have been running slightly lower most americans think theres an obligation to vote says jason brennan a georgetown university professor and author of ethics of voting though percentages may be inflated because they think thats what theyre supposed to say he doesnt agree likening it to a lottery when an individual ticket or vote isnt likely to make a difference that said he generally does vote himself a look demographics of those least likely to vote explains why democrats are more focused turnout efforts than republicans a majority of eligible latino voters and of voters under didnt go to polls in both groups support clinton over trump and they make up significant blocs in some battleground states millennials in colorado and virginia hispanics in florida and nevada clinton has vowed to encourage million new americans to register to vote before nov she and her top campaign surrogates increasingly are focused registering voters before state deadlines loom and encouraging supporters to cast early ballots that said trump has done well among another group of voters not inclined to cast ballots those who dont have a college education only a third of eligible voters who dont have a high school diploma voted in and less than half of those with only a high school education did so most nonvoters stay away from polls not because they are making an ideological statement but because they say they are too busy or they dont like either candidate or they dont think it matters if they vote as a group they are younger less educated and less affluent than voters they express less interest in politics and public affairs although a usa todaysuffolk university poll of unlikely voters in found twothirds of them said they were registered they are much more likely than voters to favor an activist government eight in said government plays an important role in their lives and usa today survey found they supported obama over romney by more than a far wider margin than not challenge those state laws and said that in prosecutions it would prioritize marijuana offenses such as distribution to minors asked about her own views marijuana lynch said she does not support washington two capitol hill panels that police ethics of members of congress appear to be battling about has authority over lawmakers accused of unwittingly accepting improper travel and gifts from state oil company of azerbaijan a washington post story published wednesday detailed contents of a confidential office of congressional ethics oce investigation of a trip to azerbaijan a former republic of soviet union in central asia oce was created in to vet ethics cases and recommend action to ethics committee ethics committee retains sole authority to judge whether members have broken rules and to mete out punishment committee apparently asked oce to drop this case there no evidence committee has ever made such a request before oce report indicated that members of congress had improperly accepted travel and gifts from stateowned oil company of azerbaijan secretly paid for trip and not nonprofit groups that purportedly sponsored it lawmakers said they had no idea nonprofits were not true sponsors of trip and apparently neither did ethics committee approved members travel in advance report found no evidence that lawmakers made any effort to aid an azeri pipeline project that stateowned oil company was pushing time rep gregory meeks dny one of lawmakers involved in case said ethics committee asked oce to stop its investigation of trip because committee had started one of its own oce declined however post reported such a refusal pretty outrageous said chris delacy a partner law firm holland knight who represents lawmakers in ethics cases its like weve generated two separate ethics committees and oce has gone rogue oce report indicates that meeks did not cooperate with its investigation according to post but meeks office says that not true meeks gave oce documents and other information in response to its request said spokeswoman sophia lafargue but oce did not tell him that ethics committee had asked it to end its review despite house rules requiring it do so this why he would not be interviewed by oce and this why oce said in leaked report that he declined to cooperate lafargue said congressman meeks committed to cooperate with ethics committee in its review of this matter house resolution that created oce states that when it notified that ethics committee investigating a case board shall refer such matter to committee and cease its preliminary or secondphase review and so notify any individual who subject of review oces own rules however state it will stop its investigation when ethics committee starts an investigatory subcommittee committees rarely used formal investigative process committee makes a public announcement when it creates those subcommittees and has not announced one for azerbaijan trip oce may draw a lot of flak for azerbaijan case but process worked as it should except for leak said craig holman government affairs lobbyist liberalleaning watchdog group public citizen oce investigated and reported to committee holman said adding that if committee prevailed upon oce to not do a report case probably would have been buried report shows a real concern holman said use of bogus nonprofits by a foreign government to circumvent congressional travel rules holman said dispute between two bodies reminiscent of early days of oce when two publicly feuded repeatedly over jurisdiction and rules of investigations but he said do not see it escalating to point saw in very beginning case also involves first leak of a full oce report before its public release has led lawyers who defend lawmakers to call entire process unfair there have been more ethics case since oce started delacy said a trend for office deserves some credit but if intent of law was to spur for ethics committee investigations and committee has launched a probe in a case there no reason for oce to keep investigating earlier this year house changed its own rules to give lawmakers a new defense against ethics investigations language stated that two ethics bodies may not take any action that would deny any person any right or protection provided under constitution of united states an apparent reference to longsimmering concerns that oce does not allow subjects of investigations to see evidence against them time elliot berke a lawyer who has defended several republicans in ethics cases said from day one of oces existence there have been serious concerns about lack of due process oce report apparently found that members who traveled to azerbaijan meeks jim bridenstine rokla yvette clarke dny danny davis dill rubén hinojosa dtexas sheila jackson lee dtexas leonard lance rnj michelle lujan grisham dnm ted poe rtexas and thencongressman steve stockman rtexas were unaware that trip was improperly funded by a foreign government nevertheless in similar past cases ethics committee has required lawmakers to pay back costs of travel for this trip would total thousands of dollars for each traveler in past cases lawmakers have been allowed to use campaign money instead of their personal funds to pay these costs airfare for members and their spouses totaled more than post reported

w bush joined campaign trail in south carolina to help his brother jeb trump went right after bushs war in iraq and blasted it as a mistake all of this talk seems to be just fine with many republicans nashville tennessee cnn as opposition to common core began to swell in tennessee jeb bush showed up to an education forum in march of last year advocating for state officials to keep higher standards in place now a little more than a year later bush returned to nashville saturday night to address states gop dinner but his push for state to hold onto common core didnt succeed earlier this month republican gov bill haslam signed a bill to review controversial standards and rebrand them with a tennesseespecific focus bill was widely viewed as a compromise between common core opponents who wanted to get rid of it altogether and supporters including haslam who wanted to stay track with new assessments what happened in tennessee has already happened in a number of states as political momentum against common core has caught fire in past two years fueled by an onslaught of criticism from conservatives who decry standards as federal overreach and with new jersey gov chris christie a oncestaunch enthusiast of common core announcing this week that his state will gut standards and come up with something else bush who has yet to declare his allbutcertain candidacy remains only toptier presidential hopeful who still defending common core that doesnt mean he takes issue with states doing what tennessee and new jersey are attempting hes made it clear that hes fine with states choosing an alternative path as long as those states maintain high benchmarks for students think governor haslam has every right to do anything as it relates to education policy bush told reporters saturday night with haslam standing by his side this should be a state issue and washington should have nothing to do with this all still bush unlike other ers not backing away from his endorsement of common core as an effective method of measuring student learning his brother after all pushed and ultimately signed no child left behind in early imposed test score standards schools with lowerincome students that received federal funding as for common core jeb bush has expressed frustration with how standards have been prone to misinformation that have in part helped turn issue into a political football because people have a different view of what common core am supposed to back away from something that know works he asked same event in bedford as twoterm governor of florida from bush focused heavily education reform included implementing higher standards as well as a school voucher program after he left office he launched a nonprofit called foundation for excellence in education that ultimately became a leading proponent of common core contrary to what some believe common core standards were not developed by federal government but by national governors association along with help from state education leaders parents and teachers standards dictate what students in elementary school and high school should know by end of each grade and its up to states to come up with testing and curriculum that align with those measurements federal government got involved when it gave states financial incentives to adopt higher standards those standards didnt have to be common core but since standards were available states have adopted them since though some have since changed or eliminated them while common core has sometimes been misunderstood perception reality in politics said gregory gleaves a republican strategist in tennessee if perception out there that it comes from dc then it a political problem and that was case for those who supported common core despite praising it during his first term christie thursday noted that hes heard constant complaints from parents and educators felt like had to give it a fair chance think did weve given it a fouryear chance he said they feel as if its been imposed upon them from washington wisconsin gov scott walker and louisiana gov bobby jindal also potential republican presidential contenders were initially board with common core and helped implement standards during their first terms but later moved to get rid of them jindal especially has become one of most vocal opponents of standards and a lawsuit he launched against federal government over common core got a hearing in a baton rouge court thursday thats why some states renamed standards to give them a more local feel iowa for example named it iowa core this method was encouraged last year by former arkansas gov mike huckabee who reportedly told state education officials to lose common core name rebrand it refocus it but dont retreat for his part bush has politely tried to correct some misunderstandings about common core trail without trying to get to deep in weeds an event in manchester a retired math teacher told bush that he got frustrated with spending more time testing than teaching bush explained that tests are only administered state and local level not by federal government and proposed that states should mandate that school districts tell parents why they are giving so many tests that would better facilitate a conversation between parents and educators how to strike a balance and thats way it should be can do that in new hampshire he said urging man john potucek whos also a state representative to push back state about his complaints but please make sure have accountability around students cause net result youre going to have a decline after event potucek told cnn he was surprised by bushs answer and while he didnt agree with everything bush said he said his common core stance wouldnt be a dealbreaker he explained it in a way that wasnt expecting and have to kind of chew it a little bit he said it doesnt always go well though a town hall in dubuque iowa a retired local school board member named les feldman grilled bush about his support for common core after a tense backandforth that lasted several minutes a somewhat exasperated bush ultimately concluded with im just for higher standards man people want a clear yes or no answer while opponents dislike common core for a number of reasons republican primary voters mostly disagree with it because they see it as federal overreach into what should be a local issue andy ogles tennessee state director for conservative group americans for prosperity said a onesizefitsall approach to education doesnt work education needs in silicon valley versus rural iowa versus tennessee are very different he said ogles lead a grassroots campaign that helped mobilize opposition to common core in tennessee and pressure state officials to search for a different approach as for bush ogles said he doubts conservative voters will buy likely presidential candidates argument that he supports both common core as well as a states decision to opt out and choose their own standards theres certain core issues that most people want a clear yes or no answer and think this one of those issues cant be for it and then also be for state standards he said ultimately hes going to have to make up his mind especially here in southern states aides say theres no conflict in bushs position states had to opt in and they can opt out bottom line bush has argued that students need better accountability and there are a variety of ways to make that happen with higher standards theres got to be a better way bush said an event in manchester new hampshire earlier this month saying only half of high school graduates are college or careerready cant just keep fooling and just say oh its because kids in poverty cant learn thats crap a man in audience interjected thank brother bush said forcefully that crap
