hillary rodham clinton s tenure as secretary of state was supposed to be a central argument for her forthcoming run for president her globetrotting record as nation s chief diplomat her role championing women s empowerment and gay rights and her experience tough national security issues were all supposed to confer credentials that none of her possible gop opponents would possess but over past two weeks with backtoback revelations that she was working with foreign countries that gave millions of dollars to her family s charitable foundation and that she set up and exclusively used a private email system that argument has been put in peril instead of a fresh chapter in clinton came into her own her time as country s top diplomat now threatens to remind voters of what some people dislike about her a tendency toward secrecy and defensiveness along with whiff of scandal that clouded presidency of her husband bill clinton that side of hillary clinton also plays directly into main republican argument against her that she a candidate of yesterday as sen marco rubio of florida recently put it who comes with decades of baggage country no longer need carry part of reason story gaining traction that it reminds people of what clinton white house was like said american university political science professor jennifer lawless it reminds people of scandals secrecy and lack of transparency that were often associated with bill clinton s eight years in washington clinton was already certain to face sharp questions during a presidential campaign about her handling of deadly attacks us facilities in benghazi libya in she has never been shown to have any direct role in events leading up to deaths of us ambassador and three other americans but an inquiry that democrats call a fishing expedition has been given new life by revelation that her emails were not immediately given to congress rep trey gowdy rsc who chairs a special congressional committee benghazi has subpoenaed clinton s emails and plans to call her as a witness once his investigation further along that will mean a showdown in middle of a presidential campaign in she will be trying to reintroduce herself to voters do not need a law degree to have an understanding of how troubling this gowdy said in a news conference last week clinton has said nothing about email controversy beyond a tweet promising to seek to make messages public sunday sen dianne feinstein dcalif said clinton needs to talk in more detail about issue from this point silence going to hurt her feinstein said nbc s meet press clinton s problems state department also make it easier for republicans to connect her to what they see as president obama s shaky foreign policy and his broken promise to operate most transparent administration in history even smallest things are being looked anew iconic image of her state department she chose as her twitter avatar shows her seated aboard a military transport plane reading something perhaps email her blackberry former florida governor jeb bush who weighing a bid cast her reliance private email as a serious security risk it s a little baffling to be honest with that didn t come up in secretary clinton s thought process republican said in a friday radio interview and while clinton remains overwhelming favorite for her party s nomination some democrats last week were more open about their misgivings about her candidacy friday night in new hampshire former maryland governor martin o malley who might run against her for first time broke his silence her use of private email account saying that openness and transparency are required of governing in modern age for clinton state department years were a kind of protective cocoon from partisan politics even though she was a visible member of a democratic administration that was doing regular battle with congressional republicans she was rarely called upon to take public positions partisan issues her history as a politically active first lady senator and failed democratic candidate for president were rarely mentioned in daytoday news coverage of her trips and priorities as secretary clinton s busy pace she visited a record countries made it easy to deflect questions about common assumption that she would make another run for white house when her time state department was over after leaving office she wrote a memoir of her time state department that was published last year book hard choices marked her unmistakable entry into presidential race and amounted to a virtual campaign manifesto have to use all of america s strengths to build a world with more partners and fewer adversaries more shared responsibility and fewer conflicts more good jobs and less poverty more broadly based prosperity with less damage to environment clinton wrote clinton posted a twitter message wednesday night two days into email controversy saying she wants public to see emails she said she has asked state department to review them for release that review could also establish whether she broke any rules about handling of sensitive information email arrangement meant that clinton s work emails were being routed through a private server in her chappaqua ny home and were not being archived by government as now required she handed over pages of emails upon state department s request last year more than a year after she left her post she has not explained reason for un or tho dox arrangement and her husband declined to weigh in sunday m not one to judge that have an opinion but have a bias bill clinton said in response to a reporter s question during a florida appearance according to bloomberg politics he added shouldn t be making news this white house has distanced itself from growing controversy obama said in an interview with cbs saturday that he did not know about hillary clinton s use of private email until reading news reports last week president does have expectation that everybody in his administration takes steps that are necessary to be in compliance with federal records act and with presidential records act white house press secretary josh earnest said friday and again that means using as often as possible using official government email when re conducting official government business white house officials communicated with clinton her private account and it not clear that any white house official flagged practice as a potential problem email revelation came after bill hillary and chelsea clinton foundation acknowledged in february that it had accepted a foreigngovernment donation in without submitting it for an ethics review as required in a agreement with obama administration agreement had been reached to avoid appearance of conflicts of interest while hillary clinton served as secretary of state under agreement foundation was to submit any donation from a foreign government that had not previously given money to foundation goal was to allow ethics officers to analyze whether it might appear that government was trying to influence us policy with a donation to a charity so closely linked to nation s top diplomat foundation told washington post that it had failed to follow that process in case of an unsolicited donation from government of algeria to assist in earthquake relief in haiti donation coincided with a spike in lobbying by algeria was defending its human rights record to us officials state department spokeswoman jen psaki said donation did not pose a problem because us support for rebuilding efforts in haiti was well known before algerian donation came in prominent clinton backers have dismissed recent controversies as minor distractions unimportant to voters and blamed email flap a republican attack machine but other democrats expressed exasperation what they called a slow and hamhanded response to email controversy by her small group of advisers there no inhouse campaigngrade rapidresponse operation and president obama said thursday that he will keep us troops in afghanistan into ending his ambitions to bring home most american forces from that wartorn country before he leaves office president s decision came after an extensive monthslong review that included regular discussions with afghanistan s leaders his national security team and us commanders in field move reflected a painful if predictable reality ground in afghanistan where taliban has seized new territory over past year as afghan troops have taken over vast majority of fighting afghan forces are still not as strong as they need to be obama said thursday morning from white house explaining his decision meanwhile taliban has made gains particularly in rural areas and can still launch deadly attacks in cities including kabul islamic state making these afghans long for taliban obama said he will also dramatically slow pace of reduction of american forces and plans to maintain current us force of through most of post force will still be focused training and advising afghan army with a special emphasis its elite counter terrorism forces united states will also maintain a significant counterterrorism capability of drones and special operations forces to strike alqaeda and other militants who may be plotting attacks against united states revised troop plans came after afghan forces were driven from kunduz first major city to fall to taliban since war began in two weeks passed before afghans with some support from us planes and special operations advisers took city back from taliban militants are now threatening other cities bottom line in key areas of country security situation still very fragile and in some places there s risk of deterioration obama said president praised afghan government under leadership of president ashraf ghani as a willing partner and he lauded afghan troops who have taken significant casualties both were critical factors in his decision to keep us troops in country every single day afghan forces are out there fighting and dying to protect their country obama said they re not looking for us to do it for them president insisted that his decision to abandon his plans to bring home us troops was not a disappointment even as he acknowledged nation s war weariness after more than years of fighting in afghanistan and iraq do not support idea of endless war he said his decision to keep troops in afghanistan follows surprising collapse of much of ustrained iraqi army last year under pressure from islamic state militants republican critics have charged that obama withdrew troops too quickly from iraq precipitating collapse of iraqi army and rise of islamic state president did not mention iraqi failures in his statement from white house white house officials said that iraq setbacks did not influence obama s decision and that two situations are not comparable because afghan government was eager to maintain a longterm us presence such conditions did not exist in iraq afghan officials thursday welcomed move to keep troops in country it s very positive in light of continued problems that this region facing said mohammad daud sultanzoy a presidential candidate in who now allied with ghani security forces have shown will and capability to fight but still need support of allies especially united states in ghazni province where afghan forces are locked in a bloody fight with taliban gen sayed malok called it a good decision moment but a temporary solution he called for a more robust effort to train and equip afghan forces decision a significant departure from exit plan that obama announced in a white house rose garden speech in may in keeping with his promise to turn page costly wars launched by his predecessor obama said then that he would reduce us footprint to about troops all based in kabul by end of it also a stark illustration of how persistent militant threats have stood in way of obama s promises to end ground wars that have dominated us foreign policy since sept terrorist attacks in addition to a resurgent taliban alqaeda appears to have staked out new ground in afghanistan far from group s mountain enclaves to northeast last week us forces began a major operation against alqaeda in kandahar launching airstrikes militant training bases david sedney a former pentagon official who a senior associate center for strategic and international studies said fighting season was most massive fiercest yet taliban are already signaling that next year s offensive will be bigger and bloodier than this year s taliban are bad daesh worse sedney said referring to emerging islamic state presence in afghanistan administration officials portrayed decision as a natural extension of a strategy that was making progress rather than an indication that president s original plan had failed don t think anyone ever intended that job so to speak would be finished despite obama s time table said lisa monaco a senior white house official always said that would continue to have a presence there after kunduz taliban now targeting other afghan cities under new plan us military will retain bases in kabul as planned but also have forces bagram air base and bases outside kandahar and jalalabad largest cities in afghanistan s southern and eastern regions obama emphasized that afghans would continue to take lead role in fighting with americans providing advice and some counterterrorism support from bases outside kabul these bases will give us presence and reach forces require to achieve their mission he said larger force of troops projected to cost about billion a year or about billion more than smaller person kabulbased force would have cost defense secretary ashton b carter speaking pentagon said next president will probably need to make decisions about longterm position of us forces in afghanistan it going to be forever he told reporters can only say this that best estimate now of what should plan for and are planning for and budgeting for for although us deaths have fallen off dramatically in recent years change may also mean more us casualties this year american service members and civilians have been killed in country obama emphasized that relatively small american military presence down from war s peak would not decide war s outcome and he emphasized that peace talks offered sole viable solution to long bloody civil war by now it should be clear to taliban and all who oppose afghanistan s progress only real way to achieve full drawdown of us and foreign troops from afghanistan through a lasting political settlement with afghan government obama said sudarsan raghavan sayed salahuddin and mohammad sharif in kabul contributed to this report in afghanistan art of fighting extremism afghans who once watched war from afar forced to flee as front lines shift afghan forces straining to keep taliban bay

democrats loyal to vice president biden have a threeword response to politicians and pundits who say hillary rodham clinton s performance in tuesday s democratic debate makes a biden campaign less likely not so fast appearing eager to tamp down talk that window of opportunity for vice president to launch a campaign nearly closed after debate loyalists are taking steps to assure potential supporters that there still a path to nomination however competitive and challenging if biden decides to run clearest evidence came friday as international association of fire fighters informed vice president union will endorse him if he enters race according to a source familiar with union s thinking that followed a letter emailed late thursday to biden s political support network by former senator ted kaufman of delaware who said a biden campaign would be optimistic from heart and unscripted if he runs he will run because of his burning conviction that need to fundamentally change balance in economy and political structure to restore ability of middle class to get ahead kaufman wrote even as initial polls showed a bump for clinton after her performance biden s lingering shadow continued to leave a sense of instability over democratic race leading to questions subject for president obama during a friday news conference with south korean president am not going to comment what joe doing or not doing obama said think can direct those questions to very able vice president and in a television interview friday clinton declined to embrace a suggestion from john podesta her senior campaign adviser that biden must make a decision soon because race already engaged that s up to vice president biden she said cnn s lead with jake tapper certainly m not in any way suggesting or recommending that vice president accept any timetable other than one that clicking inside of him he has to make this decision kaufman s words were arguably less significant than author behind them former senator appointed to seat after his exboss became vice president biden s longestserving adviser bridging divide between generation of staffers who worked for biden as a senator in s and a new crop of lieutenants who came up through past decade of family s public service more important kaufman has been frequently portrayed as most reluctant of trio of innercircle aides to vice president to launch a bid and known to be angry frequent portrayals of biden as being brink of announcing his candidacy a note from him to alumni network treated as having more credibility than any leak to national press corps main issue still apparently unresolved remains whether biden and his family feel ready for rigors of a presidential campaign so soon after death of his oldest son beau in may nothing biden has said publicly in recent weeks has suggested an affirmative answer but he has not addressed issue directly since before pope francis s visit to united states last month according to those tracking vice president s deliberations a decision could come any time although only certain timetable one set by deadlines for qualifying for primary and caucus ballots throughout country those deadlines began to take effect end of month outside biden s tight circle of advisers many democrats have long assumed that beyond personal and family considerations biggest factor in vice president s decisionmaking involved clinton s political vulnerability a month ago as controversy over her use of a private email account while serving as secretary of state damaged her image she appeared seriously weakened that prompted calls from nervous democrats or those in party never particularly partial to clinton for biden to jump in family issues weigh heaviest biden as he considers a run but assessments of clinton brightened considerably after she delivered an impressive performance in tuesday s debate with sen bernie sanders ivt former maryland governor martin o malley and two other candidates biden loyalists have tried not to be swept up by shifting assumptions about strength or weakness of democratic frontrunner believing she was never as damaged as some were suggesting a month ago same time they are not persuaded that this week s debate fundamentally changed race in her favor there outside evidence backing up that view including strong interest in sanders social media and fact that senator from vermont raised more than million in hours after debate in recent days biden has kept in touch with leaders of key constituency groups including calls friday morning one recipient of a biden call who requested anonymity to speak freely about private conversation said vice president actively discussed emerging campaign strategy its structure and what constituencies he would win over biden believes he has a persuasive message for democratic voters and would have resources to run a competitive and potentially lengthy campaign call recipient said moreover biden s view of debate this source said was that it confirmed that clinton s current competition subpar best polls and other evidence suggest that if biden was to enter democratic nomination campaign would quickly become a threeway contest clinton would remain favorite but it clear that sanders has more than enough money to continue well beyond four earliest states iowa new hampshire nevada and south carolina biden has told those he has spoken with that he believes he too would have money to remain in race past those states it remains unclear what other unions would follow firefighters in endorsing biden any conversations ve had with vice president would be private harold a schaitberger general president of international association of fire fighters said in a telephone interview friday night none of first four contests appears ready to tip to biden although because he not a declared candidate polls are not reliable but some of those partial to biden have seized a littleremembered fact from to offer hope that early defeats need not drive a candidate out of nomination contest as biden weighs a campaign does he want to be anticlinton in that campaign bill clinton lost of first contests from early february through first days of march he then secured a string of victories in western and southern caucuses and primaries followed by wins in illinois and michigan that effectively broke back of his opponents hillary clinton s next major event will be an appearance thursday before house committee investigating deaths of four americans in benghazi libya in september along with her email account that highly charged appearance has taken a turn in her favor as a result of house majority leader kevin mccarthy rcalif suggesting goal of committee had been to weaken her politically then oct clinton and other democratic candidates will speak jeffersonjackson dinner in iowa a quadrennial proving ground for presidential hopefuls given breadth of organizing that clinton s team has done in hawkeye state biden s advisers assume event will give her an additional boost although sanders will have plenty of supporters there too all that would suggest that biden could delay announcing a decision until after those two events there were many lines in kaufman s letter that suggested biden still wasn t ready to make a decision despite encroaching deadlines know in daily ups and down of political swirl all get bombarded with tactics so sometimes it s good to take a step back and get real again let s stay in touch kaufman wrote if he decides to run will need each and every one of yesterday

genetically modified crops in us fail to deliver expected yields all gov controversy over genetically modified crops has long focused largely unsubstantiated fears that they are unsafe to eat but an extensive examination by new york times indicates that debate has missed a more basic problem genetic modification in united states and canada has not accelerated increases in crop yields or led to an overall reduction in use of chemical pesticides promise of genetic modification was twofold by making crops immune to effects of weedkillers and inherently resistant to many pests they would grow so robustly that they would become indispensable to feeding world s growing population while also requiring fewer applications of sprayed pesticides twenty years ago europe largely rejected genetic modification same time united states and canada were embracing it comparing results two continents using independent data as well as academic and industry research shows how technology has fallen short of promise an analysis by times using un data showed that united states and canada have gained no discernible advantage in yields when measured against western europe a region with comparably modernized agricultural producers like france and germany also a recent national academy of sciences report found there was little evidence that introduction of genetically modified crops in united states had led to yield gains beyond those seen in conventional crops same time herbicide use has increased in united states even as major crops like corn soybeans and cotton have been converted to modified varieties and united states has fallen behind europe s biggest producer france in reducing overall use of pesticides includes both herbicides and insecticides one measure contained in data from us geological survey shows stark difference in use of pesticides since gm crops were introduced in united states two decades ago for crops like corn cotton and soybeans use of toxins that kill insects and fungi has fallen by a third but spraying of herbicides are used in much higher volumes has risen percent by contrast in france use of insecticides and fungicides has fallen percent and herbicide use has decreased percent to learn more

fatal expense of american imperialism by jeffrey d sachs boston globe single most important issue in allocating national resources war versus peace or as macroeconomists put it guns versus butter united states getting this choice profoundly wrong squandering vast sums and undermining national security in economic and geopolitical terms america suffers from what yale historian paul kennedy calls imperial overreach if next president remains trapped in expensive middle east wars budgetary costs alone could derail any hopes for solving vast domestic problems it may seem tendentious to call america an empire but term fits certain realities of us power and how it s used an empire a group of territories under a single power nineteenthcentury britain was obviously an empire when it ruled india egypt and dozens of other colonies in africa asia and caribbean united states directly rules only a handful of conquered islands hawaii puerto rico guam samoa northern mariana islands but it stations troops and has used force to influence who governs in dozens of other sovereign countries that grip power beyond america s own shores now weakening scale of us military operations remarkable us department of defense has as of a inventory military facilities of are in united states are in overseas us territories and are in foreign countries and foreign territories in all regions of world not counted in this list are secret facilities of us intelligence agencies cost of running these military operations and wars they support extraordinary around billion per year or percent of us national income when one adds budgets of pentagon intelligence agencies homeland security nuclear weapons programs in department of energy and veterans benefits billion in annual spending roughly onequarter of all federal government outlays united states has a long history of using covert and overt means to overthrow governments deemed to be unfriendly to us interests following classic imperial strategy of rule through locally imposed friendly regimes in a powerful study of latin america between and for example historian john coatsworth counts cases of successful usled regime change for an average rate of one government overthrow by united states every months for a century and note coatsworth s count does not include failed attempts such as bay of pigs invasion of cuba this tradition of usled regime change has been part and parcel of us foreign policy in other parts of world including europe africa middle east and southeast asia wars of regime change are costly to united states and often devastating to countries involved two major studies have measured costs of iraq and afghanistan wars one by columbia colleague joseph stiglitz and harvard scholar linda bilmes arrived cost of trillion as of a more recent study by cost of war project brown university puts price tag trillion through over a year period trillion amounts to roughly billion per year and more than combined total outlays from to for federal departments of education energy labor interior and transportation and national science foundation national institutes of health and environmental protection agency it nearly a truism that us wars of regime change have rarely served america s security needs even when wars succeed in overthrowing a government as in case of taliban in afghanistan saddam hussein in iraq and moammar khadafy in libya result rarely a stable government and more often a civil war a successful regime change often lights a long fuse leading to a future explosion such as overthrow of iran s democratically elected government and installation of autocratic shah of iran was followed by iranian revolution of in many other cases such as us attempts with saudi arabia and turkey to overthrow syria s bashar alassad result a bloodbath and military standoff rather than an overthrow of government what deep motivation for these profligate wars and for farflung military bases that support them from to superficial answer would have been cold war yet america s imperial behavior overseas predates cold war by half a century back to spanishamerican war in and has outlasted it by another quarter century america s overseas imperial adventures began after civil war and final conquests of native american nations that point us political and business leaders sought to join european empires especially britain france russia and newly emergent germany in overseas conquests in short order america grabbed philippines puerto rico cuba panama and hawaii and joined european imperial powers in knocking doors of china as of s united states was by far world s largest economy but until world war ii it took a back seat to british empire in global naval power imperial reach and geopolitical dominance british were unrivaled masters of regime change for example in carving up corpse of ottoman empire after world war yet exhaustion from two world wars and great depression ended british and french empires after world war ii and thrust united states and russia into forefront as two main global empires cold war had begun economic underpinning of america s global reach was unprecedented as of us output constituted a remarkable percent of global output with soviet union roughly a third of that around percent cold war fed two fundamental ideas that would shape american foreign policy till now first was that united states was in a struggle for survival against soviet empire second was that every country no matter how remote was a battlefield in that global war while united states and soviet union would avoid a direct confrontation they flexed their muscles in hot wars around world that served as proxies for superpower competition over course of nearly a half century cuba congo ghana indonesia vietnam laos cambodia el salvador nicaragua iran namibia mozambique chile afghanistan lebanon and even tiny granada among many others were interpreted by us strategists as battlegrounds with soviet empire often far more prosaic interests were involved private companies like united fruit international and itt convinced friends in high places most famously dulles brothers secretary of state john foster and cia director allen that land reforms or threatened expropriations of corporate assets were dire threats to us interests and therefore in need of usled regime change oil interests in middle east were another repeated cause of war as had been case for british empire from s these wars destabilized and impoverished countries involved rather than settling politics in america s favor wars of regime change were with few exceptions a litany of foreign policy failure they were also extraordinarily costly for united states itself vietnam war was of course greatest of debacles so expensive so bloody and so controversial that it crowded out lyndon johnson s other far more important and promising war war poverty in united states end of cold war in should have been occasion for a fundamental reorientation of us gunsversusbutter policies occasion offered united states and world a peace dividend opportunity to reorient world and us economy from war footing to sustainable development indeed rio earth summit in established sustainable development as centerpiece of global cooperation or so it seemed alas blinders and arrogance of american imperial thinking prevented united states from settling down to a new era of peace as cold war was ending united states was beginning a new era of wars this time in middle east united states would sweep away sovietbacked regimes in middle east and establish unrivalled us political dominance or least that was plan quarter century since has therefore been marked by a perpetual us war in middle east one that has destabilized region massively diverted resources away from civilian needs toward military and helped to create mass budget deficits and buildup of public debt imperial thinking has led to wars of regime change in afghanistan iraq libya yemen somalia and syria across four presidencies george hw bush bill clinton george w bush and barack obama same thinking has induced united states to expand nato to russia s borders despite fact that nato s supposed purpose was to defend against an adversary soviet union that no longer exists former soviet president mikhail gorbachev has emphasized that eastward nato expansion was certainly a violation of spirit of those declarations and assurances that were given in regarding future of eastwest security there a major economic difference however between now and much less start of cold war in united states produced around percent of world output as of when dick cheney and paul wolfowitz dreams of us dominance were taking shape united states accounted for around percent of world production by now according to imf estimates us share percent while china has surpassed united states around percent by according to projections by international monetary fund united states will produce roughly percent of global output compared with china s percent united states incurring massive public debt and cutting back urgent public investments home in order to sustain a dysfunctional militarized and costly foreign policy thus comes a fundamental choice united states can vainly continue neoconservative project of unipolar dominance even as recent failures in middle east and america s declining economic preeminence guarantee ultimate failure of this imperial vision if as some neoconservatives support united states now engages in an arms race with china are bound to come up short in a decade or two if not sooner costly wars in middle east even if continued much less enlarged in a hillary clinton presidency could easily end any realistic hopes for a new era of scaledup federal investments in education workforce training infrastructure science and technology and environment far smarter approach will be to maintain america s defensive capabilities but end its imperial pretensions this in practice means cutting back farflung network of military bases ending wars of regime change avoiding a new arms race especially in nextgeneration nuclear weapons and engaging china india russia and other regional powers in steppedup diplomacy through united nations especially through shared actions un s sustainable development goals including climate change disease control and global education many american conservatives will sneer very thought that united states room for maneuver should be limited in slightest by un but think how much better off united states would be today had it heeded un security council s wise opposition to wars of regime change in iraq libya and syria many conservatives will point to vladimir putin s actions in crimea as proof that diplomacy with russia useless without recognizing that it was nato s expansion to baltics and its invitation to ukraine to join nato that was a primary trigger of putin s response in end soviet union bankrupted itself through costly foreign adventures such as invasion of afghanistan and its vast overinvestment in military today united states has similarly overinvested in military and could follow a similar path to decline if it continues wars in middle east and invites an arms race with china it s time to abandon reveries burdens and selfdeceptions of empire and to invest in sustainable development home and in partnership with rest of world jeffrey d sachs university professor and director of center for sustainable development columbia university and author of age of sustainable development

donald trump has summoned a tornado of negative stories that threaten to rip his campaign from its foundation if he doesnt stop supporters inside and outside trumps orbit are warning several top backers including republican national committee chairman reince priebus former house speaker newt gingrich and former new york mayor rudy giuliani are trying to persuade trump to move past his feud with parents of late iraq war soldier humayun khan stop bashing fellow republicans like house speaker paul ryan and sen john mccain and refocus his attacks hillary clinton but hopes arent high among republican allies that trump can make such a fundamental change this point and trump campaign publicly denies that any intervention occurring all reality that dont think anyone has any influence over trump a party source told nbc news within gop strategists are increasingly wondering what point candidates will aggressively break with trump or party will divert resources from presidential race to head off a collapse down ticket they dont want to but hes forcing peoples hands gop strategist ryan williams told nbc news williams described campaign as freewheeling and careening from one selfinflicted controversy to another trumps allies within party can complain but they cant say they werent warned trump has made outrageous and offensive statements from minute he announced his campaign last year and he slimed republican rivals every step along way hes resisted previous entreaties from party elders to change course after calling mexican immigrants rapists proposing a ban muslim visitors to united states linking sen ted cruzs father to john f kennedy assassination and bringing up a federal judges mexican heritage as proof of bias he lacks any kind of selfcontrol a gop operative said thats not opinion its well demonstrated as trump regularly reminds voters rallies however hes also survived numerous socalled crises to reach his current position but general election isnt primaries and timing and intensity of current episode stand out cnbcs john harwood quoted an unnamed ally of campaign manager paul manafort tuesday night who called mood suicidal and said manafort was mailing it in after concluding that trump was incapable of taking his advice another source inside campaign told nbc news that harwoods account was all true and that environment was way worse than people realize lending particular urgency to this weeks chaos trumps declining position in polls against clinton who appears to be enjoying a major bump from last weeks democratic convention surveys indicate clinton not only eliminating trumps gains from his own convention but also regaining solid lead she held before fbi director james comeys criticism of her email practices depressed her support most recent nbc newssurveymonkey poll puts him behind clinton percent to percent with registered voters a fox news poll released wednesday gave clinton a percentto percent lead convention bounces are sometimes fleeting but once race settles there are few opportunities for candidates to make up ground making trumps missteps especially dangerous olympics begin this weekend making it harder for trump to win free media for much of month he could make it up by buying tv time but clinton campaign and allied groups have reserved million in ads through november compared to less than million trumps side less likely trump looks to win and more toxic he looks with key voting groups more pressure republican candidates in competitive races will face to denounce him if trump wants us to make a choice between senators and representatives and him it wont be him another gop operative in a swing state told nbc news but its a delicate balance if republican candidates abandon trump en masse party operatives warn depressed voters might stay home and leave federal and state candidates vulnerable to an electoral wipeout while republican candidates in some swing states have polled ahead of trump by distancing themselves from his more controversial positions they still need conservatives to show up and split their ticket to prevail so far few highprofile republicans running for office have repudiated trump even sen kelly ayotte of new hampshire sticking by him despite trumps recent insults and ayottes longtime discomfort with his rhetoric further trump sinks however harder it becomes to hold everyone together a veteran gop strategist suggested that party should be able to win competitive house and senate races with smart campaigns if trump trailing clinton by to points but if he starts to trail by double digits in polls as he did in foxs survey it could create a panic as republicans rush to disavow his candidacy in hope that they might survive coming disaster publicly trump and his top aides argue that talk of campaign troubles are overblownwere great its a terrible week for hillary clinton campaign spokesman jason miller told nbc news with a smile wednesday trump miller said has always been antiestablishment candidate his refusal to support ryan against a protrump primary opponent only reinforced that brand and campaign has had some good news mixed with bad this week it raised an impressive million in campaign contributions along with party allies short of clintons million but a strong improvement for a campaign that built its fundraising operation from scratch after trump largely selffunded during primaries some also note that clinton and other democrats are vulnerable to negative stories wall street journal reported wednesday that obama administration shipped million in cash to iran as part of a deal over sanctions same time iran released americans it had detained state department said that payment was unrelated and that negotiations were kept separate but republicans accused it of paying a ransom fact checkers have also pilloried clinton this week for claiming that fbi corroborated her previous defenses of her email practices but other campaign aides expressed concern that trump failed to capitalize democratic weaknesses or emphasize his core strengths because hes too often distracted by side stories and settling scores within gop weve got to get back to these basic issues that got him this far former trump adviser barry bennett told msnbc trumps afternoon speech wednesday in daytona beach fla demonstrated difficulty he began with a long and focused recounting of iran story and clintons statements her use of a private email server he also praised sen marco rubio for endorsing him extending an olive branch to a onetime critic after having baited ryan and mccain day before campaign doing really well trump said its never been so well united but usual trump still nursing past grudges and falling into bizarre tangents wasnt far behind after discussing iran he turned to his old feud with megyn kelly in he said fox news host had blood coming out of her wherever trump who made initial comment one year ago this week told crowd that perverted democrats wrongly portrayed it as a comment menstruation in attack ads when in fact he meant her nose her ears her mouth despite his outward confidence trump has sounded more concerned about his position in several interviews and speeches hes made unsubstantiated claims that election could be rigged against him raising concerns that he might be laying groundwork to delegitimize a clinton presidency after a loss wouldnt that be embarrassing to lose to crooked hillary clinton trump said in florida that would be terrible

confusion reigned budapests main railway station thursday morning after hungarian police allowed hundreds of migrants to enter building but countrys railway operator said that no direct trains would depart for western europe intended destination for many refugees a reuters photographer estimated that up to migrants from middle east africa and asia had poured into keleti railway station from square outside photographer also reported that some people had stormed first trains they could find and were trying to push themselves and their children into carriages through doors and windows associated press reported that announcements were made over station loudspeakers in several languages including english that trains were not heading west some migrants could be seen getting off domestic trains while others remained carriages amid confusion a train that managed to leave station was bound for a town called sopron was stopped by police about an hour from budapest guardian reported passengers were ordered to get off train some of passengers grabbed hold of train track to prevent police from taking them to a refugee camp some reportedly banged windows chanting no camp no camp some of migrants had been forced to sleep in streets of budapest for two nights after hungarian police closed terminal to them tuesday that time migrants with valid tickets but no travel documents were prevented from boarding trains to austria and germany there was no immediate explanation from police or other authorities about why migrants were allowed to enter terminal thursday rail company said its stance was due to railway transport security reasons a hungarian government spokesman confirmed to sky news that no international trains would be leaving station for safety reasons later thursday hungarian prime minister viktor orban said his countrys military would be deployed countrys southern border with serbia as part of a crackdown migrants and human traffickers orban told associated press in brussels that hungarian parliament pushing through new measures that will create a new legal situation borders even more strict than it was he added that migrant problem not a european problem problem a german problem nobody would like to stay in hungary all of them would like to go to germany more than migrants have crossed into hungary this year as members of nation eu squabble over how to deal with its worst humanitarian crisis since end of world war ii wednesday leaders of germany italy and france called for a fair distribution of refugees throughout eu germany alone expected to take in migrants this year four times more than its total meanwhile italy and greece have had to deal with an increase in migrants attempting dangerous mediterranean sea crossing have complained that their resources are being overwhelmed by sheer numbers crisis was given new urgency wednesday when images of a drowned threeyearold boy whose body had washed up shore of turkeys bodrum peninsula spread rapidly social media turkish authorities said boy was one of syrians who had drowned trying to reach greece when boat they were traveling in sank boys mother and fiveyearold brother were also among victims back in hungary migrants had threatened wednesday to walk miles to austrian border if police would not let them board trains to their desired destinations hungary tantalizingly opened way monday allowing more than migrants to pack westbound trains and inspiring a migrant surge to capital before it withdrew option hours later hungary for months had permitted most applicants to head west after short bureaucratic delays now says it wont let more groups deeper into european union and claims eu backing for move with an estimated people camping outside station conditions had grown increasingly squalid despite efforts of volunteers distributing water food medicine and disinfectants associated press contributed to this report

by bar editor and columnist dr marsha adebayo t he revolving rigged system that purports to be american democracy was revealed in all its corporate vulgarity a baltimore university stage last week two us senate candidates of duopoly parties pretended to support green party s candidate s right to join debate but failed to protest when cops hauled her away this was their rosa parks moment when they could have stood for integrity and democracy but failed test corporate media and political duopoly collaborated to ensure that green party message would not be heard us corruption during this campaign season full display for entire world to ponder no one paying even scant attention can deny thin veneer that used to hide state sponsored police murder of africans structural poverty and cozy relationship between rulers in democratic and republican parties green party candidates such as jill stein ajamu baraka and margaret flowers have forced sunlight s disinfectant power to expose a rigged racist and revolting political system that politically and economically devours communities of color condones police murders of black youth intentionally exposes communities like flint michigan to poisoned water promotes drone warfare and pilfering of natural resources of africa and south america system however finding it more difficult to block out voices of dissent such was situation this week university of baltimore college of public affairs where dr margaret flowers green party candidate for maryland us senate seat was refused opportunity to participate in only televised debate critics of transpacific partnership trade deal and senate bill to give president fast track authority to negotiate it say it will hurt american workers here a look some of those claims president obama delivers remarks trade nike corporate headquarters in beaverton ore friday mr obama pressed fellow democrats to support his push for a trade deal with asian countries obama administration has a tough sell convincing congress to grant it fast track trade negotiating authority so it can complete negotiations biggest regional trade deal in united states history transpacific partnership agreement involving a total of nations though not china administration sees this as a tremendous opportunity for economic growth it wants fast track would require congress only an upordown vote a final agreement to help it secure best deal it can opponents mostly democrats but some republicans too say fast track and tpp will cost america jobs but supporters say their approach new and improved below are some of critics chief concerns and how fast track and tpp supporters respond critics say free trade kills jobs business says it creates them however it s not that simple most economists agree that broadening trade results in economic growth but benefits are shared unevenly trade liberalization promotes overall economic growth among trading partners but there are both winners and losers nonpartisan congressional research service said in an april report it s also extremely difficult to calculate how a trade deal affects job creation and loss due to a lack of data and important theoretical and practical matters crs said in reviewing north american free trade agreement crs found that nafta did not cause huge job losses feared by critics or large economic gains predicted by supporters obama white house admits that while overall benefits of trade expansion may be large they may also be unevenly distributed trade therefore can also have adverse effects for some workers council of economic advisers cea said in economic benefits of us trade a report issued earlier this month so white house arguing to members of congress that policies administration supports such as investment in infrastructure worker training and education can offset jobs lost as a result of trade deals assistance for displaced it enough trade adjustment assistance taa programs provide help beyond traditional jobless benefits for workers displaced by trade displaced workers get help to train for a new career without such extra help thinking goes these workers might find new jobs but have to take a big cut in pay or they might drop out of job market entirely programs are also designed to boost chances of passing trade legislation drawing in some democrats who might otherwise be in no camp in a separate bill taa has been beefed up to raise benefits and to include service sector workers for first time whether taa works however a matter of debate one labor department study found that after four years participants had earnings comparable to a group of nontaa job losers that can be viewed as bad news even with help they arent doing any better than other people recovering from joblessness or as good news this a particularly hardhit group of workers who might have fared worse without training politically if taa made even a relatively modest contribution to ease of enacting freetrade policies program s benefits would outweigh its costs said report when countries such as china and japan artificially lower value of their currencies it costs americans jobs because foreign imports become cheaper and us exports more expensive so critics want language in fasttrack bill against currency manipulation bill s supporters don t dispute dangers that s why for first time fasttrack legislation makes issue a negotiating objective for administration but an enforcement mechanism too strong a remedy to include in bill administration says claiming that it would derail trade negotiations it could also endanger america s ability to manage its own currency and lead to retaliation and trade wars us officials say they point to modest success in recent years in pressuring china to increase value of its currency sen charles schumer d of new york who calls currency manipulation most significant trade challenge this country faces succeeded in getting a currency amendment in a related customs bill it s not yet clear what will happen to this measure or other attempts to address issue don t know what s in tpp that s because it s top secret criticizes sen elizabeth warren d of massachusetts a vocal opponent of fast track that was a complaint that her colleague sen ron wyden d of oregon heard a lot town halls as a result really went to mat these secrecy issues senator wyden said a recent monitor breakfast senator wyden who administration s point man trade in senate points out that with fasttrack bill full text of tpp and any other trade agreements will be public for first time days before congress starts voting it under fast track any member of congress can have access to classified negotiating texts members can attend negotiating rounds or be briefed by administration request that s not good enough for senator warren she s pushing a petition that tells us trade officials no vote a fasttrack for trade agreements until american people can see what s in this tpp deal members of congress also complain about restricted access for their staffs whom they heavily rely critics are up in arms over way corporations settle disputes over alleged violations of freetrade agreements investorstate dispute settlement or isds lets foreign countries sue governments in special tribunals rather than going through country s court system problem with this they argue that tribunals grant huge payments to corporations for violations such big payments that countries are then incentivized to change their regulations not only do supporters of labor object they worry that foreign companies will use tribunals to force changes in us regulations and laws administration says it writing safeguards into tpp that will prevent this from happening president obama also told yahoo politics there no chance zero chance that us would be sued something like financial regulations and food safety and various environmental regulations that have in place mainly because treat everybody same he added treat own companies same way treat somebody elses companies for first time a nonfreemarket economy such as vietnam will be included in a broad trade deal involving us and that s a problem say critics americans should not be forced to compete against desperately poor workers like those in vietnam who make as little as cents an hour sen bernie sanders of vermont wrote in a letter to to us trade representative michael froman last month in us trade deficit with vietnam was billion but supporters argue that fast track and tpp are precisely intended to protect against this problem fasttrack bill raises bar for labor environment and human rights standards says wyden trading partners must adopt and maintain core international labor and environmental standards with trade sanctions if they do not comply he said in a statement last month staff writers david cook and mark trumbull contributed to this report

not and would not be held hostage to autocratic whims of royalty but rather a full partner to a free and robust electoral process that guarantees not just meaningful input from governed but ability to challenge state policies as winds of change blow from river to sea once again recent events have proven this to be just so much a perverse myth empty rhetoric second only to brazen unfounded israeli boast of having most humane army in world even as body count of palestinian children grows in cemeteries and prisons that have become very much its own unique brand of st century youth hostel recently hagai elad an israeli and jew who serves as executive director of b tselem israel information center for human rights in occupied territories spoke before un security council urging it to take immediate action against israel s illegal settlements demagoguery and inhumanity exposed not quite words in its entirety one paragraph in particular of elad s testimony sums up life for millions of those captured by a democracy that sees day as night pain as pleasure crushing despite its brevity power and pain of these words could easily be part of an opening statement by a war crimes prosecutor a tribunal called to hold israel accountable for crimes unseen since nuremburg tribunals some years ago what does it mean in practical terms to spend years a lifetime under military rule when violence breaks out or when particular incidents attract global attention get a glimpse into certain aspects of life under occupation but what about rest of time what about many ordinary days of a daylong occupation still going strong living under military rule mostly means invisible bureaucratic daily violence it means living under an endless permit regime controls palestinian life from cradle to grave israel controls population registry israel controls work permits israel controls who can travel abroad and who cannot israel controls who can visit from abroad and who cannot in some villages israel maintains lists of who can visit village or who allowed to farm fields permits can sometimes be denied permits must always be renewed thus with every breath they take palestinians breathe in occupation make a wrong move and can lose freedom of movement livelihood or even opportunity to marry and build a family with beloved in a free democratic society these comments while perhaps controversial would certainly not constitute sedition in an open healthy state these words would surely give reason to pause and reflect but never serve as a rational tripwire to strip their speaker of his birthright as an unbound citizen empowered to support his government for policies he finds just but condemn it for those that bear star of tyranny it a distinction that israel has failed to adopt or learn over course of its year subjugation of millions whose only crime to be born palestinian in occupied land with signposts everywhere that simply say jews only beating of chests not long after elad s powerful speech before a world body entrusted with securing fundamental rights and liberty for all of its citizenry hue and cry could be heard among israeli political elite to silence such subversive talk thus coalition chairman mk david bitan of likud party undertook first steps of reprisal by announcing he was considering submitting a bill to knesset that could remove citizenship of israelis who act against their country in international organizations according to bitan elad s actions security council are a blatant violation of trust citizens must have for their country so he should go find another country where he could be a citizen alarming one might ask no not all merely another in an endless daily stream of steps by a government second to none when it comes to autocratic indeed dictatorial control of every fiber of its citizens freedoms particularly their ability to access and exchange information without fear of retribution much known and largely ignored about thousands of palestinian civilians that have been targeted and slaughtered by israeli military machine in occupied palestine whether in gaza or west bank indeed killing fields of gaza or execution alleys of back street jerusalem no longer acquire more than a passing fancy or footnote in evening news spread across a world now busy with outrages of more recent vintage after years of slaughter it s just so much business as usual so too have seemingly become numbed to reality that thousands of palestinian political prisoners languish in isolation many sitting year after year some for decades in administrative detention cells of political prisons uncharged undefended and untried tortured in ways that leave spirits of those still roaming now empty cellblocks of south africa s notorious robben island relieved their misery was ended quickly through state sanctioned executions by suicide mighty censor s sword closures of palestinian news rooms and television stations are commonplace yet no more remarkable than assaults by israel upon palestinian journalists that long ago moved into triple digits and show no sign of abating palestinian centre for development and media freedoms mada has documented a pattern of such attacks by israel running for some time now almost per year although exact number of palestinian journalists killed or injured by israel over just last decade may never be known it has been documented that seventeen lost their lives in gaza alone during months of bombings it endured in dozens of palestinian journalists and private bloggers have been arrested by israel and held for violating vague administrative codes that typically come down to application of entirely undefined prohibitions such as incitement dareen tatour a yearold poet and become linchpin of state s view of what appropriate knowledge and speech and what not thus of late israel has begun to demand of social media giants such as facebook and twitter that they have input criticizing israeli policies time association for civil rights in israel described proposal as a severe blow to israeli democracy and critics labeled policy as mccarthyist recently a variation that upon returning home to firestorm awaiting him following his speech before un hagai elad surely felt what it must have been like to be an activist leftist jew in united states during dark days of daily reality snack georgia abandons ukraines antirussian obsession after a brief period in both ukraine and georgia appeared to be united against russia it now appears that two nations are moving along very different paths originally appeared russia direct in october georgia didn t support any of ukraine s resolutions denouncing kremlin s foreign policy within parliamentary assembly of council of europe pace that surprising given how many analysts had by now assumed that georgia and ukraine were same page when it came to russia two resolutions deal with political implications of russia s aggression in ukraine and human rights abuses occupied ukrainian territories by supporting them pace recognized military conflict in ukraine as russian aggression for first time and called kremlin to withdraw its forces from eastern part of ukraine moreover it denounced parliamentary elections recently conducted by russia in crimea when georgian delegation in pace didn t support these resolutions nation s prowestern parties reacted strongly for example united national movement lambasted georgian government and accused country s former prime minister bidzina ivanishvili of supporting russia moreover mikheil saakashvili former georgian president and now governor of odessa region in ukraine described such a stance as a disgraceful move however an immediate response came from one of members of georgian delegation in pace eka beselia she retorted that tbilisi needed to defend its own national interests even though this statement seems to have alleviated increasing conflict video of russianukrainian journalist matwey ganapolsky who accuses georgia of betraying ukraine in favor of russia fuelled tensions in contrast russian pundits see unwillingness of georgia to vote for pace resolution as a sign of improvement in tbilisimoscow relations in reality reluctance of georgian dream ruling party in georgia to approve these resolutions just logical conclusion of complicated relations with kiev since start of color revolutions in postsoviet space georgia and ukraine were largely in same boat after success of rose revolution in tbilisi and orange revolution in kiev newly elected governments were closely connected with each other and teamed up against russia this resulted from friendly relations between ukraine s former prime minister yulia timoshenko and former president viktor yushchenko one hand and georgia s saakashvili other hand however their relationship was rather pragmatic in its nature although officially tbilisi recognized ukraine as one of its closest allies since democratic processes in two countries have started moving in a reverse direction saakashvili s penchant for conducting an aggressive policy as well as his authoritarian inclinations was increasing while ukraine faced corruption and political rivalry between timoshenko and yushchenko more impact this had countries stability and development more obvious became fact that ruling elites from both sides did not support democratic reforms but only regimes that were friendly to them thus georgianukrainian relations could be seen as a form of cooperation between governments not between people and this trend became relevant until presidential elections when georgia s civil society and population called government to support democratic processes and regime change in its brother country from then georgia has been shying away from supporting political regime in ukraine and focusing more support of country s own population however ukraine refused to consider such tactics with its official representatives criticizing georgian dream coalition for supporting russia during parliamentary elections moreover kiev cooperated with georgia s united national movement was openly accused of building an authoritarian regime and egregious human rights abuses logically new georgian government under ivanishvili cannot help paying attention to this fact but it was relatively reticent and didn t respond even when georgian volunteers came to fight in eastern ukraine to support kiev and accused tbilisi of supporting russia that had some implications for georgian dream it was seen as a political force that capable of defending country s national interests moreover georgian voters also saw fact that saakashvili was appointed as governor of odessa region as an unfriendly move from ukraine as a slap in face because former georgian president was legally prosecuted in his home country meant that ivanishvili couldn t fulfill his pledges and restore justice during election campaign he promised to put saakashvili in jail for corruption and abuse of power editor s note problem was exacerbated when kiev granted saakashvili ukrainian citizenship made it impossible to imprison former georgian president saakashvili crossed red line during latest parliamentary elections in georgia during campaign first his colleagues from united national movement visited ukraine second he openly called for a coup d état against georgian government he sees as prorussian in fact he threatened to conduct a new revolution in georgia this was last straw for georgian dream it safe to say that current georgian political elites started seeing ukraine as a real headache and shelter for dubious and controversial georgian politicians from united national movement accused of different wrongdoings and legal violations however with victory of georgian dream in parliamentary elections a lot has changed moreover odds of party of winning constitutional majority are really high it means that influence of party growing in georgian parliament and even more could change as a result government won t necessarily have to take into account views of other political forces to take decisions it can be pretty outspoken now that it won t put up with antigovernment moves and initiatives like ones promoted by saakashvili moreover georgian voters who are seeking to have those involved in violations during saakashvili s tenure prosecuted so in this regard electorate supports georgian dream thus all this indicates that georgianukrainian relations have always been more complex and nuanced than they seemed to be first glance during saakashvili s tenure there was cooperation between his government and ones of timoshenko and yushchenko however eventually tbilisi shifted its priority from supporting top political officials to supporting society and people ukrainian politicians should keep in mind that russian factor not only one that determines ukrainiangeorgian agenda providing shelter to saakashvili also does matter so to improve relations with tbilisi kiev should take into account its national interest and support georgian people instead of country s politicians

european union s sharpening divisions over a spiraling refugee crisis broke into open thursday with two leaders strongly disagreeing in public over whether asylumseekers were threatening europe s christian roots that was language used by hungarian prime minister viktor orban as he warned europe against allowing in mostly muslim families a day after a drowned syrian toddler washed up turkish coast another european leader retorted that christian values demanded helping less fortunate furious exchange a rare breach of eu s buttoneddown decorum came as hungarian authorities apparently laid a trap for thousands of asylumseekers who had packed budapest s central train station after days of worsening conditions outside station police had blocked them from entering station for days but allowed them in early thursday but a refugeepacked train apparently bound for austrian border came to a halt just west of budapest in a small town where dozens of police officers were waiting platform they tried to force people off train to take them to a migrantprocessing center threatening their chances to make it onward to western europe by day s end there was a standoff with packed train surrounded by police and migrants refusing to budge some of passengers received medical treatment platform european railways become ground zero for migrant crisis orban hungary s nationalist leader who has spearheaded attempts to turn back migrants said thursday that he had little choice but to seal his nation s border with razor wire soldiers and a high fence hungarians are full of fear people in europe are full of fear because see that european leaders among them prime ministers are not able to control situation he said in brussels in a raw joint appearance with european parliament president martin schulz orban and schulz a veteran politician from germany made no attempt to paper over their differences or their distaste for each other hungarian leader blamed germany for crisis saying that its opendoor policy toward syrian asylumseekers was propelling a wave of migrants to undertake dangerous journeys toward europe s heart germany expects asylumseekers this year and it has said that it does not plan to turn away syrians moral human thing to make clear please don t come why do have to go from turkey to europe turkey a safe country stay there it s risky to come orban said hundreds of thousands of people fleeing war in syria iraq and afghanistan have overwhelmed europe s capacity to respond in recent months opening stark divisions some leaders believe that world s largest economic bloc a vast territory of million residents more than capable of accommodating refugees others including hungary s orban believe continent s population in a far more delicate state divisions could threaten some of most basic tenets of eu an alliance built ashes of world war ii in a bid never again to allow such destruction german chancellor angela merkel said this week that a europe with no internal borders a key achievement of european unification could be in question if no solution to refugee crisis found he warned that splits emerging during refugee crisis could do lasting damage to nation alliance was founded a spirit of consensus and burdensharing this a crucial moment for european union schulz said a deeper split of union a risk cannot exclude orban s fears are shared by many eastern european nations have pushed hard against any attempt to require them to take in asylumseekers slovakia has said it will accept only christians in estonia where fewer than migrants have been resettled police thursday were investigating a suspicious earlymorning fire a dormitory that was housing victims of syria s war britain takes in so few refugees from syria they would fit a subway train but concerns also extend to britain there fewer syrians than would fit in a london subway train have been accepted this year british media reported thursday that prime minister david cameron would soon announce plans to take in thousands more syrian refugees a striking turnaround for a leader who a day earlier had said that answer to crisis was not simply taking in more and more refugees elsewhere in europe refusals brought mounting anger from leaders who are more sympathetic to growing crowds of asylumseekers for a christian it shouldn t matter what race religion and nationality person in need represents european council president donald tusk said thursday launching into orban before two met in brussels tusk a former prime minister of poland proposed resettling least refugees across europe in france president françois hollande said death of toddler a tragedy but it s also a call to european conscience europe made of values and principles images of child s body lying facedown and partly in water a turkish beach evoked consternation around world hollande said he had just reached an agreement with germany a proposal for mandatory quotas to more equitably spread refugees across eu some european countries are not shouldering their moral obligations he said merkel meanwhile said she understood that not every nation was prepared to take as large a burden as her country was doing but she said it was impossible for germany sweden and austria alone to continue taking in vast majority of incoming asylumseekers geneva convention does not apply only to germany but also to every state she said referring to international treaty that requires countries to take in refugees of war orban has vowed to seal hungary s borders by sept empowered by emergency measures expected to be approved by country s parliament in coming days measures would give authorities broad powers to crack down illegal migration outside budapest s elegant
