presidential campaign by bar executive editor glen ford barack obama tried to woo republicans into a grand bargain that would have gutted social security bill clinton let loose banks but donald trump s destruction of republican party will allow hillary clinton to gather whole of ruling class under same party banner in one big tent where grandest of bargains can be conceived and achieved without crossing an aisle rich are about to get their best deal yet hillary s big tent obama s grand bargain steroids by bar executive editor glen ford exodus from gop has suddenly transformed democratic party into primary political instrument of ruling class when donald trump took a wrecking ball to republican party he provided unexpected catalyst for completion of corporate project begun by bill clinton al gore and other white democrats in s with founding of democratic leadership council dlc to counter relentless attrition of whites to gop in their home states these beleaguered mostly southern democrats sought national corporate funding to turn their party decisively to right they reckoned correctly that a steady stream of corporate capital would allow them to control new wave of black voters and politicians that had been mobilized by rev jesse jackson s two presidential campaigns while strengthening hand of south in national democratic party calculations bill clinton became first dlc president in and moved swiftly and methodically to narrow ideological differences between duopoly parties he completed much of ronald reagan s agenda claiming it as his own destroyed welfare as knew it vastly expanded mass black incarceration regime pushed nafta through congress over objections of majorities in his own party engineered corporate monopolization of broadcast media and removed last safety straps from wall street banks clinton arranged deployment of thousands of foreign jihadists to bosnia and kosovo in foreign affairs clinton initiated what was to become doctrine of humanitarian military intervention dismantling and partially occupying socialist nation of yugoslavia in process clinton arranged deployment of thousands of foreign jihadists to bosnia and kosovo thus keeping operational network created by us saudi arabia and pakistan during previous decade in afghanistan in africa clinton conspired with uganda and exiled tutsi rebels to overthrow hutu majority government in rwanda setting off a bloodbath in followed two years later by an invasion of congo that has killed more than six million people and still counting barack obama was second dlc president although he lies about his membership he too moved with unseemly haste to reach a grand bargain with gop not of necessity since he had won a huge electoral mandate with overwhelming financial backing of wall street but as a matter of ideological principle in january of before even taking oath of office obama told editorial boards of new york times and washington post that all entitlements including medicare and social security would be table for cutting in his administration obama s first project now considered centerpiece of his legacy was to resurrect rightwing heritage foundation s corporate health insurance scheme adopted by republican presidential candidate bob dole in and made into state law by republican massachusetts governor mitt romney in obama s affordable care act was literally written by lobbyists for insurance and drug industries and now collapsing like a poorly constructed house end of its mortgage for better part of two years obama debased himself all but begging republicans to consummate his grand bargain with democratic majority in congress in no mood to tamper with social security and medicare obama tried to maneuver targeted entitlements into a financial crisis trap he named two dependable reactionaries alan simpson and erskine bowles as cochairmen of his national commission fiscal responsibility also called commission deficit reduction they dutifully recommended trillion in budget cuts mostly to social programs including cuts to social security although full commission did not endorse chairs recommendations and congress failed to pass bills modeled document obama used simpsonbowles formula as a basis for negotiating what he hoped would be a bipartisan gop plus obama and a minority of democrats massacre of entitlements for better part of two years obama debased himself all but begging republicans to consummate his grand bargain congressional black caucus chairman emanuel cleaver of kansas city called deal a satan s sandwich but obama continued to pursue a political marriage made in hell until reelection campaign clock called a halt to spectacle a de facto superparty of bourgeoisie quest for a grand bargain was barack obama s failed attempt to best bill clinton in erasing distinctions between two major parties to create a de facto superparty of bourgeoisie it was republicans who ran away from altar and democrats did eat much of satan s sandwich through sequestration and austerity that ravaged social programs by other means why did republicans reject deal although both halves of duopoly ultimately answer to wall street republicans like any other party have an institutional interest in winning office it true that obama had crafted a deal that any republican would love but it was still his deal and he planned to run for reelection as an historical dealmaker probably just as importantly republican party white man s party meaning white supremacy its organizing principle central to its identity among much of masses to embrace obama no matter how advantageous to their big business patrons was a hug too far for gop racism doomed grand bargain hallelujah a new bigger bargain recently released wikileaks emails reveal hillary clinton speaking to bankers morgan stanley in a year after debacle simpsonbowles framework and big elements of it were right she said thanks to donald trump s demolition of republican party conditions have been created for hillary clinton as dlc president to achieve what and could not gather whole of ruling class under same party banner in one big tent where grandest of bargains can be conceived and achieved without crossing an aisle with most of ruling class and its attendants having vacated building republican party has been reduced to donald trump and his deplorables as hillary calls them trump s opposition to corporate trade deals violated holy grail against prohibiting capitalists from moving money and jobs around world as they see fit and his reluctance to support regime change as an inherent right of american exceptionalism has frightened and outraged military industrial complex national security establishment and all sectors dependent maintenance of empire an inherently unstable arrangement clinton s big tent not a temporary election season dwelling it how she plans to govern exodus from gop has suddenly transformed democratic party into primary political instrument of ruling class while same time party nominally represents most of folks who are abused and misused by that ruling class it an inherently unstable arrangement and will soon be wracked by splits as a posttrump gop attempts to lure its fat cats back and darker and poorer constituencies consigned to latrine area of hillary s high class tent break to left for air but in interim clinton will have a unique opportunity to cut grand austerity deals with all big elements of simpsonbowles to renege her corporate trade promises and to wage war with great gusto in name of a united country ever since democratic national convention it has been clear that clintonites are encouraged to consider everyone outside of their grand circle to be suspect subversive or depraved their inclusive rhetoric really an invocation of a ruling class consensus now that trump has supposedly brought ruling class together under one banner in hillary s tent boardrooms are always in session bar executive editor glen ford can be contacted email protected

republican presidential candidate john kasich arrives to central medford high school in medford ore april ohio governor suspended his campaign wednesday john kasich who ran as a hugproviding political insider in a year dominated by voter anger and rise of outside candidates suspended his political campaign wednesday move leaves donald trump standing alone as presumptive republican presidential nominee mr trump sounded conciliatory after news broke that governor kasich was quitting he said that ohio governor might be helpful in buckeye state for general election and that he would be interested in vetting kasich as a possible vice presidential running mate think john s doing right thing trump told cnn s wolf blitzer did he not everyone was so sure kasich supporters bemoaned loss of what they felt was last source of civility in race over months of a campaign where other candidates resorted to deeply personal insults innuendo and shouting ohio governor stood alone in emphasizing complexity of political problems and need to work with others to find answers if this his last campaign he went out admirably tweeted yahoo news national political correspondent matt bai wednesday others pointed out that kasich had finally reached his strategic goal a oneonone matchup with trump only to withdraw whether he could have beaten trump or not was irrelevant in this view he d have been able to rally antitrump forces in a way ted cruz could not and show that trumpism had not conquered entire republican party problem that trump if not trumpism does conquer all in gop republican national committee chairman reince priebus last night referred to trump as presumptive nominee given that and fact that trump almost certain to pile up a winning majority of delegates by end of primary season kasich felt he had no choice but to look for exits and there were never enough of those supporters anyway kasich won only his home state of ohio and by end was less than percent in national polls in a threeman race technically kasich finished in fourth even though he was last losing contender to drop out trump senator cruz and marco rubio all won more delegates it s amazing that he stayed in race so long given that he won more than percent of vote only once in first gop primaries or caucuses notes fivethirtyeight polling guru harry enten most candidates would have seen writing wall and gone home kasich didn t see wall or writing or he didn t care writes mr enten would trump actually ask kasich to be his running mate and if so would kasich accept trump s said he wants a politician as a vp and ohio governor would provide balance to billionaire in terms of experience and message pair never really clashed though trump did mock kasich s losing ways and his enthusiastic methods of eating for kasich a vp slot might validate his quixotic effort and provide a cap to his long and varied political career but kasich also described himself as a prince of light and hope and he d be joining a presidential candidate who s shown no eagerness to embrace either of those characteristics

first read a morning briefing from meet press and nbc political unit days most important political stories and why they matter after bombshell does remarkably stable race stay stable theres no hyperbole in stating that hillary clintonvsdonald trump presidential race has been craziest weve covered consider all of jawdropping moments mexican rapists wikileaks vladimir putin access hollywood video alicia machado trumps visit to mexico basket of deplorables birtherism and debates all of them despite them all however race has been remarkably stable just look national nbcwsj poll numbers since sept but does that stability change after fbi director james comeys bombshell friday that his organization learned of existence of new emails that appear pertinent to its previous investigation into clintons email practices one one hand polling weve seen so far suggests that voters remain in their partisan corners according to this weekends washington postabc poll of voters said fbis review makes no difference and among who say it makes them less likely to back clinton those voters are disproportionately republicans and gopleaning independents whats more postcomey polls weve seen here and here havent really budged least not yet other hand it has never been a positive for clinton throughout this entire presidential race when focus has been her especially subject of emails eight days to go latest developments in comey surprise meanwhile here are all of latest developments in comey surprise fbi obtained a warrant to search emails related to probe of hillary clintons private server that were discovered excongressman anthony weiners laptop also sunday senate minority leader harry reid accused comey of violating hatch act bars government officials from using their authority to influence elections former attorney general eric holder wrote a scathing oped condemning fbi director james comey for his handling of investigation into hillary clintons use of a private email server fear he has unintentionally and negatively affected public trust in both justice department and fbi and nbcs ari melber has a piece concluding that newly discovered emails even if they contain classified information would unlikely change earlier conclusion not to charge clinton fbis review of emails could be quick additionally nbcs pete williams reports that its possible fbis review of emails could end quickly now that fbi obtained a warrant to search them theyll narrow them down to look just those dating from time hillary clinton was secretary of state then theyll weed out any that are not about government business agents will use automated software to search whats left for duplicates theyve already found during investigation of clinton email server any that remain will be checked for classified information williams reported today this morning officials say theres no way to tell how long that will take but they say if it goes quickly and nothing classified found fbi could say so within next few days it largely depends how many of emails are duplicates and how many are new to investigators nbcwsjmarist polls clinton up in nc deadlocked race in fl over weekend released brandnew nbcwsjmarist polls of florida and north carolina were conducted before fridays bombshell news numbers polls conducted oct also measure early voting in these two states among of likely voters in florida who say theyve already voted clinton ahead among those who havent voted in sunshine state trump up and clinton leads by a margin among of north carolinians who say theyve already voted its amazing when news about donald trump no matter how controversial gets drowned out by other events still dont miss this washington post piece into trumps charitable giving or lack thereof for as long as he has been rich and famous donald trump has also wanted people to believe he generous he spent years constructing an image as a philanthropist by appearing charity events and by making very public even nationally televised promises to give his own money away it was in large part a facade a monthslong investigation by washington post has not been able to verify many of trumps boasts about his philanthropy instead throughout his life in spotlight whether as a businessman television star or presidential candidate post found that trump had sought credit for charity he had not given or had claimed other peoples giving as his own how violence and retaliation are constant themes in trumps rhetoric also do read nbcs benjy sarlin how violence and retaliation are common themes in trumps rhetoric and in his events in his eyes world an unforgiving place where cities are war zones where rapists are streaming across border and where jealous rivals are hatching plots to humiliate america and trump personally to prevail in such an environment he suggests response to any slight must be swift and overwhelming dwelling limits imposed by law or tradition usually a will middle east be as unstable years from now as it today posed that question this week to a class of students harvard university s kennedy school of government about half answered yes that things will be as bad or worse no matter what united states does these graduate students sense what many middle east experts are beginning to vocalize region caught in a turbulent vortex of change that s likely to continue for many years perhaps decades drivers are political social economic and demographic forces over united states and other outside powers have little control for next generation instability may be rule in middle east rather than exception predictions about future based current trends are always risky because analysts can t foresee unexpected black swan events that produce radical change nobody could have anticipated that a tunisian fruit vendor s selfimmolation in would begin a cascade of revolution and civil war in middle east similarly can t forecast process that may eventually restore balance economic and demographic data show a region already stressed by slow growth and high youth unemployment these problems are compounded by refugee flows from wars in iraq syria yemen and libya and worsened by falling oil prices that hinder saudi arabia s ability to provide a buffer international monetary fund s latest world economic outlook released this month presents a grim picture unemployment will top percent in egypt and tunisia this year and reach nearly percent in algeria economies aren t growing fast enough to provide enough jobs for young people growth this year expected to be percent in iraq percent in lebanon percent in algeria percent in jordan and percent in egypt saudi arabia s growth forecast has been slashed more than points to percent this year and projected to decline further next year because of oil prices saudis are waging a bloody air war in yemen apparently convinced that threats to their security are external but more intractable problems may be internal with saudi budget falling into what imf warns will be substantial deficit this year and next tepid growth and falling oil exports mean larger trade and budget deficits predicts imf region will suffer a cumulative currentaccount deficit of percent of gross domestic product this year even oilexporting countries will run a deficit of percent deepening conflicts and security disruptions in a number of oilexporting countries could further undermine economic activity delay reforms and dampen confidence warns imf middle east s body politic enfeebled by these disorders has been ravaged by sectarian and civil wars international rescue committee estimated this month that million syrians or about half population have fled their homes syrian disaster has affected whole region to take just one example poverty has more than doubled in iraqi kurdistan according to a recent world bank report as weak governance structures have buckled across middle east extremist groups have become more powerful most potent islamic state exploded out of remnants of alqaeda in iraq but many other groups across region are fusing popular rage with sectarian and tribal bases of support even if islamic state also known as isis can be degraded in iraq and syria over next three years as president obama hopes underlying disorder in region could spawn isis or even observing devastation in middle east a bit like watching a hurricane pummel a vulnerable coastline outsiders can try to mitigate destruction and provide humanitarian relief they must also try to protect themselves from collateral damage but they can t stop raging winds and surging tides from leveling fragile structures as disastermanagement experts have learned a big storm has to blow itself out before rebuilding can begin graham allison who heads belfer center here argues that us government should be careful about trying to fix problems in middle east until it understands them better he contrasts deadly ebola virus with ideological contagion of islamic state know what causes ebola and how to stop it allison wrote recently in time magazine but there no such clarity about how islamic state toxin spreads or how it can be cured what do know that this extremist virus has taken root in a body that already severely weakened and showing no signs of recovering soon read more from david ignatius s archive follow him twitter or subscribe to his updates facebook

quasiparticle can behave just like a majorana fermion and it could help us to finally understand this incredibly weird phenomenon if re not familiar with majorana fermion it was first proposed by italian theoretical physicist ettore majorana in he predicted that a type of particle called a fermion could act as its own antiparticle in standard model of physics every particle has an antiparticle these antiparticles are usually an entirely separate particle with same mass but opposite charge of their partner even electrically neutral particles have antiparticles such as neutron made of quarks and antineutron made of antiquarks in very rare cases a particle with no mass and no charge can act as its own antiparticle and ve only identified a few examples of these so far photons light particles hypothetical gravitons and wimps majorana fermions if they exist fall into this final category and if can find them and harness them it would change everything about how record and process information in next generation of quantum computers search for this particle for condensedmatter physicists what higgs boson search was for highenergy particle physicists physicist leonid rokhinson from purdue university noted back in it a very peculiar object because it a fermion yet it its own antiparticle with zero mass and zero charge unlike regular computers that use bits of and quantum computers use quantum bits that can exist in a state of or a superposition of both problem with building a computer out of quantum bits or qubits that it s incredibly difficult to make a record of what state they previously held once they ve been switched and there s no point having a computer that can t retain information but physicists think majorana fermions could be key to solving all of that information could be stored not in individual particles but in their relative configuration so that if one particle pushed a little by a local force it doesn t matter said rokhinson as long as that local noise not so strong that it alters overall configuration of a group of particles information retained it offers an entirely new way of dealing with information in april this year a team from oak ridge national laboratory in tennessee discovered first real proof of existence of majorana fermions in something called a quasiparticle unlike a regular particle a physical object that makes up an atom a quasiparticle an entity that has some characteristics of a distinct particle but made up of a grouping of multiple particles instead finding a majorana fermion quasiparticle one thing but real goal finding a majorana fermion particle fastforward to now and physicists from chinese academy of sciences say they ve identified another type of quasiparticle that behaves just like a majorana fermion called majorana zero modes mzms team was able to synthesise these quasiparticles inside a quantum simulation and manipulate them in ways that would work within a quantum computer system most significantly they showed that they could retain information encoded in their majorana zero modes even when errors and noise were applied to system demonstrate immunity of quantum information encoded in majorana zero modes against local errors through simulator they describe in their paper published in nature communications if team s simulation can be replicated in experimental conditions it means could have another candidate for majorana fermionlike behaviours hands and another shot something to build quantum computers of future with in meantime here s more those elusive majorana fermions source science alert

taking water thanks to staff shakeups organizing failures and a big loss in wisconsin donald trounced his rivals in media capital of world but it s probably not enough as he tries to secure delegates he needs to clinch nomination before reaching gop convention in cleveland most likely scenario will have trump getting about delegates here s why new york marks a crucial geographic segue in race as calendar turns away from ted cruzfriendly interior and into trump s midatlantic wheelhouse with bound delegates stake next tuesday test for cruz and john kasich will be how many they can pick off can start by penciling in delegates for trump as likely statewide winner in delaware pennsylvania and maryland exceeding percent mark in connecticut would mean another delegates who would otherwise be split among field and trump can expect to win about half of rhode island s proportionally allotted delegates rest will be awarded to respective winners of each congressional district best delegatepoaching opportunity for kasich comes in affluent maryland suburbs of washington dc where polls show trump dead last behind cruz winning three or four districts between beltway and connecticut gold coast would be a big success best news for trump opponents that single biggest trove of delegates will be unbound pennsylvanians elected independently of candidate affiliation or obligation while pacific northwest states split their delegates proportionally and even west virginia in heart of trump s appalachian sweet spot has a delegate system so complicated that he may come away shortchanged bellwether to watch will be indiana criminally underpolled state holds promise both for trump open primary bluecollar sensibilities and his opponents affluent indianapolis suburbs for kasich grassroots conservatives and evangelicals for cruz and awards atlarge delegates to statewide winner if cruz can reprise midwestern magic that carried him in wisconsin he can stop trump offensive in its tracks short of that he and kasich must peel away as many congressional districts as possible to prevent trump from heading into june needing only a glorified chip shot to clinch in end race will come down to california and its massive cache of delegates in what will amount to unpredictable miniprimaries across disparate terrain ninety delegates will be awarded in districts where president obama received percent or more of vote twothirds of those will be decided in seats where mitt romney won percent or less including eight bona fide rotten boroughs where he couldn t even crack percent trump can hardly expect a new yorkstyle romp here meaning he needs least delegates coming in cruz s argument an open convention will be stronger if he can claim more than delegates while keeping deficit to a minimum california endgame will hinge what happens in indiana if cruz can battle through coming adversity for a hoosier state victory odds are re headed to cleveland bottom line even if trump wins indiana and staves off kasich in burbs he d probably end up just shy of what he needs perhaps as close as delegates but it s more likely that indiana proves tougher for him and leaves him closer to delegates putting all eyes cleveland

it was finally moment democrats have been waiting for former secretary hillary clinton and senator bernie sanders facing off oneonone unencumbered by extraneous candidates and audience questions last night s msnbc debate crackled with heat as two candidates stood very close to each other stage and made their case to watching public answering questions from moderators rachel maddow and chuck todd it led to both candidates becoming their most provocative clinton was forceful and dismissive while sanders was stern and evasive first hour exacerbated tension between them as both candidates seemed to entrench in their worldviews no matter how unpopular two bickered over definition of word progressive it comes from word progress did know and of who exactly part of establishment as observed yesterday during cnn democratic town hall both sanders and clinton were attempting to finesse compromises or semblance thereof between polar opposites rhetorical equivalent of walking plank but debates are about differentiation and primaries are about being choosy so here stood watching mom and dad argue after dinner as if whole state of free world depended it things got kind of intense they dug into each other s differences trying to make sound untrue what patently obvious to outside observer sanders a stubborn college professor obsessively dedicated to principle clinton a bureaucrat s bureaucrat doggedly focused process and frankly neither candidate served by a debate this isn t an election where adulation for a candidate s ideology swept other nominees à la thensenator barack obama in this an election between two pragmatic prickly politicians two grandparents who have been in public service for decades debates make them fighters and while this headtohead matchup was long overdue first segment of debate was honestly exhausting to watch especially after introspection of informal town hall perhaps democratic party just a little too bleedingheart to revel in bloodsport of two strong liberal politicians trying to take each other down but it certainly seemed that every attack made from one candidate to other looked mostly bad for that candidate when clinton railed against sanders for his artful smear of her politics she was booed and when sanders dismissed clinton as a shill of establishment he dismissed for obvious fact that a woman candidate for president a revolution in and of itself process and principle sparred again and again and moderators maddow and todd did well to let two speak while also finding incisive specific questions to address to each it s difficult for any moderator to hold a candle to rachel maddow but especially when grilling both liberal candidates she dazzled she directed a question to sanders that struck to heart of practical difficulties of his platform asking him how he planned to work with corporations that advanced progressive agendas those corporations that had to be dealt with to pass affordable healthcare act for example she asked clinton for chance to see transcripts of those goldman sachs speeches maddow asked sanders how he really intended to win presidency as essentially a thirdparty candidate and she asked clinton to justify how she still considered herself a viable candidate multiple scandals into her career if sanders and clinton were bickering last night over custody of american people maddow was impish child playing them against each other indeed night s most heated exchanges came as a result of her pointed questioning might be why about halfway through debate tension of night noticeably defused maybe sanders and clinton wised up to maddow s game maybe it was too late in day and too late in campaign trail to waste so much energy being angry television of course clinton s farther right than sanders come general election she s going to be trying to appear moderate of course sanders policies will be a bit harder to enact than clinton s he in his own words literally instigating a revolution segment foreign policy allowed candidates to take a breather clinton immediately was more confident going over her area of expertise and diverted from his primary issue of capitalism sanders was a bit of a wallflower allowing for some breathing room in discussion by time todd and maddow returned to discussing scandals of campaign both candidates seemed like they d gotten sparring out of their system a question about clinton s emails elicited a steadfastly neutral response from sanders there s not a day that goes by when am not asked to attack her that issue and have refrained from doing that and will continue to refrain from doing that and when maddow asked clinton if she wanted seconds to discuss an allegedly misleading advertisement from sanders campaign clinton just stared a bit with an exasperation she deployed well benghazi hearings and then simply responded no it was a very parental no final but with some warmth attached and it indicated a deep respect for either other candidate or for optics of cooperation often are exactly same think that sanders and clinton have real issues with each other but also think that neither sanders nor clinton really want to fight with each other and end of day despite whizbang of zingers neither do beyond artificial dichotomy of debate sanders and clinton are all ve got two viable candidates against a stack of wildcards that are still funded and still in race over other team of course calling them mom and dad reductive and genderessentialist am being a little facetious but there s a certain degree of unearned faith m pinning both that same desperate confidence all invest in parents when need to believe them when they say that they will keep us safe from danger and it s not just that feels it as habit of kids everywhere todd rushed into awkward camaraderie between candidates with a toosoon toointimate question secretary clinton ve made it clear when look senator sanders do not see a president but do see do see a vice president clinton laughs would unite party by trying to pick senator sanders as running mate there s no better evidence of democratic party s desperate desire for a united front against looming specter of loony republicans than this question political equivalent of politely asking mom and dad if they will get back together debate was revealing but questions were even more so arguments about how to arrange deck chairs gave way to realization that titanic sinking sanders quipped worst days think it fair to say are times better than any republican candidate crowd cheered and after closing statements when maddow and todd said goodbye to candidates maddow made sure to hug each

japanese internment its front page new york times has said trumps idea more typically associated with hate groups dan balz of washington post has called trumps rhetoric demagogic while buzzfeed editorinchief ben smith has informed staff that it acceptable to refer to trump social media as a mendacious racist because he said those are facts several others have said trumps proposal poses a national security threat this not small ball actually this matters richard engel nbc news chief foreign correspondent said monday it isa black spot collective foreign policy and conscience and it also just feeds into isis narrative willingness to use such language and draw such analogies represents a watershed moment in medias coverage of presidential campaign several journalists and political observers told cnn for first time in six months news organizations are abandoning concerns about impartiality and evenhandedness and stating what they believe are objective truths about republicans most popular presidential candidate what trump doing wildly outside american traditions and values and thats what were covering and responding to and think see that across major media outlets smith late in coming as his comments have become more extreme mainstream media has been more willing to describe him accurately in view as a demagogue a national security threat and a racist but frankly his racism and demagoguery were apparent from his first speech announcing his candidacy back june said ryan lizza new yorkers washington correspondent and cnn political analyst to be sure many journalists have been characterizing trumps campaign in these terms for some time in august lizza compared trump to farright parties in europe and said that he was running a candidacy that based resentment of nonwhite people but for most part journalists stopped short of calling trump demagogic or racist despite fact that he has often relied false information about minorities to appeal to popular chief strategist barack obamas presidential campaigns and cnn senior political commentator new phase of trumpmedia relations has ushered in an unusual moment in american politics said media experts frank sesno director of school of media and public affairs george washington university and a former cnn washington bureau chief said journalism has moved into have no shame mode there are thresholds and several have been crossed and that blows up socalled objective reporting there are times when hesaid shesaid isnt enough kelly mcbride vice president for academic programs poynter institute stressed that while some of terms were appropriate it was important for journalists to put trumps remarks in context rather than just calling him racist theres more journalistic value in pointing out why its racist she said so like it when see newsrooms making historical analogies like editorials pointing out that hes dangerous and why question now what impact will media backlash have if any trump has proven time and time again that incendiary remarks only bolster his support moreover his supporters harbor little respect for american media and often give candidate a standing ovation when he calls media scum frank rich new york magazine columnist said medias disdain would only enhance his support from his fans rich lowry editor of national review agreed media disdain a badge of honor for trump and his supporters he said so this latest bout of criticism from press wont hurt him perhaps only way media could dent trump would be to have him tv less often and become less obsessed with himbut thats not going to happen lowry added bottom line another republican candidate going to have to outmaneuver trump or just flatout beat him its that simple

could hillary clinton be next richard nixon now that s a provocative question but it isn t quite what think other day watched hillary and bill clinton take in their close friend terry mcauliffe s inauguration as governor of virginia neither clinton spoke but their presence said it all virginia mother of presidents long before it was a modern swing state will be seeing a lot of clintons and now they have a readymade homeawayfromhome in gubernatorial mansion that adorns richmond s capitol square every time observe clintons can t help but ponder long and winding road of their year electoral saga beginning with bill s unsuccessful run for congress in arkansas their lives are so suffused with politics that it seems incredible to consider that hillary might not run in after all with just one exception a clinton has always tried for public office whenever a tantalizing opportunity presented itself rulebreaker was bill s aborted run for president in cycle eve of his expected candidacy announcement in july with national press gathering in little rock his longsuffering chief of staff betsey wright she later told pbs huddled with her boss and presented a list of women he was alleged to have been seeing after a number of responses along lines of she ll never talk clinton belatedly awakened to reality that he could selfdestruct in postgary hart world hart had been forced out of democrats presidency sweepstakes just a couple of months earlier following allegations of adultery next day clinton declined to run stunning news media with unconvincing excuse that he had decided to spend more time with his family nearly three decades later hillary needs no cover story should she surprise us and spurn another white house tour now in her mid s she knows as much as any human being alive what an arduous journey lies ahead even for a heavily favored contender inevitably she will consider how much she wants or able to keep going a killer pace throughout her s and more important her chances of prevailing in november much of it out of her hands low job approval numbers for president obama should they persist will make it difficult for any democrat to win even with party s seeming electoral college edge and growing demographic advantages among minorities and young just ask john mccain how president george w bush s unpopularity affected his white house bid of course can t rule out very real chance that republicans will rescue eventual democratic nominee by putting forward an outofthemainstream nominee clintons are nothing if not shrewd and they ve lived through entire era of postwar american politics so hillary clinton would be last to believe what have heard with increasing frequency that in end no one of real heft even vice president joe biden will challenge her for democratic nomination she nearly won in and she will steamroll over minor contenders who do most frequently mentioned in minor category are former gov brian schweitzer of montana and gov martin o malley of maryland o malley also made a littlenoticed appearance mcauliffe inauguration sen elizabeth warren of massachusetts would be a major opponent should she run but she insists she will not when california gov jerry brown also bowed out nbc news s first read called it a reminder that hillary clinton will probably face little to no serious competition if she runs possible sure but history s guide tells us otherwise a consensus choice for a majorparty presidential nomination exceedingly rare and this where nixon comparison comes in incumbent presidents often find their secondterm nominations nearly unopposed though even in this rarefied group there are notable exceptions harry truman lyndon johnson jimmy carter and george hw bush truman and johnson withdrew in and respectively partly because of intraparty opposition but when no incumbent was running only precedent for a consensus choice in entire postworld war ii era richard nixon in this impressive feat was nonetheless achieved with some difficulty and embarrassment nixon thought he had averted a serious gop challenge when new york gov nelson rockefeller withdrew as a potential candidate in december but nixon s unappealing persona and substantial baggage from political wars of s and s worried many republicans eager for a third consecutive white house victory rockefeller sensed it and reconsidered print although members of congress are now absolutely outraged pentagon trying to recoup bonuses given out to thousands of troops it turns out congress actually knew about this problem for least two years andreas mueller chief of federal policy for california national guard wrote an email to california congressional delegation stating guard told members of congress about bonus reclamation issue two years ago los angeles times reports in fact mueller noted that guard had even offered a solution but congress took zero action in effect scandal stems back to about a decade when national guard was called upon to supply more troops guard officials were only supposed to give out bonuses to highvalue positions like intelligence or civil affairs to incentivize more soldiers to head to iraq and afghanistan during a marked troop shortage but that rule was ignored and thousands of soldiers received bonuses they weren t actually supposed to unbeknownst to them now pentagon wants those bonuses back and with interest since story first broke saturday members of congress have declared how abhorrent it for veterans to be targeted with tax liens and wage garnishment for refusing to pay back these bonuses gop rep duncan hunter a marine corps reservist wrote a letter to secretary of defense ash carter sunday asking for him to get involved as to find a solution to this boneheaded decision other side of aisle house democratic leader nancy pelosi sent house speaker paul ryan a letter monday arguing that as soon as congress gets back in session members should immediately pass legislation to halt pentagon s collection efforts democratic sens dianne feinstein and barbara boxer also wrote a letter to carter insisting that he had power to simply forgive debts without any action from congress thousands of service members are paying price for mistakes made by california national guard managers some of whom are now serving jail time or paying restitution for their crimes feinstein and boxer said it outrageous to hold these service members and their families responsible for illegal behavior of others but in meantime california guard certainly does not have legal power to forgive debts however much it might want to do so article reposted with permission from daily caller shares

a texas jury reached a guilty verdict in murder trial of eddie ray routh exmarine charged with killing former us navy seal chris kyle author of memoir american sniper routh was sentenced to life in prison without parole for shooting kyle and his friend chad littlefield to death a gun range near fort worth in defense lawyers had argued that routh suffers from paranoid schizophrenia routh had pleaded not guilty by reason of insanity kyles autobiography published in spent months new york times bestseller list and was adapted by director clint eastwood in film american sniper in kyle portrayed by bradley cooper

as marco rubio settles into his new role as a rising toptier candidate most of his opponents in republican presidential race are showing a reluctance and even an unwillingness to engage him directly national stage spotlight rubio intensifying in media as journalists investigate senator s political record and background but he otherwise left facing relatively low hurdles for now bypassing kind of heated personal clashes that have shaped nomination race for rubio it a return to lofty status he had after he burst onto national scene five years ago many republican elites are once again celebrating him as party s golden boy if not its strongest generalelection candidate and fear seeing him bruised too badly during primary season he s articulate attractive and young his rivals don t want him to win but no one wants to lose him said vin weber a prominent jeb bush supporter of course politics a roughandtumble sport and he ll need to take a few punches but end of day this a party that needs to find ways to appeal to hispanic voters and having him side an asset other candidates have not figured out how to deal with what some are calling marco moment hinting critiques and possible antirubio ads to come but hesitating to make rubio their main target dan balz ten months in gop race has no obvious frontrunner donald trump has castigated rubio stump and in twitter messages as a total lightweight who weak immigration but under primetime glare of tuesday night s republican presidential debate outspoken mogul fell silent about senator from florida when sen ted cruz tex tried to draw a contrast he did so only with a thickly veiled line about sugar subsidies something few voters could connect to rubio and although bush s campaign previewed a litany of possible attacks rubio former florida governor didn t say a word in milwaukee about his onetime protege bush sees rubio as a direct competitor for support of wealthy donors and party leaders bush s campaign and its allied super pac right to rise have signaled possible lines of attack in a strategy presentation to donors a couple of weeks ago bush campaign branded rubio disparagingly as a gop obama in candidates oct debate bush lunged rubio over his spotty senate voting record even suggesting that he resign his seat but offensive backfired and in two weeks since bush has steered away from rubio dilemma further complicated by pressure from many of bush s floridabased financial backers who also like rubio and want bush to go easy him asked why bush did not scrutinize rubio during milwaukee exchange campaign manager danny diaz said debate was a serious one where important issues were discussed and he urged reporters to stay tuned for next debate dec look forward to catching up with all good friends in las vegas diaz said bush s surrogates indicated that more heat rubio would arrive in short order it s a long campaign al cardenas a floridabased bush supporter who also close to rubio told reporters in milwaukee everybody s going to be scrutinized those who do well will get scrutinized more gop strategist ari fleischer put it more bluntly candidates will chop each other up if they thought it would help them every one of those candidates think it should be them and they can give every reason why it shouldn t be rubio fourth gop debate more about party fault lines than personal attacks mostly substantive nature of tuesday s debate sponsored by fox business network and wall street journal did not invite many personal skirmishes in one exception rand paul went after rubio for wanting to increase military spending but in a tense exchange with senator from kentucky rubio responded by articulating a hawkish worldview and portrayed paul as championing an isolationist foreign policy trump described debate s atmosphere as lacking fireworks it was amazing because lights went and never know what s going to happen are going to be attacked and do go for kill right he said wednesday msnbc s morning joe that was not really necessary trump s campaign manager corey lewandowski said billionaire contender will continue to talk about his differences with rubio especially immigration people are going to keep looking rubio s record lewandowski said they re going to ask themselves if they really want another firstterm senator as president someone who may not have right experience those concerns aren t going away rubio s advisers presented lack of engagement tuesday night as a sign of rubio s strength phrase where come from there s no education in second kick of mule terry sullivan rubio s campaign manager told reporters kind of feel like folks figured out that taking marco isn t such a great idea don t think anybody s really itching to take marco debate stage perhaps best encapsulation of rubio s good fortune came when moderator maria bartiromo asked him how his résumé stacks up against democrat hillary rodham clinton s decades of service rubio could not help but grin it was softest of softballs an opening for yearold senator to cast himself as candidate of future eric fehrnstrom who was an adviser to gop nominee mitt romney said rubio has a gift of golden tongue but he added don t think any candidate that stage thinks that they re somehow immune from political attacks rubio s biggest vulnerability immigration while he did not address that length in tuesday s debate would expect when attacks go up tv that immigration will be in mix supporters of rival campaigns voiced surprise that rubio was not drawn into immigration discussion considering his leadership in senate s gang of eight pushing for comprehensive immigration reform rubio has since disavowed legislation and has hardened his immigration positions overall there should be more scrutiny of rubio no doubt about it said bob smith a former senator from new hampshire who backing cruz it s almost as if media ignoring his immigration record or least not giving it much coverage conservatives up here however are well aware of it and sense that they ll continue to vet rubio their own dan pfeiffer a former senior adviser to president obama said other republican candidates may be afraid of tangling with rubio in a debate rubio skilled delivering a scripted rehearsed counterpunch and they had to know he would be prepared pfeiffer said he added that cruz seems to be patiently waiting for his moment to make this attack cruz wants to see whites of rubio s eyes before he fires his biggest guns indeed cruz has been laying foundation for contrast he hopes to make with rubio if nominating contest narrows to two of them as some pundits predict in debate after debate cruz has positioned himself as a hardline opponent of amnesty and more generally as a conservative purist in his immigration comments tuesday night cruz did not single out rubio but his advisers did with reporters afterward freshman texan will draw contrasts issues cruz spokesman rick tyler said senator rubio was for gang of eight senator cruz wasn t right now re just beginning that and ve got three months till iowa jose a delreal in washington contributed to this report

three things are inevitable in this life death taxes and conservative claims that simply can t afford to give lowwage workers a pay boost nobel prizewinning economist takes latter inevitability in his new york times column today seizing recent developments to illustrate why arguments against wage increases don t withstand serious scrutiny take mcdonald s announcement this week that it would pay its workers above minimum wage per hour outlets owned and operated by mcdonald s itself move still falls well short of what workers and their advocates seek affects workers but it doesn t apply to workers employed by mcdonald s american franchisees while modest in scopemcdonald s move coming heels of similar wage boosts by boldfaced names like walmart and target suggests that maybe it s not that hard to give american workers a raise after all krugman writes while free market fundamentalists might retort that global competition will sink firms that boost worker pay krugman responds by noting that most american workers are employed in service industries that aren t exposed to international trade what about technology isn t it an unassailable maxim of modern economics that can afford to pay larger wages only to highly skilled workers ones who can t be replaced by machines as mike konczal wrote recently technology but a small part of larger inequality phenomenon key factors he pointed out include tax policy financialization of american economy deunionization and conscious political choice not to raise minimum wage what s more krugman observes workers are people relations between employers and employees are more complicated than simple supply and demand this truism borne out in empirical evidence with comparisons of states that raised their minimum wages with neighboring states that didn t showing that higher pay does not mean fewer jobs similar factors explain another puzzle about labor markets way different firms in what looks like same business can pay very different wages classic comparison between walmart with its low wages low morale and very high turnover and costco offers higher wages and better benefits and makes up difference with better productivity and worker loyalty true two retailers serve different markets costco s merchandise higherend and its customers more affluent but comparison nonetheless suggests that paying higher wages costs employers a lot less than might think and this in turn suggests that it shouldn t be all that hard to raise wages across board suppose that were to give workers some bargaining power by raising minimum wages making it easier for them to organize and crucially aiming for full employment rather than finding reasons to choke off recovery despite low inflation given what now know about labor markets results might be surprisingly big because a moderate push might be all it takes to persuade much of american business to turn away from lowwage strategy that has dominated society for so many years there s historical precedent for this kind of wage push middleclass society now dwindling in rearview mirrors didn t emerge spontaneously it was largely created by great compression of wages that took place during world war ii with effects that lasted for more than a generation

well be harbinger of transcendence apparently there s another force in universe capable of standing up to american indeed global militaryindustrial status quo slowly slowly this force organizing itself and taking human shape this isn t a simple process after all game of empire game called war game of domination has been coalescing political power for several thousand years now but current military budget was birthed by wars of th century william hartung writing recently tomdispatch shows fascinating connectedness of wars that followed ve and vj days as corporate beneficiaries of big war aligned with mainstream politicians of both major parties and coalesced into washington consensus over decades they have engaged in an ongoing struggle to maintain military spending breathtakingly high levels and avoid any sort of transition to something called peace two pillars of this consensus as hartung points out are ideology of armed exceptionalism that a shifting array of enemies are out there itching to destroy us but will persist in mission to maintain order in every corner of planet and strategic placement of arms production facilities and military bases in key states and congressional districts ensuring entrenched political power for arms lobby so world war ii initially as know morphed into cold war america s crusade against communism was set into motion in by a longclassified report nsc prepared by state and defense departments and cia urging vigorous containment of soviet expansion and development of hydrogen bomb president truman was somewhat taken aback costs associated with report s recommendations according to historycom and he hesitated to publicly support a program that would result in heavy tax increases for american public particularly since increase would be spent defending united states during a time of peace peace what a nuisance but thank god korea came along as an aide to secretary of state dean acheson put it time korean war gave militarists enemy they needed and nuclear arms race and so much else was born military budget was set for decades as hartung points out next terrible hurdle budget boys faced was in wake of vietnam war ended in that war and universal draft that essentially pulled whole country into a frontrow seat for it spawned a passionate antiwar movement and ultimately a moldering national disgust for war known as vietnam syndrome cold war continued but us military was confined to proxy wars for a while and had to rethink its strategy two things happened universal draft was ended removing most of american middle class from a lifeanddeath stake in military operations and saddam hussein ally was recast as adolf hitler and in president george hw bush embarked a month of war with iraq known as operation desert storm not only did us win this war and kill over iraqis and ultimately cause hideous health problems for american soldiers but perhaps most importantly by god ve kicked vietnam syndrome once and for all so president proclaimed unfortunately for war consensus soviet union dissolved a short time later and cold war ended and a peace dividend loomed hartung notes that gen colin powell then chairman of joint chiefs of staff expressed situation thus m running out of demons m running out of villains m down to castro and kim ilsung would military spending be diverted to infrastructure repair free education universal health care well no bill clinton presidency found a few new demons it fought a war in kosovo and domestic front vastly expanded prisonindustrial complex meanwhile neocon think tanks cogitated and one of them project for a new american century reflecting fact that geopolitical thinking had stopped with world war ii decided that what america needed was a new pearl harbor and then came presidency of george w bush and global war terror and us military budget was carved seemingly in stone barack obama was elected president in promise of serious change sustainable warfare and here are immersed in a noname global war that has no logical end and few serious critics in world of mainstream media and politics but maybe american democracy not closed disagree about many things but with a few exceptions they all support an enormous military budget an expensive overseas expeditionary force and unilateral acts of force when necessary to protect us national interests understood broadly it s an odd paradox that trump who blathers about making america great again departs from this consensus he won t win and he shouldn t win for a million reasons but in his recklessness he has touched raw anger of a sizable chunk of american electorate sanders speaking with compassion and integrity and delivering a far different message managed to tap same well of public outrage and as feffer noted mainstream worried that political parties will realize that bring war dollars home message can win a national election and disrupt comfortable revolvingdoor consensus and world war ii will finally end not this year but maybe four years from now if refuse to let war consensus have any peace in interim robert koehler an awardwinning chicagobased journalist republican donald trump s and democrat hillary clinton s efforts to portray themselves as assertive adversaries of islamic state terror group are increasingly defining presidential race and never more so than in wake of massacre in nice france this week although authorities have not yet tied attack to jihadistinspired terrorism both candidates immediately responded to latest in a string of attacks home and abroad that have heightened voter anxiety by vowing aggressive efforts to combat islamic state they have done so in strikingly different ways she with specific proposals and he with broad promises but together their messages effectively assure a wartime posture in white house next year no matter who becomes president and they mark a sharp departure campaign trail from noninterventionist sentiment that thensen barack obama rode into office eight years ago intense jockeying was encapsulated in a round of dueling telephone interviews thursday evening with fox news host bill o reilly following deadly truck attack in southeastern france that killed more than people assembled to celebrate bastille day interview was especially notable for clinton who rarely speaks to fox news but may have been trying to engage with conservative voters who care deeply about national security and may not be sold trump ve got to do more to understand that this a war against these terrorist groups radical jihadist groups clinton said it s a different kind of war need to be smart about how wage it but have to be determined that re going to win it in his interview trump ticked through a list of recent attacks as evidence that a change in management style top a necessity to blunt future attacks look san bernardino look paris a hundred and thirty people killed and so many injured in paris from that attack and look orlando it s out of control trump said and bill unless get strong and know really strong and very very smart leadership it s only going to get worse in truck rampage experts see potential shift toward cruder deadlier acts of terror backtoback interviews underscored differences in two candidates approaches trump has embraced debate over terrorism to emphasize his proposed ban most foreign muslims clinton vigorously opposes and advance a muscular but nebulous strategy to stamp out terrorists abroad clinton has been more specific arguing for a smart but strong effort to combat islamic state focusing ratcheting up intelligence cooperation between united states and its allies and fighting radical propaganda online clinton must walk a fine line even as she pursues centrist republicans including veterans of bush administration s foreign policy shop she trying to consolidate support among democrats after a bruising primary with sen bernie sanders of vermont many liberals already see her as too hawkish going back to her vote for war in iraq in contributed heavily to her primary defeat six year later against obama there s a lot of concern about another war in middle east conceded sen jeff merkley dore a sanders endorser who helped candidate win a landslide in his own state s primary but look this not a choice between hillary clinton and gandhi this a choice between hillary clinton and donald trump and d be far more terrified of trump s reckless foreign policy than of hillary clinton trump faces challenges of his own while his freewheeling rhetoric and his controversial muslim ban and proposed wall along mexican border were big hits in republican primary they have exposed him to accusations in general election that he not serious and illprepared for rigors of major security decisions with republican and democratic conventions taking place over next two weeks clinton and trump will be auditioning national stage a time when public s attention has been repeatedly directed to global terrorism threats it s an electorate that profoundly insecure and unsure about what happens next both in terms of economy and increasingly in terms of international policy and terrorism said anita dunn former white house communications director in obama administration making case to american people that are person to address their issues and to make country feel secure again really predominant challenge for both campaigns assailant was entirely unknown to antiterror units prosecutor says in his fox news interview trump said he would ask congress for a war declaration islamic state a move rarely used in american history if look it this war coming from all different parts and frankly it s war and re dealing with people without uniforms trump said in her interview clinton used similar language to describe threat posed by islamic state but asked cnn whether she would endorse trump s proposal to seek authorization from congress for war she made clear that she has no intention of drawing us or nato troops into a fight with islamic state she called it islamic state s dream to pull us ground troops into a war in region would point people to read more about what hopes and ambitions of isis happen to be clinton said using a common acronym for group they would love to draw united states into a ground war in syria they actually think end times could be hastened if had some great confrontation in that region she added hours after coup attempt in turkey clinton became first candidate to issue a statement calling for calm and respect for laws institutions and basic human rights and freedoms sentiment closely echoed comments of obama and secretary of state john f kerry and offered an implicit contrast with trump who had not yet commented situation still clinton has not shied away from supporting an aggressive and frontal approach to threat she has argued that united states and its allies should continue to reclaim territory from islamic state boxing their fighters into smaller and smaller territory she has criticized allies in region for not doing enough to stop radicalization within their own borders and she called countries including saudi arabia and qatar to invest more in global effort to fight islamic state she has also embraced language of more conservative politicians after orlando attack a gay nightclub by a selfradicalized convert to islamic state clinton said radical jihadist terrorism and radical islamist terrorism are virtually same after once tuesday night s republican presidential debate was first since islamic stateinspired terrorist shooting in san bernardino california and not surprisingly most of questions tossed out by cnn moderators dealt with national security and terrorism in days since san bernardino public fears over terrorism have grown and president obama has been doing what he can to tamp down anxiety and encourage people not to give in to fear that terrorists work to inspire but as cnn debate made painfully clear republican presidential candidates have quite opposite goal in mind they want everyone to be scared witless by looming terrorist menace and worried that they will be next to die new jersey gov chris christie back main stage thanks to cnn s expansive inclusion rules and his own improved poll numbers in new hampshire did most to actively scare shit out of every person unfortunate enough to be watching in his opening statement he announced that america has been betrayed by leadership that barack obama and hillary clinton have provided to this country as proof of that betrayal and treason christie pointed to yesterday s closing of los angeles unified school district based a threat he never got around to mentioning that threat had long since been revealed as a hoax but still christie was worried about children filled with anxiety and mothers who will take those children tomorrow morning to bus stop and fathers of los angeles who tomorrow will head off to work and wonder about safety of their wives and their children they live in terror these nameless gendered stereotypes because obama couldn t protect them from threat posed by madbombercockli asked what he would do to ensure that fear does not paralyze american in aftermath of terrorism christie explained that paralyzing fear unfortunately new normal under barack obama everywhere in america a target for these terrorists he said giving everyone a reason to be afraid always christie s closing statement was a riff many of friends and others in neighborhood lost their lives that day christie even offered himself as a menacing presence leaning forward and shifting back and forth behind his lectern like a rhinoceros sizing up a jeep full of safari tourists his message all evening was blunt and terrifying will die unless vote for not far behind christie was sen marco rubio who made a point of spelling out in detail menacing nature of islamic state this most sophisticated terror group that has ever threatened world or united states of america he warned this a very significant threat face and president has left us unsafe he made a special point of noting islamic state s sophistication this a radical jihadist group that increasingly sophisticated in its ability for example to radicalize american citizens this not just most capable it most sophisticated terror threat have ever faced he warned about next time there an attack this country while pushing for reinstatement of metadata collection provisions in patriot act sen ted cruz went a slightly different route linking republican panic over syrian refugees to both san bernardino terrorist attack had nothing to do with refugees and senate immigration reform bill to score a bankshot hit rubio cruz because front line with isis isn t just in iraq and syria it s in kennedy airport and rio grande border security national security and know one of most troubling aspects of rubioschumer gang of eight bill was that it gave president obama blanket authority to admit refugees including syrian refugees without mandating any background checks whatsoever now ve seen what happened in san bernardino when are letting people in when fbi can t vet them it puts american citizens risk all in all it was a grim bleak and frightful debate that saw several leading candidates try to stoke heightened public anxiety over terrorism there were no acknowledgements that acts of terrorism are exceptionally rare with just americans killed in jihadist terrorist attacks since more americans have died as a result of homegrown rightwing terrorism but that threat didn t earn a single mention debate even though one such attack took place just days before san bernardino this was more about resurrecting national security politics of bush years when republicans would conjure threat of imminent terrorist slaughter american soil to cast democrats as weak and to justify rollback of civil liberties in interest of safety a scared voter a motivated voter and republicans have every interest in keeping people as terrified as possible

that represents a shift in trump s direction since last month s postabc poll showed clinton leading by points in new poll clinton leads by points among all adults percent to percent compared with a point lead among this wider group last month both candidates remain highly unpopular two most unpopular in history of postabc polling by about to percent to percent americans view trump unfavorably clinton s numbers are not quite as negative percent favorable and percent unfavorable half of all registered voters say they have strongly unfavorable views of trump while percent say they have almost in say they are dissatisfied with choice of trump vs clinton fiftyfour percent of clinton s supporters say they are mainly voting against trump while percent of trump supporters say they are careless in their handling of sensitive classified material in their email exchanges previous postabc poll showed clinton with a larger lead than some other national surveys taken around same time whether or how much shift toward trump in current survey was affected by how fbi investigation was resolved can t be measured other recent polls show difference in race nationally to be in low single digits with clinton generally enjoying advantage republicans begin their nominating convention here in cleveland monday and will conclude thursday with expected nomination of trump with gov mike pence of indiana as his running mate democratic convention in philadelphia will begin july with clinton poised to become first woman nominated for president by a major party she still mulling her lean toward trump to percent although winning independents no guarantee of winning presidency four years ago mitt romney won among independents while losing to president obama clinton enjoys support of in selfidentified liberals while in conservatives back trump moderates go decisively for clinton to percent contest between clinton and trump highlights one potential shift in electorate that will of to percent but collegeeducated white voters have been shifting toward democrats and poll underscores that competition for those voters will be hardfought and potentially decisive in election s divided percent trump percent clinton with an outsize percent volunteering support for neither when gender included in analysis poll finds that white women with college degrees narrowly support point margin among registered voters question of candidate would do most to bring needed change to washington by a margin of five points he seen as more honest and trustworthy clinton has a similar
