videos atttime warner merger another media consolidation that puts profits over consumers deals are driven by wall street s insatiable desire for shortterm growth any cost a media analyst a consumer advocacy ngo wrote warning about risks of deal november be sociable share minneapolis media analysts warn that a proposed merger between att and time warner more likely to enhance corporate bottom lines and pad pockets of wall street investors than benefit consumers big mergers like this inevitably mean higher prices for real people to pay down money borrowed to finance these deals and compensate top executives said matt wood policy director free press an ngo that protects net neutrality and online press freedom in an oct press release media first reported that att was in informal talks to merge with time warner oct by oct att announced that time warner had agreed to be bought out by telecommunications giant for billion currently time warner represents one of a shrinking number of mass media conglomerates that increasingly control vast majority of news available to americans att one of world s largest providers of mobile phone and landline services and as owner of directv a major player in television marketplace as well corporate executives have promised that merger could make more content available to consumers offer new options for mobile viewing and provide alternatives to traditional cable tv representatives of both companies have also tried to mollify concerns that deal would violate antitrust laws claiming it represents vertical integration of two different markets rather than a merger of competitors many media experts have expressed concern and skepticism about these claims particularly in regard to potential benefits to consumers wood suggested att s buyout of directv was completed in july should serve as a warning about possible effects of this new larger merger model he warned deals are driven by wall street s insatiable desire for shortterm growth any cost and just as att s recent purchase of directv was quickly followed by price hikes there s every reason to expect this potential tieup would cost internet users and tv viewers dearly too kevin kelleher a reporter time magazine weighed in oct he wrote that deal makes sense for media executives less so for consumers as it s unclear how bringing content creators and internet service providers together would actually benefit end user he continued for now concerns over deal seem to be outweighing benefits could end up being negligible for decades pipes that streamed digital content remained largely independent from companies that provided content and no consumers complained meanwhile several senators have come out in opposition to proposed merger citing concerns about ultimate implications for consumers role of washington s revolving door into corporate world and what this buyout could mean for future of media consolidation sunday al franken a former tv actor and senator representing minnesota told new york times media reporter jim rutenberg that merger could increase prices and reduce number of choices available to consumers when company that controls pipes so to speak owns this very very large content provider it can cause a whole bunch of different horribles for consumers franken said elizabeth warren a senator from massachusetts known for her consumer advocacy objected to christine varney s involvement in deal varney an antitrust lawyer who has been hired to oversee att and time warner merger previously worked for obama administration investigating antitrust claims monday warren told fortune americans have had it with regulators like varney who talk a good game about holding bad guys accountable while counting down days until they can collect a fat paycheck from corporations they were supposed to regulate revolving door out of control if want to hold corporate lawbreakers accountable can t ask their friends to do it bernie sanders senator from vermont and former democratic presidential candidate also objected to deal in an open letter published wednesday medium in addition to echoing concerns shared by others like franken he warned that buyout could provoke future media mergers that would further consolidate an already limited market a time when telecommunications and media industries are already too concentrated should be focused opening those markets to more competition not less he wrote in case that merger does go through att s ties to national security state may also give rise to serious privacy concerns a day after atttime warner merger was officially announced kenneth lipp a reporter daily beast revealed that att storing customer information and selling it for profit that of course came more than three years after edward snowden leaked classified information detailed telecommunications giant s close collaboration with nsa to spy millions of americans where go what watch text and share with whom speak all internet searches and preferences all gathered and vertically integrated sold to police and perhaps in future to any number of att s corporate customers democracy now host amy goodman and her frequent collaborator denis moynihan wrote in an editorial published thursday can t know if alexander graham bell envisioned this brave new digital world when he invented telephone but this future that fast approaching unless people rise up and stop this merger be sociable share

for jeb bush s campaign august was a cruel month donald trump s attacks former florida governor as a lowenergy politician were beginning to stick and two were bickering over immigration issue before bush team was what to do about it some advisers argued for an aggressive response even to point of challenging trump to some kind of oneonone confrontation others resisted believing trump s candidacy was unsustainable while some cautioned against getting into a pigpen with a pig as one adviser recalled others described it as trying to wrestle with a stump those summer days crystallized plight of a campaign that had begun with enormous expectations and extraordinary resources as scion of one of america s dynastic political families sought to follow his father and brother to presidency what would become a crucial moment bush s team had no clear strategy for a rival who was beginning to hijack republican party that bush family had helped to build other than to stay course set months earlier of telling bush s story to voters there was no consensus senior strategist david kochel said of discussions about how to combat threat of trump s candidacy other campaigns were wrestling with same problems but as frontrunner in polls time bush would suffer more than others saturday night candidacy that had begun with such promise ended quietly after a disappointingly weak fourthplace finish in south carolina ever gracious realist bush announced in his concession speech that he would end his campaign as trump continued to soar as gop frontrunner have stood ground refusing to bend to political winds he said whether jeb bush ever had a chance to win republican nomination in a campaign year that proved so ill fitting for a rusty politician who preferred policy papers to political combat a question that will be debated long after race has ended donald trump channeled worst fears frustrations and anxiety of voters but he also magnified those same feelings sally bradshaw bush s chief strategist and confidant said sunday in an email it would be difficult for any solutionsoriented conservative to tackle trump in this environment much less one who was seen as having been so much a part of establishment he was never going to be an angry guy and voters wanted angry mike murphy chief strategist for bush s super pac right to rise explained what had happened this way sunday theory was to dominate establishment lane into actual voting primaries he said that was strategy and it did not work think it was right strategy for jeb problem was there was a huge antiestablishment wave establishment lane was smaller than thought it would be marketplace was looking for something different and ll find out how that ends when have a nominee result one of most startling failures in modern history of american politics fall of house of bush it a human story about struggles of one of most successful former governors in america in his bid to become president like his father and brother set against backdrop of one of strangest political cycles country has seen in years beyond underestimating anger in electorate three other problems led to bush s downfall first candidate and his team misjudged degree of bush fatigue among republicans aides said an internal poll conducted last fall showed discouraging news roughly twothirds of voters had issues with bush s family ties bush stuff was holding him back said one aide who saw polling data obviously knew it was an issue but even still gap between it and other issues don t think thought it would be that big for jeb bush challenge remains making it about jeb not bush second bush and his team miscalculated role and power of money and traditional television commercials in race during first six months of bush raised more than million most of it stockpiled in right to rise a strategy that seemed right time but came cost of not dealing with other pressing needs didn t use that time to introduce him as a unique brand said vin weber an outside adviser used it to raise money don t want to say they made an obvious and clear mistake but in retrospect it was a mistake aggressive fundraising came to be known as shock and awe an echo of initial bombing of iraq by usled forces before invasion in campaign context it could be read as code to other potential candidates to get out of way but prodigious fundraising of bush s broad network scared off no one as bush campaign would learn every credible candidate today has a few billionaire friends who can enrich a super pac in end all that money came to symbolize frustration rather than power third bush ran a campaign that whether deliberate or not was rooted in past managed by loyalists who admired bush and enjoyed his confidence but who like candidate found themselves in unfamiliar political terrain his advisers were convinced from start that more voters learned about what bush had done as governor of florida from to they would flock to him as their presidential candidate bush stubbornly held to that approach even as evidence mounted that it was out of step with voters doug gross a prominent iowa republican recalled meeting with bush in july in kennebunkport maine to talk about impending campaign he definitely wanted to run he s always had it in him and knew this was his last chance gross said he was trying to figure out how to do it his own way was struck by his obstinate avoidance of any political discussion he wanted to do it his way or no way in contrast to doldrums of august july seemed a glorious time for bush team early that month team jeb gathered in kennebunkport to celebrate that campaign and two allied political committees had together raised nearly an unprecedented million numbers were made public as nearly major bush fundraisers assembled to mingle with bush family and campaign advisers guests were transported in blackandred trolleys to walker s point bush family compound group gathered for a photo with former president george hw bush and barbara bush that evening bush touted team s record fundraising as guests dined lobster rolls and hamburgers a luxury resort tucked among a forest of birch groves and balsam fir it was incredibly memorable to be there with several generations said jay zeidman a houstonbased investor who helped raise money from young professionals next day donors got briefings from senior bush aides including bradshaw campaign manager danny diaz and finance director heather larrison they laid out how campaign planned to take contenders such as sen ted cruz of texas sen marco rubio of florida and new jersey gov chris christie throughout there was little mention of donald trump that time jeb bush invited top donors to his parents house none of us thought he would last summer said one person who was in attendance that moment however trump was already in process of undermining bush s candidacy if bush had ever gone up against someone like trump it didn t show trump was a new and different kind of rival one given to personal insults rather than policy debates who monopolized media coverage and got away with provocative statements that would have sunk normal politicians after marching in two july parades a rainy saturday in new hampshire reporters asked bush about trump s claim that mexico was allowing immigrants to illegally cross into united states it was one of hundreds of times he would face such questions bush said absolutely he was offended by trump s rhetoric re going to win when re hopeful and optimistic and big and broad rather than grrrrrr he said literally growling just angry all time that very night trump attacked bush as soft immigration and took aim as well bush s wife columba who was born in mexico and entered country legally retweeting and then deleting a disparaging comment about her nothing however cut as close to bone as trump s claim that bush was too lowenergy to serve as president accusation was laughable until it began to stick trump s charge was in fact a proxy for a different and more difficult argument to combat that bush was neither strong nor edgy enough for a party seething with anger grass roots inside bushtrump melodrama decades of tension and discomfort nobody tapped into it for all polling all focus groups said theresa kostrzewa a north carolina lobbyist who raised money for campaign biggest thing they did was miss was just how angry american electorate was and that trump would be their captain ahab bush s advisers would contest that claim they could see anger they said issue was what to do about it donors political operatives and big thinkers from around country urged us to ignore trump for months bradshaw said there was no one in news media or operative class time who felt trump would ultimately be a serious contender for nomination same time others feared that engaging trump was almost beneath bush and would thrust candidate into a neverending game of chargecountercharge jeb should be bigger than this another aide recalled thinking over right to rise murphy sent a clear signal trump not fight right now if other campaigns wish that re going to uncork money donald trump they ll be disappointed murphy told washington post in late august trump frankly other people s problem that moment bush team s analysis showed that no trump voters were likely to shift their support to bush sunday murphy said that attacking trump would only have benefited other candidates bush s campaign needed to consolidate establishment lane while hoping that trump and cruz would sort out competition among antiestablishment candidates bradshaw also dismissed complaints from some donors that she cut candidate off from advice noting that bush long has been active email bradshaw responded in a message by saying donors constantly gave conflicting advice attack trump don t attack trump smile more smile less look like are smirking didn t tell people they were wrong not style did a lot of listening and m sure there were things could have done better but withholding info from governor simply did not happen in campaign for much of autumn bush s engagement with trump was onagain offagain skirmish then turning away not until late last year did he truly start a concerted and sustained series of attacks aides said bush was particularly affected by terrorist attacks in paris and san bernardino calif and felt as one adviser put it that it was time to stand up to bully jeb was only candidate with political courage time and frankly throughout last six months to take trump directly for doing something that governor felt was very harmful to party and to country bradshaw wrote there was no hesitation that point given his comments about women about hispanics his lack of knowledge issues of national security and and bush s failure to come to terms with one of downsides of his family name came to a head over a fourday period in may when he stumbled over decision by his brother former president george w bush to go to war in iraq changing his answer a daily basis bush came across as a flatfooted campaigner clearly uncomfortable articulating his views most critical moment of his brother s presidency but it also highlighted doubleedged nature of being a candidate named bush in a january washington postabc news poll nearly in americans held an unfavorable view of bush he was only republican with a negative favorability rating percent said they had a favorable impression of former governor while percent rated him negatively his rankings grew worse as campaign progressed a fundamental weakness supporters said was lack of a coherent rationale for bush s candidacy and failure to make inroads with activists right end of day it wasn t clear name was ever surmountable said a bush donor if name was going to be surmounted it would have to be because there was a fresh set of ideas bush offered ideas but in a campaign dominated by trump they were ignored or lost to most voters one of biggest tactical advantages bush appeared to have early a richly endowed super pac was not invincible weapon his team thought it would be it cut off his access to a key adviser murphy whom he installed group s helm it also meant that during first six months of last year nearly all of coverage about bush focused how he was socking away millions into super pac all while maintaining that he had not decided whether to run in an election brimming with anger toward wealthy elite bush seemed almost flippant about his pursuit of big dollars murphy was convinced that much of what was taking place was noise and that when voters began to check in super pac s financial might would be overpowering first committee would use it to lay out bush s biography then as necessary group would turn its arsenal his rivals job just to amplify his story and what he s saying and banked enough cash that nobody s turning speaker off murphy told bloomberg politics in october back campaign headquarters team hewed to that timetable and sensibility once bush record was burned into voters minds attitudes would shift bradshaw said time a bush donor complained murphy had a timetable and nothing mattered until december and january by end of january right to rise had raced through least million out of million it had collected according to federal election commission filings almost million went into a barrage of television ads online videos slick mailers and voter phone calls to no avail mel sembler a former bush ambassador who helped raise money for super pac and served its governance board said he believes group s strategy was sound had confidence in mike and think did best could in deploying of resources sembler said that s not where problem timing was not right for jeb candidate was just not connecting with electorate final months were difficult for bush after a particularly weak performance during a debate in boulder colo in october in rubio appeared to get better of him there were suggestions that he might quit campaign right then reporters who made inquiries about possibility were brushed off in middle of it all bush spotted a reporter who was a regular trail with him hey didn t drop out did he shouted know that kind of stuff really gets juices going m going to win this thing and when do re going to give a big hug through it all bush attempted to keep both good humor and determination in face of inevitable was stunned by how well he handled last month of this campaign when writing was wall said tim miller bush s communications director it hard to go out there every day and put a fake smiley face he was in really high spirits and didn t lash out people in private throughout last two months final indignity in a campaign that had suffered through many came three days before saturday s primary when south carolina gov nikki haley endorsed rubio rather than a man she described as a friend and mentor when it ended saturday night bush told saddened supporters put forward details innovative conservative plans to address mounting challenges that face because despite what might have heard ideas matter policy matters his final remarks as a presidential candidate were a reflection of campaign he had constructed from start one he had built to his unique specifications nonetheless proved to be a mismatch for a political environment that caught him by surprise and for he paid a hefty price

pinterest it s been a quartercentury since supreme court justice clarence thomas made history by becoming only second black judge ever to serve nation s highest court but won t see celebrations recognition or even acknowledgement of this feat in fact thomasderangement as daily caller calls it has not abated one bit this despite fact that impact thomas has had unequaled thomas one of nation s most influential lawmakers and has admirers from all legal corners yet he ridiculed belittled and assaulted with type of disgusting comments usually reserved for common criminals and deviants writing in new york times maureen dowd referred to justice as creepy guy who acted pervy toward anita hill and won this barrage of insults not just among his legal and intellectual peers either thomas has been completely wiped clean from any mention in new smithsonian national museum of african american history and culture his accuser anita hill however given prominent display exhibit features testimonies trumpeting her courage and surge of women s activism that ensued while making only peripheral reference to thomas despite these slights thomas continues to speak out a speech heritage foundation justice warned that are destroying institutions some point are going to have to recognize that are destroying institutions he said acknowledging that court might also partially be fault what have done to gain their confidence he asked perhaps should ask what have done to not earn it or to earn it court s lone africanamerican justice also defended his view that even prior supreme court precedents must be bent or broken to comply with constitution something most of his colleagues don t believe ve got lots of precedents out there that have been changed thomas said believe are obligated to rethink things constantly thomas ended with some positive words about former justice antonin scalia who he affectionately referred to as nino court has become evenly divided between liberals and conservatives a change that could become even more pronounced if hillary clinton wins presidency and controls who will replace scalia but thomas chose to dwell more his friendship with nino than ponder future without him he was from north and was from south but wound up same place he said referring to their views paramount importance of constitution and individual freedoms scalia he said fretted over big principles as well as smaller things such as punctuation and syntax

a collaborative effort between patrick miller of university of kansas and pamela johnston conover of university north carolina chapel hill offers new insight into growing phenomenon behind political polarization in united states study titled red and blue states of mind partisan hostility and voting in united states was published in political research quarterly march when only partisan voters vote only partisan candidates are elected authors argue that voters who are most likely to participate in elections are those who hold a very strong partisan identity as a result elections become less about substance and more reminiscent of a sports game where goal to win any cost university of kansas news service summed up study saying they found that many average voters with strong party commitments both democrats and republicans care more about their parties simply winning election than they do either ideology or issues unlike previous research study found that loyalty to party itself was source of partisan rivalry and incivility instead of a fundamental disagreement over issues trend toward greater polarization within american electorate has been happening for years but contributing factors are numerous and complex a study by pew research shows polarization has steadily increased since miller and conover s study examined a few possible causes one of was tendency for partisans to consume only media content that reinforces their own worldview in a video miller explained that these tendencies are having an impact congress itself by stifling compromise and breeding a political environment that lacks civility re not thinking about politics in way that most founders wanted to think about issues be open to compromise and not be attached to parties said miller re looking politics through a simplistic partisan view in think side good and their side bad among other things study found

rise in fatal shootings of us police new york state police engage in a manhunt for two prisoners richard matt and david sweat in friendship new york sunday heavilyarmed police converged towns in western new york state saturday to investigate possible sightings of two convicted murderers who escaped a maximumsecurity prison two weeks ago police said latest sighting of two convicts who escaped from a new york prison now places them close to canadian border more than miles from where state police conducted their search less than a day before investigators and military trucks arrived mountain view and owls head in franklin county ny late sunday in response to reports that a person had been seen fleeing from a hunting camp in area after breaking into a cabin glenn macneill acting franklin county district attorney told local nbc affiliate wptz associated press reported monday afternoon that state police maj charles guess said a news conference that authorities had specific items from adirondack cabin some miles west of prison and sent them to labs for dna and other testing he would not elaborate items but characterized latest search effort one of many over past days as a confirmed lead there are a number of factors that make this a complex search weather terrain environment and frankly vast scope of north country of adirondacks major guess said police said cabin sighting was unconfirmed but authorities nonetheless shifted hunt for clinton correctional facility inmates david sweat and richard matt from a rural mountainous area near pennsylvania border to upstate new york witnesses told wptz that helicopters are in air and checkpoints are in place roads in area miles west of prison where two inmates used power tools to cut holes through their cell walls to escape june still state police said in a news release late sunday that though they will continue to respond to reports of sightings a primary focus of search continues to be in dannemora area close to clinton correctional statement urged dannemora residents to stay alert adding if these men are spotted please call immediately do not approach as both are considered to be very dangerous new york state offering a reward of for information that leads to capture of either suspect for both us marshals service has placed sweat and matt their most wanted fugitives list and also offering a reward for information that leads to capture of either suspect will search under every rock behind every tree and structure until are confident that that area secure state police maj michael j cerretto said a news conference sweat was serving a life sentence without parole for killing a sheriffs deputy while mr matt was doing years to life for kidnapping torture and murder of his former boss christian science monitor reported that prison worker joyce mitchell remained in custody charges that she provided two men hacksaw blades chisels and other tools to aid in their escape she has pleaded not guilty officials said a corrections officer also has been placed administrative leave as part of investigation into mens escape state police will hold a media briefing with clinton county district attorney andrew wiley and county sheriff dave favro monday noon to provide updates search

wore a bandana over his face he was arrested by bureau of indian affairs police and later released without charge in video aired by democracy now as host amy goodman described man carrying rifle who has been identified as kyle thompson points a rifle protectors as he attempts to flee into water a standing rock sioux tribal member says he saw man driving down highway toward main resistance camp with an ar rifle passenger side of his truck goodman reported protectors chased down his truck and then pursued him foot in efforts to disarm him protectors said inside man s truck they found a dapl security id card and insurance papers listing his vehicle as insured by dapl that s dakota access pipeline according to goodman dallas goldtooth an organizer with indigenous environmental network witnessed encounter between armed contractor and water protectors he said democracy now it was a very terrifying moment for a lot of us watching mean to see this man pulling an assault rifle water protectors and think that many blessings and gratitude to some of military veterans within security from within oceti sakowin camp who stepped up to negotiate and to deescalate this man to really talk to him to make sure that he did not hurt anybody until bureau of indian affairs police officers could show up thompson appeared scene about same time that hundreds of police with militarized equipment surrounded a newly formed camp called treaty camp was setup by water protectors to reclaim unceded dakota territory affirmed as part of standing rock reservation in ft laramie treaty of sacred stone camp has led indigenous resistance to dakota access pipeline reported that police cleared blockades attacked water protectors with pepper spray and concussion grenades and used shotguns to fire rubber bullets a sound cannon was also deployed against water protectors as well as police brutally tore down encampment dakota access denies thompson was working for company however thompson posted facebook and claimed he was doing his job to photograph burning company equipment when he was confronted by demonstrators according to aptn national news standing rock sioux tribe apparently claims thompson fired off shots while thompson vehemently denies that any shots were ever fired he maintains fbi agents who took him into custody could back up his story however thompson s story becomes incredibly suspicious as he insists water protectors had knives and were dead set using those knives he says a water protector fired a flare video of thompson s confrontation in water definitely does not show any knifewielding water protectors trying to attack him what troubling fact that he was not dressed in a manner that would clearly indicate he was a security contractor for dapl he looked like an infiltrator one wonders what would have happened if he made it to treaty camp and engaged in disruptive behavior that police could then use to justify brute force used against water protectors it unknown what company thompson worked for but he was previously deployed in wars in iraq and afghanistan separately another suspicious act against indigenous water protectors occurred overnight october when a fire spread near oceti sakowin camp there was some mysterious incident of a vehicle that came out of nowhere that was almost acting as a distraction was spinning doughnuts in middle of road and then it sped off to south goldtooth shared democracy now and immediately after that flames were seen top of hill to west there s documented footage of what appears to be a drip line from what understand a technique used in firefighting mean it was very very clear that that brush fire that happened was an act of arson by unknown individuals given recent events with dakota access worker given escalation of law enforcement that know a lot of fingers are pointing towards dakota access as being a culprit behind this late fire and thank god that wind was pushing away from camp fire spread pretty large post armed dakota access contractor accused of trying to infiltrate water protectors appeared first shadowproof

everything umiyak kasi soap to makakarelate talaga lahat bawat character namin simulang kuwento aiko ako talaga tinamaan dahil nga maagang kinuha akin panginoon tatay ko pero masaya ako dahil alam ko while im doing this soap gift giver alam kong this christmas papa jimi melendez ko po proud po akin dahil tinanggap ko po ako naman pinipigilpigilan ko rin umiyak kasi papa naman passed away when im and sister passed away about years before that sabi naman dimples and this project really for some reason hindi ko nga alam kung ako lang maemosyon dahil buntis ako pasensiya po ha ano kasi to very dear to heart and in fact wasnt expecting to do a teleserye until gave birth was telling them nga production kasi buntis nga ako so tinayming ko patapos yung taon wala akong gagawin but with this project it just reminded of how much pain still have of losing papa kasi everyday tinitake for granted lang natin nandidiyan lang magulang natin kasi busy tayo sarili nating pamilya kailangan nating gawin sarili natin and when this project came along parang siguro this papas way of telling know its christmas and miss something like that this project hits mark of heart of filipino family whether lost someone didnt lose someone fact miss somebody some point in life tuwing pasko yun eh bawat isa dito mayroon kang konekta wala namang maikuwentong sakit nararamdaman sina carlo louise dahil pareho pang kumpleto pamilya tinanong sina aiko dimples panay punas mata kung naging mabuting anak ba sila kung may pagsisisi ba sila ngayong wala kanilang ama po ba workshop natawang tanong dimples ako kasi lang nang maulila ama so can imagine really started working kasi mama wanted to work work and papa had path magschool muna ako ha tigil muna ako magartista sabi niya papa okayokay two months bago ako magcollege nawala papa actually aneurysm siya thats why maraming regrets akin kasi hindi ako nakapaggoodbye so tuwing may ganitong kuwento malapit talaga puso ko and siyempre atin bilang artista mas gusto ko po maibigay yung totoong nararamdaman ko so yung regrets ko lang as a daughter hindi lang enough akala ko kasi dami kong oras dahil akala ko marami akong oras hinayaan ko lang hindi ako masyadong tumawag hindi ako masyadong nangumusta so yun lang naman yung regrets laking pasalamat ko kasi bawat ginagawa kong eksena ngayon bago ko iarte or whatever always have a silent corner papa want to know if do this will make him proud if do this matutuwa ba siya akin ano bang sasabihin niya akin so kahit wala siya buhay buhay siya akin naiiyak kuwento aktres madamdamin naging relasyon aiko ama dahil tatlong buwan lang niya itong nakasama ako kasi open book naman yung naging relationship ko dad ko jimi melendez hindi kami naging okay nung una pero yung regret ko noong naging okay kami three months after kinuha siya lord buhay ko unang pahayag aktres naghong kong kami nandoon yung bonding namin tatay ko ulit yung regret ko yung kuwestiyon ko kaya nga siguro yung pagkakaiba ko personality hanggang ngayon yung bakit ganun kung kailan lahat okay doon pa kinuha papa sabi aiko nasabayan pagtulo luha saka yung papa ko hindi naman siya namatay dahil sakit eh namatay papa ko nanonood pelikula ko cinema one pinapanood niya yung mmk movie nachoke yung papa ko dahil naiyak siya eksena movie so missed him by five minutes hospital so yung pakiramdam ko ganun pala ako kamahal tatay ko akala ko kasi nung iniwan niya kami nung bata kami pinagpalit ibang pamilya dinenay may anak siya kasi di ba dati uso kapag matinee idol ka dapat wala kang anak thats why nga nung una akong ilaunch paredes aking apelyido hindi melendez kasi nga sabi tatay ko hindi kami puwedeng malaman kami yung unang pamilya so hindi naging maganda yung relationship namin pero nung okay sabi ko magiging mabuting anak ako kasi mahal ko tatay ko kasi naman itsura pa lang halata naman pinagbiyak kaming bunga female version ako ako tatay ko tapos nung kinuha nga yung tatay ko nanonood pelikula ko so
